SSC CGL Exam Syllabus & Pattern 2026

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) conducts the Combined Graduate Level (CGL) exam to recruit officers for Group B and Group C posts in various ministries and departments of the Government of India. Below is the detailed syllabus and pattern for Tier 1 and Tier 2.

Tier 1 Exam Pattern

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: 25 Questions (50 Marks)
  • General Awareness: 25 Questions (50 Marks)
  • Quantitative Aptitude: 25 Questions (50 Marks)
  • English Comprehension: 25 Questions (50 Marks)

Total Time Duration: 60 minutes. There is a negative marking of 0.50 marks for each wrong answer.

Tier 2 Exam Pattern

Tier 2 consists of Paper-I (Compulsory for all posts) and Paper-II/III for specialized posts. Paper-I includes Mathematical Abilities, Reasoning, English Language, General Awareness, and Computer Knowledge modules.

title: HSSC PST Admit Card 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: HSSC PST Admit Card 2026 - Download Link, Steps & Exam Details for Police Constable Posts Advertisement The Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC) has released the Physical Scre
jobDescription:
HSSC PST Admit Card 2026 - Download Link, Steps & Exam Details for Police Constable Posts

Advertisement

The Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC) has released the Physical Screening Test (PST) admit card for Police Constable recruitment 2026, conducted under Advertisement No. 01/Candidates who qualified the earlier stages of selection can now download their PST hall ticket from the official admit card portal at adv012026.hryssc.com using their CET registration number or roll number along with their date of birth or password. The hall ticket carries essential exam-day details such as the candidate's name, roll number, exam date, reporting time, and venue address, and must be carried to the test centre along with a valid photo ID.

What Is the HSSC PST Admit Card 2026?

This section explains what the PST admit card is and why it matters for Police Constable candidates in Haryana.

The PST (Physical Screening Test) admit card is an official hall ticket issued by HSSC to candidates who have cleared the preceding stages of the Haryana Police Constable recruitment process. It is a mandatory document for entry into the test venue, as candidates without a valid admit card and photo ID are not permitted to appear for the test. The admit card confirms the candidate's allotted date, batch, reporting time, and test centre for the Physical Screening Test.

Also Read: UP GIC Lecturer Answer Key 2026

HSSC Police Constable PST Admit Card 2026: Key Details

This section gives a quick overview of the recruitment in table form for easy reference.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC)
Post Name Police Constable (General Duty)
Advertisement No. 01/2026
Test Stage Physical Screening Test (PST)
hssc.gov.in
Login Credentials Required CET Registration Number/Roll Number + Date of Birth/Password
Mandatory Documents Admit Card + Valid Photo ID

How to Download the HSSC PST Admit Card 2026

This section walks candidates through the exact steps to download their hall ticket from the official portal.

Candidates can follow these simple steps to download their PST admit card:

  1. Visit the official admit card portal at adv012026.hryssc.com, or go to hssc.gov.in and look for the relevant admit card notice.
  2. Locate and click on the link for the "PST Admit Card" related to Advertisement No. 01/2026.
  3. Enter your login credentials CET Registration Number/Roll Number and Date of Birth/Password.
  4. Submit the details to view your admit card on the screen.
  5. Check all details carefully, then download and take a clear printout for the exam day.

Direct Link to Download HSSC PST Admit Card 2026

This section provides the verified official link candidates can use to access their hall ticket.

Candidates can use the link below to reach the official login page for the PST admit card:

HSSC PST Admit Card 2026 – Click Here to Download

This is the same dedicated recruitment portal used by HSSC for Advertisement No. 01/2026 and is the official source for downloading the hall ticket.

What Is the Physical Screening Test (PST)?

This section explains the purpose of the PST stage within the overall Constable selection process.

The Physical Screening Test is a qualifying stage in the Haryana Police Constable selection process, conducted after the Physical Measurement Test (PMT), which checks height and chest measurements. The PST assesses the physical fitness and endurance of candidates through a timed running race, and only those who clear this stage move forward in the recruitment process toward the Knowledge Test (written exam), followed by Document Verification and Medical Examination.

Also Read: SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026

Documents to Carry for the PST Exam

This section lists what candidates must bring to the test centre to avoid being denied entry.

  • Printed copy of the PST admit card
  • A valid original photo ID proof
  • Any additional documents specifically mentioned on the admit card

Candidates should also avoid carrying banned items such as mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators, and other electronic or Bluetooth devices, as these are not allowed inside the exam premises.

What to Do If There Is an Error on Your Admit Card

This section explains the recommended action if candidates spot a mistake in their hall ticket details.

If a candidate notices any discrepancy in their name, photograph, roll number, or other details printed on the admit card, they should immediately contact the HSSC helpline or visit the hssc.gov.in for the correction procedure or grievance redressal contact information.

description: SEBI Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026 - Download Officer Grade A Interview Call Letter Advertisement The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has released the Admit Card
jobDescription:
SEBI Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026 - Download Officer Grade A Interview Call Letter

Advertisement

The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has released the Admit Card / Call Letter for the SEBI Assistant Manager (Officer Grade A) 2025 Recruitment Exercise - Interview Round. Eligible candidates who have cleared the earlier stages of the selection process can now download their SEBI Assistant Manager Interview Call Letter directly from the official SEBI website at https://sebi.gov.in/. The call letter is a mandatory document to carry to the interview venue, and no candidate will be permitted to appear without it.

What Is the SEBI Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026?

The SEBI Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026 is the official interview call letter issued by SEBI to candidates who have successfully qualified the previous rounds of the Officer Grade A (Assistant Manager) Recruitment Exercise This call letter serves as an entry pass for the interview stage and contains critical information such as the candidate's name, roll number, interview date, time, and venue. Candidates must download and print this document before attending the interview.

Key Highlights - SEBI Officer Grade A Call Letter 2026

Here is a quick overview of the most important details related to the SEBI Assistant Manager Interview Admit Card 2026.

Detail Information
Conducting Body Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
Post Name Officer Grade A (Assistant Manager)
Recruitment Exercise Year 2025
Stage of Admit Card Interview / Call Letter
Admit Card Status Available for Download
Official Download Link Click Here
Login Credentials Required Roll Number + Registration Number + Mobile/Email OTP
Category Admit Card / Hall Ticket

How to Download SEBI Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026

Downloading the SEBI Officer Grade A Call Letter 2026 is a simple process that takes only a few minutes. Follow the step-by-step instructions below to access and save your interview call letter from the official SEBI portal.

  1. Visit the Official SEBI Website: Go toon your browser.
  2. Enter Your Roll Number: Type your Roll Number in the designated field exactly as provided during .
  3. Enter Your Registration Number: Fill in the Registration Number you received at the time of application.
  4. Select Validation Field: Choose your preferred validation method - either Mobile Number or Email ID (the same one used during registration).
  5. Enter Mobile Number or Email ID: Based on your selection, enter the registered mobile number or email ID.
  6. Complete the CAPTCHA: Fill in the captcha code displayed on the page (without spaces). A text-to-speech option is also available for accessibility.
  7. Click Submit: Press the Submit button to proceed.
  8. Download and Print: Once your details are verified, your SEBI Assistant Manager Interview Call Letter will appear on screen. Download it as a PDF and take a clear printout.

Direct Link to Download SEBI Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026

Candidates can access the official SEBI call letter download page directly using the link below. This is the only authorized portal for downloading the admit card -candidates are advised not to rely on any third-party sources.

Click Here to Download SEBI Officer Grade A Interview Call Letter 2026

Information Mentioned on SEBI Assistant Manager Call Letter

The SEBI Grade A Interview Call Letter contains several important details that candidates must verify immediately after downloading. Any discrepancy should be reported to SEBI's Human Resources Department without delay.

  • Candidate's Full Name
  • Roll Number
  • Registration Number
  • Post Applied For (Officer Grade A - Assistant Manager)
  • Interview Date and Time
  • Interview Venue / Reporting Address
  • Important Instructions for the Interview Day
  • Documents Required for Verification

Documents to Carry Along With SEBI Admit Card 2026

Candidates must carry their printed SEBI Assistant Manager Call Letter along with a set of supporting identity and educational documents to the interview venue. The following documents are generally required for SEBI recruitment interviews.

  • Printed copy of the SEBI Officer Grade A Interview Call Letter
  • Original and self-attested photocopy of a valid photo ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Passport, Voter ID, or Driving Licence)
  • Recent passport-size photographs (as specified in the call letter)
  • Original and photocopies of educational qualification certificates
  • Original and photocopies of category/caste certificates (if applicable)
  • Experience certificates (if applicable to the post/stream)
  • Any other documents mentioned in the official call letter

Important Tips for SEBI Interview Admit Card Download

Keep the following points in mind to ensure a hassle-free download experience for your SEBI Grade A Call Letter 2026.

  • Use the same mobile number or email ID that you registered with during the application process.
  • Enter all credentials carefully and correctly to avoid login errors.
  • If the CAPTCHA is not visible, refresh the page or use the text-to-speech option available on the portal.
  • Save the downloaded call letter as a PDF and keep both a digital copy and a physical printout.
  • Carry a color printout of the call letter for best results at the venue.
  • Do not share your login credentials with anyone.
  • In case of login issues, contact SEBI's Human Resources Department directly through the .

title: Pawan Hans Various Vacancies Online Form 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: Pawan Hans Limited, a Miniratna Government of India helicopter carrier enterprise, has invited applications for 22 vacancies across 7 posts including Assistant (HR&A), Junior Engin
jobDescription:
Pawan Hans Limited, a Miniratna Government of India helicopter carrier enterprise, has invited applications for 22 vacancies across 7 posts including Assistant (HR&A), Junior Engineer (Civil), Quality Assistant, Assistant (CAMO), Station-in-Charge, and Trainee Technician. All posts are based in Delhi, Northern Region, with applications open now and closing on 10 July Both regular and contractual positions are on offer, with pay ranging from Rs. 16,500 to Rs. 27,000 basic per month.

Pawan Hans Recruitment 2026 - Overview

Advertisement
Particulars Details
Organisation Pawan Hans Limited (A Miniratna Government of India Enterprise)
Total Posts 7 categories, 22 vacancies
Location Delhi, Northern Region
Engagement Type Mix of Regular and Contractual basis
Application Mode Online (plus signed hard copy by post)
Application Start 19 June 2026
Last Date to Apply 10 July 2026
Application Fee Rs. 354 (inclusive of GST), exempted for SC/ST and PwD
www.pawanhans.co.in

About Pawan Hans Limited

Candidates wondering about the recruiting organisation can note that Pawan Hans is a Government of India helicopter transport enterprise that operates a fleet of more than 50 helicopters ranging from 5 to 26 seaters. As a Miniratna PSU under the Ministry of Civil Aviation, Pawan Hans offers a professional aviation work environment along with an attractive compensation package, including PF, Gratuity, Leave Encashment, and Pension benefits as applicable to each post.

  • Download Pawan Hans Recruitment 2026 Notification

Post-Wise Vacancy Breakdown (22 Posts)

Candidates checking how the 22 vacancies are distributed can refer to the table below, which lists every post, its engagement basis, and number of openings. The largest single opportunity is for Trainee Technician with 13 vacancies, making it the most accessible entry point for fresher diploma and AME-qualified candidates.

Post Post Code Engagement Vacancies
Assistant (HR&A) PHL/NR/HR/ASST.(HR&A)/REGULAR/2026 Regular 01
Junior Engineer (Civil) PHL/NR/HR/JE (CIVIL)/2026 Contract 01
Quality Assistant PHL/NR/HR/QA/2026 Contract 02
Assistant (CAMO) PHL/NR/HR/ASST. (CAMO)/2026 Contract 02
Assistant (HR&A) PHL/NR/HR/ASST. (HR&A)/CONTRACTUAL/2026 Contract 01
Station-in-Charge PHL/NR/HR/STATION-IN-CHARGE/2026 Contract 02
Trainee Technician PHL/NR/HR/TR. TECH/2026 Contract 13
Total 22

All posts are based in Delhi, Northern Region, with Station-in-Charge positions falling under the administrative control of the Delhi office.

Eligibility Criteria for Each Post

Eligibility for Pawan Hans Recruitment 2026 varies significantly by post, ranging from a basic graduate degree to specialised aviation diplomas. The breakdown below covers the exact qualification and experience required for each role.

Assistant (HR&A) - Regular and Contractual

For the Regular basis post, candidates need a Graduate Degree in any discipline with 3 years of post-qualification experience in the relevant field, OR an MBA/PG Degree/2-year Diploma in HR/HRM/HRD/PM & IR with 1 year of post-qualification experience. For the Contractual basis post, the requirement is Graduation with 3 years post-qualification experience, OR Graduation with a 2-year Diploma in IR&PM plus 1 year of relevant experience.

Junior Engineer (Civil) - Contract Basis

This post requires a Diploma in Civil Engineering from a recognised institute, with a minimum of 5 years of post-qualification experience in preparing estimates, Bill of Quantity (BoQ), and tenders for repair and maintenance work. Experience in civil maintenance of large buildings, malls, metro stations, and high-rise apartments is required, and knowledge of computers is preferable.

Quality Assistant and Assistant (CAMO) - Contract Basis

Both these aviation-focused roles require a Diploma in AME/Aeronautical or BSc in Aviation, along with 3 years of aviation experience - specifically in Quality CAR-145 for the Quality Assistant post, and in CAMO (Continuing Airworthiness Management Organisation) for the Assistant (CAMO) post.

Station-in-Charge - Contract Basis

Candidates need a Graduate degree in any discipline with 3 years of relevant post-qualification experience, OR a PG Degree/2-year Diploma in Marketing/Finance/Aviation or equivalent with 1 year of relevant post-qualification experience.

Trainee Technician - Contract Basis

This is a fresher-friendly entry-level post requiring a Diploma in Mechanical/Aeronautical/Electronics/Electricals/Electronics & Communication/Instrumentation, OR an AME Course from a DGCA-recognised institute, OR an Aircraft Engineering Maintenance Course in B1.3 or B2 Category from a DGCA-recognised institute. Freshers may apply, and preference is given to candidates with a B2 Category AME qualification or those who have passed DGCA Modules.

Age Limit

The upper age limit differs between the experienced posts and the Trainee Technician post, both calculated as on the application closing date.

Post Upper Age Limit (as on 10.07.2026)
Assistant (HR&A) - Regular & Contractual 28 Years
Junior Engineer (Civil) 28 Years
Quality Assistant 28 Years
Assistant (CAMO) 28 Years
Station-in-Charge 28 Years
Trainee Technician 25 Years

Reservation and age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PwD candidates will apply as per Government of India directives.

Salary and Pay Scale

Candidates asking about the salary structure can note that Pawan Hans offers Industrial Dearness Allowance (IDA)-linked pay scales for most posts, along with PF, Gratuity, and Leave Encashment benefits as applicable to regular and contractual roles respectively.

Post Pay Scale (IDA) Basic Pay Approx. CTC
Assistant (HR&A) - Regular WC-5/C-3 (Rs. 24,000 - 1,07,000) Rs. 24,000 Rs. 5.83 lakhs
Junior Engineer (Civil) WC-7/C-5 (Rs. 27,000 - 1,13,000) Rs. 27,000 Rs. 5.91 lakhs
Quality Assistant WC-5/J-3 (Rs. 24,000 - 1,07,000) Rs. 24,000 Rs. 5.83 lakhs
Assistant (CAMO) WC-5/J-3 (Rs. 24,000 - 1,07,000) Rs. 24,000 Rs. 5.83 lakhs
Assistant (HR&A) - Contractual WC-5/C-3 (Rs. 24,000 - 1,07,000) Rs. 24,000 Rs. 5.83 lakhs
Station-in-Charge WC-5/C-3 (Rs. 24,000 - 1,07,000) Rs. 24,000 Rs. 5.83 lakhs
Trainee Technician All Inclusive Rs. 16,500 -

The Regular basis Assistant (HR&A) post additionally carries Pension benefits along with PF, Gratuity, and Leave Encashment, which distinguishes it from the contractual roles.

Pawan Hans Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

The application fee for Pawan Hans Recruitment 2026 is a flat amount applicable across posts, with exemptions for specific reserved categories. Payment can be made online, or via Demand Draft for offline applicants.

Category Fee
General/OBC/EWS Candidates Rs. 354 (inclusive of 18% GST)
SC/ST Candidates Exempted
Persons with Disabilities (PwD) Exempted

If the fee is paid online, candidates submitting offline applications do not need to attach a Demand Draft. The Demand Draft, where applicable, must be drawn in favour of "Pawan Hans Limited", payable at Delhi, and is non-refundable.

How to Apply for Pawan Hans Recruitment 2026

Pawan Hans accepts applications through a combined online-plus-postal process, meaning an online submission alone is not sufficient - a signed hard copy must also reach the office by the deadline. Candidates unable to can also use an offline route.

  1. Visit the Careers tab on www.pawanhans.co.in and .
  2. Take a printout of the duly filled and signed online application form.
  3. Affix a recent passport-size photograph on the printed form.
  4. Attach self-attested copies of testimonials supporting age, caste/class, qualification, experience, Pay/CTC, and licence/medical status.
  5. Send the complete application through proper channel to reach the office on or before 10 July 2026.

Offline Application Process (For Candidates Unable to )

Candidates who cannot complete the online application may instead download the Application Blank Form from www.pawanhans.co.in, fill and sign it, affix a photograph, attach self-attested testimonials, and include a Demand Draft of Rs. 354 (unless fee is paid online) drawn in favour of Pawan Hans Limited, payable at Delhi.

Postal Address for Application Submission

Detail Information
Addressee JGM (HR&A), Pawan Hans Limited
Office Northern Region, Rohini Heliport, Sector 36, Rohini, New Delhi - 110085
Envelope Instruction Superscribe the post applied for on the envelope
Last Date for Receipt 10 July 2026

Candidates applying for more than one post must submit a separate application for each post - failing to do so means the application will only be considered for the first post mentioned.

Documents Required for Application

Candidates preparing their application should keep self-attested copies of the following documents ready, since applications without supporting proof are liable to rejection.

  • Proof of age (e.g., Matriculation certificate)
  • Proof of caste/class certificate, where applicable
  • Educational qualification certificates matching the post applied for
  • Experience certificates showing post-qualification experience (teaching experience and training/induction periods are not counted)
  • Proof of Pay/CTC in current or previous employment
  • Licence/medical status documents, particularly for aviation-related posts like Quality Assistant, Assistant (CAMO), and Trainee Technician

All qualifications must be from Universities/Institutions recognised by UGC/AICTE or other appropriate statutory authorities in India.

Selection Process and General Conditions

Pawan Hans follows a merit-based shortlisting process rather than calling every eligible applicant, giving the management discretion over each stage of recruitment.

  • The management will call only candidates ranking highest on eligibility criteria - mere eligibility does not guarantee a test call.
  • The number of candidates called for test may be restricted based on the response received for each post.
  • The management reserves the right to reject applications, cancel posts, or relax age/experience/qualification criteria at its discretion.
  • Only post-qualification experience is counted - teaching experience and induction/training periods are excluded.
  • Candidates called for the test must produce all original documents at the time of test; failure to do so means they will not be permitted to appear, and travelling allowance will not be reimbursed.
  • Outstation candidates called for the test are reimbursed 2nd Class Rail/Bus fare by the shortest route, subject to proof of journey.
  • All appointments are subject to medical fitness as per Pawan Hans Limited rules.
  • Selected candidates may be transferred anywhere in India or abroad at the Management's discretion.
  • Persons with disability of not less than 40% as per GOI guidelines are eligible for PwD benefits.

Common Mistakes Candidates Should Avoid

Many applicants risk rejection due to procedural errors rather than lack of eligibility. Reviewing these common pitfalls before applying can help ensure a smooth submission.

  • Forgetting the postal submission step: Applying online alone is not enough - the signed printout with photograph and testimonials must physically reach the Delhi office by 10 July 2026.
  • Submitting one application for multiple posts: Candidates interested in more than one post must submit a separate application for each, or only the first post mentioned will be considered.
  • Skipping self-attestation: Submitting unattested or incomplete testimonial copies can lead to outright rejection of the application.
  • Ignoring the Demand Draft requirement for offline applications: Offline applicants who haven't paid online must enclose a Rs. 354 DD in favour of Pawan Hans Limited - applications without proof of fee payment are liable to rejection (except for exempted SC/ST/PwD candidates).
  • Counting ineligible experience: Teaching experience and induction/training periods do not count toward the required post-qualification experience - only genuine post-qualification work experience is considered.
  • Missing original documents at the test stage: Candidates who don't bring original documents (qualification, experience, Pay/CTC, reservation proof) to the test will not be permitted to appear and will lose travel allowance reimbursement.
  • Relying on newspaper notices for updates: Pawan Hans publishes all corrigenda, addenda, or cancellations only on its website, not in newspapers, so candidates must check www.pawanhans.co.in regularly.

Pawan Hans Recruitment 2026 - PDF Download Link

The complete recruitment details for all 7 posts, including post codes, qualifications, and general conditions, are available on the official Pawan Hans careers page.

Pawan Hans Recruitment 2026 Notification Click Here

Eligible candidates must apply through the official Pawan Hans website and then complete the mandatory postal submission step to finalize their application.

title: BEL Project Engineer Recruitment 2026: for 9 Project Engineer-I Posts, Salary Up to ₹55,000/Month
description: BEL Project Engineer-I Recruitment 2026: A Strong Opening for Experienced Engineers Great news for experienced engineering graduates! Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) , a Navratna
jobDescription:
BEL Project Engineer-I Recruitment 2026: A Strong Opening for Experienced Engineers

Great news for experienced engineering graduates! Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL), a Navratna company under the Ministry of Defence, has released a fresh notification to recruit 9 Project Engineer-I posts for its Naval Systems (S&CS) division. These are contract roles based at major cities like New Delhi, Vizag, Kolkata, Kochi, Chennai and Mumbai, with a consolidated salary rising up to ₹55,000 per month. If you hold a BE/B.Tech degree and have project experience, this is a wonderful chance to work with one of India’s top PSU defence employers.

The online application window is open from 17 June 2026 to 4 July 2026, so don’t wait till the last day. This guide covers everything in simple language — vacancy details, eligibility, salary, selection process, fees and how to . Read it fully before filling your form, and bookmark so you never miss an important update.

Key Highlights — BEL Project Engineer 2026

BEL is India’s premier professional electronics company, and a Project Engineer-I role here gives you valuable defence-sector experience. Here are the most important points of this recruitment at a glance, so you can quickly decide whether to .

  • Organisation: Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL), Bengaluru Complex
  • Post Name: Project Engineer-I for NS (S&CS)
  • Total Vacancies: 9 posts
  • Salary / Pay: ₹40,000 to ₹55,000 per month (consolidated)
  • Job Location: New Delhi, Gurgaon, Gandhinagar, Vizag, Kolkata, Kochi, Chennai, Port Blair and Mumbai
  • Last Date to Apply: 4 July 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — BEL Project Engineer-I 2026

The 9 vacancies are divided across two post codes based on the engineering specialisation. The table below shows the category-wise reservation for each code so you can check where you fit.

As per government rules, 4% of posts are reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). Where reservation is not available for a category, candidates may still apply for an unreserved post if they meet the eligibility.

Eligibility Criteria — BEL Project Engineer 2026

Before applying, make sure you meet both the education and age conditions. BEL is strict about the branch on your degree matching the post code, so read this part carefully.

Educational Qualification

You need a full-time BE/B.Tech with at least a Pass Class. The eligible branches differ slightly by post code:

  • PRE-I (SSI-2026): Electronics & Communication, Electronics, Telecommunication, Communication, Electrical & Electronics, Electrical, Mechanical, or Computer Science.
  • PRE-I (TSCS-2026): Electronics & Communication, Electronics, Telecommunication, Communication, Electrical & Electronics, Electrical, Electronics & Instrumentation, or Instrumentation.

If you are an engineering graduate looking for government jobs, check the full branch list in the PDF.

Age Limit (as on 01.06.2026)

The upper age limit is 32 years for General and EWS candidates. Age relaxation applies as per rules:

  • OBC candidates: up to 3 years (max 35 years)
  • SC / ST candidates: up to 5 years (max 37 years)
  • PwBD candidates: 10 years extra, over and above the above relaxation

Work Experience

You must have a minimum of 2 years of post-qualification experience in installation and commissioning of electronic or electrical systems projects. Experience gained before completing your degree, teaching/research work, and internships do not count.

What Is the Salary for BEL Project Engineer 2026?

BEL pays a competitive consolidated salary that increases every year of the contract. This is one of the most attractive parts of the job, especially for early-career engineers building experience.

  • 1st Year: ₹40,000 per month
  • 2nd Year: ₹45,000 per month
  • 3rd Year: ₹50,000 per month
  • 4th Year: ₹55,000 per month

Selected engineers also receive a consolidated ₹12,000 per year towards medical insurance, life insurance, uniform and footwear, plus a retention bonus of ₹1,00,000 payable on completing the full 4-year tenure.

The engagement is on a temporary basis for an initial period of 3 years, extendable up to a maximum of 4 years based on project requirement and performance.

What Is the Selection Process for BEL Project Engineer 2026?

BEL follows a simple, merit-based two-stage process. Understanding it early helps you prepare in the right direction. You can also review the exam syllabus and pattern pages for general guidance.

  1. Written Test carrying 85 marks for candidates who meet the eligibility criteria.
  2. Interview carrying 15 marks, for which shortlisted candidates are called category-wise in the ratio of 1:5 based on vacancies.

The minimum qualifying marks in the Written Test and Interview are 35% for General/EWS/OBC and 30% for SC/ST/PwBD. The venue for the test and interview will be intimated later by email.

How to for BEL Project Engineer 2026?

Applying is fully online — no hardcopy applications are accepted. Follow these steps carefully and submit before the last date.

  1. Visit the official BEL careers portal (link available in Essential Links below).
  2. Open the relevant advertisement for Project Engineer-I, NS (S&CS) and click the application .
  3. Pay the application fee through SBI Collect (online or at any SBI branch) and note the SBI Collect Reference Number.
  4. Fill the online form correctly, enter the SBI reference number, and upload all required documents.
  5. Verify every detail, submit the form, and save a printout for your records.

Important Dates — BEL Project Engineer 2026

Mark these dates in your calendar so you complete every step on time. Late applications will not be accepted.

Application Fees — BEL Project Engineer 2026

The application fee is to be paid online through SBI Collect. Once paid, the fee is non-refundable, so apply only after confirming your eligibility.

While paying through SBI Collect, make sure you use the same mobile number and email ID that you entered in the online application form.

Documents Required — BEL Project Engineer 2026

Keep scanned copies of these documents ready before you start filling the form, so the process is smooth and quick.

  • SSLC/Matriculation certificate (as proof of date of birth)
  • 12th/Diploma marks card or certificate
  • BE/B.Tech marks cards of all semesters and the provisional/final degree certificate
  • Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS or PwBD) in the prescribed format, where applicable
  • Post-qualification work experience certificate from previous/current employer
  • No Objection Certificate (if currently working in a PSU/Government organisation)
  • Identity proof (Aadhaar card / Driving Licence / Voter ID)

Essential Links — BEL Project Engineer 2026

  • Page
  • for BEL Project Engineer 2026
  • Visit BEL

This BEL Project Engineer-I recruitment is a solid opportunity to gain defence-sector project experience with a respected Navratna PSU, with a salary that grows to ₹55,000 a month plus a retention bonus. If this role matches your branch and experience, apply well before 4 July For more defence and PSU job notifications, keep visiting every day.

A1. A total of 538 vacancies have been announced for various permanent posts.

Q2. What is the last date to apply for UPSC Advertisement No. 06/2026?

A2. The last date to is 3 July 2026 (18:00 hours).

Q3. Is there any written examination for these posts?

A3. Recruitment is being done by selection method. UPSC will decide the selection process for each post.

Q4. Are UT Ladakh posts open for all candidates?

A4. No. All posts under UT Ladakh are exclusively reserved for Domiciles of UT Ladakh only.

Q5. What is the application fee?

A5. ₹25 for General/OBC/EWS male candidates. Female, SC, ST and PwBD candidates are exempted from fee.

title: SSC CGL 2026 Recruitment 2026

jobDescription:
SSC CGL 2026: for Approx. 12,256 Group B & C Vacancies. The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has officially released the SSC CGL 2026 notification for the Combined Graduate Level Examination 2026 to fill various Group ‘B’ and Group ‘C’ posts in different Ministries/Departments/Organizations of Government of India and various Constitutional Bodies/Statutory Bodies/Tribunals, etc. This is one of the largest graduate level recruitment opportunities in 2026.

The online application started on 21 May 2026 and the last date to is 22 June 2026 (23:00 hours). Eligible male and female candidates can apply through the https://ssc.gov.in.

SSC CGL Recruitment 2026 – 12256 Posts

Examination Name Combined Graduate Level Examination 2026 (SSC CGL 2026)
Total Vacancies Approx. 12,256 Posts
Post Names Various Group B & Group C Posts (Assistant Audit Officer, Assistant Section Officer, Inspector of Income Tax, Sub Inspector, Auditor, Tax Assistant, etc.)

Application Fee ₹100 (Women/SC/ ST/ PwBD/ ESM – Exempted)

✅ SSC CGL 2026 Salary & Benefits

Salary varies according to Pay Level of the post:

  • Pay Level-8: ₹47,600 – ₹1,51,100 (Assistant Audit Officer, Assistant Accounts Officer)
  • Pay Level-7: ₹44,900 – ₹1,42,400 (Assistant Section Officer, Inspector of Income Tax, Assistant Enforcement Officer, Sub Inspector in CBI, etc.)
  • Pay Level-6: ₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400 (Assistant, Executive Assistant, Research Assistant, Divisional Accountant, Sub Inspector in NIA, Junior Statistical Officer, etc.)
  • Pay Level-5: ₹29,200 – ₹92,300 (Auditor, Accountant)
  • Pay Level-4: ₹25,500 – ₹81,100 (Postal Assistant, Senior Secretariat Assistant, Tax Assistant, etc.)

Selected candidates will also get Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance, Transport Allowance, and other Central Government benefits as per rules.

✅ SSC CGL 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Age Limit (as on 01.08.2026):

  • Generally 18-30 years. Age varies by post – 18-27 years for some Group C posts, 20-30 years for some Group B posts, and 18-32 years for Junior Statistical Officer.
  • Age Relaxation: SC/ST – 5 years, OBC – 3 years, PwBD – 10 years (13 years for OBC, 15 years for SC/ST), Ex-Servicemen – 3 years after deduction of military service, Defence Personnel (disabled) – 3 years (8 years for SC/ST).

Educational Qualification:

  • For most posts: Bachelor’s Degree from a recognized University.
  • Assistant Audit Officer / Assistant Accounts Officer: Bachelor’s Degree + desirable CA/CMA/CS/M.Com/MBA (Finance) etc. Candidates have to qualify Subordinate Audit/Accounts Service Examination during probation.
  • Junior Statistical Officer: Bachelor’s Degree with 60% in Mathematics in 12th OR Statistics as a subject in graduation.
  • Statistical Investigator Grade-II: Bachelor’s Degree in Statistics/Mathematics/Economics/Demography/Computer Science/Data Science/AI etc.
  • For Assistant Audit Officer (State Cadre) & Assistant Accounts Officer (State Cadre): Additional proficiency in the Regional/Official language of the concerned State is required.

✅ SSC CGL 2026 Selection Process

  1. Tier-I Examination (Computer Based Test)
  2. Tier-II Examination (Computer Based Test)
  3. Document Verification (DV)

Subject Questions Marks

Total 100 200

Note: Negative marking of 0.50 marks for each wrong answer.

Tier-II Examination

Paper-I (Compulsory for all posts)

Session Section Subject Questions Marks
Session-I (2 hours 15 minutes) Section-I Mathematical Abilities 30 180
Reasoning & General Intelligence 30
Section-II English Language & Comprehension 45
Session-II General Awareness 25
Session-II (15 minutes) Section-III Computer Knowledge Test 20 60
Session-II (15 minutes) Section-IV Data Entry Speed Test One Data Entry Task Qualifying

✅Important Dates for SSC CGL 2026

Last Date to 22 June 2026 (23:00 hours)

Application Correction Window 29 June 2026 to 01 July 2026
Tier-I Examination (Tentative) August – September 2026
Tier-II Examination (Tentative) December 2026

✅Application Fee

  • General / OBC / EWS : ₹100
  • Women candidates, SC, ST, PwBD and Ex-Servicemen : Exempted (No fee)
  • Fee can be paid online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or Debit Cards (Visa/Mastercard/Maestro/RuPay).

✅How to Apply for SSC CGL Recruitment 2026

Q1. How many vacancies are announced in SSC CGL 2026?

A1. Approx. 12,256 vacancies have been announced for various Group B & Group C posts.

Q2. What is the last date to apply for SSC CGL 2026?

A2. The last date to is 22 June 2026 (23:00 hours).

Q3. What is the educational qualification required for SSC CGL 2026?

A3. Bachelor’s Degree from a recognized University. Specific additional qualifications are required for AAO, JSO and Statistical Investigator posts.

Q4. What is the salary in SSC CGL 2026?

A4. Salary ranges from Pay Level-4 to Pay Level-8 (₹25,500 – ₹1,51,100) depending on the post.

Q5. What is the selection process for SSC CGL 2026?

title: SBI PO Recruitment 2026: 1500 Probationary Officer Posts
description: SBI PO 2026 Recruitment: Eligibility, Salary, Exam Pattern & Online Application Guide Good news for all banking job aspirants! The State Bank of India (SBI) has released the much-a
jobDescription:
SBI PO 2026 Recruitment: Eligibility, Salary, Exam Pattern & Online Application Guide

Good news for all banking job aspirants! The State Bank of India (SBI) has released the much-awaited SBI PO 2026 notification for the post of Probationary Officer (PO). This year SBI is recruiting for a huge 1,500 vacancies, making it one of the biggest banking job opportunities of the year. A career as an SBI PO is a dream for lakhs of graduates — it brings respect, job security, and a starting CTC of around ₹21.97 lakh per year. If you are a graduate, this is a wonderful chance to join the country’s largest bank.

The online application starts on 18 June 2026 and the last date to apply is 8 July 2026, so you have limited time to fill the form. Any candidate with a graduation degree in any discipline can apply, even final-year students. In this article we will explain everything in very simple language — vacancy details, eligibility, salary, exam pattern, selection process, important dates, and the step-by-step apply process. Read this full guide carefully so you don’t miss any point. For trusted job updates, keep following .

SBI PO 2026 Key Highlights — Vacancy, Salary & Important Dates

The State Bank of India is the largest public sector bank in India, and its Probationary Officer exam is conducted by the Central Recruitment & Promotion Department (CRPD). This recruitment (Advt. No. CRPD/PO/2026-27/09) aims to fill 1,500 Probationary Officer posts across the country. Here are the most important details at a glance:

  • Post Name: Probationary Officer (PO)
  • Total Vacancies: 1,500 posts
  • Salary / Pay Scale: ₹48,480 – ₹85,920 per month (JMGS-I)
  • Job Location: All India
  • Application Start Date: 18 June 2026
  • Last Date to Apply: 8 July 2026

SBI PO 2026 Vacancy Breakdown — Category-Wise Posts

The total of 1,500 vacancies includes 1,446 regular posts and 54 backlog posts. The category-wise distribution is given below:

A 4% horizontal reservation is provided for Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD), covering visual, hearing, locomotor and other listed disabilities. These vacancies are provisional and may change as per the bank’s actual requirement.

What is the Eligibility for SBI PO 2026?

Before applying, please check both the education and age rules carefully so that your form is not rejected later.

Educational Qualification

  • Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university is the basic requirement.
  • Candidates in their final year or final semester of graduation can also apply, provided they show proof of passing on or before 30 September 2026.
  • Those with professional degrees like Engineering, Medical, Chartered Accountant (CA) or Cost Accountant are also eligible.
  • Integrated Dual Degree (IDD) holders must have passed on or before 30 September 2026.

Since the degree can be in any stream, this exam is open to almost every graduate looking for government jobs.

Age Limit

The candidate’s age should be between 21 and 30 years as on 1 April 2026 (born between 2 April 1996 and 1 April 2005, both days inclusive). Reserved categories get extra age relaxation as per government rules:

What is the Salary of an SBI PO in 2026?

The State Bank of India offers one of the best pay packages in the banking sector. A newly joined Probationary Officer is placed in the Junior Management Grade Scale-I (JMGS-I) with four advance increments, which makes the starting salary very attractive.

  • Basic Pay: ₹48,480 per month (with 4 advance increments)
  • Pay Scale: ₹48,480 – ₹85,920 (JMGS-I)
  • Approximate CTC: ₹21.97 lakh per year (at a Mumbai posting)

Along with the basic pay, an SBI PO also receives Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), City Compensatory Allowance (CCA) and other benefits, which push the annual CTC to around ₹21.97 lakh per year.

Apart from the salary, officers enjoy benefits like contributory pension (NPS), medical facilities, lease rental accommodation and leave fare concession. There is also a clear path of fast promotion to higher management grades, which makes this job great for your long-term future.

What is the Selection Process for SBI PO 2026?

The selection happens in three phases. You can check the full exam pattern and syllabus details to plan your preparation the right way.

Phase 1: Preliminary Examination

This is an online objective test of 100 marks to be completed in 1 hour, with separate timing of 20 minutes for each section — English Language (40 questions), Quantitative Aptitude (30 questions) and Reasoning Ability (30 questions). The Prelims is only qualifying in nature, which means these marks are not added to the final merit list.

Phase 2: Main Examination

The Mains has an objective test of 200 marks (170 questions, 3 hours) plus a descriptive test of 30 marks (30 minutes). The objective part has four sections: Reasoning & Computer Aptitude (60 marks), Data Analysis & Interpretation (60 marks), General/Economy/Banking Awareness (60 marks) and English Language (20 marks). The descriptive test checks your communication skills through email and report/essay writing typed on the computer.

Phase 3: Psychometric Test, Group Exercise & Interview

Candidates who clear the Mains are called for the final phase. It includes a Psychometric Test, a Group Exercise (20 marks) and a Personal Interview (30 marks). Remember, there is a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every wrong answer in the objective tests. The final merit gives 75% weight to the Main exam and 25% weight to the Group Exercise and Interview — Prelims marks are not counted.

How to for SBI PO 2026?

Before you start, keep your scanned photograph, signature, left thumb impression and a handwritten declaration ready, along with an active email ID and mobile number. Then follow these simple steps:

  1. Visit the official SBI careers portal (direct link given in the Essential Links section below).
  2. Open the “Current Openings” page and click on the SBI PO 2026 advertisement to read it fully.
  3. Click on “New Registration” and create your login using your name, email and mobile number.
  4. Fill in your personal, educational and category details correctly in the application form.
  5. Upload your photograph, signature, thumb impression and handwritten declaration in the required size.
  6. Pay the application fee online using Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking or UPI.
  7. Submit the form and take a printout of the final application for your record.

SBI PO 2026 Important Dates — Application Timeline

SBI PO 2026 Application Fee — Category-Wise

The fee is to be paid online only through Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking or UPI. Once paid, the application fee is non-refundable.

SBI PO 2026 Essential Links

  • Page
  • for SBI PO 2026
  • Visit SBI

This is a wonderful chance to build a respected and high-paying career with India’s largest bank. If you are a graduate and meet the eligibility, don’t wait till the last moment — apply before 8 July 2026 and start your preparation today. Believe in yourself; with honest and steady effort, this dream job can surely be yours. For more such latest banking and government job updates, keep visiting .

title: BOI Officers Interview Call Letter 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: BOI Officers Admit Card 2026 - Download Interview Call Letter @ ibpsreg.ibps.in Advertisement BOI Officers Admit Card 2026 has been officially released by Bank of India (BOI) for t
jobDescription:
BOI Officers Admit Card 2026 - Download Interview Call Letter @ ibpsreg.ibps.in

Advertisement

BOI Officers Admit Card 2026 has been officially released by Bank of India (BOI) for the Interview Call Letter under the Recruitment of Officers in Various Streams up to Scale IV (Project No. 2024-25/05, Notice dated 01.10.2025). Shortlisted candidates can now download their Bank of India Officers Interview Call Letter 2026 from the official IBPS portal at ibpsreg.ibps.in. The call letter is a mandatory document to carry on the day of the interview. Read this complete guide for the direct download link, step-by-step process, interview dates, documents required, and all important instructions.

BOI Officers Admit Card 2026 - Quick Overview

Before you proceed to download, here is a quick summary of all the important details related to the BOI Officers Interview Call Letter 2026 so you can plan ahead without missing anything.

Particulars Details
Conducting Body Bank of India (BOI)
Recruitment Name Recruitment of Officers in Various Streams up to Scale IV
Project Number 2024-25/05, Notice dated 01.10.2025
Stage Interview (Final Stage)
Call Letter Type Interview Call Letter
bankofindia.bank.in
Login Credentials Required Registration Number / Roll Number + Date of Birth / Password

BOI Officers Admit Card 2026 - Direct Download Link

The quickest way to get your BOI Officers Interview Call Letter 2026 is through the direct IBPS login portal . Candidates who have been shortlisted for the interview round can access the call letter using the link below.

  • Click Here: Download BOI Officers Admit Card 2026
  • Official Page: bankofindia.bank.in

Important: The above link opens the official IBPS login page hosted at ibpsreg.ibps.in. Only candidates who have been shortlisted for the interview round and have a valid Registration Number and Date of Birth / Password can successfully log in and download their call letter.

How to Download BOI Officers Interview Call Letter 2026 - Step-by-Step Guide

Downloading the BOI Officers Admit Card 2026 is a straightforward process. Follow the steps below carefully to avoid any login errors. Make sure you have your registration details ready before you begin.

  • Step 1: Visit the official IBPS recruitment portal at ibpsreg.ibps.in or click the direct download link provided above.
  • Step 2: The BOI Officers Interview Call Letter login page will open on your screen.
  • Step 3: Enter your Registration Number or Roll Number in the first field.
  • Step 4: Enter your Date of Birth (DD/MM/YYYY) or Password in the second field.
  • Step 5: Enter the Captcha Code as displayed on the screen.
  • Step 6: Click on the "Login" or "Submit" button.
  • Step 7: Your BOI Officers Interview Call Letter 2026 will be displayed on screen. Verify all details carefully.
  • Step 8: Click "Download" or "Print" to save a copy of your call letter.
  • Step 9: Take at least two colour printouts of the admit card for use on the day of interview.

Pro Tip: Use a desktop or laptop browser for the best experience when downloading. If the page does not load, try clearing browser cache or using an alternate browser such as Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox.

Details Mentioned on BOI Officers Interview Call Letter 2026

Your BOI Officers Admit Card 2026 contains all the key details needed for your interview day. Candidates should verify every detail on the call letter immediately after downloading it. Any discrepancy should be reported to the bank before the interview date.

  • Candidate's Full Name
  • Registration Number / Roll Number
  • Category (General / OBC / SC / ST / EWS / PwBD)
  • Post Applied For (Stream and Scale)
  • Interview Date and Time
  • Interview Venue / Centre Address
  • Reporting Time
  • Candidate's Photograph and Signature
  • Important Instructions for Interview Day

Documents to Carry for BOI Officers Interview 2026

Carrying the right set of documents is just as important as downloading the call letter. The Bank of India typically requires candidates to present both originals and self-attested photocopies of several documents at the interview venue for identity verification and eligibility confirmation.

Mandatory Documents

  • Printed copy of the BOI Officers Interview Call Letter 2026 (colour printout preferred)
  • One valid original Photo ID proof - Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Driving Licence, or Passport
  • Self-attested photocopy of the same Photo ID proof
  • Recent passport-size photographs (same as uploaded during application)

Supporting Documents (Originals + Photocopies)

  • Class 10 Certificate / Marksheet (proof of date of birth)
  • Class 12 Certificate / Marksheet
  • Graduation and Post-Graduation Degree Certificates and Marksheets
  • Caste Certificate (for SC / ST / OBC / EWS candidates), issued by the competent authority
  • PwBD Certificate (if applicable)
  • Experience Certificate(s) from current and previous employers
  • Bank Account details / Pay Slip as proof of current designation and salary (if required for the stream)
  • NOC (No Objection Certificate) from current employer, if currently employed

Note: Candidates should carry all documents in a well-organised folder. Do not carry only photocopies - original documents are mandatory for verification at the interview centre.

BOI Officers 2024-25 Recruitment - Selection Process

Understanding the complete selection process helps candidates know exactly what stage they are at and what comes next. The recruitment of BOI Officers in various streams up to Scale IV involves multiple phases, and the interview is the final and most decisive step.

Stage Mode Marks / Status
Online Examination (for applicable posts) Online (Computer-Based) Qualifying / Shortlisting
Shortlisting of Candidates Merit-Based Based on Online Exam Score
Interview (Current Stage) Offline / In-Person Panel Interview 100 Marks
Document Verification At Interview Venue Mandatory
Final Result Merit List based on Interview Score Published on bankofindia.bank.in

About the Interview Round

The interview is a panel-based personal interview conducted in English or Hindi. It assesses the candidate's technical or domain knowledge (relevant to the applied stream), communication skills, banking awareness, analytical thinking, and overall personality. The panel typically consists of senior bank officials and external experts.

Important Instructions for BOI Officers Interview 2026

Candidates appearing for the BOI Officers Interview 2026 must follow all official instructions to avoid disqualification. Here are the key guidelines you need to be aware of before attending the interview.

  • Arrive at the interview venue at least 30-45 minutes before the reporting time mentioned on the call letter.
  • Carry the call letter in printed form - digital / mobile copies may not be accepted at the centre.
  • Bring the original valid photo ID along with at least one self-attested photocopy.
  • Dress professionally and formally for the interview. Bank interviews require business/formal attire.
  • Mobile phones and electronic devices may not be allowed inside the interview hall - check venue guidelines.
  • Candidates who have registered as PwBD should carry their valid disability certificate.
  • OBC candidates must carry the most recent Non-Creamy Layer Certificate in the format prescribed by the Government of India.
  • Do not bring unauthorised materials such as study notes, books, or electronic gadgets into the interview room.
  • Candidates shortlisted for interview must ensure their application details match the documents being presented.

Quick Tips to Prepare for BOI Officers Interview 2026

The interview is the final hurdle between you and a confirmed officer position at Bank of India. Here are some practical preparation tips that can help you make a strong impression before the panel.

  • Know your CV thoroughly - Be ready to answer questions on every project, role, and achievement you have mentioned.
  • Brush up on banking fundamentals - Topics like RBI monetary policy, credit risk, NPA norms, Basel III, and MSME lending are commonly discussed.
  • Stay current with financial news - Read The Economic Times or RBI bulletins in the week leading up to your interview.
  • Understand Bank of India's recent performance - Know the bank's key financial indicators, schemes, and latest initiatives.
  • Practice structured answers - Use the STAR (Situation, Task, Action, Result) method for experience-based questions.
  • Prepare for stream-specific questions - If you have applied for a credit or specialist stream, expect deep domain knowledge questions.

title: TN PWD Apprentice Recruitment 2026 — 760 Graduate, Diploma & Non-Engineering Apprentice Vacancies, at NATS
description: Apprenticeship Training Jobs in Tamil Nadu: TN PWD Apprentice 2026 Here is a wonderful chance for fresh graduates and diploma holders in Tamil Nadu! The Public Works Department (PW
jobDescription:
Apprenticeship Training Jobs in Tamil Nadu: TN PWD Apprentice 2026

Here is a wonderful chance for fresh graduates and diploma holders in Tamil Nadu! The Public Works Department (PWD), Tamil Nadu has invited online applications for the engagement of Apprentices for the year 2026-27. A total of 760 training places are available across engineering and non-engineering streams, with a monthly stipend of up to ₹9,000. This one-year apprenticeship is a great way to gain real work experience inside a respected government department and make your resume stronger.

The online application window is open now and closes on 25 June 2026, so apply quickly. This opening is ideal for Civil, Electrical & Electronics, Architecture and Arts/Science/Commerce graduates looking for government opportunities in Tamil Nadu. In this article from , we explain everything in very simple language — categories, stipend, eligibility, selection, important dates and how to apply on the NATS portal. Please note this is a training apprenticeship, not a permanent job.

Key Highlights — TN PWD Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The Public Works Department of Tamil Nadu is engaging apprentices under the Apprenticeship (Amendment) Act, through the Board of Apprenticeship Training (Southern Region). This is a paid, one-year training programme — perfect for freshers who want hands-on experience. If you are a graduate searching for government jobs, this is a strong first step.

  • Recruiting Body: Public Works Department (PWD), Government of Tamil Nadu
  • Apprentice Type: Graduate, Technician (Diploma) & Non-Engineering Graduate Apprentices
  • Total Training Places: 760
  • Monthly Stipend: ₹8,000 to ₹9,000
  • Training Duration: One Year
  • Last Date to Apply: 25 June 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — TN PWD Apprentice 2026

The 760 training places are divided across three categories and several disciplines. Choose the one that matches your qualification:

Eligibility Criteria — TN PWD Apprentice 2026

Educational Qualification

The qualification depends on the apprentice category you choose:

  • Graduate (Engineering) Apprentice: Full-time degree in Engineering/Technology in Civil, Electrical & Electronics, or Architecture.
  • Technician (Diploma) Apprentice: Full-time diploma in Engineering/Technology in Civil, Electrical & Electronics, or Architecture.
  • Non-Engineering Graduate Apprentice: A full-time degree such as B.A., B.Sc., B.Com., BBA, BCA or BBM from a recognised university.

Candidates must have passed in 2022, 2023, 2024 or 2025 and should be from Tamil Nadu. If you are a diploma holder looking for government openings, the Technician category is for you.

Age Limit and Other Conditions

The age limit is as per the Apprenticeship Rules (no fixed minimum or maximum is mentioned in the notification). Importantly, candidates who have already done an apprenticeship, or who have one year or more of work experience, are not eligible — this scheme is only for freshers.

What is the Stipend / Salary for TN PWD Apprentice 2026?

During the one-year training, apprentices receive a fixed monthly stipend (not a regular pay scale). The amount depends on your category:

  • Graduate (Engineering) Apprentice: ₹9,000 per month
  • Technician (Diploma) Apprentice: ₹8,000 per month
  • Non-Engineering Graduate Apprentice: ₹9,000 per month

The stipend is paid monthly throughout the one-year apprenticeship period.

Please remember that after the training ends, PWD Tamil Nadu has no obligation to give permanent employment. The real value here is the experience and the training certificate you earn.

What is the Selection Process for TN PWD Apprentice 2026?

There is no written exam and no interview. Selection is based purely on merit — your percentage of marks in the qualifying degree or diploma. The steps are simple:

  1. Shortlisting based on the percentage of marks in your qualifying course.
  2. Intimation to shortlisted candidates through their registered email ID.
  3. Certificate verification at PWD, Chennai on the notified dates.

Entering your correct percentage of marks on the NATS portal is compulsory — if you do not enter it, your application will not be considered.

How to for TN PWD Apprentice 2026?

Applications are accepted only online through the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) portal. Follow these steps:

  1. Visit the NATS student portal (link available in Essential Links below).
  2. New candidates must first enrol and generate their Enrolment Number.
  3. Already-enrolled candidates simply log in to their account.
  4. Go to “Apply against advertised vacancies” and search for “Public Works Department Tamilnadu”.
  5. Fill the application, enter your percentage of marks, and upload your certificates.
  6. Submit the application and save a copy for your record.

Documents Required for Application

Keep these ready before applying:

  • Provisional certificate or consolidated mark sheet of your degree/diploma
  • Proof of percentage of marks (mandatory entry on the portal)
  • Community / reservation certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL), if claiming reservation
  • PwD certificate, if applicable
  • Recent photograph and signature

Important Dates — TN PWD Apprentice 2026

Application Fees — TN PWD Apprentice 2026

There is no application fee for this apprenticeship engagement. Applying through the NATS portal is completely free of cost for every candidate.

Essential Links — TN PWD Apprentice 2026

  • Page
  • for TN PWD Apprentice 2026
  • Visit Tamil Nadu Government

This TN PWD Apprentice 2026 engagement is a great opportunity for Tamil Nadu freshers to gain valuable government work experience and a recognised training certificate. The last date is 25 June 2026, so do not wait till the end — apply today. For more Tamil Nadu government job updates and apprenticeship notifications, keep visiting . All the best for your career!

title: SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026
description: SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 Out - Download FRTA Merit List PDF & Cut Off Marks Advertisement The SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 has been officially released by the Staff
jobDescription:
SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 Out - Download FRTA Merit List PDF & Cut Off Marks

Advertisement

The SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 has been officially released by the Staff Selection Commission on 18 June 2026 at ssc.gov.in. The result covers the Stenographer Grade C and Grade D Examination 2025 and includes the First Round of Tentative Allocation (FRTA) for a total of 1,715 posts. Candidates who appeared in the Computer-Based Test (CBT) held from 6 to 8 August 2025 and the subsequent Stenography Skill Test can now download the result PDF and verify their qualifying status using their roll number.

Along with the final merit list, SSC has also published category-wise cut off marks and announced the schedule for the Sliding Mechanism, which will be held physically from 1 to 2 July Read on for all the details including result download steps, vacancy statistics, cut off marks, skill test qualifying criteria, tie-breaking rules, and next steps after selection.

SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 - Overview

This table covers all the key highlights of the SSC Stenographer Grade C and D Final Result 2026 at a glance.

Particulars Details
Exam Name SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Examination 2025
Conducting Body Staff Selection Commission (SSC)
Total Vacancies 1,715
Result Status Released
SSC Stenographer Final Result Date 18 June 2026
Minimum Qualifying Marks (CBT) UR: 30% | OBC/EWS: 25% | Other Categories: 20%
Sliding Process Slot Selection 19 to 22 June 2026
Physical Sliding Process 1 to 2 July 2026
Posts Stenographer Grade C & Grade D
ssc.gov.in

Is SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 Out?

Yes. The SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 has been declared on 18 June 2026 on the official SSC website at ssc.gov.in. The result has been published in PDF format separately for Grade C and Grade D posts. It contains the roll numbers of all candidates who have been provisionally shortlisted under the First Round of Tentative Allocation (FRTA).

Through the CBT stage, a total of 8,624 candidates qualified for the Grade C Skill Test and 22,456 candidates qualified for the Grade D Skill Test. Of these, 4,907 candidates actually appeared for the Grade C Skill Test and 12,564 candidates appeared for the Grade D Skill Test. Based on combined performance in the CBT and Skill Test, SSC has now published the FRTA list along with final cut off marks.

How to Download SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 PDF

Candidates can follow these simple steps to download the SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Final Result 2026 from the .

  1. Visit the official SSC website at www.ssc.gov.in.
  2. Click on the "Results" tab available on the homepage.
  3. Locate and click on the link for SSC Stenographer Grade C & D Examination 2025 - FRTA List.
  4. The result PDF will open on the screen.
  5. Press Ctrl + F and type your roll number to search for your name.
  6. If your roll number appears, you have been provisionally shortlisted.
  7. Download and save the PDF for future reference and document verification.

Direct Link: Download SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 PDF

SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 - Important Dates

The table below lists all key dates related to the SSC Stenographer 2025 final result and subsequent processes.

Event Date
Online Option-cum-Preference Submission 5 May to 8 May 2026
SSC Stenographer Final Result Released 18 June 2026
Sliding Process Venue/Date/Slot Selection 19 June 2026 to 22 June 2026
Physical Sliding Process 1 July 2026 to 2 July 2026

SSC Stenographer Grade C Final Result 2026 - Vacancy-wise Statistics

The SSC has recommended 228 candidates against 232 available vacancies for Grade C posts in the FRTA. A few posts remain unfilled due to the non-availability of eligible candidates in certain reserved categories. The table below provides a category-wise and PwBD/ESM breakdown.

Category Vacancies Candidates Recommended

OBC 50 50

SSC Stenographer Grade D Final Result 2026 - Vacancy-wise Statistics

For Grade D posts, SSC has recommended a total of 1,483 candidates against 1,483 vacancies in the FRTA. Allocation was done based on candidates' merit rank and department preferences submitted earlier.

SSC Stenographer Cut Off Marks 2026 - Category-wise

The SSC Stenographer Final Cut Off 2026 has been published alongside the result. These are the marks secured by the last selected candidate in each post and category combination. Candidates scoring below these marks were not recommended for allocation.

SSC Stenographer Skill Test Qualifying Criteria

The Stenography Skill Test was conducted as a qualifying stage. Candidates had to stay within the maximum permissible error limit to be considered for selection. The error allowance differed for Grade C and Grade D posts and also varied by category.

Post Category Maximum Errors Allowed
Grade C UR Up to 5%
Grade C Reserved Categories Up to 7%
Grade D UR Up to 7%
Grade D Reserved Categories Up to 10%

Details Mentioned in the SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026 PDF

The official FRTA result PDF contains the following candidate details:

  • Candidate's Name
  • Roll Number
  • Category
  • Post Applied For (Grade C / Grade D)
  • Marks Obtained in CBT and Skill Test
  • Provisional Qualifying Status

SSC Stenographer Sliding Mechanism 2026 - What You Need to Know

SSC has introduced a one-time Sliding Mechanism to maximise vacancy utilisation. Through this process, recommended candidates may be reallocated to a higher-preferred department if vacancies open up due to non-joining of already-allocated candidates. This ensures that as many posts as possible are filled in the current recruitment cycle.

Key Points About the Sliding Process

  • Candidates eligible for the sliding process must select their preferred venue, date, and time slot online between 19 June and 22 June 2026 through the SSC website.
  • The physical sliding process will take place at the respective SSC Regional Offices on 1 and 2 July 2026.
  • Physical attendance is mandatory. Candidates who fail to appear in person will not be considered for reallocation and will retain their existing FRTA allocation.
Event Date
Slot Selection (Online) 19 June - 22 June 2026
Physical Sliding Process 1 July - 2 July 2026
Mode Offline (Regional SSC Offices)
Benefit Upgrade to Higher Preferred Department

What Happens After SSC Stenographer Final Result 2026?

Candidates shortlisted in the FRTA should be aware of the complete post-result process to avoid missing any critical step.

  • Sliding Process Participation: Eligible candidates must register online for their preferred slot (19-22 June 2026) and attend the physical sliding process (1-2 July 2026) at their allotted regional SSC office.
  • Final Allocation List: After the sliding process concludes, SSC will publish the final department-wise allocation list on the .
  • Marks of All Candidates: SSC is expected to upload the marks of both selected and non-selected candidates on ssc.gov.in after the final allocation.
  • Document Verification: Recommended candidates will be called for document verification by their respective user departments before appointment letters are issued.
  • Appointment: Joining and appointment formalities will be handled by the allotted central government department or ministry.

title: Delhi Cantonment Board AE / JE Recruitment 2026 — 9 Assistant Engineer & Junior Engineer Vacancies
description: Engineering Government Jobs in Delhi: Delhi Cantonment Board Recruitment 2026 Good news for engineering graduates and diploma holders! The Delhi Cantonment Board (DCB) has released
jobDescription:
Engineering Government Jobs in Delhi: Delhi Cantonment Board Recruitment 2026

Good news for engineering graduates and diploma holders! The Delhi Cantonment Board (DCB) has released its AE / JE Recruitment 2026 notification under Advertisement No. 01/A total of 9 vacancies are open for the posts of Assistant Engineer (AE) and Junior Engineer (JE) across the Civil, Mechanical and Electrical branches. Selected candidates will earn an attractive salary under the 7th Pay Commission, going up to ₹1,42,400 per month. If you hold an engineering degree or diploma, this is a wonderful chance to join a stable government organisation right in the national capital.

The online application window opens on 15 July 2026 and closes on 14 August 2026. This opening is perfect for Civil, Mechanical and Electrical candidates searching for government jobs based in Delhi. In this article from , we explain everything in very simple language — vacancy details, eligibility, salary, selection process, important dates, fees, and how to apply step by step. Read till the end so you don’t miss any important point. Don’t worry, we have made it very easy for you.

Key Highlights — Delhi Cantonment Board AE & JE Recruitment 2026

The Delhi Cantonment Board is a civic administration body under the Ministry of Defence that manages the Delhi Cantonment area. It offers stable jobs with good pay and full government benefits. This recruitment is a great opening for engineering candidates who want a secure career. If you are searching for engineering government jobs, keep reading.

  • Recruiting Body: Delhi Cantonment Board (DCB), Ministry of Defence
  • Post Names: Assistant Engineer (Civil & Mechanical), Junior Engineer (Civil & Electrical)
  • Total Vacancies: 9
  • Salary / Pay Level: Pay Level 6 (₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400) and Pay Level 7 (₹44,900 – ₹1,42,400)
  • Job Location: Delhi
  • Last Date to Apply: 14 August 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — Delhi Cantonment Board AE & JE 2026

The 9 vacancies are divided across four engineering posts. Here is the clear breakdown so you can pick the post that matches your branch:

Eligibility Criteria — Delhi Cantonment Board AE & JE 2026

Educational Qualification

The qualification is different for each post, so please read your post carefully:

  • Assistant Engineer (Civil): Degree in Civil Engineering from a recognised university or institute.
  • Assistant Engineer (Mechanical): Degree in Mechanical Engineering from a recognised university or institute.
  • Junior Engineer (Civil): Degree in Civil Engineering, OR a 3-year Diploma in Civil Engineering from a recognised board/institute.
  • Junior Engineer (Electrical): Degree in Electrical Engineering, OR a 3-year Diploma in Electrical Engineering from a recognised board/institute.

If you are a diploma holder looking for government jobs, the Junior Engineer posts are open to you.

Age Limit

The notification does not mention a fixed minimum or maximum age figure. Your age and qualification will both be counted as on 14 August 2026. Standard age relaxation is given to reserved categories — SC/ST 5 years, OBC 3 years, PwBD up to 10 years, and Ex-Servicemen as per government rules. For the exact age limit applicable to you, please check the PDF.

What is the Salary for Delhi Cantonment Board AE & JE 2026?

The salary for these posts is attractive and follows the 7th Pay Commission pay matrix. Your pay depends on the post you apply for:

  • Assistant Engineer (AE): Pay Level 7 — ₹44,900 to ₹1,42,400 per month
  • Junior Engineer (JE): Pay Level 6 — ₹35,400 to ₹1,12,400 per month

Starting in-hand salary will be around ₹35,400+ for JE and ₹44,900+ for AE, plus allowances as per rules.

Apart from the basic pay, employees also receive government allowances and the New Pension Scheme (NPS) benefit. It is a stable and respected job with steady growth.

What is the Selection Process for Delhi Cantonment Board AE & JE 2026?

The selection is simple and based only on a Computer Based Test (CBT) — there is no interview. After the CBT, shortlisted candidates go through document verification and a medical fitness check.

  1. Computer Based Test (CBT) — an objective MCQ examination
  2. Document Verification — checking of your original certificates
  3. Medical Fitness Test — a final fitness check before joining

You can also check the exam syllabus and pattern guide to plan your preparation properly.

Exam Pattern — Delhi Cantonment Board AE & JE 2026

The CBT has 120 questions for 120 marks, to be completed in 120 minutes. There is negative marking of 0.33 marks for each wrong answer, so attempt carefully.

How to for Delhi Cantonment Board AE & JE 2026?

Applications are accepted only online through the . Follow these simple steps:

  1. Visit the official Delhi Cantonment Board recruitment portal (link in Essential Links below).
  2. Register with your basic details and create your login.
  3. Fill the online application form carefully with your personal and educational details.
  4. Upload your photograph, signature, and the required documents.
  5. Pay the application fee online (if applicable to your category).
  6. Submit the form and take a printout for your record.

If you wish to apply for more than one post, you must submit a separate application for each post.

Documents Required for Application

Keep these documents ready before you start filling the form:

  • Matriculation certificate (for date of birth proof)
  • Educational mark sheets and your degree/diploma certificate
  • Caste or category certificate (SC, ST, OBC or EWS), if applicable
  • PwBD disability certificate, if applicable
  • Recent passport-size photograph and signature
  • Valid photo ID (Aadhaar / PAN / Voter ID / Driving Licence / Passport)

Important Dates — Delhi Cantonment Board AE & JE 2026

Application Fees — Delhi Cantonment Board AE & JE 2026

The fee is payable online only and is charged per post. A large number of candidates are exempted, as shown below:

Women candidates, SC/ST, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen and transgender applicants do not have to pay any application fee.
