title: Indian Navy Recruitment 2026
description: Indian Navy 10+2 (B.Tech) Cadet Entry 2026 — Become a Permanent Commissioned Officer After Class 12 Big news for 12th-pass students with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics (PCM) — ...
jobDescription:
Indian Navy 10+2 (B.Tech) Cadet Entry 2026 — Become a Permanent Commissioned Officer After Class 12

Big news for 12th-pass students with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics (PCM) — the Indian Navy 10+2 (B.Tech) Cadet Entry Scheme for Jan 2027 course is open with 60 vacancies (30 Mechanical Engineering + 30 Electronics & Communication Engineering). Selected cadets get a fully-paid 4-year B.Tech degree at the Indian Naval Academy (INA), Ezhimala, awarded by Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) — books, clothing, messing and all training costs are borne by the Indian Navy. After successful completion, you are commissioned as a Sub Lieutenant — Permanent Commissioned Officer in the Technical Branch of the Indian Navy. If you have been searching for Defence Govt Jobs right after Class 12, this is the cleanest route.

The online application window is 29 May 2026 to 18 June 2026 through the Indian Navy careers portal. There is no separate written exam — shortlisting is done purely on your JEE (Main) 2026 All India Common Rank List (CRL), followed by SSB Interview and medical examination. Up to 8 women cadets (4 in each branch) are eligible, and from the Mechanical Engineering pool, 7 top-performing cadets are streamed into B.Tech in Naval Architecture & Shipbuilding at NCW, Kochi. In this article from GovtJobsAlert.in, we will walk through eligibility, dates, SSB, training and the exact step-by-step application process.

Key Highlights — Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry 2026

This scheme is open to unmarried male and female candidates born between 02 July 2007 and 01 January 2010 (both dates inclusive), with at least 70% PCM and 50% English in Class 10 or 12, who have appeared in JEE (Main) 2026.

  • Recruiting Body: Indian Navy (Ministry of Defence)
  • Entry Scheme: 10+2 (B.Tech) Cadet Entry, Course commencing January 2027
  • Total Vacancies: 60 (Mechanical Engg 30 + Electronics & Communication Engg 30)
  • Women Sub-Quota: Up to 8 (4 in each branch)
  • Training Institute: Indian Naval Academy (INA), Ezhimala (Kerala)
  • Degree Awarded By: Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU)
  • Application Window: 29 May 2026 to 18 June 2026
  • SSB Interview: September 2026 onwards

Vacancy Breakdown — Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Entry 2026

The 60 vacancies are split equally between two engineering branches. Vacancies are tentative and may change as per service requirements. Up to 4 women cadets are eligible in each branch.

Special Note: From the Mechanical Engineering selectees, 07 top-performing cadets at INA will be streamed into B.Tech in Naval Architecture & Shipbuilding at the Naval College of Engineering (NCW), Kochi.

Eligibility Criteria — Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry 2026

Only unmarried Indian citizens — both men and women — can . Marrying during training will lead to discharge and refund of full training cost.

Educational Qualification

  • Passed Senior Secondary Examination (10+2 Pattern) or equivalent from any recognised Board
  • Minimum 70% aggregate marks in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics (PCM)
  • Minimum 50% marks in English in Class X or Class XII
  • Must have appeared in JEE (Main) 2026 (for B.E. / B.Tech)
  • Naval Headquarters will publish the JEE Main 2026 CRL cut-off for SSB shortlisting; only candidates above that cut-off are called for SSB

Age Limit (Date of Birth Range)

  • Born between: 02 July 2007 and 01 January 2010 (both dates inclusive)
  • No age relaxation; only DOB window applies

Medical & Physical Standards

All SSB-recommended candidates must clear the medical examination applicable to the 10+2 (B.Tech) entry. Candidates already declared medically fit for NDA / INA Jan 27 need not repeat the medical. Detailed standards (height, weight, vision, tattoo policy) are on the official Indian Navy site. No relaxation on any grounds. For other Class 12 Govt Jobs, see the dedicated category page.

What is the Salary for Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Officer?

During the 4-year B.Tech training at INA Ezhimala, the entire cost of training — books, reading material, uniform, food and accommodation — is fully borne by the Indian Navy. Cadets also receive a monthly stipend as per current entitlements.

  • Training Stipend (4-year B.Tech at INA Ezhimala): As per Navy’s current cadet entitlements
  • On Commissioning as Sub Lieutenant: 7th CPC Level 10, starting basic Rs 56,100, plus standard military allowances (DA, MSP, HRA / Naval Pay Premia, kit allowance, ration in kind)

In-hand range on commissioning (Sub Lieutenant): approximately Rs 85,000 — Rs 1,10,000 per month depending on station, including Military Service Pay and allowances, with CSD canteen, AGIF group insurance, defence medical care (ECHS), and full defence pension benefits after qualifying service.

Cadets enjoy the defence officer lifestyle — duty travel allowance, leave passages, subsidised messing, sports and adventure exposure, foreign training opportunities, and structured promotions from Sub Lieutenant to Lieutenant, Lieutenant Commander, Commander, Captain and beyond based on time-and-merit policy.

What is the Selection Process for Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Entry 2026?

There is no separate written exam for this entry — selection is entirely on JEE Main 2026 score + SSB + Medical. The stages are:

  1. JEE Main 2026 CRL Shortlisting — Naval HQ fixes the CRL cut-off and issues SSB call-up letters by email/SMS
  2. SSB Interview — 5-day Service Selection Board process at Bengaluru / Bhopal / Kolkata / Visakhapatnam (from September 2026 onwards). Includes Officer Intelligence Rating (OIR), Picture Perception & Discussion Test, Group Testing Officer (GTO) tasks, Psychological tests and Personal Interview
  3. Medical Examination — At designated military hospital, applicable to 10+2 (B.Tech) entry
  4. Common Merit List — Based on SSB marks + medical fitness
  5. Police Verification & Character Verification — Final appointment subject to PV/CV clearance and vacancy

Change of SSB centre is not permitted. AC 3-tier rail fare is admissible for first-time SSB candidates — carry your bank passbook / cheque copy with IFSC code. To stay updated on all defence intakes, refer to the Defence Govt Jobs category page.

How to Apply Online for Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Entry 2026?

Application is fully online through the Indian Navy careers portal. Keep your 10th & 12th marksheets, JEE Main 2026 score card showing CRL rank, recent passport-size photograph ready as scanned originals (JPG/TIFF) before you start.

  1. Visit the Indian Navy careers portal — link available in Essential Links below.
  2. Register on the portal using a valid email ID and active mobile number (these cannot be changed till selection is over).
  3. Fill personal details exactly as per your Matriculation / Class 12 certificate.
  4. Enter your JEE Main 2026 CRL rank in the application — this is the most important field.
  5. Upload scanned originals in JPG / TIFF format: DOB proof (10th certificate), 10th marksheet, 12th marksheet, JEE Main 2026 score card, recent passport-size colour photograph.
  6. Review every field carefully — once submitted, no amendments are permitted.
  7. Submit the application before 18 June 2026 (1800 Hrs).
  8. Take a printout of the finally submitted application and carry it along with all originals to the SSB interview.

Important: Multiple applications by the same candidate lead to cancellation. Illegible scans cause auto-rejection. The Navy may reject the application at any stage if found ineligible / invalid.

Important Dates — Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Entry 2026

Application Fees — Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Entry 2026

Note: Indian Navy officer entries are traditionally fee-free. Please verify on the application portal at the time of applying.

Essential Links — Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Entry 2026

  • Official Notification Page
  • Apply Online for Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Entry 2026
  • Visit Indian Navy Official Recruitment Website

If you have a strong JEE Main rank and the right PCM scores, this is one of the most prestigious 12th-pass openings in India — a fully-funded B.Tech, a permanent commission in the Indian Navy, and a structured officer career. Do not miss the 18 June 2026 deadline. Read the notification PDF carefully, enter your JEE Main CRL correctly, and submit your application well in time. For more such defence and 12th-pass openings, keep visiting GovtJobsAlert.in — your trusted partner for every fresh notification.

title: IIFCL Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026
description: IIFCL Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026 Out - Download Call Letter Advertisement The IIFCL Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026 has been officially released on 16th June India I
jobDescription:
IIFCL Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026 Out - Download Call Letter

Advertisement

The IIFCL Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026 has been officially released on 16th June India Infrastructure Finance Company Limited (IIFCL) has made the call letter available for candidates who applied for Assistant Manager (Grade A) and Manager (Grade B) posts. The online examination is scheduled to be held on 28th June 2026, and candidates must download their admit card immediately from the official portal managed by IBPS. The direct link to download the IIFCL call letter 2026 is active - candidates need their Registration Number and Password/Date of Birth to log in and access it.

IIFCL Admit Card 2026 - Quick Overview

Before proceeding to the download link, candidates can refer to the overview table below for all key details related to the IIFCL Grade A and Grade B recruitment and admit card.

Particulars Details
Organisation India Infrastructure Finance Company Limited (IIFCL)
Post Name Assistant Manager (Grade A), Manager (Grade B)
Total Vacancies 37
Admit Card Release Date 16th June 2026
Exam Date 28th June 2026
Selection Process Online Written Examination + Interview
Conducting Body IBPS (on behalf of IIFCL)
iifcl.in

IIFCL Assistant Manager Admit Card 2026 - Direct Download Link

The IIFCL call letter 2026 is now available on the IBPS portal. Candidates are strongly advised to download and print their admit card well in advance to avoid last-minute technical difficulties. Entry to the examination centre will not be permitted without a valid printed admit card.

IIFCL Admit Card 2026 - Download Here

How to Download IIFCL Grade A & B Admit Card 2026

Candidates can follow the step-by-step process below to download the IIFCL Assistant Manager and Manager call letter 2026 without any difficulty.

  1. Visit the official IIFCL website at www.iifcl.in or directly go to the IBPS portal link provided above.
  2. Click on the "Admit Card / Call Letter" link for IIFCL Grade A/B Recruitment 2026.
  3. Enter your Registration Number and Password or Date of Birth (DOB) in the respective fields.
  4. Fill in the captcha code and click on "Submit".
  5. Your IIFCL admit card 2026 will appear on the screen.
  6. Verify all the details carefully - name, roll number, exam centre, date, and time.
  7. Download and take at least two printouts of the admit card for exam day use.

Also Read: Punjab Police Constable Exam Date 2026

Vacancy Breakdown - IIFCL Recruitment 2026

The IIFCL Recruitment 2026 notification was issued for a total of 37 vacancies across two posts. Candidates who applied against these posts are eligible to download the admit card and appear for the online examination.

Post Vacancies
Assistant Manager (Grade A) 33
Manager (Grade B) 4
Total 37

Details Mentioned on IIFCL Call Letter 2026

The IIFCL admit card contains all essential information required for examination day. Candidates must carefully verify every detail on their call letter after downloading it. In case any discrepancy is noticed, the candidate should contact IIFCL/IBPS authorities immediately.

The following details are printed on the IIFCL call letter 2026:

  • Candidate's Full Name
  • Roll Number / Registration Number
  • Photograph and Signature
  • Post Applied For (Grade A / Grade B)
  • Date and Time of Examination
  • Reporting Time at the Exam Centre
  • Exam Centre Name and Address
  • Important Instructions for Exam Day

Documents to Carry to the IIFCL Exam Centre

Along with the printed IIFCL admit card 2026, candidates must carry one valid photo identity proof in original. Without these documents, entry to the examination hall will be denied.

  • Printed IIFCL Admit Card 2026 (mandatory)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (original) - any one of the following:
    • Aadhaar Card
    • PAN Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Passport
    • Driving Licence
  • Passport-size photographs (as applicable)

IIFCL Exam Pattern 2026

The IIFCL online examination consists of two sections totalling 150 questions for 200 marks, to be completed in 120 minutes. Understanding the exam structure helps candidates allocate time effectively on the examination day.

Section Subject / Topics Questions Marks Duration
Section I Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, English, Current Affairs 100 100 60 Minutes
Section II Domain Knowledge (Project Finance, Corporate Banking & Treasury) 50 100 60 Minutes
Total - 150 200 120 Minutes

IIFCL Selection Process 2026

The selection process for the IIFCL Assistant Manager and Manager posts comprises two stages. Candidates who qualify the online written examination will be shortlisted for the interview round.

  1. Online Written Examination - 28th June 2026
  2. Interview

Important Exam Day Instructions for IIFCL 2026

Candidates appearing for the IIFCL Grade A and Grade B exam on 28th June 2026 must adhere to the following instructions. Non-compliance with any of these guidelines may result in disqualification.

  • Report early - Reach the exam centre at least 60 minutes before the scheduled reporting time.
  • No electronic devices - Mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators, Bluetooth devices, and earphones are strictly prohibited inside the examination hall.
  • Photo ID is mandatory - Carry a valid original photo identity proof matching the details filled in the application form.
  • Gate closing time is strict - Candidates arriving after the gate closing time will not be permitted to enter the venue under any circumstances.
  • Photograph matching - Ensure the photo on your ID proof matches the photograph submitted during the application process.
  • Follow invigilator instructions - Maintain discipline throughout the exam and comply with all instructions given by the exam centre authorities.
  • Clear printout - Ensure all details on the admit card printout are clearly visible and not blurred.

title: Bank of Baroda Various Managers Online Form 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: Bank of Baroda is hiring for 86 vacancies across 5 officer-level posts in its Corporate and Institutional Credit (C&IC) department, ranging from Manager to Chief Manager grade in b
jobDescription:
Bank of Baroda is hiring for 86 vacancies across 5 officer-level posts in its Corporate and Institutional Credit (C&IC) department, ranging from Manager to Chief Manager grade in both Credit Analyst and C&IC Relationship roles. Online registration opened on 16 June 2026, and the last date to apply and pay the fee is 6 July Eligible candidates need a postgraduate finance qualification or CA/CMA/CS/CFA, relevant banking credit experience, and willingness to serve anywhere in India.

Bank of Baroda C&IC Recruitment 2026: Key Dates You Need to Know

Advertisement

This recruitment drive was announced under advertisement number BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/There are only two dates that matter for candidates: when the application window opens and when it closes, since the bank has not yet announced an online test date.

Event Date
Online Registration Starts 16 June 2026
Last Date for Application Submission & Fee Payment 6 July 2026

Registration is treated as complete only once the application fee is paid online on or before 6 July 2026; the date of the online test, if conducted, will be announced separately to candidates through their registered email and the bank's website.

Also read: NHSRCL Recruitment 2026 - 209 DE & JE Posts on Absorption Basis, Eligibility Criteria & Pay Scale

86 Vacancies Across 5 Posts: Full Position-wise Breakdown

The 86 vacancies are divided into two job families: Credit Analyst posts and C&IC Relationship posts, each spanning three or two seniority grades respectively. Vacancy count, age limit, and required post-qualification experience vary by grade, so candidates should check the exact post they qualify for before applying.

Credit Analyst Posts (Manager, Senior Manager & Chief Manager)

Credit Analyst posts focus on appraising corporate credit proposals and carry the highest vacancy count in this recruitment, with the Senior Manager grade alone accounting for 38 of the 86 total seats.

Position Grade/Scale Vacancies
Manager - Credit Analyst MMG/S-II 4
Senior Manager - Credit Analyst MMG/S-III 38
Chief Manager - Credit Analyst SMG/S-IV 1

Age & Minimum Experience

Position Age (Years) Minimum Experience
Manager - Credit Analyst 25-30 3 years in Credit, in any Public/Private/Foreign Bank or NBFC in India
Senior Manager - Credit Analyst 28-35 6 years in Credit, in any Public/Private/Foreign Bank or Financial Institution in India
Chief Manager - Credit Analyst 32-42 8 years in Banks/Financial Institutions, including 5 years in Credit Appraisal/Processing of Large/Mid Corporate Credit

C&IC Relationship Posts (Senior Manager & Chief Manager)

C&IC Relationship posts are client-facing roles centred on sourcing and managing corporate borrowers rather than appraising proposals, and together they account for 43 of the 86 vacancies.

Position Grade/Scale Vacancies
Senior Manager - C&IC Relationship MMG/S-III 36
Chief Manager - C&IC Relationship SMG/S-IV 7

Age & Minimum Experience

Position Age (Years) Minimum Experience
Senior Manager - C&IC Relationship 28-35 6 years in Banks/Financial Institutions, including 2 years handling MSME/Mid/Large Corporate Credit
Chief Manager - C&IC Relationship 32-42 8 years in Banks/Financial Institutions, including 3 years handling MSME/Mid/Large Corporate Credit

Across all 5 posts, work experience of less than 6 months in any organisation as on 1 June 2026 will not count toward eligibility unless it forms part of a candidate's current employment, and time spent in a clerical cadre role is not counted at all.

Eligibility Criteria: Qualification, Age & Work Experience

The educational qualification for Bank of Baroda C&IC Recruitment 2026 is identical across all 5 posts, while the age limit and experience requirement increase with seniority. Candidates should also note that the bank will only consider qualifications from institutions recognised by the Government of India or bodies like the AICTE.

  • Educational Qualification: Graduation in any discipline, plus a Post Graduate Degree or Diploma with specialisation in Finance (or its equivalent), OR a professional qualification such as CA, CMA, CS, or CFA.
  • Age: Calculated as on 1 June 2026, ranging from a minimum of 25 years (Manager grade) to a maximum of 42 years (Chief Manager grade).
  • Nationality: Indian citizens are eligible, along with certain categories of Nepali, Bhutanese, Tibetan refugee, and Indian-origin candidates from specified countries, subject to an eligibility certificate from the Government of India where applicable.
  • Posting Condition: Only candidates willing to serve anywhere in India should apply, since the bank can post selected officers to any branch or office at its discretion.

Reservation & Category-wise Vacancy Distribution

Reservation in Bank of Baroda C&IC Recruitment 2026 is applied separately for each of the 5 posts rather than as one combined pool, and candidates cannot change their declared category after registering online. Out of 86 total vacancies, 69 carry some form of category-based roster point while 17 remain unreserved.

Position Vacancies Category wise Vacancy
Manager - Credit Analyst 4 SC 0 | ST 3 | OBC 0 | EWS 1 | UR 0
Senior Manager - Credit Analyst 38 SC 12 | ST 7 | OBC 4 | EWS 10 | UR 5
Chief Manager - Credit Analyst 1 SC 0 | ST 0 | OBC 0 | EWS 1 | UR 0
Senior Manager - C&IC Relationship 36 SC 5 | ST 5 | OBC 16 | EWS 8 | UR 2
Chief Manager - C&IC Relationship 7 SC 0 | ST 4 | OBC 3 | EWS 0 | UR 0
Total 86 SC 17 | ST 19 | OBC 23 | EWS 20 | UR 7

OBC candidates falling under the creamy layer, or whose caste does not appear in the Central List, are not entitled to OBC reservation or age relaxation and must apply as General. EWS candidates must hold a valid Income and Asset Certificate, and there is no reservation for ex-servicemen in the officers' cadre for this recruitment.

Also Read: Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Recruitment 2026

Age Relaxation Rules for Reserved Categories

The age limits mentioned above apply to General category candidates as on 1 June Candidates from reserved categories get additional years added to the upper age limit, but only for posts where reservation roster points actually exist.

Category Age Relaxation
SC / ST 5 years
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years
Persons with Disability 10 years (Gen/EWS), 13 years (OBC), 15 years (SC/ST)
Eligible Ex-Servicemen / Commissioned Officers 5 years (Gen/EWS), 8 years (OBC), 10 years (SC/ST)

Pay Scale and Salary Structure for Each Grade

Pay for Bank of Baroda C&IC officers follows the standard bipartite settlement scale used across the banking industry, with the figure increasing through fixed annual increments up to a ceiling for each grade. Selected candidates also receive allowances and perquisites as per the bank's policies, in addition to this basic pay.

Grade/Scale Pay Scale (₹) Maximum Basic Pay (₹)
MMG/S-II 64,820 - 2340/1 - 67,160 - 2680/10 - 93,960 93,960
MMG/S-III 85,920 - 2680/5 - 99,320 - 2980/2 - 1,05,280 1,05,280
SMG/S-IV 1,02,300 - 2980/4 - 1,14,220 - 3360/2 - 1,20,940 1,20,940

In-Hand Salary Calculator 2026

CIBIL Score Requirement: A Credit History Check Unique to This Recruitment

Unlike most government recruitment notifications, this advertisement makes personal credit history a formal eligibility condition. Candidates need a minimum CIBIL score of 680 at the time of joining the bank, since this post involves handling corporate credit decisions.

Anyone who has defaulted on loan repayments or credit card dues, or who has adverse reports from CIBIL or other external credit assessment agencies, will be treated as ineligible for appointment. The bank has reserved the right to reject a candidate's selection at any stage, including after joining, if their credit profile turns out to be unsatisfactory.

Probation Period and Mandatory Service Bond

Selected candidates do not join in a permanent capacity immediately; they first complete a probation period, and they must also commit to a minimum service period backed by a financial bond.

  • Probation Period: 12 months (1 year) of active service from the date of joining.
  • Service Bond: Candidates must serve the bank for a minimum of 3 years from the date of joining, or pay ₹5 lakh in lieu of completing the bond period.

The exact bond terms will be confirmed in each selected candidate's offer letter and are subject to revision as per the bank's prevailing policy.

Application Fee for Bank of Baroda C&IC Recruitment 2026

The application fee for this recruitment is fixed by category and must be paid online; it is non-refundable even if the online test is not conducted or the candidate is not shortlisted for an interview.

Category Application Fee
General, EWS & OBC ₹850 (inclusive of GST) + Payment Gateway Charges
SC, ST, PWD, ESM/DESM & Women ₹175 (inclusive of GST) + Payment Gateway Charges

Selection Process: Online Test, Psychometric Assessment & Interview

Selection for Bank of Baroda C&IC Recruitment 2026 can include application scrutiny, an online test, a psychometric assessment, a group discussion, and a personal interview, though the bank retains the right to skip or modify any stage. Final selection depends on combined performance across whichever stages are actually conducted.

Online Test Pattern (Tentative)

If a written test is held, it will be a 150-mark, 150-minute online exam split into four sections, three of which only need to be passed rather than scored well in.

Section Test Questions Marks Duration
1 Reasoning 25 25 75 minutes (combined)
2 English Language 25 25
3 Quantitative Aptitude 25 25
4 Professional Knowledge 75 150 75 minutes
Total 150 225 150 minutes

Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and Professional Knowledge will be available bilingually in English and Hindi, while the English Language section will be in English only. Sections 1 to 3 are qualifying only and do not count toward the final score; candidates need at least 40% in General/EWS or 35% in Reserved categories to clear each of these sections, and there is no negative marking anywhere in the test.

Personal Interview & Final Merit

Candidates who clear the online test (or are otherwise shortlisted) move on to a psychometric assessment and/or personal interview, where the minimum qualifying score is 60% for General category and 55% for Reserved category candidates. If two or more candidates tie at the cut-off mark, the older candidate is ranked higher in the merit list.

The online test, if conducted, is tentatively planned across more than two dozen cities, including Mumbai, Delhi/NCR, Bangalore, Chennai, Kolkata, Hyderabad, Pune, Ahmedabad-Gandhinagar, Jaipur, Lucknow, Baroda, Bhopal, Chandigarh-Mohali, and several other regional centres, with the final allotted centre confirmed in the call letter.

Also Read: Karnataka Police Constable Notification Out for 3991 Vacancies

Documents Required During the Recruitment Process

Candidates do not need to upload every certificate while registering online, but they must be ready to produce the following documents in original, along with self-attested photocopies, at the interview stage or whenever the bank asks for them.

  • Printout of the interview call letter and the system-generated online application form
  • Proof of Date of Birth (Birth Certificate, SSLC/Std. X marksheet, or equivalent)
  • A valid photo identity proof such as PAN Card, Passport, Driving Licence, Voter's Card, or Aadhaar card (Ration Card is not accepted)
  • Year-wise/semester-wise mark sheets and the final degree/diploma certificate
  • Caste certificate for SC/ST/OBC candidates, with the OBC certificate carrying a valid non-creamy-layer clause
  • Disability certificate from the District Medical Board, for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities
  • Income and Asset Certificate, for candidates applying under the EWS category
  • Service/discharge book, pension payment order, and rank proof, for ex-servicemen candidates
  • No Objection Certificate from the current employer, for candidates already working in government, quasi-government, or public sector roles
  • Documentary proof of declared work experience, such as appointment letters, salary slips, or relieving letters

Step-by-Step Guide: How to

Bank of Baroda accepts applications for this recruitment only through its ; no offline or postal applications will be considered.

  1. Visit www.bankofbaroda.bank.in and navigate to the Careers section, then Current Opportunities.
  2. Register using a valid personal email ID and contact number that you can keep active until the recruitment process is complete.
  3. Fill in personal, educational, and work experience details carefully, since no changes are allowed once the form is submitted.
  4. Upload your photograph (200 x 230 pixels, 20-50 KB) and signature (140 x 60 pixels, 10-20 KB) in JPG/JPEG format, signed in your normal handwriting and not in capital letters.
  5. Upload your resume, Date of Birth proof, educational certificates (combined in one PDF), and category/PWD/experience certificates where applicable, each as a PDF on A4 size not exceeding 500 KB.
  6. Pay the applicable application fee online using a Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or UPI.
  7. Save and print the system-generated application form and fee receipt for your future reference.

for Bank of Baroda C&IC Recruitment 2026

Job Profile: What Will You Actually Do in This Role?

The two job families under this recruitment involve noticeably different day-to-day work, which is worth understanding before choosing which post to apply for.

Credit Analyst roles (Manager/Senior Manager/Chief Manager) are appraisal-focused: examining new and existing corporate credit proposals, conducting credit due-diligence on financial, industry, and business risk, coordinating with Relationship Managers, and ensuring compliance in corporate accounts.

C&IC Relationship roles (Senior Manager/Chief Manager) are client-facing: maintaining relationships with existing corporate clients, sourcing new-to-bank corporate borrowers, cross-selling ancillary business, and creating account plans with opportunity income analysis for corporate borrowers.

Mistakes That Could Cost You This Opportunity

Several avoidable errors can disqualify an otherwise eligible candidate from Bank of Baroda C&IC Recruitment 2026, so it helps to check these before hitting submit.

  • Assuming any postgraduate degree qualifies - only a finance-specialised PG degree/diploma or a CA/CMA/CS/CFA qualification meets the educational criteria.
  • Not checking your CIBIL score in advance - a score below 680, or any default history, can lead to rejection even after selection.
  • Counting clerical-cadre banking experience toward the eligibility criteria, which is explicitly excluded.
  • Including work experience of less than 6 months from a previous organisation, which will not be counted unless it is part of your current employment.
  • Submitting more than one application - only the latest valid one is retained, and fees paid for earlier ones are forfeited.
  • Selecting a category casually, since no change of category is allowed after registration and an invalid OBC/EWS claim without supporting certificates can affect your candidature.
  • Overlooking the 3-year/₹5 lakh service bond commitment before deciding whether to accept the role if selected.
  • Waiting until the last date to register, risking last-minute website load issues for which the bank takes no responsibility.

Bank of Baroda C&IC Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

Link Details
Click Here
PDF Click Here
Online Application Apply Here

title: HPRCA Physics Teacher Admit Card 2026
description: HPRCA Physics Teacher Admit Card 2026 - Download Hall Ticket Advertisement The Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA) has released the Teacher Admit Card 2026 for multiple tea
jobDescription:
HPRCA Physics Teacher Admit Card 2026 - Download Hall Ticket

Advertisement

The Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA) has released the Teacher Admit Card 2026 for multiple teaching posts on its at hprca.hp.gov.in. Candidates who applied for posts such as Junior Basic Teacher (JBT), TGT Arts, TGT Non-Medical, Mathematics Teacher, History Teacher, Economics Teacher, and others can now download their HPRCA hall ticket 2026 using their Application ID and password. The admit card is a mandatory document to carry to the examination centre -no candidate will be allowed entry without it. Read on for a complete guide including exam dates, download steps, details on the hall ticket, and important exam day instructions.

Direct Link to Download HPRCA Teacher Admit Card 2026

HPRCA Teacher Admit Card 2026 - Overview

This section provides a quick summary of all key details related to the HPRCA Teacher Admit Card 2026, covering the conducting body, post names, exam mode, and admit card availability.

Particulars Details
Conducting Authority Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA), Hamirpur
Post Names JBT, TGT Arts, TGT Non-Medical, TGT Medical, Mathematics Teacher, History Teacher, Economics Teacher, English Teacher, and others
Exam Mode Computer-Based Test (CBT)
Admit Card Status Released (varies by post)
hprca.hp.gov.in
Login Credentials Required Application ID and Password / Date of Birth

HPRCA Teacher Exam Date 2026 - Post-Wise Schedule

HPRCA has announced examination dates for several teacher recruitment posts under various post codes. Candidates must check the exam date relevant to their applied post and plan their preparation accordingly.

  • JBT (Junior Basic Teacher) - Post Code 26015: Exam held on February 25, 26, and 27, (600 vacancies)
  • Mathematics Teacher - Post Code 26003: Exam conducted in March (312 vacancies)
  • TGT Arts: Admit card released; exam date announced in January 2026.
  • History Teacher - Post Code 26025: Exam scheduled for June 28, 2026.
  • Economics Teacher - Post Code 26030: Exam scheduled for June 28, 2026.
  • JBT (Second Phase): CBT-based exam scheduled in June 2026 across various centres in Himachal Pradesh.

Note: Admit cards for upcoming exams (History, Economics, JBT June) will be released approximately one week before the respective exam dates. Candidates are advised to regularly check hprca.hp.gov.in.

How to Download HPRCA Teacher Admit Card 2026

Downloading the HPRCA Teacher Hall Ticket 2026 is a straightforward process. Candidates need their registered Application ID and password or date of birth ready. Follow these step-by-step instructions to access your admit card:

  1. Visit the official HPRCA website at hprca.hp.gov.in.
  2. On the homepage, click on the "Admit Card" tab or section.
  3. Locate and click the link relevant to your post (e.g., "Download Admit Card for the Post of Teacher (History) Post Code-26025").
  4. You will be redirected to the login page. Enter your Application ID and Password (or Date of Birth, as applicable).
  5. Click on Submit.
  6. Your HPRCA Teacher Admit Card 2026 will be displayed on the screen.
  7. Verify all details carefully. Download the hall ticket and take a printed copy for the examination centre.

You can also use the direct login link provided by HPRCA: HPRCA Admit Card Direct Login Link

Details Mentioned on HPRCA Teacher Hall Ticket 2026

The HPRCA Teacher Admit Card 2026 carries all the essential information a candidate needs on exam day. It is important to verify every detail on the hall ticket immediately after downloading, and report any discrepancy to the commission immediately.

  • Candidate's Name
  • Roll Number / Registration Number
  • Father's Name
  • Date of Birth
  • Category
  • Post Name and Post Code
  • Exam Date
  • Shift Timing and Reporting Time
  • Examination Centre Name and Address
  • Candidate's Photograph and Signature
  • Important exam day instructions

Documents to Carry Along with HPRCA Admit Card 2026

Candidates must carry the printed admit card along with a valid photo identity proof to the examination centre. Failure to present these documents may result in denial of entry.

  • Printed copy of the HPRCA Teacher Admit Card 2026
  • Any one valid original photo ID proof such as:
    • Aadhaar Card
    • PAN Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving Licence
    • Passport

HPRCA Teacher Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Understanding the full selection process helps candidates prepare comprehensively for each stage. The HPRCA Teacher Recruitment 2026 follows a structured, multi-stage process.

  1. Computer-Based Test (CBT) / Written Screening Test: The primary stage that shortlists candidates based on objective-type questions.
  2. Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates are required to submit original documents for verification.
  3. Medical Examination: Candidates who clear document verification undergo a physical fitness assessment.

HPRCA JBT Exam Pattern 2026

The HP JBT Teacher Computer-Based Test consists of 200 questions carrying a total of 100 marks. The duration of the examination is two hours. Candidates must manage their time efficiently during the test.

Important Instructions for HPRCA Exam Day 2026

HPRCA has issued specific guidelines that all candidates must follow on the day of the examination. Non-compliance with these instructions may lead to cancellation of candidature.

  • Carry a printed copy of the admit card to the examination centre -digital copies are not accepted.
  • Bring an original valid photo identity proof.
  • Reach the examination venue well before the reporting time mentioned on the admit card.
  • Do not carry electronic gadgets, smart watches, calculators, mobile phones, or any other prohibited items inside the examination hall.
  • Follow all examination guidelines mentioned on the hall ticket.
  • Preserve the admit card until the entire recruitment process, including final appointment, is completed.
  • Candidates who registered via SMS will receive roll number and exam centre details on their registered mobile number.

HPRCA Teacher Recruitment 2026 - Vacancies

HPRCA has announced a large-scale recruitment drive for teaching positions across Himachal Pradesh in The table below highlights the known vacancies for key posts.

Post Name Post Code Total Vacancies
Junior Basic Teacher (JBT) 26015 600
Mathematics Teacher 26003 312
History Teacher 26025 To be notified
Economics Teacher 26030 To be notified
English Teacher To be notified To be notified
TGT Arts To be notified To be notified

title: SAC ISRO Apprentice Walkin 2026
description: The Space Applications Centre (SAC), a premier centre of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) under the Department of Space, Government of India, has released an
jobDescription:
The Space Applications Centre (SAC), a premier centre of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) under the Department of Space, Government of India, has released an official recruitment notice inviting applications for Apprenticeship Training Candidates holding a Degree, Diploma, or ITI qualification in relevant disciplines can walk in directly at SAC's Ahmedabad campus between 20 June and 3 July This is a one-year training opportunity registered under the Apprentices Act, 1961 - one of the most prestigious apprenticeship programs available to science and engineering graduates in India.

SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Quick Overview

Advertisement

SAC ISRO is recruiting Graduate, Technician & Trade Apprentice Posts with Degree, Diploma, or ITI as qualification, Walkin on 20-06-2026 to 03-07-2026.

Detail Information
Organisation Space Applications Centre (SAC), ISRO
Department Department of Space, Government of India
Notice Number SAC: RMT: APP: 2026
Notice Date 08 June 2026
Apprentice Types Graduate, Technician, Trade
Total Post Codes 16
Training Duration 1 Year
Walk-In Dates 20 June 2026 – 03 July 2026
Walk-In Time 10:00 hrs to 13:00 hrs
Venue Building No. 30-D, SAC-ISRO, Jodhpur Tekra, Ahmedabad – 380015
Selection Basis First Come, First Serve
Application Mode Offline (in-person only)

Download SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 PDF

What Is the SAC ISRO Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026?

SAC ISRO's apprenticeship program is a structured, government-regulated training initiative conducted under the Apprentices Act, The Space Applications Centre at Ahmedabad - responsible for remote sensing, satellite communication, and space-based applications research - offers hands-on training exposure to fresh graduates, diploma holders, and ITI-qualified candidates in technical and non-technical streams.

This is not a permanent job offer. The apprenticeship is a one-year training contract that gives candidates valuable on-the-job experience within ISRO's operational environment - a significant credential for any engineering or science fresher.

SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Post Codes and Disciplines

SAC has notified 16 post codes covering three categories of apprentices. Each category has distinct educational requirements and eligible disciplines.

Graduate Apprentice (Post Codes 01–05)

Graduate Apprentices must hold a B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Arch. / B.Com / B.Lib or an equivalent qualification from a Government-recognized University, Board, or Institute with a minimum aggregate of 60% marks.

Post Code Discipline
01 Electronics & Communication Engineering
02 Mechanical Engineering
03 Architecture
04 Bachelor of Commerce
05 Library Science / Library & Information Science

Technician Apprentice (Post Codes 06–08)

Technician Apprentices must have a Diploma in Engineering or Technology in the relevant field with First Class from a recognized State Board of Technical Education.

Post Code Discipline
06 Electronics & Communication Engineering
07 Mechanical Engineering
08 Civil Engineering

Trade Apprentice (Post Codes 09–16)

Trade Apprentices must have passed SSC / Matriculation / 10th and hold an ITI (NTC/STC) certificate in the relevant trade from a recognized institute.

Post Code Trade
09 COPA
10 Painter (General)
11 Draughtsman Mechanical
12 Machinist
13 Fitter
14 Attendant Operator Chemical Plant
15 Refrigeration and Air-Conditioning
16 Electronics Mechanic

SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Eligibility Criteria

Candidates should carefully read all eligibility conditions before appearing for the walk-in. Appearing without meeting these conditions will result in disqualification.

Nationality

Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply for SAC ISRO Apprentice 2026.

State-Wise Eligibility Condition

This is one of the most important eligibility filters and is frequently misunderstood by applicants.

  • Graduate and Technician Apprentices (Post Codes 01–08): Candidates must have obtained their Degree or Diploma from a Government-recognized University/Board/Institute located in any of the following states/UTs:
    • Maharashtra
    • Gujarat
    • Goa
    • Chhattisgarh
    • Madhya Pradesh
    • Rajasthan
    • Dadra Nagar Haveli & Daman & Diu (UT)
  • Trade Apprentices (Post Codes 09–16): Candidates must have completed their ITI (NTC/STC) from a recognized institute in Gujarat State only.

Educational Qualification

  • Graduate Apprentice: Minimum 60% aggregate in B.E./B.Tech./B.Arch./B.Com/B.Lib or equivalent.
  • Technician Apprentice: First Class in Diploma in Engineering/Technology in the relevant discipline.
  • Trade Apprentice: SSC/10th pass + ITI (NTC/STC) in the relevant trade.

Age Limit

Post Codes Minimum Age Maximum Age
01 to 08 (Graduate & Technician) 18 years 28 years
09 to 16 (Trade Apprentices) 18 years 35 years

Age is calculated as on the date of joining.

Age Relaxation:

  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer): 3 years relaxation in upper age
  • SC / ST candidates: 5 years relaxation in upper age
  • PwBD candidates: As per Government of India rules

Year of Passing Condition

Candidates who passed their Degree/Diploma/ITI in or after July 2023 are eligible. Candidates who have completed three or more years since obtaining their qualifying degree as on the expected date of joining are not eligible, even if they passed after July 2023.

Prior Apprenticeship or Work Experience

Candidates who have already completed or are currently undergoing any apprenticeship training, or who have one year or more of job experience as on the date of joining, are not eligible.

SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Stipend

The monthly stipend offered to selected apprentices is fixed by the Government and varies by apprentice category.

Apprentice Type Monthly Stipend
Graduate Apprentice ₹12,300/- per month
Technician Apprentice ₹10,900/- per month
Trade Apprentice (Post Code 09 – COPA) ₹10,560/- per month
Trade Apprentice (Post Codes 10–16) ₹11,040/- per month

The training period is 12 months from the date of execution of the Apprenticeship contract.

SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Walk-In Schedule and Venue

Unlike most central government recruitment processes, this apprenticeship follows a walk-in, first-come-first-serve model. No online application or postal submission is accepted.

  • Walk-In Dates: 20 June 2026 to 03 July 2026
  • Days: All working days (Saturdays, Sundays, and declared holidays excluded)
  • Walk-In Timing: 10:00 hrs to 13:00 hrs
  • Venue: Building No. 30-D (Recruitment Section), Space Applications Centre, ISRO, Jodhpur Tekra, Ahmedabad – 380015

Important: On arrival at SAC, candidates must enter their details in the attendance sheet to confirm their order of appearance. This is mandatory.

SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Documents Required

Candidates must carry self-attested photocopies of all required documents along with originals at the time of the walk-in. Missing documents may lead to rejection of the application on the spot.

  • Mandatory documents for all candidates:
  • Proof of Date of Birth (10th passing certificate / School Leaving Certificate / Birth Certificate)
  • Aadhaar Card and PAN Card
  • SSC (10th) Marksheet and Certificate
  • Qualifying Exam Marksheets & Certificates:
  • Trade Apprentices: ITI (NTC/STC) in the relevant trade
  • Technician Apprentices: Final/Provisional Diploma + all semester marksheets
  • Graduate Apprentices: Final/Provisional Degree + all semester marksheets
  • Latest OBC (NCL – FY 2026-27) Certificate, if applicable
  • Caste/Tribe Certificate (SC/ST), if applicable
  • PwBD Certificate, if applicable
  • EWS Income and Asset Certificate, if applicable
  • Local Police Verification cum Character Certificate (in Original)
  • Blood Group Report
  • SBI Bank Account details

Portal Registration (mandatory before walk-in):

  • Trade Apprentices: Register at https://apprenticeshipindia.org
  • Graduate & Technician Apprentices: Register at https://nats.education.gov.in

How to Apply

The application process is entirely offline and in-person. There is no provision for online, email, or postal submission of applications.

  1. Download and fill the official application form attached to the SAC notification (available on the official ISRO/SAC website).
  2. Register on the appropriate apprenticeship portal (NATS or Apprenticeship India) before appearing for the walk-in.
  3. Gather all required documents in self-attested photocopies along with originals.
  4. Visit Building No. 30-D, Space Applications Centre, ISRO, Jodhpur Tekra, Ahmedabad – 380015, on any working day between 20 June and 3 July 2026 during 10:00–13:00 hrs.
  5. Enter your name in the attendance sheet on arrival - this is mandatory to confirm your order in the first-come-first-serve queue.
  6. Submit your application personally at the Recruitment Section.

Applications submitted by post or email will not be considered under any circumstances.

Selection Process

There is no written examination or interview for this apprenticeship. Selection is done purely on a first-come-first-serve basis, subject to verification of documents and eligibility.

  • Candidates appearing earlier in the walk-in period have a higher chance of selection, given limited seats.
  • If any discrepancy is found in name, age, qualification results, year of passing, or caste/tribe details at any stage, the candidature will be immediately disqualified.
  • Candidates who do not meet medical fitness criteria at the time of joining will have their candidature cancelled automatically.

Important Terms and Conditions

Understanding these conditions is critical before appearing for the walk-in, as violations at any stage lead to disqualification.

  • Apprentices will have no claim for regular employment at SAC-ISRO, any other ISRO Centre, or under the Department of Space after completion of training.
  • The apprenticeship contract is registered under the Apprentices Act, 1961 and Amendment Act, 1973.
  • Candidates in existing Government service must disclose this in their application.
  • Seats are limited; early walk-in increases your chance of selection.
  • All decisions regarding eligibility, selection, and joining rest with SAC-ISRO administration.

Why Apply for SAC ISRO Apprenticeship 2026?

For engineering and science freshers, an apprenticeship at ISRO's Space Applications Centre is a rare opportunity that carries significant weight in the job market and for higher studies abroad. Here's why candidates across disciplines actively seek this opportunity:

  • Prestigious credential - ISRO is among the world's leading space agencies; SAC-specific experience adds unique value to a resume.
  • Government-regulated stipend - Monthly stipend of up to ₹12,300 during the training year.
  • Hands-on technical exposure - Direct involvement in space-application projects across electronics, mechanical, civil, and IT domains.
  • NATS/Apprenticeship India certification - Completion leads to a nationally recognized apprenticeship certificate useful for both private-sector jobs and government competitive exams.
  • No bond or service obligation - Unlike some training programs, there is no post-training bond.

Source: Official Notice No. SAC: RMT: APP: 2026 dated 08.06.2026, issued by the Administrative Officer, Space Applications Centre (ISRO), Ahmedabad.

title: Central Sanskrit University Teaching Online Form 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: Central Sanskrit University (CSU), Delhi has released Advertisement No. 04/2026 dated 10.06.2026 , inviting online applications for 90 Teaching and Other Academic posts across its
jobDescription:
Central Sanskrit University (CSU), Delhi has released Advertisement No. 04/2026 dated 10.06.2026, inviting online applications for 90 Teaching and Other Academic posts across its three campuses — Vedvyas (Himachal Pradesh), Shri Raghunath Kirti (Uttarakhand), and Ekalavya (Tripura). Online applications can be submitted from 10 June 2026 to 10 July 2026 (11:30 PM) through the CU Chayan Portal at curec.samarth.ac.in.

Central Sanskrit University Recruitment 2026 - Quick Summary

Advertisement

Central Sanskrit University is recruiting 90 Teaching posts including Associate Professor (Level-13A) & Assistant Professor with Ph.D pass qualification, last date 10 July 2026.

Particulars Details
Organization Central Sanskrit University (CSU), Delhi
Advertisement No. 04/2026, dated 10.06.2026
Posts Associate Professor (Level-13A) & Assistant Professor / Other Academic (Level-10)
Total Vacancies 90 Posts
Application Mode Online via curec.samarth.ac.in
Online Application Start 10 June 2026 (Wednesday)
Online Application Last Date 10 July 2026 (Friday), 11:30 PM IST
Hard Copy Submission Within 15 days from last date of online application
www.sanskrit.nic.in
  • Download Central Sanskrit University Teaching Recruitment 2026 PDF

Important Dates for CSU Recruitment 2026

Candidates planning to apply should keep the following timeline in mind, as the University has clearly stated that the online application link will be disabled after the closing date.

Event Date
Notification Release Date 10 June 2026
Online Registration Start Date 10 June 2026 (Wednesday)
Online Application Last Date 10 July 2026 (Friday), 11:30 PM IST
Hard Copy Submission Deadline Within 15 days from the online application closing date

The criteria date for determining a candidate's eligibility in all respects is the last date of submission of the online application, meaning all qualifications, NET/Ph.D., experience, and publications must be completed by 10 July 2026.

CSU Vacancy Details 2026: Post-wise Breakdown

CSU has divided the 90 vacancies into two parts — Part A covers Associate Professor posts at Pay Level-13A, while Part B covers Assistant Professor and other academic posts at Pay Level-Below is the complete post-wise vacancy distribution.

Part A: Associate Professor Posts (Level-13A) – Total 23 Vacancies

Post / Subject UR SC ST OBC EWS Total
Associate Professor (Sahitya) - 1 - 1 1* 3

Adding both parts, the grand total stands at 90 vacancies (36 UR, 14 SC, 7 ST, 24 OBC, 9 EWS), with 7 posts reserved for PwBD candidates across categories (a), (b), and (c).

Campus-wise Posting Locations

Candidates often want to know which campus a particular post belongs to, since CSU posts under this advertisement are campus-specific. The three campuses are:

  • Ekalavya Campus – Vill. Sipai Para, P.O. Lembucherra, West Tripura – 799210
  • Vedvyas Campus – Balahar, Kangra, Himachal Pradesh – 177108
  • Shri Raghunath Kirti Campus – Devprayag, Pauri Garhwal, Uttarakhand – 249301

Eligibility Criteria for CSU Recruitment 2026

For candidates wondering about the educational qualifications and age limits for these posts, CSU has specified that the detailed eligibility for each post is given separately in Annexure-I of the . Qualifications and other eligibility criteria are determined as per the norms of the UGC, NCTE, NCISM, and the Statutes/Ordinances of CSU, including amendments made from time to time.

The age limit and relaxations are governed by applicable UGC Regulations and Government of India norms, along with the Statutes/Ordinances of CSU. The criteria date for determining eligibility in every respect — including qualifications, NET/Ph.D., teaching/research experience, and publications — is the last date of online application submission, i.e., 10 July 2026. Candidates should refer to Annexure-I of the for post-wise qualification details.

CSU Application Fee 2026: Category-wise Details

The application fee for CSU Recruitment 2026 depends on the post level (10 or 13A) and the candidate's category, and must be paid only through online mode. Here is the complete fee structure:

Post Level Category Application/Exam Fee Processing Charges Total Fee
Level-10 UR/OBC/EWS ₹1000 ₹500 ₹1500
Level-10 SC/ST/Women/PwBD/Transgender Nil ₹500 ₹500
Level-13A UR/OBC/EWS ₹1000 ₹1000 ₹2000
Level-13A SC/ST/Women/PwBD/Transgender Nil ₹1000 ₹1000

It's important to note that fees once paid are non-refundable under any circumstances, and a separate application along with the prescribed fee is required for each subject/post applied for. One registration is sufficient to apply for multiple posts, but separate fee payment is mandatory for each subject.

CSU Selection Process 2026: Step-by-Step

Candidates wondering how the selection will be conducted can note that the process differs slightly for Associate Professor and Assistant Professor posts, though both involve shortlisting, assessment, and interview stages.

  • Step 1 – Online Application & Scrutiny: Candidates and upload documents; applications are provisionally scrutinized for eligibility.
  • Step 2 – Written Test (for Assistant Professor / Level-10 posts only): An OMR-based test with Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) and a Descriptive Test assessing Research Aptitude and Writing Skills. If eligible candidates for a subject are fewer than 20, the written test may be dispensed with.
  • Step 3 – No Written Test for Associate Professor (Level-13A): These posts go directly to shortlisting based on Video Presentation, Research Skill assessment, and PPT presentation.
  • Step 4 – Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates undergo verification of original certificates related to age, qualification, experience, and category.
  • Step 5 – Face-to-Face Interview: The final stage for all shortlisted candidates across both Level-10 and Level-13A posts.

Detailed information on the selection procedure, shortlisting criteria, syllabus, and weightage will be announced separately on the CSU website (www.sanskrit.nic.in).

Examination Centres

Candidates can choose any one examination centre from Delhi, Mumbai/Pune, Kolkata, or Bengaluru, regardless of which campus they have applied for. Those applying for multiple posts are advised to select only one city to avoid scheduling conflicts.

Examination Schedule Groups

To help candidates applying for multiple subjects avoid clashes, CSU has grouped the subjects into three schedules. Exams within the same schedule are held simultaneously, so a candidate can appear for only one subject per schedule.

Schedule 1 Schedule 2 Schedule 3
Sahitya Vyakarana Mimamsa
English Jyotisha Phalita Nyaya/Vaiseshika
Jyotisha Siddhanta Dharma Shastra/Mimamsa/Law Shiksha Shastra (Education)
Veda, Pourohitya & Karmakanda Vedanta (Advaita / Other branches) Computer Sciences
Bauddha Darshan Physical Education & Sports Yogic Sciences
Assistant Librarian - Hindi

How to Apply for CSU Recruitment 2026 Online

Applicants who want to know the exact application steps can follow this simple process, which is entirely online except for the final hard-copy submission.

  1. Visit the CU Chayan Portal at curec.samarth.ac.in or access it via www.sanskrit.nic.in under "Recruitment/Notification."
  2. Register yourself on the portal to generate a registration number.
  3. Select the post/subject you wish to apply for and fill in the online application form.
  4. Upload clear, high-quality scanned copies of all required documents, certificates, and publications.
  5. Pay the prescribed application/examination fee online based on your category and post level.
  6. Submit the application before 10 July 2026, 11:30 PM IST (no correction window is available after submission).
  7. Take a printout of the application and fee receipt, sign it, attach self-attested document copies, and superscribe the envelope with the Advertisement Number and post/subject applied for.
  8. Send the hard copy by registered post to "The Registrar, Central Sanskrit University, 56-57, Institutional Area, Janakpuri, New Delhi-110058" so it reaches within 15 days from the online application closing date.

Candidates already employed in Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies must additionally submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) and, where applicable, a Vigilance Clearance Certificate from their employer.

Salary and Pay Level for CSU Posts

Candidates curious about the pay structure can refer to the Pay Level (as per 7th CPC) mentioned against each post in the .

  • Associate Professor posts (Part A) are placed at Pay Level-13A.
  • Assistant Professor, Assistant Director, and Assistant Librarian posts (Part B) are placed at Pay Level-10.

The University has not specified exact in-hand salary figures in the notification; candidates can refer to the standard 7th Pay Commission pay matrix corresponding to Level-10 and Level-13A for indicative pay ranges.

Document Requirements & Caste/Category Certificates

Reserved category candidates often ask which certificates are mandatory — CSU requires self-attested copies of the Caste/Class Certificate and, for PwBD candidates, a Medical Certificate from the Competent Authority in the Government of India prescribed format.

  • OBC Certificate: Must be a valid Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) certificate issued on or after 01.04.2026 for this advertisement. Certificates issued before this date will not be accepted.
  • EWS Certificate: Must be an Income & Assets certificate valid for the current financial year, issued by a competent authority such as a District Magistrate, Tehsildar, or Sub-Divisional Officer, and issued on or before the last date of online application.
  • PwBD Certificate: A valid Disability Certificate showing a disability of not less than 40% must be uploaded to claim PwBD benefits.

Candidates failing to submit valid category certificates will not be considered for that reservation and will instead be treated as Unreserved (UR) candidates.

Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying

Many applicants lose their candidature due to avoidable errors during the CSU application process. Keep the following points in mind to ensure a smooth application:

  • Don't wait until the last day: Heavy server load near the deadline (10 July 2026) can prevent successful submission, and CSU will not entertain complaints about this.
  • Double-check before final submission: No correction window is provided once the online form is submitted, so verify all details — name, category, post, photo, and signature — carefully.
  • Use a valid OBC certificate date: Certificates issued before 01.04.2026 will be rejected for OBC claims under this advertisement.
  • Match signatures and photographs: Any variation between the photo/signature on the form and on supporting documents can lead to summary rejection.
  • Pay the correct fee for each subject: A separate application and fee is required for every individual post/subject applied for.
  • Send hard copy on time with correct superscription: The envelope must clearly mention the Advertisement Number and post/subject; missing this can delay or invalidate your application.
  • Choose one exam city if applying for multiple posts: This avoids scheduling conflicts between different examination slots.

SSA resource persons get age relaxation equivalent to years served. Women candidates also get a general age relaxation of 10 years.

DSSSB Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

The application fee is ₹100 (One Hundred only). Women candidates and candidates belonging to SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Serviceman categories are fully exempted from paying the fee. Ex-Servicemen who have already secured regular civil employment under the Central Government or Government of NCT of Delhi after availing ESM reservation benefits are not eligible for fee exemption.

Payment must be made only through SBI ePay. No other payment mode will be accepted, and fees paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

DSSSB Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be made through a Computer Based Written Examination under one of three examination schemes depending on the post applied for.

One Tier General (I-T-G) — Post Codes 34/26, 39/26, 40/26: Duration: 2 hours | 200 MCQs | 200 marks. Five sections of 40 marks each: General Awareness, General Intelligence & Reasoning Ability, Arithmetical & Numerical Ability, Hindi Language & Comprehension, and English Language & Comprehension. Final merit is based on written examination marks only.

One Tier Technical (I-T-T) — Post Codes 21/26 to 31/26, 32/26, 33/26, 35/26 to 38/26, 45/26: Duration: 2 hours | 200 MCQs | 200 marks. Section A: 100 marks covering General Awareness, Reasoning, Arithmetic, Hindi, and English (20 marks each). Section B: 100 marks on the domain subject for the post concerned. Minimum qualifying marks apply only to Section B; combined marks of both sections determine the final merit list.

One Tier Technical/Teaching (I-T-T) — Post Codes 41/26, 42/26, 43/26, 44/26: Duration: 2 hours | 200 MCQs | 200 marks. Section A: 100 marks (same five general subjects, 20 marks each). Section B: 100 marks from subject-specific content including teaching methodology/B.Ed. Minimum qualifying marks apply only to Section B; combined marks of both sections determine final merit.

Minimum Qualifying Marks:

Category Minimum Marks
General / EWS 40%
OBC (Delhi) 35%
SC / ST / PwBD 30%
Ex-Servicemen 5% relaxation in respective category (minimum 30%)

Negative marking applies: 0.25 marks will be deducted for each wrong MCQ answer. Skill Test, Aptitude Test, Trade Test, or Physical Endurance Test may be conducted additionally as per job requirements. Marks will be normalized (if required) using the Score Normalisation Based on Deviation Method formula published by DSSSB vide Notice No. 10(271)/Sec.Cell/DSSSB/18/989 dated 11.07.2018.
