How to Apply

The application process for DSSSB Recruitment 2026 is entirely online. No applications sent by post, by hand, or by email will be accepted.

  1. Visit the official DSSSB registration and application portal at https://dsssbonline.nic.in.
  2. If you are a new user, complete the one-time registration process. Note down your User ID and password — the same credentials are to be used for all future DSSSB examinations; do not register multiple times.
  3. Log in using your registered credentials and select the relevant post code from Advertisement No. 03/2026.
  4. Fill in all fields of the online application form accurately. Once submitted, no change or correction — including change of category — will be permitted under any circumstances.
  5. Upload a recent, clear colour photograph (plain white or off-white background, full face, front view, eyes open) and your signature. Blurred or illegible photographs/signatures will result in rejection.
  6. Pay the application fee of ₹100 through SBI ePay (exempted categories need not pay).
  7. Submit the form and take a printout for your own records. Keep the registration number and application number safely.

The application portal will be live from 16 June 2026 (12:00 Noon) until 15 July 2026 (11:59 PM), after which the link will be disabled.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

Candidates applying for DSSSB 2026 should be careful about a few common pitfalls in the online application process.

Photo and signature uploads are a frequent reason for rejection — ensure the image files are clear, correctly sized, and within the file-size limit specified on the portal. Blurred or illegible uploads will cause outright rejection of candidature. If the payment gateway shows an error after fee deduction, do not immediately retry; wait for a few hours to check whether the payment was credited before attempting again through the portal's payment history section. Use an updated browser (Chrome or Firefox preferred) and avoid mobile browsers for form submission, as they may not render all fields correctly. Save your User ID and password immediately after registration and store your application number carefully — all future communications from DSSSB will reference these. Avoid applying on the last day; DSSSB expressly states it will not be responsible for inability to submit due to heavy server load near the closing date.

DSSSB Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

Resource Link
PDF Click Here
Official DSSSB Website https://dsssb.delhi.gov.in
Online Application Portal https://dsssbonline.nic.in
Vacancy Details Page https://dsssb.delhi.gov.in/dsssb-vacancies

title: ISRO URSC Junior Research Fellow Online form 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: U R Rao Satellite Centre (URSC), Bengaluru - the lead satellite technology centre of ISRO under the Department of Space, Government of India - has invited online applications for 1
jobDescription:
U R Rao Satellite Centre (URSC), Bengaluru - the lead satellite technology centre of ISRO under the Department of Space, Government of India - has invited online applications for 17 posts of Junior Research Fellow (JRF) vide Advertisement No. URSC:02:2026 dated 12 June 2026. The online application window is open from 12 June 2026 to 11 July 2026 at www.isro.gov.in under the "CAREERS" section. Only Indian nationals who have already passed their qualifying degree are eligible - candidates awaiting final results need not .

: Advertisement No. URSC:02:2026 - www.isro.gov.in

ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 - Quick Summary

Advertisement

This recruitment is for 17 Junior Research Fellow positions at URSC Bengaluru under ISRO. Applications are accepted online only through the ISRO website between 12 June 2026 and 11 July The post name, recruiting organisation, total positions, and application mode are listed below.

Post Name Junior Research Fellow (JRF)
Advertisement No. URSC:02:2026
Organisation U R Rao Satellite Centre (URSC), ISRO, Bengaluru
Total Positions 17
Application Start Date 12 June 2026
Last Date to Apply 11 July 2026
Application Mode Online - www.isro.gov.in
Interview Location Bengaluru

ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates

The online application portal opens on 12 June 2026 and the last date for receipt of online applications is 11 July 2026. The date 11 July 2026 is also the cut-off date for all purposes including determination of age and qualification. No applications will be accepted after the portal closes.

Event Date
Notification Date 12 June 2026
Online Application Opens 12 June 2026
Last Date to 11 July 2026
Age & Qualification Cut-off Date 11 July 2026

ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 - Position-Wise Vacancy Details

URSC has notified 17 JRF positions across Electronics, Computer Science, Optics, Mechanical, and allied engineering disciplines. Each position carries a distinct area of work and a specific educational qualification requirement. The number of positions is provisional and may vary depending on actual requirements.

Position No. Vacancies Essential Qualification (Discipline) Area of Work
JRF-E1 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Microelectronics or equivalent Microcircuits Assembly & Packaging; ECAD software for layout design of Microcircuits/Hybrid Microcircuits/Multichip Modules; Advanced microelectronics packaging; Thick & Thin film processes for high density interconnection substrates
JRF-E2 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Computer Science / Information Technology or equivalent User Interface development; Modelling of Mission analysis packages
JRF-E3 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Computer Science / Information Technology or equivalent Development of telemetry and telecommand software packages for spacecraft operations
JRF-E4 02 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Power Electronics or equivalent DC-DC converter development; 100V power bus development
JRF-E5 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Digital Electronics / Micro-Electronics / Signal Processing / VLSI / Embedded Systems or equivalent Design, development and testing of spacecraft testing equipment; Analog, digital and mixed signal circuits; Micro-controller and FPGA based embedded system development; FPGA/VHDL firmware development
JRF-E6 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Engineering Physics / Applied Optics or equivalent / Integrated M.Tech in Optical Engineering Development of segmented Mirror Optics; Characterization of Mirror segment; Alignment of segments to form large telescope mirror
JRF-E7 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Applied Physics / Material Science / Material Engineering / Opto-electronics or equivalent THz detector material studies and characterization; Lithographic Micro-Antenna for THz/mm wavelength; THz Optics and resonant filter development
JRF-E8 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Optical Engineering / Applied Optics / Opto-electronics or equivalent / Integrated M.Tech in Optical Engineering Optical subsystem design and simulation; Free space transmission studies through atmosphere for optical wavelengths; Link budget estimation and APD sensitivity studies
JRF-E9 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Electronics & Communication or equivalent FPGA & ASIC development and testing; Digital signal processing; AI/ML algorithms and data analysis
JRF-E10 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Digital Electronics / Micro Electronics / Signal Processing / Mechatronics or equivalent FPGA/VHDL design for digital logics, control applications, Board and System Design, integration and testing
JRF-E11 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Digital Electronics / VLSI / Embedded Systems / VLSI and Embedded Systems or equivalent VLSI design, Embedded System Design and High Performance Computing Systems Design
JRF-E12 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Electronics & Communications / Avionics / Instrumentation Design, development and testing of digital circuits; Hardware Package Interface; FPGA development, MATLAB, Python, C++, LINUX Device Drivers
JRF-E13 01 M.Sc in Mathematics / Physics OR M.Tech or equivalent in Aerospace Analysis of Flight Dynamics products (Satellite Ephemeris, eclipse prediction) for mission planning; Tracking data analysis and Orbit determination
JRF-M1 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Thermal Engineering / Thermal Science & Engineering / Thermal Science & Energy Systems / Heat Transfer in Energy Systems or equivalent Numerical modelling of active thermal devices; Analysis of experimental data for AI/ML; Data driven model development; Use of MATLAB, UGNx-TMG, VC++, Python, JAVA, SQL
JRF-M2 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Mechanical Engineering / Aerospace Engineering or equivalent Reliability modelling of engineering systems; Failure theories; Life Testing & Environmental Testing; Statistical distributions & Optimization; Risk Assessment
JRF-M3 01 M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Mechanical Engineering / Machine Design or equivalent Design of mechanical elements; Stress analysis using finite element methods; Kinematic & Dynamic analysis of mechanisms; 3D modelling software tools

The position of JRF does not confer any claim or right for regular appointment in URSC or in any ISRO/Department of Space centres.

ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Eligibility for ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 is two-part: a qualifying postgraduate degree with the required minimum marks, and a valid national-level qualifying test score. Both conditions must be satisfied simultaneously as on the last date, i.e., 11 July 2026. Only passed candidates are eligible - students awaiting final results should not .

Educational Qualification

For positions JRF-E1 to JRF-E12, JRF-M1, JRF-M2, JRF-M3: M.E./M.Tech./M.Sc.(Engg.) or equivalent postgraduate degree in First Class with an aggregate minimum of 60% or CGPA/CPI of 6.5 on a 10-point scale.

For position JRF-E13 (M.Sc. route): M.Sc. in First Class with an aggregate minimum of 65% average of all semesters or CGPA/CPI of 6.84 on a 10-point scale.

For position JRF-E13 (M.Tech. route): M.Tech. or equivalent in First Class with an aggregate minimum of 60% or CGPA/CPI of 6.5 on a 10-point scale.

All degrees must be duly recognised by AICTE/UGC or any other organisation authorised by the Government of India. Essential qualifications must be completed within the stipulated duration of the course as prescribed by the university. Candidates who pursued professional courses through Open and Distance Learning (ODL) are not eligible to apply for JRF positions.

Where a university mentions both CGPA and percentage in its degree certificate or consolidated mark sheet, at least one criterion must meet DOS/ISRO eligibility norms. Conversion of CGPA into percentage of marks is not permitted to determine eligibility regardless of any conversion formula prescribed by the university. Rounding of percentage/CGPA is not allowed - values must be entered to the nearest 2 decimal places (e.g., 8.235 entered as 8.23; 8.236 as 8.24).

NET / NET-Equivalent Requirement

Candidates must have qualified one of the following national-level examinations. This is a mandatory requirement - possession of the degree alone does not make a candidate eligible to .

  • CSIR-UGC National Eligibility Test including NET-Lectureship (Assistant Professorship)
  • GATE (Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering) conducted by MoE
  • JAM (Joint Admission Test) conducted by MoE
  • JEST (Joint Entrance Screening Test)
  • National level examinations conducted by Central Government Departments and their agencies/institutions such as DST, DBT, DAE, DOS, DRDO, MoE, ICAR, ICMR, IIT, IISc, IISER, NISER, etc.

Age Limit (as on 11 July 2026)

The maximum age for all JRF positions is 28 years as on 11 July 2026. Candidates wondering about age relaxation can note that SC/ST candidates get 5 years of relaxation and OBC candidates get 3 years. Additional relaxations as per Government of India orders apply to Ex-Servicemen, Persons with Benchmark Disabilities, Widows, Divorced Women, and Women judicially separated from their husbands and not remarried.

Category Age Relaxation
SC / ST 5 years
OBC 3 years
Ex-Servicemen As per Government of India orders
Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) As per Government of India orders
Widows, Divorced Women, Women judicially separated (not remarried) As per Government of India orders

ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 - Fellowship and Stipend

Selected JRFs at ISRO URSC receive a monthly fellowship stipend of ₹37,000 per month. After completion of two years as JRF, at the sole discretion of ISRO/DOS/URSC, the fellow may be awarded Senior Research Fellow (SRF) status with a stipend of ₹42,000 per month. HRA at prescribed rates is paid if hostel accommodation is not provided at the place of posting. Medical benefits for self are admissible as per extant rules. No Dearness Allowance (DA) is paid. The fellowship amount is treated as basic pay for this purpose.

JRFs are eligible only for casual leave. All female JRFs are entitled to maternity leave as per Government of India instructions. JRFs are also eligible for subsidised canteen facilities. Selected candidates are not entitled to any other benefits or concessions admissible to regular employees. The initial engagement is for two years, extendable based on assessment of work and project requirements. The maximum combined period of engagement as JRF and SRF together shall not exceed five years. JRFs with aptitude for research will be encouraged to register for a Ph.D. with an external university.

ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for ISRO URSC JRF 2026 involves an initial screening stage followed by an interview. Meeting the minimum qualification is necessary but does not automatically entitle a candidate to be called for interview - shortlisting is based on academic performance and other parameters in the online application.

  1. Initial Screening - Based on academic performance and other parameters given in the online application. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for interview.
  2. Interview - Conducted in Bengaluru only on a given date. Call letters for the interview will be sent by e-mail only. The interview result will be announced on the ISRO website.

URSC/ISRO reserves the right to change the venue/date of the interview. Outstation candidates shortlisted for interview will be reimbursed to-and-fro Second Class Sleeper Railway fare by the shortest distance from the address in the online application to the interview venue, or non-A/C government bus fare, or actual fare - whichever is less - on production of proof of journey. If a candidate travels by a higher class, only second class rail fare excluding reservation/sleeper charges will be paid. Travel allowance is payable only to candidates allowed for interview after successful document verification.

How to Apply

Applications for ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 must be submitted online only. All further communications will be made exclusively by e-mail and through the ISRO website. Candidates are advised to check their e-mail regularly and visit www.isro.gov.in periodically for updates.

  1. Visit www.isro.gov.in and navigate to the "CAREERS" section
  2. Find Advertisement No. URSC:02:2026 and select the JRF position you wish to apply for
  3. Register online - note your Registration Number carefully and preserve it for future reference
  4. Fill in all required fields including a valid and correct e-mail ID - hall tickets will be sent by e-mail only
  5. Upload the following mandatory documents in soft copy:
    • Recent colour passport-size photograph (not older than 6 months) - JPG/JPEG, maximum 500 KB
    • Signature - JPG/JPEG, maximum 500 KB
    • Mark cards of each semester/year with final pass certificate - single PDF, not exceeding 1 MB
    • SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Serviceman certificate if seeking age relaxation - PDF, not exceeding 1 MB
    • CSIR-UGC-NET score card or GATE score card or equivalent national examination score card - PDF, not exceeding 1 MB
  6. Submit the application before 11 July 2026
  7. Candidates already employed under Central/State Government, Public Sector Undertaking, or Autonomous Body must follow up the online application with a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer, indicating name, position applied for, Position No., and Registration No. on the reverse of the NOC
  8. Avoid submitting multiple applications for the same position - if multiple applications are submitted, only the latest application submitted before the closing date will be processed

For any clarification, candidates may write to [email protected]. No interim correspondence will otherwise be entertained. Canvassing in any form will result in disqualification of candidature.

Document Requirements at Interview

Shortlisted candidates called for the interview must bring originals and self-attested photocopies of all of the following documents to the interview venue in Bengaluru.

  • All certificates and mark sheets of all degrees and postgraduate degree
  • Equivalency Certificate from Association of Indian Universities, New Delhi - in case of a foreign degree
  • Proof of work experience
  • Age proof
  • CSIR-UGC-NET score card or GATE score card or score card of equivalent national level examination
  • Caste certificate - for SC/ST/OBC candidates seeking age relaxation
  • PwBD certificate - for candidates claiming PwBD age relaxation
  • Relevant certificates issued by Competent Authority for Ex-Servicemen and other categories claiming age relaxation

Troubleshooting and Common Mistakes

Applying through an online government portal involves several steps where errors can cause rejection. The following general guidance helps candidates complete their application successfully.

  • Photo upload errors: The photograph must be in JPG/JPEG format, not older than 6 months, and not exceeding 500 KB. Uploading any object other than your own passport-size photograph in the designated photo field will result in your candidature being rejected at screening stage under any circumstances.
  • Incomplete applications rejected outright: Applications submitted without complete information or without a photograph will be summarily rejected. Verify every mandatory field before final submission.
  • Last-day server load: Online portals experience high traffic close to the deadline. Submit your application well before 11 July 2026 to avoid gateway timeouts or submission failures.
  • Save your Registration Number: Upon successful registration, an online Registration Number is provided. Store it securely - it is required for all future reference, hall ticket download, and communications.
  • E-mail ID accuracy: The e-mail ID entered in the application must be valid and actively monitored. Hall tickets and all official communications will be sent exclusively by e-mail. Errors in e-mail ID cannot be corrected after submission.
  • Multiple applications: If you accidentally submit more than one application for the same position, only the latest application submitted before the closing date will be processed. Ensure that application is complete in all respects.
  • PDF file size: Each supporting document PDF must not exceed 1 MB. Compress your PDF if the file is larger before uploading.
  • ODL degree: Candidates who completed their qualifying postgraduate degree through Open and Distance Learning are not eligible. Verify your mode of study before applying.

ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

Description Link
Online Application / Click Here
Online Application Click Here
Corrigendum / Updates www.isro.gov.in
Clarification E-mail [email protected]

title: Punjab and Sind Bank Various Posts Online Form 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: Punjab & Sind Bank , a Government of India Undertaking, has released a notification inviting applications for 8 contractual posts across its Cloud Centre of Excellence and Artifici
jobDescription:
Punjab & Sind Bank, a Government of India Undertaking, has released a notification inviting applications for 8 contractual posts across its Cloud Centre of Excellence and Artificial Intelligence verticals. The posts span three grade levels - MMGS II, MMGS III, and SMGS IV. Online applications open on 12 June 2026 and close on 28 June 2026. Experienced IT professionals in cloud engineering and AI/ML development should apply at the bank's , https://punjabandsind.bank.in/.

Punjab and Sind Bank Recruitment 2026 - Quick Summary

Advertisement
Field Detail
Organisation Punjab & Sind Bank (Govt. of India Undertaking)
Posts Manager / Senior Manager / Chief Manager - Cloud CoE; Manager / Senior Manager - AI Engineers
Total Vacancies 8
Application Mode Online only
Apply Start Date 12 June 2026
Last Date to Apply 28 June 2026
https://punjabandsind.bank.in/

The last date for online application, application editing, and online fee payment is 28 June 2026. No other mode of application is accepted.

Punjab and Sind Bank Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates

Candidates must note these dates carefully, as the bank will not entertain any request for extension or change of date.

Event Date
Online Application Opens 12 June 2026
Last Date to (including Edit & Fee Payment) 28 June 2026
Cut-off Date for Age Eligibility 01 June 2026
Cut-off Date for Work Experience 28 June 2026

The eligibility cut-off date for age is 01 June 2026, while the cut-off for reckoning post-qualification work experience is 28 June 2026. Any experience or qualification completed after these respective dates will not be counted.

Punjab and Sind Bank Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Breakdown

Punjab & Sind Bank has advertised 8 posts across five designations and two technology domains. All vacancies are contractual. The category-wise distribution is as follows.

S.No. Post Scale OBC UR Total
1 Manager - Cloud Centre of Excellence MMGS II 1 3 4
2 Senior Manager - Cloud Centre of Excellence MMGS III 0 1 1
3 Chief Manager - Cloud Centre of Excellence SMGS IV 0 1 1
4 Manager - Artificial Intelligence (AI) Engineers MMGS II 0 1 1
5 Senior Manager - Artificial Intelligence (AI) Engineers MMGS III 0 1 1
Total 1 7 8

There are no vacancies reserved for SC, ST, EWS, or PwBD categories in this notification. The largest pool - 4 posts - is for Manager (Cloud CoE) at MMGS II level.

Punjab and Sind Bank Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

All eligibility conditions are assessed as on the respective cut-off dates stated above. Candidates must independently verify they meet every criterion before applying; merely submitting an application does not constitute a right to be called for selection.

Age Limit (as on 01 June 2026)

Scale Minimum Age Maximum Age Date of Birth Range
SMGS IV 33 years 45 years 02 June 1981 – 01 June 1993
MMGS III 31 years 43 years 02 June 1983 – 01 June 1995
MMGS II 29 years 41 years 02 June 1985 – 01 June 1997

The maximum ages above apply to General category and EWS candidates. SC/ST candidates receive 5 years of relaxation in the upper age limit; OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates receive 3 years; Persons with Benchmark Disabilities receive 10 years; Ex-Servicemen (with at least 5 years' military service) receive 5 years; and persons affected by the 1984 riots receive 5 years. SC/ST/OBC candidates may combine their category relaxation with only one of the remaining relaxation categories.

Educational Qualification and Work Experience

Post 1 - Manager, Cloud Centre of Excellence (MMGS II) Mandatory qualification: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Science & Engineering, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or Electronics & Communications Engineering (or equivalent) from a university/institute recognized by the Government of India, UGC, or AICTE. Preferred certifications include AWS Solutions Architect (Associate/Professional), Azure Administrator, DevOps Engineer, or Google Associate/Professional Cloud Engineer.

Work experience required: Minimum 4 years of post-qualification experience in IT Industry, BFSI, or the IT vertical of an organization, of which at least 2 years must be specifically in cloud administration or cloud engineering roles.

Post 2 - Manager, AI Engineers (MMGS II) Mandatory qualification: Full-time B.E./B.Tech in Computer Science, Information Technology, Electronics, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, or Machine Learning - OR - full-time MCA/M.Tech in AI, Machine Learning, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field - from a university/institution recognized by the Government of India, AICTE, or UGC. Candidates must also hold at least one professional certification (valid as on the date of eligibility) in AI, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Data Science, or Generative AI from a recognized university, reputed technology organization, or professional certification body.

Work experience: Minimum 6 years (post B.E./B.Tech/MCA) in AI/ML development, Data Science, or Advanced Analytics, including at least 2 years leading or managing AI/ML projects or teams - OR - Minimum 5 years (post M.Tech) with the same specialization, including the same 2-year leadership requirement.

Post 3 - Senior Manager, Cloud Centre of Excellence (MMGS III) Same mandatory degree requirement as Post Preferred certifications: AWS Solutions Architect (Professional), Azure Administrator, DevOps Engineer, or Google Associate/Professional Cloud Engineer.

Work experience: Minimum 6 years post-qualification experience in IT/BFSI, of which at least 3 years in cloud architecture or cloud engineering roles.

Post 4 - Senior Manager, AI Engineers (MMGS III) Same mandatory degree and certification requirements as Post 2 (Manager AI Engineers).

Work experience: Minimum 4 years (post B.E./B.Tech/MCA) in AI/ML development, Data Science, or Advanced Analytics - OR - Minimum 3 years (post M.Tech) in the same domains.

Post 5 - Chief Manager, Cloud Centre of Excellence (SMGS IV) Same mandatory degree as Posts 1 and 3, plus a mandatory intermediate certification from Microsoft Azure. Preferred additional qualifications: MCA or M.Tech/M.Sc in Computer Science, IT, or Electronics & Communications Engineering. Preferred certifications: AWS Solutions Architect (Professional), Azure Solutions Architect Expert, or Google Professional Cloud Architect.

Work experience: Minimum 8 years post-qualification experience in IT/BFSI, of which at least 4 years in cloud architecture, cloud engineering, or DevOps roles.

Candidates should note that 59.99% will be treated as less than 60%, and 54.99% as less than 55%, when calculating percentage of marks. Where a university awards CGPA/OGPA, candidates must convert it to percentage and indicate that figure in the online application.

Punjab and Sind Bank Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

The application fee is non-refundable. Payment must be made online only - cash, cheques, money orders, and postal stamps are not accepted. Bank transaction charges for the online payment gateway are borne by the candidate.

Category Application Fee
SC / ST / PwBD ₹100 + applicable taxes + payment gateway charges
General, EWS & OBC ₹850 + applicable taxes + payment gateway charges

Once submitted, an application cannot be withdrawn, and the fee will not be refunded under any circumstances, nor held in reserve for any other examination.

Punjab and Sind Bank Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The bank will use a multi-stage selection process for all posts. All three scales - SMGS IV, MMGS III, and MMGS II - follow the same procedure: Written Test → Short-listing → Personal Interview.

The written test, where conducted, will have the following structure:

Section Questions Maximum Marks Duration
English Language 20 20 15 minutes
General Awareness 20 20 30 minutes
Professional Knowledge 60 60 60 minutes
Total 100 100 105 minutes

The minimum qualifying score in each section is 40% for General and EWS candidates and 35% for Reserved Category candidates. However, only Professional Knowledge marks are used for drawing the merit/rank list and shortlisting candidates for the Personal Interview; marks in English Language and General Awareness are not included in the final merit calculation.

Candidates clearing the written test are called for a Personal Interview. The minimum qualifying score for the interview is 40% for Unreserved and EWS candidates and 35% for Reserved categories. Final selection is based on the combined marks from the written test and Personal Interview, in order of merit ranking. Where two candidates tie at the cut-off, they are ranked in descending order of age.

The Personal Interview will be conducted in New Delhi unless otherwise specified. Call letters will be sent by email only. Candidates must attend at their own cost.

The bank shortlists candidates based on experience and suitability at its own discretion - meeting the minimum qualification does not guarantee an interview call.

Examination Centres

The bank has designated examination centres across India. Candidates may opt for a centre in their state, subject to availability. The bank reserves the right to allot a different centre or cancel/add centres. No request for a change of examination centre, date, or session will be entertained. Selected centres include: Vishakhapatnam (Andhra Pradesh), Guwahati (Assam), Patna and Bhagalpur (Bihar), Chandigarh/Mohali, Raipur (Chhattisgarh), Delhi/Delhi-NCR, Panaji (Goa), Ahmedabad/Gandhinagar (Gujarat), Ambala (Haryana), Hamirpur (Himachal Pradesh), Jammu (J&K), Bengaluru (Karnataka), Ernakulam (Kerala), Bhopal (Madhya Pradesh), Mumbai/Thane/Navi Mumbai/MMR (Maharashtra), Bhubaneswar (Odisha), Patiala (Punjab), Jaipur (Rajasthan), Chennai (Tamil Nadu), Hyderabad (Telangana), Prayagraj, Lucknow and Meerut (Uttar Pradesh), Dehradun (Uttarakhand), and Asansol and Siliguri (West Bengal), among others.

Terms of Contract

This engagement is on a contractual basis, not a regular/permanent appointment. The initial period is three years, subject to annual performance review. Depending on need and performance, the bank may extend the contract on an annual basis for up to a maximum of two additional years at its discretion.

The bank may terminate the contract at any time without assigning any reason by giving one month's notice or paying one month's fixed component of compensation in lieu. Similarly, the appointee must give the bank one month's clear notice - or equivalent payment - to exit the contract before its expiry.

Additional conditions: the selected individual must not take up any assignment with any other organization during the contract period; no vigilance case or adverse observation from previous employers should be pending; and the candidate must have a minimum CIBIL score of 650 at the time of joining.

How to Apply

Applications are accepted online only through the bank's . The steps below reflect the application flow described in the notification.

  1. Visit https://punjabandsind.bank.in/ and navigate to the Recruitment section on the home page.
  2. Click on the relevant notification for Cloud CoE / AI Engineers.
  3. Before starting registration, scan and prepare the following files:
    • Passport-size photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 20–50 KB; JPG/JPEG/PNG)
    • Signature on white paper in black ink (10–20 KB)
    • Left thumb impression on white paper (20–50 KB; JPG/JPEG)
    • Hand-written declaration in English, on white paper in black ink - text: "I, [Name], hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required." (50–100 KB; JPG/JPEG; NOT in capital letters)
    • Supporting documents in PDF format: Brief Resume, ID Proof, Age Proof, Educational Certificates, Experience Certificates/NOC, Caste Certificate (if applicable)
  4. Complete the online registration form. Keep the registered email ID and mobile number active throughout the process.
  5. Upload all documents at the specified fields. The system will not proceed until photographs and signatures meet specifications.
  6. Pay the application fee online. Transaction charges are borne by the candidate.
  7. Submit the application and save/print the system-generated application form with the registration number.
  8. Apply separately for each post if applying for more than one position.

In case of multiple submissions for the same post, only the last valid completed application will be retained; fees paid for earlier submissions will be forfeited.

Documents Required at Document Verification

Candidates called for the interview stage must bring originals and self-attested photocopies of all relevant documents. Documents not produced on the day of verification will disqualify the candidate from further participation.

Required documents include: Date of Birth proof (SSC/School Leaving Certificate); printout of call letter; system-generated online application printout; all educational certificates and mark sheets from Class X onwards (semester-wise mark sheets and provisional/final degree certificate); all work experience certificates and NOC from the current employer if employed with a public sector bank or government/quasi-government organization; caste/category certificates in the prescribed Government of India format (OBC certificate must be no more than one year old and contain the non-creamy layer clause); disability certificate from District Medical Board (if applicable); Income and Asset Certificate (for EWS candidates, valid as on the date of application); two passport-size photographs identical to the one in the application; and valid photo identity proof (PAN Card, Passport, Driving Licence, Voter's Card, Aadhaar, Employee ID, etc. - Ration Card and Learner's Driving Licence are not accepted).

No documents should be sent directly to the bank before or after the interview.

Troubleshooting & Common Mistakes

Photo/Signature upload errors are among the most common reasons for application failure. Ensure your photograph is between 20 KB and 50 KB and your signature between 10 KB and 20 KB. Files larger than the specified limit will be rejected by the system. Use a scanner set to at least 200 DPI and save files in JPG/JPEG format. All supporting documents must be in PDF format.

Payment gateway failures can occur during high-traffic periods near the deadline. If a payment is deducted but the fee status is not updated, wait 24 hours and check again before attempting re-payment; duplicate payments are generally not refunded. Avoid applying on the last day to reduce the risk of server overload.

Hand-written declaration errors are a unique pitfall in this recruitment. The declaration must be in English, in the candidate's own handwriting, in lowercase (not capital letters). A typed or third-party-written declaration will invalidate the application.

Name mismatches between the application and identity documents can cause disqualification at any stage. Ensure the name (and father's/husband's name where applicable) is spelled exactly as it appears on your certificates and mark sheets. Candidates who have changed their name must produce an original Gazette notification, marriage certificate, or affidavit.

CIBIL score is a condition of joining, not selection. Candidates are advised to check their CIBIL score before applying - a score below 650 will disqualify them at the time of joining even if selected.

title: EIL Associate Engineer Online Form 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: Engineers India Limited (EIL), one of India's premier Navratna Public Sector Undertakings and the country's leading Total Solutions Engineering Consultancy Company, has released an
jobDescription:
Engineers India Limited (EIL), one of India's premier Navratna Public Sector Undertakings and the country's leading Total Solutions Engineering Consultancy Company, has released an official recruitment notification inviting experienced engineers to join on a Fixed Term Basis. The advertisement reference is HRD/Rectt./Advt./2026-27/05, and the recruitment is conducted through a Walk-in Interview process.

If you are an experienced engineer in disciplines such as Mechanical, Chemical, Civil, Electrical, Instrumentation, Environment, or Materials & Corrosion, this is a significant career opportunity with a reputed PSU offering consolidated monthly pay ranging from ₹72,000 to ₹96,000 depending on grade and city category.

[] at http://www.engineersindia.com | [PDF Download Link] – Download the for complete details.

Overview of EIL Walk-in Interview 2026

Advertisement

This section gives you a quick snapshot of the key details of the Engineers India Limited Fixed Term Engineer Recruitment 2026 so you can immediately assess whether this opportunity is right for you.

Detail Information
Organization Engineers India Limited (EIL)
Advertisement No. HRD/Rectt./Advt./2026-27/05
Post Names Associate Engineer Grade II & Grade III
Total Vacancies 41 (approx.)
Employment Type Fixed Term Basis
Experience Required 5 Years (Grade II) / 9 Years (Grade III)
Upper Age Limit 37 Years (Grade II) / 41 Years (Grade III)
Monthly Salary ₹72,000 – ₹96,000 (incl. HRA & PF)
Place of Posting National Capital Region (NCR)
Application Window 16 June 2026 to 30 June 2026
Interview Date (Tentative) From 13 July 2026
Interview Cities Gurugram, Kolkata, Mumbai, Chennai, Bengaluru, Vadodara
www.engineersindia.com
Cut-off Date 31 May 2026

About Engineers India Limited (EIL)

Engineers India Limited is a Government of India undertaking and a Navratna PSU that has built a formidable reputation as India's foremost engineering consultancy company. EIL provides complete engineering and project management services across sectors including oil & gas, petrochemicals, refineries, fertilizers, infrastructure, and more. Working with EIL offers engineers the opportunity to contribute to large-scale national and international energy projects.

Position-Wise Vacancy Details for EIL Recruitment 2026

This section breaks down every available position in the EIL Walk-in Interview 2026, including the exact discipline, number of seats, educational qualification needed, and the nature of experience required. Understanding these details thoroughly before applying is essential to avoid disqualification.

Associate Engineer Grade II Positions (5 Years Experience | Age Limit: 37 Years)

Candidates applying for Associate Engineer Grade II positions must have a minimum of 5 years of post-qualification relevant experience and must be 37 years of age or below as on 31 May 2026.

Position A – Associate Engineer Grade II (Process)

  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engg.) – Chemical Engineering
  • Vacancies: 5 (UR-1, OBC-3, EWS-1)
  • Experience: Preferred experience as a Process Engineer in Refinery, Petrochemical, Gas Processing, Fertilizer, Chemical, Offshore Plants, Pipelines, or LNG Projects/Facilities

Position B – Associate Engineer Grade II (SMED-II – Mechanical Rotating/Package)

  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engg.) – Mechanical Engineering
  • Vacancies: 5 (UR-2, SC-1, ST-1, OBC-1)
  • Experience: Design & Engineering as a Mechanical Engineer (Rotating/Package) for Refinery, Petrochemical, Gas Processing (Onshore/Offshore), Fertilizer, or Chemical Plants; experience in Engineering for Procurement of complex equipment packages; familiarity with API/ISO/International codes preferred

Position C – Associate Engineer Grade II (SMED-I – Static Equipment)

  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engg.) – Mechanical Engineering
  • Vacancies: 4 (UR-2, SC-1, EWS-1)
  • Experience: Design & Engineering as a Mechanical Engineer (Static Equipment) for Refinery, Petrochemical, Gas Processing, Fertilizer, Chemical, or LNG Facilities; experience in design of reactors, tall columns, large storage tanks, heat exchangers; familiarity with PVElite, Bently AutoPipe, API, ASME, TEMA codes preferred

Position D – Associate Engineer Grade II (Environment, Water & Safety)

  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engg.) – Chemical / Civil / Environment / Industrial Pollution Abatement
  • Vacancies: 1 (OBC-1)
  • Experience: Design & Engineering of Water Treatment Plants, Wastewater Treatment Plants, Wastewater Recycle Plants, Zero Liquid Discharge Plants, or Waste to Energy Plants

Associate Engineer Grade III Positions (9 Years Experience | Age Limit: 41 Years)

Associate Engineer Grade III is a senior-level role requiring a minimum of 9 years of post-qualification relevant experience. The upper age limit is 41 years as on 31 May 2026.

Position E – Associate Engineer Grade III (Process)

  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engg.) – Chemical Engineering
  • Vacancies: 13 (UR-6, SC-2, ST-1, OBC-3, EWS-1)
  • Experience: Preferred experience as a Process/Safety Engineer for Refinery, Petrochemical, Gas Processing, Fertilizer, Chemical, Offshore Plants, Pipelines, or LNG Projects/Facilities

Position F – Associate Engineer Grade III (Electrical)

  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engg.) – Electrical Engineering
  • Vacancies: 1 (SC-1)
  • Experience: Design & Engineering as an Electrical Engineer for Refinery, Petrochemical, Gas Processing, Fertilizer, Chemical Plants, Offshore Plants, Pipelines, or LNG Projects; proficiency in Smart Plant Electrical and ETAP preferred

Position G – Associate Engineer Grade III (Instrumentation)

  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engg.) – Instrumentation & Electronics / Instrumentation & Control
  • Vacancies: 4 (UR-1, SC-1, OBC-1, EWS-1)
  • Experience: Design & Engineering as an Instrumentation & Control System Engineer for Refinery, Petrochemical, Gas Processing, Fertilizer, Chemical Plants, Offshore Plants, Pipelines, or LNG Projects; experience in Smart platforms preferred

Position H – Associate Engineer Grade III (Piping)

  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engg.) – Mechanical Engineering
  • Vacancies: 5 (UR-1, SC-1, ST-1, OBC-1, EWS-1)
  • Experience: Design & Engineering as a Piping Engineer for Refinery, Petrochemical, Gas Processing, Fertilizer, Chemical Plants, or LNG Facilities; experience in stress analysis using CAESAR preferred

Position I – Associate Engineer Grade III (SMMS – Materials/Corrosion)

  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engg.) / Integrated Dual Degree – Metallurgy / Materials / Corrosion
  • Vacancies: 1 (UR-1)
  • Experience: Material Selection, Material Failure, or Corrosion Risk Assessment Studies for Oil & Gas Production, Refinery, Petrochemical, Gas Processing, Fertilizer, or Chemical Industries

Position J – Associate Engineer Grade III (Structural/General Civil)

  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engg.) – Civil Engineering
  • Vacancies: 2 (OBC-2)
  • Experience: Civil Engineer for Refinery, Petrochemical, Gas Processing, Fertilizer, Chemical, or LNG Projects; experience in Analysis & Design of RCC, Structural Steel & Composite Structures, Buildings (pre-fabricated, pre-cast, pre-engineered), and Foundations (including for dynamic machines)

Eligibility Criteria for EIL Fixed Term Engineer Recruitment 2026

Understanding the eligibility conditions for EIL Recruitment 2026 is crucial before you proceed to apply, as failing to meet even one criterion can lead to disqualification at any stage of the process.

Educational Qualification

All candidates must hold a B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Sc (Engineering) degree in the relevant discipline from a recognized university or institution, with a minimum of 60% marks or equivalent CGPA. The exact CGPA equivalents are as follows:

% Marks 10-pt Scale 9-pt Scale 8-pt Scale 7-pt Scale 6-pt Scale 5-pt Scale 4-pt Scale
60% 6.5 5.4 4.8 4.2 3.6 3.0 2.2

Note: Round off percentage to the lower whole number (e.g., 60.9 = 60) and CGPA to the lower decimal (e.g., 6.95 = 6.9).

Experience Requirements

  • Associate Engineer Grade II: Minimum 5 years of post-qualification relevant experience
  • Associate Engineer Grade III: Minimum 9 years of post-qualification relevant experience
  • The cut-off date for experience calculation is 31 May 2026

Age Limit

  • Associate Engineer Grade II: Maximum 37 years as on 31 May 2026
  • Associate Engineer Grade III: Maximum 41 years as on 31 May 2026

Age Relaxation and Concessions

EIL provides age relaxation for several reserved and special categories in line with Government of India directives. This ensures that eligible candidates from diverse backgrounds have a fair opportunity to .

Category Age Relaxation
SC / ST 5 years
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years
Persons with Disability (PwD) – General / EWS 10 years
PwD – OBC (NCL) 13 years
PwD – SC / ST 15 years
Ex-Servicemen As per GoI rules

PwD Note: Only persons with 40% or more disability are eligible to apply under the PwD category.

Salary and Remuneration for EIL Fixed Term Engineers 2026

One of the most attractive aspects of this EIL recruitment is the competitive consolidated monthly remuneration, which varies by city category and grade. The pay package includes HRA and PF components.

Designation X Category Cities Y Category Cities Z Category Cities
Associate Engineer Grade II ₹80,000/month ₹76,000/month ₹72,000/month
Associate Engineer Grade III ₹96,000/month ₹91,200/month ₹86,400/month

Additional Benefits

Beyond the monthly consolidated pay, EIL also provides a set of value-added benefits to all fixed term engineers:

  • Medical Insurance: Reimbursement of Medical Insurance Premium for self, up to ₹2,00,000 per annum
  • Out-of-Pocket Expenses: Reimbursement for working beyond standard working hours
  • GPAI Scheme: Coverage under the Group Personal Accident Insurance Scheme as per Company Policy
  • Travelling Allowance: Payment for official travel outside the place of posting
  • Paid Leave: 2 days of paid leave for each completed month of service

Terms and Conditions of EIL Fixed Term Employment

Before applying, candidates must be aware of the contractual nature of this engagement. EIL's fixed term employment comes with specific conditions that differ from permanent PSU roles.

  • The engagement is purely on a fixed term basis, initially for 2 years, extendable up to a maximum of 3 years from the date of joining, subject to satisfactory performance
  • The engagement will automatically terminate on the expiry of the term unless extended
  • Candidates may be required to work anywhere in India and abroad
  • Only medically fit candidates will be permitted to join
  • No claim for regular/permanent appointment in EIL shall be entertained on the basis of fixed term service
  • Selected candidates must join within 15 days of the announcement of results

Place of Posting

The primary place of posting for all selected candidates will be in the National Capital Region (NCR). However, EIL may deploy engineers anywhere in India or abroad based on project and organizational requirements. Candidates must be prepared for transfers and outstation postings as a condition of this role.

Walk-in Interview Schedule, Venue, and Cities

The walk-in interview for EIL Recruitment 2026 is scheduled across major cities in India, providing candidates from various regions a convenient opportunity to appear. This section covers the tentative timeline and the cities where interviews will be held.

  • Tentative Interview Start Date: 13 July 2026
  • Exact Date: Will be communicated separately via the candidate's registered email ID
  • Interview Cities: Gurugram, Kolkata, Mumbai, Chennai, Bengaluru, and Vadodara
  • Interview Timing: Interviews may be held up to 8:00 PM on the same day and, if required, continued the next day. Candidates must make their own arrangements for accommodation.

Candidates based in Chennai will have the option to attend their interview locally, making this a convenient opportunity for Tamil Nadu-based engineers.

Documents Required for the Walk-in Interview

Candidates must carry all required documents in original along with one set of self-attested photocopies. Missing any document may result in disqualification.

Mandatory Documents

  1. 6 copies of the duly filled and submitted online application form with a recent colour photograph affixed
  2. Photo Identity Proof (any one): Driving License / PAN Card / Passport / Voter ID / Aadhar Card
  3. Date of Birth Proof: Matriculation / Class X Certificate / Birth Certificate
  4. Educational Certificates: Mark sheets and passing certificates for Class X, HSC, Degree, and Post Graduation (all semesters/years)
  5. CGPA Conversion Proof from the University/Institute (if applicable)
  6. Experience Certificates (originals) for all experiences mentioned in the application, clearly showing dates of joining and separation, and nature of work
  7. Additional employment proof on demand: Offer letter, confirmation order, pay slips, PF statement, ESI Card, Bank Statement

Category-Specific Documents (if applicable)

  • SC/ST Certificate (in prescribed format from competent authority)
  • Valid OBC (NCL) Certificate (in GoI prescribed format)
  • Valid EWS Certificate
  • Disability Certificate
  • Discharge Certificate and relevant proforma for Ex-Servicemen
  • No Objection Certificate (NOC) if currently employed in a Central/State Government or Autonomous Body

Important: If any original document is unavailable on the day of the interview, the candidate will not be considered for further selection.

How to for EIL Walk-in Interview 2026

The application process for EIL Recruitment 2026 is entirely online through the official EIL website. Here is the complete step-by-step guide to apply without errors.

Step 1: Visit the official EIL website career section at http://www.engineersindia.com

Step 2: Read all the instructions on the website carefully before beginning the registration

Step 3: Fill in the online form with all accurate details. Round off your percentage to the lower whole number (e.g., 60.9 → 60) and CGPA to the lower decimal (e.g., 6.95 → 6.9)

Step 4: Keep the following scanned documents ready before you begin:

  • Passport-size colour photograph in .jpg/.jpeg format (max 75 KB)
  • Signature in .jpg/.jpeg format (max 25 KB)

Step 5: After submitting the form online, take 6 printouts of the application form, affix a colour photograph on each, and bring them to the walk-in interview

Step 6: Confirm that all details are correct - the cut-off date for experience and age is 31 May 2026

[] – Visit https://recruitment.eil.co.in/ between 00:00 hrs on 16 June 2026 and 23:59 hrs on 30 June 2026

[PDF Download Link] – Download the official EIL Recruitment Notification HRD/Rectt./Advt./2026-27/05 from the EIL careers page for complete details.

Important Dates for EIL Recruitment 2026

Staying on top of key deadlines is essential to ensure you don't miss out on this opportunity.

Event Date
Online Application Opens 16 June 2026 (00:00 hrs)
Online Application Closes 30 June 2026 (23:59 hrs)
Eligibility Cut-off Date 31 May 2026
Walk-in Interview (Tentative) From 13 July 2026

Important Instructions for Applicants

Before you finalize your application, go through these critical instructions issued by EIL to avoid rejection:

  • Only candidates who meet all eligibility criteria should apply
  • Candidates wishing to apply for multiple positions must submit separate online applications for each post
  • Your registered email address must remain valid for at least 24 months from the date of application submission, as all correspondence (including interview intimation) will be sent via email
  • Any updates, corrigenda, or clarifications will be published only on www.engineersindia.com - no separate press notifications will be issued
  • Providing wrong or false information will lead to immediate disqualification
  • No travel or stay allowances will be paid by EIL for attending the interview
  • Any legal disputes will be subject to the jurisdiction of courts in Delhi
  • EIL reserves the right to cancel the advertisement or the selection process without assigning any reason

title: RBI Young Professional Online Form 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: If you are a postgraduate with expertise in fields like Artificial Intelligence, Cyber Security, Climate Finance, Quantum Technology, or Economic Research, the Reserve Bank of Indi
jobDescription:
If you are a postgraduate with expertise in fields like Artificial Intelligence, Cyber Security, Climate Finance, Quantum Technology, or Economic Research, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has opened a rare and prestigious opportunity. Under Advertisement No. RBI/TMD1/YP/06/2026-27/01 dated June 15, 2026, RBI is seeking 12 Young Professionals (YP) to work directly within its Central Office Departments (CODs) in Mumbai on short-to-medium-term policy assignments.

This is not an ordinary government job. The RBI Young Professional programme places selected candidates at the center of India's monetary policy, financial regulation, and technology governance framework. With a monthly stipend of ₹1,50,000, a contract tenure of up to 5 years, and direct exposure to top-level policy work, this engagement stands apart from conventional public sector roles.

The last date to apply is July 6, Applications must be submitted via email only.

  • [] – Send your application to [email protected]
  • [PDF Download Link] – Download the : Advt. No. RBI/TMD1/YP/06/2026-27/01 from www.rbi.org.in

RBI Young Professional 2026 Overview

Advertisement

Before diving into the details, here is a complete quick-reference summary of the RBI YP Recruitment 2026 for candidates assessing their fit.

Detail Information
Organization Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
Advertisement No. RBI/TMD1/YP/06/2026-27/01
Date of Notification June 15, 2026
Post Name Young Professional (YP)
Total Vacancies 12
Nature of Engagement Contract Basis (Not a permanent/regular job)
Age Limit 21 to 30 years as on July 6, 2026
Monthly Stipend ₹1,50,000 (fixed, subject to tax deductions)
Tenure 3 years (extendable up to 5 years)
Place of Posting Mumbai (Various Central Office Departments)
Application Mode Email only ([email protected])
Last Date to Apply July 6, 2026
Expected Joining August – October 2026 (Tentative)

What Is the RBI Young Professional Programme?

The RBI Young Professional programme is a unique initiative by the Reserve Bank of India designed to bring domain specialists into the central bank's functioning on a contractual basis. Unlike recruitment for regular officer grades, this programme targets individuals with specialized postgraduate or technical expertise who can contribute meaningfully to short-to-medium-term policy-related research and analytical assignments.

Selected YPs work within RBI's Central Office Departments in Mumbai, gaining direct exposure to India's most consequential financial, monetary, and regulatory policy decisions. The engagement is intended to enrich both the Bank's analytical capacity and the professional development of the selected candidates. It is not a pathway to permanent employment at RBI, but it is widely regarded as an extraordinary professional experience in the Indian financial policy ecosystem.

All 12 Young Professional Positions – Post Codes, Job Areas, and Departments

This section lists every available Young Professional position in RBI's 2026 recruitment drive, along with the respective post code and the Central Office Department where the selected candidate will be placed.

Sl. No. Post Code Job Area Department
1 YP0626DOS01 Cyber Security and Information Technology Risk Dept. of Supervision
2 YP0626DOS02 Policy Analytics (DoS) Dept. of Supervision
3 YP0626DOR01 Climate Change Risk and Sustainable Finance Dept. of Regulation
4 YP0626DOR02 Credit Risk Analytics & Regulatory Policy Dept. of Regulation
5 YP0626DPS01 Payment Ecosystem Dept. of Payment & Settlement Systems
6 YP0626DPS02 Policy and Research in Domestic & Cross-Border Payment Systems Dept. of Payment & Settlement Systems
7 YP0626DEP01 Policy and Research (DEPR) Dept. of Economic and Policy Research
8 YP0626FIN01 Artificial Intelligence FinTech Department
9 YP0626FIN02 Quantum Technology FinTech Department
10 YP0626FMO01 Data Analysis Financial Markets Operations Dept.
11 YP0626FMR01 Financial Markets Financial Markets Regulation Dept.
12 YP0626CEP01 Data Analytics and Policy Research Consumer Education & Protection Dept.

Total Positions: 12

Also Read: RRB Exam Calendar 2026 Out - ALP, NTPC, JE, Technician, Group D Exam Dates

Post-wise Eligibility and Job Profiles: Complete Breakdown

Every RBI YP post has its own educational qualification requirements, desirable experience profile, and a distinct job profile. Understanding these differences is critical before applying. Applying for the wrong post or ignoring the post code in your email subject line could result in rejection.

YP0626DOS01 – Cyber Security and Information Technology Risk

Department: Department of Supervision, Central Office, Mumbai

This role is designed for specialists who can help RBI assess technology risks, digital resilience, and cybersecurity threats across the regulated financial sector.

  • Essential Qualification: Postgraduate degree or equivalent in Cyber Security / Information Security / Computer Science / IT / Data Science / AI or a related field.
  • Desirable Qualifications & Certifications: CISSP, CISM, CEH, ISO 27001 Lead Auditor, AWS/Azure Security; published research in IT, Cybersecurity, or Public Policy journals.

Desirable Experience:

  • Cyber security, technology risk management, digital regulation, or fintech
  • Cloud security, cyber resilience, digital transformation projects
  • Experience with financial institutions, regulators, CERTs, or tech companies

What You Will Do: Conduct policy research on cyber security and digital resilience; draft framework documents; analyze cyber incidents and threat intelligence trends; support AI/ML, cloud security, and data governance projects.

YP0626DOS02 – Policy Analytics (DoS)

Department: Department of Supervision, Central Office, Mumbai

This data-heavy role requires candidates who can transform complex supervisory data into actionable analytical outputs using advanced tools.

  • Essential Qualification: B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech in Computer Science/IT, or Master's degree in Statistics/Data Science.

Desirable Technical Skills:

  • Excel (Power Query, Power Pivot, VBA macros, automation)
  • Power BI (Advanced DAX, interactive dashboards, drill-through functionality)
  • Python (Pandas, NumPy, ETL workflows, automated reporting)
  • Statistics (regression analysis, forecasting, hypothesis testing, predictive modelling)
  • MIS Automation and large multi-source data management

What You Will Do: Extract and analyze supervisory datasets; develop Power BI dashboards; automate recurring MIS reports using VBA and Python; prepare analytical notes and policy briefs for senior management; support RTI/legal matters through structured data review.

YP0626DOR01 – Climate Change Risk and Sustainable Finance

Department: Department of Regulation, Central Office, Mumbai

With climate-related financial risks gaining regulatory prominence worldwide, this role positions the selected candidate at the frontier of ESG and sustainable finance regulation in India.

  • Essential Qualification: Master's degree or PG qualification in Climate Change Studies / Environmental Science / Environmental Management / Climate Finance / Sustainability / Economics / Financial Economics / Finance / Statistics / Econometrics / Data Science / Public Policy / Disaster Management / Natural Resource Management / related quantitative disciplines.
  • Desirable Certifications: FRM (GARP), SCR (GARP), ESG-related certifications.
  • Desirable Skills: Knowledge of physical and transition climate risks; understanding of ESG frameworks; Python, R, SQL, Excel, or data visualization tools.
  • What You Will Do: Climate risk research and analysis; policy support and regulatory framework development; climate stress testing; ESG and sustainable finance work; preparation of reports and policy notes; stakeholder engagement and capacity building.

YP0626DOR02 – Credit Risk Analytics & Regulatory Policy

Department: Department of Regulation, Central Office, Mumbai

This post is aimed at quantitatively strong candidates who can contribute to credit risk regulation, macroprudential analysis, and evidence-based policy development.

  • Essential Qualification: Master's degree or equivalent in Economics / Econometrics / Statistics / Finance / Mathematics / Data Science / Financial Engineering or related quantitative disciplines.
  • Desirable Qualification: PhD in Finance, Economics, or Statistics; or published research in credit risk or financial stability.
  • Desirable Experience: Risk modelling in financial institutions or credit rating agencies; experience with Python and large datasets; familiarity with ECL/IFRS-9, credit bureau data, BCBS and FSB regulatory standards.
  • What You Will Do: Frame and review credit risk regulations; identify regulatory gaps; support evidence-based policy; analyze international regulatory developments; develop analytical frameworks for assessing the impact of regulatory policies.

YP0626DPS01 – Payment Ecosystem

Department: Department of Payment and Settlement Systems, Central Office, Mumbai

This role calls for candidates who can track and analyze developments in India's rapidly evolving payment ecosystem, including cross-border payment architectures.

Essential Qualification (any one):

  • PG degree in Economics / Statistics / Finance / Commerce / Public Policy / Data Science
  • B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech
  • Chartered Accountant (CA)

Desirable Experience: Payment systems, cross-border payments, public policy, or financial markets.

What You Will Do: Monitor market intelligence from international bodies (CPMI, FATF, BIS); undertake primary and secondary research on global payment architectures; generate analytical reports for international forums; statistical modelling of payment flow data.

YP0626DPS02 – Policy and Research in Domestic & Cross-Border Payment Systems

Department: Department of Payment and Settlement Systems, Central Office, Mumbai

This position supports RBI's top management with policy notes, research briefs, and market intelligence covering both domestic and international payment developments.

Essential Qualification (any one):

  • PG degree in Economics / Statistics / Finance / Commerce / Public Policy / Data Science
  • B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech
  • Chartered Accountant (CA)

Desirable Experience: Public policy, payment systems.

What You Will Do: Prepare notes and briefs for RBI's top management on payment system policy; track domestic and international payment ecosystem developments; gather market intelligence from CPMI, FATF, BIS; contribute to research and thematic studies on payment systems.

YP0626DEP01 – Policy and Research (DEPR)

Department: Department of Economic and Policy Research, Central Office, Mumbai

This is one of the most academically demanding posts in the RBI YP 2026 recruitment, designed for candidates with strong economics research credentials.

  • Essential Qualification: Master's or PhD in Economics, with at least one course paper in Mathematics, Statistics, or Econometrics during the Master's programme.
  • Desirable Qualification: Authored at least one paper in a reputed journal ranked B or above.
  • Desirable Experience: Economic research; experience with STATA, EViews, Python, or R.
  • What You Will Do: Review research papers and reports on policy and regulatory matters; prepare concise summaries and background notes for RBI's top management; support senior leadership in meetings and presentations.

YP0626FIN01 – Artificial Intelligence

Department: FinTech Department, Central Office, Mumbai

As AI transforms the financial sector, RBI seeks a specialist who can shape its approach to AI governance, ethics, and regulatory oversight.

  • Essential Qualification: Postgraduate/technical/professional degree in AI / Computer Science / Public Policy / Data Science or a related discipline.
  • Desirable Knowledge: AI governance, machine learning concepts, data ethics, emerging technology policy frameworks.
  • Desirable Experience: AI research, technology policy, data analysis; exposure to ethical AI frameworks, regulatory research, or public policy projects.
  • What You Will Do: Analyse emerging AI technologies; develop policy recommendations on ethics, governance, data privacy, and responsible AI adoption; support regulatory research and stakeholder consultations; assist in strategy formulation for AI-driven innovation ecosystems.

YP0626FIN02 – Quantum Technology

Department: FinTech Department, Central Office, Mumbai

This highly specialized and forward-looking role places a candidate at the cutting edge of India's quantum technology policy landscape.

  • Essential Qualification: Postgraduate/technical/professional degree in Quantum Computing / Physics / Electronics / Computer Science / Technology Policy.
  • Desirable Knowledge: Quantum technologies, cybersecurity implications, innovation policy research.
  • Desirable Experience: Quantum technology research, emerging technology analysis, quantum computing applications, or cybersecurity research.
  • What You Will Do: Conduct research on quantum computing, communication, and cybersecurity trends; assist in developing strategic frameworks for quantum ecosystem development; support standards-setting and international collaboration initiatives.

YP0626FMO01 – Data Analysis

Department: Financial Markets Operations Department, Central Office, Mumbai

This role suits a candidate who can translate financial market data into policy-relevant insights, particularly in the money market domain.

  • Essential Qualification: Post Graduation in Statistics / Economics with Econometrics as a subject.
  • Desirable Qualifications: CFA, FRM; proficiency in Microsoft Excel.
  • Desirable Experience: Econometrics; published research; banking dealer experience; money market exposure.
  • What You Will Do: Perform data analysis to support policy research; track domestic and global currency and yield movements; analyze money market rates on a daily basis.

YP0626FMR01 – Financial Markets

Department: Financial Markets Regulation Department, Central Office, Mumbai

A role for quantitatively oriented professionals who can harness analytical tools to study and interpret financial market behavior for regulatory purposes.

  • Essential Qualification: Postgraduate degree in Economics / Operations Research / Finance / Statistics / Mathematics.
  • Desirable Experience: Any area related to financial markets.
  • What You Will Do: Study and analyze financial market data using cutting-edge analytical tools in close coordination with the regulatory function.

YP0626CEP01 – Data Analytics and Policy Research

Department: Consumer Education and Protection Department, Central Office, Mumbai

This role focuses on using data science and machine learning to understand consumer complaint trends and translate them into policy inputs for RBI's leadership.

  • Essential Qualification: B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech, or Postgraduate degree in Mathematics / Statistics / Computer Science / Economics / Data Analytics with knowledge of statistical tools and Power BI.
  • Desirable Skills: Python, R, or SQL for automation, machine learning, and large dataset handling.
  • What You Will Do: Data mining and analytics using AI/ML tools; systematic classification of consumer complaints and trends; process automation of report generation; development of interactive real-time dashboards; preparation of quality policy inputs for RBI's top management.

Who Can Apply? General Eligibility Criteria for RBI YP 2026

Candidates must satisfy the following baseline conditions to be considered for any of the 12 Young Professional positions at RBI. These are non-negotiable and checked at the time of document verification.

  • Nationality: Must be an Indian citizen.
  • Age Limit: Must be above 21 years and not more than 30 years of age as on July 6, 2026. The notification does not mention any age relaxation for reserved categories under this recruitment.
  • Educational Qualification: Must meet the post-specific eligibility criteria as detailed in Annexure-III of the . All qualifications must be from a recognized institution in India or abroad. In cases of similar or alternative qualifications, RBI retains sole authority to determine equivalency.
  • Agreement to Terms: Candidates must agree to the Terms and Conditions of Engagement as specified in the notification.
  • Application Mode: Must apply via email in the prescribed proforma (Annexure-VI of the ).

Each post has its own essential qualification. Meeting the general age criterion alone does not guarantee eligibility - always check the post-specific educational requirement before applying.

Monthly Stipend and Terms of Engagement

Understanding what this role offers - and what it does not - is essential before applying. RBI's YP engagement is a contractual position with a generous stipend but with clearly defined boundaries.

Remuneration

A fixed monthly stipend of ₹1,50,000 will be paid throughout the engagement period, subject to applicable tax deductions. No additional allowances, HRA, or benefits are provided over and above this stipend.

Contract Duration

The initial contract tenure is 3 years, which may be extended by mutual agreement for a total period not exceeding 5 years. The extension decision is based solely on the Bank's assessment of the YP's performance and is at RBI's discretion.

Nature of Engagement

This is a contractual, full-time engagement - not a regular or permanent appointment. The YP will have no claim to any regular or permanent position at RBI by virtue of this engagement. There is no employer-employee relationship between RBI and the YP.

Leave Policy

YPs are entitled to 15 days of leave per calendar year, accrued on a pro-rata basis from the date of joining. Leave cannot be carried forward to the next year. Absence beyond this entitlement is treated as leave without pay, with a proportionate deduction from the monthly stipend. Unauthorized absence for 8 or more consecutive working days without valid reason may lead to contract termination.

Termination and Notice Period

Either party may terminate the contract with 3 months' notice. However, during the first 6 months of the initial tenure, the contract may be terminated by either side without any notice period.

Other Conditions

  • Place of engagement is Mumbai; no residential accommodation will be provided by RBI
  • No part-time work for any private or public body is permitted during the engagement without prior written permission from RBI
  • YPs must produce a medical fitness certificate and a satisfactory police verification report on selection
  • A code of conduct agreement and secrecy undertaking must be signed on joining
  • In the event of death, injury, or illness during tenure, neither the YP nor their dependents are entitled to compensation or employment from RBI

How to Apply for RBI Young Professional 2026 – Step-by-Step

The application process for RBI YP 2026 is entirely email-based. There is no online portal, no registration system, and no walk-in interview. Every step must be followed precisely to avoid rejection.

Step 1 – Download the Application Proforma: Download Annexure-VI from the document available at www.rbi.org.in. Fill it out completely. Fields marked with asterisk (*) are mandatory.

Step 2 – Prepare Your Documents: Compile all the following in PDF format:

  • Duly filled application proforma (Annexure-VI) with recent colour photograph (3.5 x 4.5 cm) pasted
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Degree certificates for essential and desirable qualifications (provisional certificate acceptable if the degree is pending)
  • Statement of Interest (explaining why you are applying for the specific post)
  • A sample of academic or policy writing
  • A reference/recommendation letter from a professional referee (preferably from your current/previous employer or academic institution)

Step 3 – Check the Email Specifications:

  • All documents must be in PDF format only - other formats may be blocked by RBI's security filters
  • Total attachment size must not exceed 5 MB
  • The email body must be concise (one or two lines) and unconditional - no additional statements

Step 4 – Format the Email Subject Line Correctly:
The subject line must follow this exact format: "YP Application – [Post Code] – [Your Full Name]"
Example: YP Application – YP0626FIN01 – Ramesh Kumar

Step 5 – Send the Application:
Email your complete application to [email protected] before the deadline.

Step 6 – Applying for Multiple Posts:
If you wish to apply for more than one position, send a separate email for each post. Each email must include a complete, independent set of all required documents.

  • [] – Send your application email to [email protected]
  • [PDF Download Link] – Download the and Annexure-VI application proforma from www.rbi.org.in

Last Date: July 6, 2026

Selection Process for RBI YP 2026

Submitting a complete and eligible application is only the first step. The selection process for RBI Young Professionals involves multiple stages, and meeting the minimum eligibility is not a guarantee of progression.

Stage 1 – Preliminary Screening: A screening committee reviews all received applications against the eligibility criteria and the quality of supporting documents. The Bank reserves the right to limit the number of candidates called for interview.

Stage 2 – Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates will be required to submit and verify original documents related to age, educational qualifications, and experience.

Stage 3 – Interview: Shortlisted candidates will receive an interview call letter via email only. No physical call letters will be sent by post. Candidates must make their own travel and accommodation arrangements for the interview.

Reserve List: RBI maintains a reserve list of suitable candidates, valid for one year from the date of publication of the selection list.

RBI's decision regarding shortlisting, interview, and all aspects of engagement is final. No correspondence will be entertained in this regard.

Also Read: Engineers India Limited Associate Engineer Recruitment 2026

Important Dates for RBI YP Recruitment 2026

Event Date
Date of Notification June 15, 2026
Application Window Opens June 15, 2026 (onwards)
Last Date to Apply July 6, 2026
Expected Joining August – October 2026 (Tentative)

Key Things to Remember Before Applying

  • Ensure you meet the age limit of 21–30 years as on July 6, 2026 - this is the cutoff date, not the notification date
  • Check the essential educational qualification for your chosen post carefully - each post has a different requirement
  • The application email subject line must exactly follow the format: "YP Application – Post Code – Candidate Name" - incorrect subject lines risk non-delivery
  • Only PDF attachments are accepted; total file size must not exceed 5 MB
  • Freshers are eligible - the experience requirements listed are desirable, not mandatory
  • No printouts or hard copies need to be sent by post or courier
  • RBI accepts no responsibility for non-delivery of emails for any technical reason
  • Work experience from the current employer requires a release certificate confirming they can be relieved on short notice if selected

jobDescription:
RRB ALP CBT 1 Result 2026 Out - Zone-wise PDF Download, Score Card & Cut Off

Advertisement

The wait is finally over for lakhs of Railway job aspirants across the country. The Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) has officially declared the RRB ALP CBT 1 Result 2026 on 12 June 2026 for the Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) posts under advertisement number CEN 01/Candidates who appeared in the Computer-Based Test conducted on 13th, 16th, 17th, 18th February 2026 and the rescheduled exam on 11th March 2026 can now check their qualifying status through the zone-wise result PDFs available on all regional RRB websites. Along with the result, the board has also released the score card and category-wise cut off marks. Shortlisted candidates will next appear for CBT 2, scheduled to be held any time after 12th July 2026.

How to Download RRB ALP CBT 1 Result 2026 PDF

Candidates can check the result in PDF format from the official regional RRB website. Since no login is required to view the result PDF, candidates only need to search for their roll number in the downloaded file. Follow these simple steps:

  1. Visit the of your respective RRB region (e.g., rrbcdg.gov.in, rrbahmedabad.gov.in, etc.).
  2. On the homepage, look for the "Results / CEN Notifications" section.
  3. Click on the link for "ALP CBT 1 Result 2026 - CEN 01/2025".
  4. The result will open in PDF format.
  5. Press Ctrl + F on your keyboard and enter your roll number or name.
  6. If your roll number appears in the list, you are declared qualified for CBT 2.
  7. Download the PDF and take a printout for future reference.

How to Download RRB ALP Score Card 2026

Unlike the result PDF, the score card must be downloaded by logging into the official portal. The score card contains normalized marks, raw score, and overall qualifying status. Candidates must download it before 27 June 2026, as the link will be deactivated after that date.

  • Visit the official RRB login portal at www.rrbapply.gov.in.
  • Log in using your Enrollment Number / Registration Number and Date of Birth.
  • Navigate to the Score Card section and click on the download .
  • Save and print the score card for records.

RRB ALP Selection Process 2025-26

The RRB ALP recruitment follows a multi-stage selection process. Candidates who have cleared CBT 1 will now proceed to the next stages. Understanding the complete pipeline helps candidates plan their preparation better.

The full selection process is as follows:

  1. CBT 1 (Computer-Based Test - Stage 1): Qualifying in nature; screens candidates for CBT Result declared on 12 June 2026.
  2. CBT 2 (Computer-Based Test - Stage 2): Merit-based; more detailed and subject-specific. Scheduled after 12 July 2026.
  3. CBAT (Computer-Based Aptitude Test): Conducted for ALP posts only; tests spatial reasoning and technical aptitude.
  4. Document Verification: Original certificate verification for shortlisted candidates.
  5. Medical Examination: Final fitness test as per Railway medical standards.
