Scientist 'B' Biology (CFSL) 35 years M.Sc. in Botany/Zoology/Microbiology/Biotechnology/Biochemistry/Physical Anthropology/Genetics/Forensic Science (with Botany or Zoology at B.Sc.) or B.E./B.Tech Biotechnology 3 years in analytical methods/research in biology
Scientist 'B' Chemistry (CFSL) 35 years (OBC: 38) M.Sc. in Chemistry/Biochemistry/Forensic Science (with Chemistry at B.Sc.) 3 years in analytical methods/research in chemistry
Scientist 'B' Documents (CFSL) 35 years (SC/ST: 40) M.Sc. in Chemistry/Physics/Forensic Science (with Chemistry or Physics at B.Sc.) 3 years in Questioned Documents Examination
Scientist 'B' Physics (CFSL) 35 years M.Sc. in Physics/Applied Physics/Biophysics/Forensic Science (with Physics at B.Sc.) or B.E./B.Tech relevant engineering 3 years in analytical methods/research in physics
Assistant Director (IT) (ORGI) 35 years MCA/M.Sc. in CS/IT/Software Engineering or B.E./B.Tech in Computer/IT/Electronics/ECE 3 years in EDP/software development/database/networking/data security
Research Officer (Map) (ORGI) 35 years M.Sc. in Geography/Applied Geography/Geoinformatics/Remote Sensing & GIS 3 years in GIS/Remote Sensing in Geography or Cartography
DCIO (Technical) (Intelligence Bureau) 35 years B.E./B.Tech in Electronics/ECE/CS/IT/Software Engg or AMIE/AMIETE or M.Sc. Physics (Electronics/Telecom) or MCA after B.Sc. Physics or M.Sc. IT/CS/Software None stated as essential
Deputy Director (Staff Training/Productivity) (DGFASLI) 40 years B.E./B.Tech in Mechanical/Electrical/Chemical/Marine/Production/Industrial/Instrumentation/Civil/Architecture or AMIE (enrolled up to 31.05.2013 only) 5 years in Engineering Workshop/Industry/Training Institute
Asst. Director Grade-II Economic Investigation (MSME) 30 years Master's Degree in Economics 2 years conducting investigations/surveys/research on small scale industries
Chemist (IBM Nagpur) 35 years M.Sc. in Chemistry 3 years in chemical analysis of ores/minerals in CSIR/DST-recognised laboratory
Assistant Mining Geologist (IBM Nagpur) 30 years M.Sc. in Geology/Applied Geology 2 years in geological appraisals/research/teaching in Geology
Mineral Officer Intelligence (IBM Nagpur) 30 years M.Sc. in Geology/Applied Geology/Economics or B.E. in Mining Engineering 2 years in mining/mineral data compilation or govt. mines/minerals dept.
Assistant Public Prosecutor (CBI) 30 years LLB from a recognised university None stated as essential
Principal, Senior Secondary School (A&N) 50 years Master's Degree + B.Ed. from recognised institution 10 years' teaching in recognised Senior Secondary School or Intermediate College
Lecturer-I posts (UT Ladakh - English, Maths, Physics, Chemistry) 40 years (ST only) Master's Degree in relevant subject (first class or equivalent at Bachelor's or Master's level) None stated as essential
Lecturer-I (Computer Engg) (UT Ladakh) 40 years (ST only) B.E./B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering or IT (first class or equivalent) None stated as essential
Lecturer-I (Civil Engg) (UT Ladakh) 40 years (ST only) B.E./B.Tech in Civil Engineering (first class or equivalent) None stated as essential
Superintendent ITI/Vice Principal (UT Ladakh) 40 years (ST only) B.E./B.Tech in any discipline None stated as essential
Scientific Officer/Programmer (UT Ladakh) 40 years (ST only) MCA/M.Sc. CS/IT or B.E./B.Tech CS/IT/Electronics 2 years in IT project in Govt./PSU/Autonomous/Statutory body or recognised institution
Assistant Registrar (Cooperative Dept., UT Ladakh) 35 years (EWS), 40 years (ST) Bachelor's Degree None stated as essential
Private Secretary (UT Ladakh) 35 years (EWS), 40 years (ST) Bachelor's Degree + Dictation at 100 wpm for 10 minutes (English) + Transcription 40 minutes on computer None stated as essential
Consultant Ophthalmologist (UT Ladakh) 50 years (ST only) MBBS (recognised under NMC Act 2019) + PG Degree or Diploma in Ophthalmology 3 years post PG Degree or 5 years post PG Diploma in the speciality

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Age Relaxation

The standard age relaxations applicable to these posts are as follows. SC candidates receive 5 additional years, OBC candidates receive 3 additional years, and all PwBD candidates receive 10 additional years over and above their category relaxation. For certain posts with a base upper limit of 50 years, the PwBD maximum is capped at 56 years - this applies to the Regional Director (NCONF), Professor Sugar Engineering (NSI), Principal (A&N), and Consultant Ophthalmologist (UT Ladakh). UPSC may relax qualifications and experience at its discretion in writing for candidates otherwise well qualified.

Grade Point to Percentage Conversion (CME Pune and UT Ladakh posts)

Where a grade point system is in use and no conversion formula has been notified by the institution, the following equivalences apply as stated in the notification:

CGPA Equivalent Percentage
6.25 55%
6.75 60%
7.25 65%
7.75 70%
8.25 75%

For UT Ladakh Lecturer and Engineering posts, ST candidates receive a 5% relaxation at Bachelor's and Master's level, making 55% (or equivalent grade) the minimum eligibility.

Special Condition - UT Ladakh Posts (Posts 60-70)

All posts under the Administration of the Union Territory of Ladakh are exclusively open to Domiciles of UT Ladakh. A valid Domicile Certificate issued by the Tehsildar under the Ladakh Civil Services Decentralization and Recruitment Rules, 2025 (published in the Gazette of India on 03.06.2025) is mandatory. Two eligible categories exist: Category A (holders of or eligible for Ladakh Resident Certificate) and Category B (children of LRC holders or those eligible). Reservation is governed by the Jammu and Kashmir Reservation Act, 2004, as extended to UT Ladakh and amended by the Ladakh Reservation (Amendment) Regulation, 2025 (No. 1 of 2025), and further by the Ladakh Reservation (Amendment) Rules, 2025 notified vide S.O. 77 dated 27.06.2025.

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

UPSC is conducting this recruitment by selection. The notification does not describe specific stages such as written examination or interview. The selection method will be as determined by UPSC for each post individually. No exam dates or result timelines are stated in the source.

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Salary / Pay Scale

All pay scales are as per the 7th Central Pay Commission Pay Matrix. The level assigned to each post determines the salary band. Key pay levels across these 70 posts are listed below.

Pay Level Posts Covered
Level 6F Assistant Registrar (Cooperative Dept., UT Ladakh)
Level 7 Junior Scientific Officer (NCONF), Assistant Engineer (Civil) NEC, Sr. Scientific Assistants (DGAQA), Botanist (BSI), Asst. Director Grade-II MSME, Asst. Mining Geologist, Mineral Officer Intelligence, Asst. Public Prosecutor (CBI)
Level 8 Junior Scientific Officer (Naval HQ), Superintendent ITI/Vice Principal (UT Ladakh), Scientific Officer/Programmer (UT Ladakh), Private Secretary (UT Ladakh)
Level 8A Lecturer-I posts (English, Maths, Physics, Chemistry, Computer Engg, Civil Engg) - UT Ladakh
Level 10 Operations Officer (DGCA), Air Safety Officer (DGCA), Jr. Technical Officer (Naval HQ), Scientist 'B' posts (ZSI, CFSL x4), Asst. Director (IT) ORGI, Research Officer (Map) ORGI, DCIO (Technical) IB, Chemist (IBM), CME Pune Asst. Professor posts
Level 11 Regional Director (NCONF), Asst. Legal Adviser (Enforcement), Deputy Director DGFASLI, Consultant Ophthalmologist (UT Ladakh)
Level 12 Regional Director (NCONF), Principal Senior Secondary School (A&N)
Level 13 Professor, Sugar Engineering (NSI Kanpur)
Level 13A1 CME Pune - Associate Professor posts
Level 14 Professor, Civil Engg (Structural) - CME Pune

The probation period is 2 years for Group B posts including Junior Scientific Officer (NCONF), Assistant Engineer (Civil) NEC, Sr. Scientific Assistants (DGAQA), Botanist (BSI), Scientist 'B' (CFSL), Research Officer (Map) ORGI, Asst. Director Grade-II MSME, and all UT Ladakh posts unless otherwise noted. The probation period is 1 year for most Group A posts. For posts under ORGI, the National Pension System (NPS) is applicable with effect from 01.01.2004, and the Unified Pension Scheme (UPS) is applicable with effect from 01.04.2025; candidates eligible for NPS have the option to enrol directly under UPS on joining.

UPSC Recruitment 2026- How to Apply

The following steps reflect the application process described in the notification for UPSC's ORA portal.

  1. Visit the UPSC Online Recruitment Application portal at https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/.
  2. Register as a new user if you do not already have an account, or log in with your existing credentials.
  3. Select Advertisement No. 06/2026 from the list and choose the relevant post.
  4. Fill in all required personal, educational, and experience details accurately.
  5. Upload your photograph, signature, and required supporting documents.
  6. Submit the application and save the system-generated confirmation receipt with your registration number.

Troubleshooting and Common Mistakes

Candidates applying through the UPSC ORA portal can avoid common problems by following these general practices. Photograph and signature files must meet the portal's specified size and dimension requirements; uploading files that are too large or in an unsupported format is among the most frequent causes of incomplete applications. If a payment transaction fails, wait for the system to confirm the failure before attempting to pay again - making duplicate payments can cause complications that require manual resolution. Use an updated, stable browser on a desktop or laptop for the best experience; mobile browsers sometimes fail to render form fields correctly. Save your registration number and password immediately after registering, as the portal requires them for every subsequent login. Do not attempt to submit your application on the final day of the application window, as heavy traffic on the server is common and can cause timeouts or session errors.

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

Resource Link
Click Here
Official UPSC ORA Portal () https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/

title: KSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: The Karnataka State Police (KSP) has officially released the KSP Recruitment 2026 notification, announcing 3,991 vacancies for the post of Civil Police Constable across two separat
jobDescription:
The Karnataka State Police (KSP) has officially released the KSP Recruitment 2026 notification, announcing 3,991 vacancies for the post of Civil Police Constable across two separate cadres. This is one of the biggest police recruitment drives in Karnataka in recent years, and online applications are now open on the KEA portal from 8th June 2026 to 3rd July The written examination is scheduled for 2nd August 2026.

If you are a Karnataka resident who has passed Class 12 (PUC) and has been looking for a stable government job in law enforcement, this is your chance. Read on for a complete breakdown of the KSP Constable Recruitment 2026 - from eligibility and vacancies to how to apply and what to expect in the selection process.

Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Advertisement

Karnataka State Police, Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Bengaluru has officially released two notifications - Advertisement No. 01/Recruitment-4/2026-27 (Kalyana Karnataka / 371(J) cadre) and Advertisement No. 02/Recruitment-4/2026-27 (Mikkili/Okkalida cadre) - both dated 05.06.2026, for direct recruitment of Police Constable (Civil) posts. The notifications include complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, exam pattern, physical standards, age relaxations, reservation, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notifications before applying.

  • Download Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 PDF - Kalyana Karnataka (596 Posts)
  • Download Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 PDF - Mikkili/Okkalida Cadre (3395 Posts)

Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 Overview

Karnataka State Police is recruiting 3991 Police Constable (Civil) posts across two cadres with PUC / equivalent qualification, last date 03 July 2026.

Detail Information
Conducting Body Karnataka State Police (KSP)
Exam Conducting Authority Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA)
Post Name Civil Police Constable (PC)
Advertisement Numbers 01/Recruitment-4/2026-27 & 02/Recruitment-4/2026-27
Total Vacancies 3,991
Application Start Date 8th June 2026
Last Date to Apply 3rd July 2026
Last Date to Pay Fee 4th July 2026
Written Exam Date 2nd August 2026
Mode of Application Online
cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea
Salary Rs. 37,500 - Rs. 76,100 per month

KSP Constable Notification 2026 - Important Dates

Missing a deadline can disqualify your candidacy altogether. Below are all the critical dates in the KSP Constable application 2026 Karnataka, listed clearly so you can plan your application well ahead of time.

Event Date
Notification Release Date 5th June 2026
Online Application Start Date 8th June 2026
Last Date to 3rd July 2026
Last Date to Pay Application Fee 4th July 2026
Written Exam Date 2nd August 2026
PST / Endurance Test Date To be notified later
Important: Apply well before the last date to avoid website congestion or technical difficulties. Do not wait until the deadline.

KSP Constable Vacancy 2026 - Category-Wise Breakdown

A total of 3,991 Civil Police Constable vacancies have been announced under two separate notifications. The vacancies are split between the Kalyana-Karnataka cadre (reserved under Article 371-J) and the General (Mikkilida/Okkalida) cadre. Candidates can apply for only one notification at a time and must choose the cadre applicable to them.

Notification 1 - Kalyana-Karnataka Cadre (Article 371-J)

Advertisement No.: 01/Recruitment-4/2026-27 | Total Vacancies: 596

Category Direct Male Direct Female Ex-Servicemen Male Ex-Servicemen Female 3rd Gender
Police Constable (Civil) 398 133 46 14 5

Notification 2 - General / Mikkilida Cadre

Advertisement No.: 02/Recruitment-4/2026-27 | Total Vacancies: 3,395

Category Direct Male Direct Female Ex-Servicemen Male Ex-Servicemen Female 3rd Gender
Police Constable (Civil) 2,266 755 257 82 35

Total Vacancy Summary

Cadre Vacancies
Kalyana-Karnataka (Article 371-J) 596
General (Mikkilida / Okkalida) 3,395
Grand Total 3,991

KSP Constable Eligibility Criteria 2026

Before submitting your PC application for 2026 Karnataka, carefully verify that you meet all eligibility conditions. Candidates who do not meet the educational, age, or language criteria will be disqualified at any stage of the process.

Educational Qualification

Candidates must have passed Class 12 / PUC or an equivalent examination from a recognised board. This includes:

  • Class 12 passed from CBSE, ICSE, any State Board, or equivalent
  • Class 12 equivalent from NIOS (National Institute of Open Schooling)
  • A 2-year ITC/Diploma (NVBT) course with one language and one academic subject from NIOS
  • A 3-year Diploma from a recognised Polytechnic institution (treated as equivalent to PUC for direct/compassionate recruitment)

Kannada Language Requirement: Candidates must have studied Kannada as a first or second language up to SSLC/10th standard. Those who have not done so must pass a Kannada Language Test conducted by KEA.

Age Limit

The minimum age for all candidates is 18 years. The upper age limit varies by category as follows:

Category Maximum Age
General - Male, Female & 3rd Gender (Direct) 33 years
SC / ST / Cat-1 / Cat-2A, 2B, 3A, 3B (Direct) 35 years
Budakattu (Forest Area) SC Candidates 38 years
Ex-Servicemen (General) 38 years
Ex-Servicemen (SC/ST/OBC) 40 years

Age is calculated as of the notification date. Candidates should refer to the official PDF for precise age cut-off dates.

KSP Constable Application Fee 2026

The application fee for the KSP Constable recruitment must be paid online only, and is non-refundable under all circumstances. Candidates must ensure payment is completed before 4th July 2026.

Category Application Fee
General / OBC (Cat-2A, 2B, 3A, 3B) Rs. 750/-
SC / ST / Cat-1 / Ex-Servicemen / 3rd Gender Rs. 500/-

How to for KSP Constable Recruitment 2026

The KSP Constable 2026 process is conducted through the KEA (Karnataka Examinations Authority) portal. Follow these steps carefully to submit a valid application for Karnataka police constable recruitment 2026.

  • Step 1: Visit the official KEA recruitment portal at https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea/
  • Step 2: Click on the active link for KSP Civil Police Constable Recruitment 2026 (Adv. No. 01 or 02, depending on which cadre you are eligible for).
  • Step 3: Register by entering your personal, educational, and category details accurately.
  • Step 4: Choose the cadre (Kalyana-Karnataka or General) you are applying for. Note: only one application per notification is permitted.
  • Step 5: Upload a recent passport-size photograph (JPEG, 50KB-200KB) and your signature (JPEG, 50KB-70KB).
  • Step 6: Pay the required application fee online through the portal.
  • Step 7: Download and save a copy of the submitted application form and the fee payment receipt.
Caution: No edits or corrections are permitted after final submission. Double-check all entries before clicking submit.
Apply Link: https://ksp-recruitment.in/

KSP Constable Selection Process 2026

The Karnataka police constable selection process 2026 consists of four clearly defined stages. Candidates must qualify each stage to proceed to the next. There is no direct interview; merit is determined through the written exam and physical assessments.

Stage 1 - Written Examination (CBT / OMR-Based)

The written test is the first and most critical stage of the pc recruitment 2026 Karnataka. It determines the candidates who will move on to physical testing.

Detail Information
Mode OMR-based (Offline)
Total Questions 100
Total Marks 100
Duration 90 Minutes (1 hour 30 minutes)
Minimum Qualifying Marks 30 out of 100
Negative Marking Yes - 0.25 marks deducted per wrong answer

Exam Timings:

  • Notification 1 (KK Cadre): 2:30 PM - 4:00 PM on 2nd August 2026
  • Notification 2 (General Cadre): 10:30 AM - 12:00 PM on 2nd August 2026

Subjects Covered:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs
  • General Science
  • Geography
  • Indian History
  • Indian Constitution
  • Karnataka State Affairs
  • Mental Ability & Reasoning
  • Ethics

Stage 2 - Physical Standard Test (PST)

Candidates who qualify the written exam must appear for physical measurements. Below are the height and chest requirements:

Category Height Chest
Male / Ex-Servicemen Min. 168 cm Min. 86 cm (expanded); expansion min. 5 cm
Female / 3rd Gender Min. 157 cm Min. weight: 45 kg
Budakattu Male (Forest Areas) Min. 155 cm Min. 75 cm; 5 cm expansion
Budakattu Female (Forest Areas) Min. 150 cm -

Stage 3 - Physical Efficiency / Endurance Test (PET)

Candidates who pass the PST will then undergo the physical endurance test. All events must be completed within the prescribed standards.

Event Male / Ex-Servicemen Female / 3rd Gender
Run 1,600m in 6 min 30 sec 400m in 2 min
High Jump Min. 1.20 m (3 chances) Min. 0.90 m (3 chances)
Long Jump Min. 3.80 m (3 chances) Min. 2.50 m (3 chances)
Shot Put (Weight) 7.26 kg; min. 5.60 m (3 chances) 4 kg; min. 3.75 m (3 chances)

Stage 4 - Medical Examination

Shortlisted candidates (in a 1:2 ratio per vacancy) will undergo a comprehensive medical examination by a Medical Board. This includes vision testing, hearing assessment, and musculoskeletal evaluation to ensure candidates are physically fit for active police duty.

Stage 5 - Document Verification

Candidates who clear the medical examination must present original academic certificates, domicile proof, category certificates, and other relevant documents for final verification.

KSP Constable Exam Pattern 2026

This section gives a concise view of the pc exam date 2026 Karnataka and the structure of the written test, helping candidates plan their preparation effectively.

Subject Questions Marks Duration
General Knowledge, Science, Geography, History, Indian Constitution, State Affairs, Mental Ability, Ethics 100 100 90 Minutes

Key Points to Remember:

  • Each correct answer carries 1 mark
  • Each wrong answer results in a deduction of 0.25 marks
  • Unanswered questions carry no penalty
  • Minimum qualifying score is 30 marks

KSP Constable Salary 2026

One of the most attractive aspects of the KSP Recruitment 2026 is the government pay structure. Karnataka police constables are placed at Pay Level 7 under the 7th Pay Commission, with a monthly pay scale of Rs. 37,500 - Rs. 76,In addition to the base salary, candidates are eligible for:

  • Dearness Allowance (DA)
  • House Rent Allowance (HRA)
  • Travel Allowance (TA)
  • National Pension Scheme (NPS) benefits
  • Other Karnataka Government allowances

Training and Probation:

  • Selected candidates undergo 9 months of basic police training at designated Police Training Schools in Karnataka.
  • Candidates are on probation for 2 years and 6 months after formal appointment.
  • Candidates who fail during training may be removed from service.

title: SSC GD Constable Answer Key 2026 Recruitment 2026
description: The SSC GD Constable Answer Key 2026 has been officially released. The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) published the provisional answer key for the Constable (GD) Examination 2026
jobDescription:
The SSC GD Constable Answer Key 2026 has been officially released. The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) published the provisional answer key for the Constable (GD) Examination 2026 on 15th June 2026 on its ssc.gov.in. Candidates who appeared in the Computer Based Examination (CBE) can now log in to the SSC portal, download their individual response sheet, compare answers, and raise objections if they find any discrepancy all before the objection window closes on 20th June 2026 at 6:00 PM.

SSC GD Constable Answer Key 2026 - Key Highlights

Advertisement

Before diving into the download steps and objection process, here is a quick overview of all the important details related to the SSC GD Constable Answer Key 2026.

Particulars Details
Exam Conducting Body Staff Selection Commission (SSC)
Exam Name SSC GD Constable Examination 2026
Post Name Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF & Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles
Total Vacancies 25,487
CBT Exam Dates 27th April 2026 to 31st May 2026
Answer Key Release Date 15th June 2026
Answer Key Type Provisional (Tentative)
Objection Window 15th June 2026 (6 PM) to 20th June 2026 (6 PM)
Objection Fee ₹50 per question challenged
Login Credentials Required Registration Number + Password / Date of Birth
ssc.gov.in

SSC GD Constable Answer Key 2026 - Direct Download Link

The SSC GD Constable tentative answer key and response sheets are now available on the official SSC website ssc.gov.in. Candidates must log in with their Registration Number and Password to access them. The direct link below will take you straight to the SSC portal.

  • SSC GD Answer Key 2026 Download Link - Click here
  • Official Notice PDF - Click Here to View

How to Download SSC GD Constable Answer Key 2026?

Downloading the SSC GD provisional answer key is a straightforward process. Follow these step-by-step instructions to access both your response sheet and the shift-wise answer key from the SSC portal.

  1. Visit the official SSC website: ssc.gov.in
  2. On the homepage, click on the "Answer Key" tab under the Latest Updates or Candidate's Corner section.
  3. Find and click the notification titled: "Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF and Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles Examination, 2026 - Uploading of Tentative Answer Keys along with Question Paper(s) cum Response Sheet(s)"
  4. You will be redirected to the SSC candidate login portal.
  5. Enter your Registration Number and Password (or Date of Birth).
  6. Your response sheet and the tentative answer key for your exam shift will appear on screen.
  7. Download both documents and save them as PDF files for future reference and score calculation.

SSC GD Response Sheet 2026 - What to Check

Along with the answer key, SSC has also uploaded the candidate response sheet, which contains all the answers marked by the candidate during the examination. By comparing the response sheet with the provisional answer key, candidates can calculate their estimated scores before the official result is announced.

Here are the key details you will find on your SSC GD 2026 Response Sheet PDF:

Detail Description
Roll Number Unique candidate ID assigned for the exam
Candidate Name Name as registered in the application form
Exam Level SSC GD Constable CBE 2026
Test Date Date on which the candidate appeared
Test Time & Shift Morning / Afternoon shift timing
Centre Name Allotted examination centre
Colour Codes Green = Correct answer selected; Red = Wrong answer selected; Yellow = Correct option; Grey = Unanswered

SSC GD Constable Answer Key 2026 Marking Scheme - How to Calculate Your Score

Candidates can estimate their scores using the official marking scheme prescribed by SSC. For every correct answer, 2 marks are awarded. For every incorrect answer, 0.25 marks are deducted. Unattempted questions carry zero marks.

Response Type Marks
Correct Answer +2 Marks
Incorrect Answer -0.25 Marks
Unattempted Question 0 Marks

Score Calculation Formula:
Total Score = (No. of Correct Answers × 2) − (No. of Wrong Answers × 0.25)

SSC GD CBT Minimum Qualifying Marks 2026

Out of a total of 160 marks, candidates must score the following minimum marks to qualify in the Computer Based Test:

  • Unreserved (UR) / General: 30% = 48 marks
  • OBC / EWS: 25% = 40 marks
  • SC / ST / Ex-Servicemen: 20% = 32 marks

SSC GD Constable Answer Key 2026 - Objection Window

The objection window for the SSC GD Answer Key 2026 is open from 15th June 2026 (6:00 PM) to 20th June 2026 (6:00 PM). During this period, candidates who find any incorrect or disputed answer in the provisional key can formally challenge it by submitting valid supporting references and paying the prescribed fee.

SSC GD Objection Fee 2026

Particulars Amount
Objection Fee Per Question ₹50 (Rupees Fifty Only)
Mode of Payment Online only
Deadline 20th June 2026 at 6:00 PM

How to Raise Objections Against SSC GD Constable Answer Key 2026?

Follow these steps to challenge any answer in the provisional SSC GD 2026 answer key:

  1. Go to the official SSC website: ssc.gov.in
  2. Log in using your Registration Number and Password.
  3. Click on the "Challenge Answer Key" or "Raise Objections" option.
  4. Select the specific question(s) you wish to challenge.
  5. Upload relevant supporting documents such as standard textbook references or authoritative sources to justify your objection.
  6. Pay the objection fee of ₹50 per question through the online payment gateway.
  7. Submit your challenge and download the acknowledgement receipt for your records.

SSC GD Constable Final Answer Key 2026 - What Happens Next?

After reviewing all the objections submitted by candidates, SSC release the SSC GD Final Answer Key It will contain revised answers if any changes are accepted by the Commission. The final answer key will then be used for preparing the SSC GD Result The final answer key cannot be challenged further and will be considered conclusive for result preparation.

Candidates are advised to keep copies of both the provisional and final answer keys for future reference.

SSC GD Constable 2026 - Vacancy & Eligibility Quick Reference

This examination was conducted to fill vacancies across Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs), SSF, and Assam Rifles. Here is a quick summary of the recruitment details:

Particulars Details
Total Vacancies 25,487
Post Name Constable (GD) in CAPFs & SSF; Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles
Educational Qualification 10th Pass (Matriculation)
Age Limit 18 to 23 years (as on 01.01.2026)
Age Relaxation Applicable as per Government of India rules

Other Active SSC Recruitment 2026

Job OpeningsLast DateApply Now
Group B & Group C Jobs - 12,256 Posts22-06-2026View Details
SSA/UDC Jobs - 227 Posts26-06-2026View Details
JSA / LDC Jobs - 149 Posts26-06-2026View Details
Language Experts Jobs 17-06-2026Expiring Soon

jobDescription:

Advertisement

Download UPSC CSE Prelims Result 2026 PDF (Official)

UPSC Prelims Result 2026 - Key Highlights

This section covers the most important facts about the UPSC CSE 2026 Preliminary result at a glance, so qualified candidates and aspirants can quickly get all critical data without searching multiple pages.

  • The UPSC CSE 2026 Prelims exam was conducted on 24 May 2026 at various exam centres across India.
  • Out of 8.19 lakh candidates who applied, 5.49 lakh appeared for the exam.
  • A total of 13,343 candidates have been shortlisted for the Civil Services (Main) Examination 2026 against 1,016 vacancies notified this year.
  • This number is slightly lower than last year, when 14,161 candidates were shortlisted for Mains against 1,087 vacancies.
  • The result is published in online PDF format only - no physical scorecards are sent to candidates.
  • Cut-off scores and answer keys for the Preliminary Examination will be published only after the entire Civil Services Examination 2026 cycle and Indian Forest Service Examination 2026 are completed.
  • Qualified candidates must fill out their cadre preferences for the Civil Services (Main) Examination 2026 from 19 June to 28 June 2026 with a fee of ₹200, unless they are female, PwBD, SC, or ST candidates.

How to Check UPSC Prelims Result 2026 Online - Step-by-Step

Candidates should follow the official process to verify their qualifying status from the UPSC result PDF. Since the UPSC website experiences extremely heavy traffic immediately after result announcements, using the direct PDF link above is the fastest approach.

  1. Visit the Official UPSC Website - Open upsc.gov.in on your browser.
  2. Go to the "What's New" Section - Scroll down on the homepage to find the latest notifications panel.
  3. Click on the Result Link - Look for "Result: Civil Services (Preliminary) Examination, 2026" and click it.
  4. Download the PDF - A new tab will open with a link to the official roll number list. Download the PDF to your device.
  5. Search Your Roll Number or Name - Open the PDF and press Ctrl + F (PC) or use the in-app search (mobile). Type your 7-digit roll number or name to check if you have qualified.

UPSC CSE Prelims Result 2026 - Name-wise vs Roll Number-wise PDF

UPSC typically releases two separate PDFs - one arranged by roll number and one arranged alphabetically by name. Both are accessible via the .

How to Check UPSC Prelims Result 2026 Name-wise

The name-wise PDF lists all qualified candidates in alphabetical order. Candidates can open the PDF and use Ctrl + F to search their full name. In case of a common name, cross-check the corresponding roll number to confirm your entry.

How to Check UPSC Prelims Result 2026 Roll Number-wise

The roll number-wise PDF is the primary official result document released first. Candidates should:

  • Open the downloaded PDF.
  • Press Ctrl + F and enter their unique 7-digit UPSC roll number.
  • If the roll number appears in the list, the candidate has qualified for UPSC Mains 2026.

Details Mentioned in the UPSC CSE Prelims Result 2026 PDF

The official result PDF released by UPSC at upsc.gov.in contains the following information:

  • Name of the Examination (Civil Services Preliminary Examination, 2026)
  • Roll numbers of all candidates who have qualified
  • Important instructions for qualified candidates regarding next steps
  • Information regarding DAF-I (Detailed Application Form) for Mains
  • Official notice from the Commission
  • Schedule details for the Mains Examination

What to Do After UPSC CSE Prelims Result 2026 - Next Steps for Qualified Candidates

Qualifying the Prelims is just the first step. Candidates who find their name or roll number in the result PDF must immediately take the following actions within the prescribed deadlines to remain eligible for the Mains stage.

Step 1 - Fill DAF-I (Detailed Application Form for Mains)

The application window for DAF-I will remain open from 19 June to 28 June 2026 on the official portal upsconline.nic.in. Candidates must log in and fill in or reconfirm personal information, educational details, examination preferences, and cadre preferences.

Step 2 - Pay the Mains Application Fee

Candidates who qualify for the Civil Services (Mains) Examination 2026 are required to pay an application fee of ₹200. Female candidates, SC, ST, and PwBD applicants are fully exempted from this fee payment.

Step 3 - Prepare for UPSC CSE Mains 2026

The UPSC Mains Exam 2026 is scheduled to begin on 21 August 2026 and will continue for five days. Qualified candidates should immediately shift their focus entirely to Mains preparation, covering essay, general studies (GS I-IV), and optional subject papers.

UPSC Civil Services Exam - Selection Process Overview

Understanding the three-stage selection process helps qualified Prelims candidates plan their next steps effectively.

The Civil Services Examination is conducted in three stages:

Stage Exam Type Marks Nature
Prelims (Paper I - GS) Objective MCQ 200 Screening (for merit)
Prelims (Paper II - CSAT) Objective MCQ 200 Qualifying (min. 33%)
Mains (9 Papers) Descriptive 1,750 + 600 (optional) Merit-based
Personality Test (Interview) Oral 275 Merit-based

Important points to remember:

  • The Prelims examination acts purely as a screening stage. Marks from this exam do not contribute to the final merit list.
  • CSAT (Paper II) is qualifying in nature. Candidates need to score at least 33% (66.66 marks out of 200) for their General Studies Paper I to be evaluated
  • The Mains examination consists of nine papers, of which two language papers are qualifying in nature.

UPSC CSE Prelims Cut-Off 2026 - When Will It Be Released?

One of the most searched queries after result declaration is about the official UPSC Prelims 2026 cut-off marks. Here is what candidates need to know.

As mentioned in the result PDF, the marks, cut-off marks, and final answer keys for the CS (P) Examination 2026 will be uploaded on the Commission's website only after the completion of the entire Civil Services Examination 2026 and Indian Forest Service Examination 2026, i.e., after the final results are declared.

Previous Years' UPSC Prelims Cut-Off Trend (General Category - GS Paper I)

Year General Category Cut-Off (out of 200) Candidates Qualified
2020 92.51 (Highest ever) ~15,000
2021 87.54 ~10,000
2022 90.01 ~13,090
2023 88.22 ~14,624
2024 87.98 14,627
2025 TBA 14,161
2026 TBA (After Final Result) 13,343

Note: 2026 cut-off will be released by UPSC only after the entire CSE 2026 cycle concludes.

UPSC CSE Prelims 2026 - Important Dates Summary

Event Date
UPSC CSE Prelims 2026 Exam Date 24 May 2026
UPSC CSE Prelims Result 2026 Declared 15 June 2026
DAF-I Submission Opens 19 June 2026
DAF-I Submission Last Date 28 June 2026
UPSC CSE Mains 2026 Exam Starts 21 August 2026
Cut-off & Answer Key Release After Final Result Declaration

title: Central Bank of India 4,500 Apprentice Recruitment 2026
description: Central Bank of India (CBI) has officially released the Apprentice Recruitment 2026 notification for 4,500 training seats under the Apprentices Act, 1961, for the financial year 20
jobDescription:
Central Bank of India (CBI) has officially released the Apprentice Recruitment 2026 notification for 4,500 training seats under the Apprentices Act, 1961, for the financial year 2026–This is one of the largest banking apprenticeship drives in India, offering graduates a paid one-year training opportunity across CBI's pan-India branch network of over 4,500 branches.

The online application window opened on June 12, 2026, and will close on June 22, 2026. Candidates must register on the NATS government portal and then complete their final application on the BFSI SSC portal. The online examination is tentatively scheduled for the first week of July 2026.

Download Central Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Quick Overview - Central Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Advertisement

Central Bank of India is recruiting 4,500 Apprentice posts with any Graduation qualification, last date 22 June 2026.

Detail Information
Organization Central Bank of India (CBI)
Post Apprentice
Total Vacancies 4,500
Registration Start Date June 12, 2026
Registration End Date June 22, 2026
Application Fee Payment Window June 12–22, 2026
Online Exam Date (Tentative) 1st week of July 2026
Educational Qualification Graduation in any discipline
Age Limit (as on 31/05/2026) 20–28 years
Training Duration 12 months
Monthly Stipend ₹15,000
Selection Process Online Exam + Language Test + Document Verification + Medical Fitness
centralbank.bank.in
Eligibility Cut-off Date May 31, 2026

Important Dates for Central Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Knowing the key dates is critical for planning your application. All events from notification release to the online exam are listed here.

Event Date
Notification Release June 12, 2026
Online Registration Opens June 12, 2026
Online Registration Closes June 22, 2026
Application Fee Payment Deadline June 22, 2026
Online Written Examination (Tentative) 1st week of July 2026

Important: The process is complete only when the application fee is paid online on or before the closing date. Incomplete applications are automatically rejected.

Eligibility Criteria for Central Bank of India Apprentice 2026

Before applying, candidates must confirm they meet all eligibility requirements as on the cut-off date of May 31, 2026.

Nationality

A candidate must be a citizen of India, or a subject of Nepal or Bhutan, or a Tibetan refugee who arrived in India before January 1, 1962 with the intention of permanently settling, or a person of Indian origin who migrated from specified countries with intent to permanently settle in India. Candidates under categories other than Indian citizen must possess an eligibility certificate issued by the Government of India.

Age Limit

  • Minimum Age: 20 years
  • Maximum Age: 28 years (for General/EWS candidates as on 31/05/2026)
  • Candidates must have been born not earlier than May 31, 1998 and not later than May 31, 2006 (both days inclusive).

Age Relaxation

Category Age Relaxation
SC/ST 5 years
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years
PwBD (UR/EWS) 10 years
PwBD (OBC) 13 years
PwBD (SC/ST) 15 years
Widows/Divorced Women (General/EWS) Up to 35 years
Widows/Divorced Women (OBC) Up to 38 years
Widows/Divorced Women (SC/ST) Up to 40 years

Educational Qualification

Candidates must hold a Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a university recognized by the Government of India. Graduation must have been completed on or after January 1, 2022. Mark sheets and provisional/degree certificates will be required at the time of verification.

NATS Registration Requirement

Candidates must be registered on the NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) to be eligible to . Registration on the government apprenticeship portal is a mandatory prerequisite.

Other Key Eligibility Conditions

  • Candidates must not have previously undergone or be currently pursuing any apprenticeship under the Apprentices Act, 1961 in any organization, including Central Bank of India.
  • Candidates who have completed one year or more of training or job experience after attaining their educational qualification are not eligible.
  • No Ex-servicemen or Disabled Ex-Servicemen are eligible, and no reservation is provided for them.

Central Bank of India Apprentice Vacancy 2026 - State-wise Distribution

The 4,500 training seats are distributed across all states and union territories. The major states by number of seats are listed below. Complete district-wise breakdowns are available in Annexure I of the .

State/UT Total Seats
Maharashtra 586
Uttar Pradesh 580
Madhya Pradesh 459
Bihar 433
West Bengal 315
Gujarat 305
Tamil Nadu 202
Rajasthan 170
Punjab 142
Haryana 137
Assam 118
Kerala 116
Chhattisgarh 114
Karnataka 105
Telangana 100
Delhi (NCT) 97
Jharkhand 87
Andhra Pradesh 128
Orissa 103

Category-wise breakdown (All India):

Category Seats
UR (General) 1,903
OBC 1,049
SC 701
EWS 454
ST 393
VI (PwBD) 45
HI (PwBD) 45
OH (PwBD) 45
ID (PwBD) 45
Total 4,500

Application Fee for Central Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The fee is non-refundable and must be paid online. GST at 18% is charged additionally on the base fee.

Category Base Fee Intimation Fee Total (incl. 18% GST)
General / EWS / OBC (Male) ₹600 ₹200 ₹944
General / EWS / OBC (Female) ₹400 ₹200 ₹708
SC / ST (Male / Female) Nil ₹200 ₹236
PwBD (Any Category) Nil ₹200 ₹236
Transgender (Any Category) Nil ₹200 ₹236

Payment is accepted through online mode only. No other payment method is accepted. Fee once paid is not refunded under any circumstances.

How to Apply for Central Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The application process involves two portals. Here is a step-by-step guide:

Step 1 - Register on NATS Portal Visit https://nats.education.gov.in and navigate to the "Student Register/Login" section. Complete your registration and note your Enrolment ID, which is required for all future correspondence.

Step 2 - Find CBI Apprenticeship on NATS After logging in, visit https://nats.education.gov.in/student_type.php to find and apply to the Central Bank of India apprenticeship advertisement.

Step 3 - Complete Application on BEEP Portal After applying on NATS, you will receive an email from BFSI SSC ([email protected]) with a link to register on https://beep.bfsissc.com. Complete your final application here by entering caste category, district preferences, and other details.

Step 4 - Pay the Application Fee Pay the applicable fee online through the BEEP portal. The application is complete only after successful payment.

Step 5 - Save Your Application Take a printout of the system-generated online application form after submitting. Do not make multiple submissions; only the last valid completed application will be retained.

Selection Process for Central Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Selection involves multiple stages. Candidates must clear all stages to be engaged as apprentices.

Stage 1 - Online Written Examination

The exam is objective type, conducted by BFSI SSC. It will be taken from the candidate's own home on their own device - no admit card or examination centre is required.

Subject Questions Marks Duration
General / Financial Awareness 20 20 -
Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude 20 20 -
Computer Knowledge 20 20 -
General English 20 20 -
Central Bank of India Products & Services 20 20 -
Total 100 100 60 minutes
  • Medium: English/Hindi (General English section in English only)
  • No negative marking for wrong answers
  • Tie-breaking rule: if two candidates score equally, the older candidate ranks higher (descending order of age)
  • Details on CBI Products & Services will be shared on the bank's in due course

Stage 2 - Test of Local Language

Candidates must be proficient (reading, writing, speaking, and understanding) in the specified official language of the state they apply for. This test is conducted after qualifying the online exam at a centre decided by the bank, at the candidate's own expense.

Candidates who have studied the specified local language at the 10th or 12th standard level (as evidenced by mark sheets) are exempt from the language test.

Stage 3 - Document Verification

Qualified candidates are called to the relevant CBI Regional/Zonal Office for verification of all original documents, including date of birth proof, graduation certificates, category certificates, and any other relevant documents.

Stage 4 - Medical Fitness Verification

Final engagement is subject to the candidate being declared medically fit by a qualified medical practitioner or MBBS doctor, as per the bank's requirements.

Stipend, Training Conditions, and Benefits

Understanding what to expect during the apprenticeship helps candidates plan ahead.

  • Monthly Stipend: ₹15,000 per month for the 12-month training period
  • No other allowances or employee benefits are provided
  • Leave: One casual leave per month; public/bank holidays as applicable; no other leave types
  • Working hours: Same as clerical staff of the bank; no overtime is permitted
  • No hostel, transport, or residential accommodation is provided by the bank
  • An assessment test may be conducted after completion, followed by certification as per prevailing guidelines
  • Apprentices are trainees, not employees - no labour laws or employee benefits apply
  • Apprentices cannot form or join any union

Contract of Apprenticeship

Candidates selected after document verification will receive a digital apprenticeship contract through the NATS government portal (https://nats.education.gov.in). They must accept the contract within the stipulated timeframe. No candidate can begin training without an approved apprenticeship contract.

Termination Conditions

The contract may be terminated in the following situations:

  • Non-reporting on the date of commencement of On-Job Training
  • Automatic termination on expiry of the 12-month training period
  • Mutual application to the Apprenticeship Advisor (BFSI SSC) before completion, with stated reasons
  • Misconduct by the apprentice, with written notice from the bank
  • If the Apprenticeship Advisor finds either party has failed to fulfil the contract terms

Documents Required at the Time of Document Verification

Candidates must carry originals plus self-attested photocopies of all the following:

  • System-generated printout of online application/NATS enrolment form
  • Date of Birth proof (Birth Certificate or Class X certificate with DOB)
  • Photo identity proof
  • Individual semester/year-wise mark sheets and final degree certificate
  • Caste certificate (for SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates) in the Government of India prescribed format
  • OBC certificate must explicitly contain the non-creamy layer clause and be valid as on the date of the Language Test (issued within one year)
  • Disability certificate from District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates)
  • Income and Asset Certificate (for EWS candidates) issued by competent authority

Reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD)

Under Section 34 of the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, reservation for PwBD is on a horizontal basis within the overall vacancies for each state. The qualifying disability categories include:

  • Visual Impairment (Blindness and Low Vision)
  • Hearing Impairment (Deaf and Hard of Hearing)
  • Orthopedic/Locomotor Disability (OA, OL, BL, OAL)
  • Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims, Muscular Dystrophy
  • Intellectual Disability (Autism Spectrum Disorder, Specific Learning Disability, Mental Illness)
  • Multiple Disabilities

A total of 45 seats each are reserved for VI, HI, OH, and ID categories, amounting to 180 PwBD seats across the country.

Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) Eligibility

Candidates not covered under SC/ST/OBC reservation and whose family gross annual income is below ₹8 lakh from all sources (salary, agriculture, business, profession) qualify for EWS reservation. Additionally, the family must not own:

  • 5 acres or more of agricultural land
  • A residential flat of 1,000 sq. ft. or more
  • A residential plot of 100 sq. yards or more in notified municipalities
  • A residential plot of 200 sq. yards or more outside notified municipalities

The EWS benefit requires an Income and Asset Certificate in the prescribed Government of India format, issued by a competent authority.

Where to Find Updates and Announcements

All announcements, addendums, and corrigenda will be published exclusively on:

  • Central Bank of India : Click here (Career section → Current Opportunities)
  • BFSI SSC portal: https://bfsissc.com
  • BEEP portal: https://beep.bfsissc.com (under "My Applied Opportunities")

jobDescription:
The All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi has released the Detailed Recruitment Advertisement for Common Recruitment Examination-5 (CRE-5) on 11 June 2026. The examination invites applications from eligible Indian nationals for a wide range of Non-Faculty Group-B and Group-C posts spread across 28 participating AIIMS and Central Government Institutes/Bodies. The last date to is 30 June 2026 (up to 5:00 PM) at www.aiimsexams.ac.in. The Computer Based Test (CBT) is tentatively scheduled for 25 to 27 July 2026.

About CRE-5: What Is It and Who Is It For?

CRE-5 is a unified, single-window recruitment platform that allows eligible candidates to apply once and be considered for similar posts across multiple participating institutes. The Examination Section, AIIMS New Delhi acts solely as the examination-conducting body; recruitment rules, service conditions, and final appointment decisions rest entirely with each participating institute independently.

Participating organizations are autonomous bodies or subordinate offices under the Government of India, functioning independently from AIIMS New Delhi. Candidates are advised to check each institute's individual recruitment rules and eligibility criteria before applying, as conditions may differ for the same post name across different institutes.

Participating Institutes (28 Total)

AIIMS Awantipora, AIIMS Bathinda, AIIMS Bhopal, AIIMS Bibinagar, AIIMS Bilaspur, AIIMS Deoghar, AIIMS Gorakhpur, AIIMS Guwahati, AIIMS Kalyani, AIIMS Mangalagiri, AIIMS Madurai, AIIMS Nagpur, AIIMS New Delhi, AIIMS Patna, AIIMS Raebareli, AIIMS Raipur, AIIMS Rajkot, AIIMS Rewari, AIIMS Rishikesh, AIIMS Vijaypur, AIIMS-CAPFIMS (New Delhi), Atal Bihari Vajpayee Institute of Medical Sciences & Dr. RML Hospital, Chittaranjan National Cancer Institute (CNCI) Kolkata, Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR), Lady Hardinge Medical College & associated Sucheta Kriplani Hospital and Kalawati Saran Children's Hospital, Pasteur Institute of India, Rural Health Training Centre (RHTC) Najafgarh, and Regional Institute of Paramedical and Nursing Sciences (RIPANS) Aizawl.

AIIMS CRE-5 Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Eligibility for CRE-5 varies by post and by participating institute. The criteria below cover the most widely advertised posts. Candidates wondering about specific eligibility must verify against the post and institute listed in Annexure-I of the , as recruitment rules differ institute to institute.

Educational Qualification (Key Posts)

Assistant Dietician (Pay Level 6) M.Sc. (Food and Nutrition) from a recognized University + 2 years' experience

AIIMS CRE-5 Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age limits are post-specific and measured as of the closing date of online registration, i.e., 30 June 2026. Most Group B posts cap at age 30 years and Group C posts cap between 18–30 years, though higher caps apply to some technical and supervisory roles (up to 35 or 40 years). Candidates wondering about age relaxation can note the following:

SC/ST 5 years
