title: AIIMS CRE 5th Group B, C Recruitment 2026: for 2,693 Posts, Salary Up to ₹1,51,100
description: AIIMS CRE-5 2026 Notification: Eligibility, Salary, Important Dates & How to Apply Great news for everyone dreaming of a stable job in the health and government sector. The All Ind
jobDescription:
AIIMS CRE-5 2026 Notification: Eligibility, Salary, Important Dates & How to Apply

Great news for everyone dreaming of a stable job in the health and government sector. The All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi has released the Common Recruitment Examination (CRE-5) notification to fill a huge 2,693 vacancies in Group B and Group C posts. This single exam covers 32 participating institutes — all AIIMS hospitals plus ESIC, ICMR, RML, Safdarjung and other central health bodies. With pay going up to ₹1,51,100, it is one of the biggest medical and healthcare government jobs drives of the year.

The online application window is open from 13 June 2026 to 03 July 2026 (up to 5:00 PM). Whether you are a 10th pass, 12th pass, ITI, diploma, graduate or postgraduate candidate, there is very likely a post for you here — from clerks and technicians to nursing officers and engineers. In this article, we will explain the vacancies, eligibility, salary, selection process, fees and the step-by-step application process in very simple language. Read it fully so you don’t miss any detail, and keep bookmarked for every update.

Key Highlights — AIIMS CRE-5 Group B & C Recruitment 2026

The AIIMS Common Recruitment Examination (CRE-5) is a single-window, computer-based exam conducted by the Examination Section of AIIMS, New Delhi. Instead of applying separately at each hospital, you apply once for a post group and your result is used to fill that post across many institutes. This saves time, money and effort. The recruitment is for regular, non-faculty Group B and Group C posts and is open to candidates across the country.

  • Post Names: Clerks (LDC/UDC/DEO), Stenographer, Nursing Officer, Pharmacist, Lab/OT/Radiography Technicians, Junior Engineers, and many more
  • Total Vacancies: 2,693 posts
  • Salary / Pay Level: Pay Level 1 to Level 8 (₹18,000 – ₹1,51,100 basic)
  • Job Location: All India (32 participating institutes)
  • Application Start Date: 13 June 2026
  • Last Date to Apply: 03 July 2026 (5:00 PM)

What are the Post-Wise Vacancies for AIIMS CRE-5 2026?

The 2,693 vacancies are spread across 59 post groups. Since there are too many individual posts to list, here is a clear group-wise breakdown of the major cadres so you can find your category quickly. Many of these posts only need 12th pass qualification, which makes this a great chance for freshers.

Category-wise, the vacancies break up into UR: 1,447, OBC: 483, SC: 363, ST: 201 and EWS: 199, with separate horizontal reservation for PwBD and Ex-Servicemen candidates.

What is the Eligibility for AIIMS CRE-5 2026 (Education & Age Limit)?

Because this notification covers so many different posts, the qualifications change from post to post. Below is a simple, post-group summary. Always check the exact requirement for your chosen post in the before applying.

Educational Qualification

  • 10th Pass / Matriculation: MTS, Hospital Attendant, Cook, Dresser, Driver, and similar support posts (some also need an ITI / trade certificate)
  • 12th / 10+2 Pass: LDC, Junior Administrative Assistant, Data Entry Operator, Stenographer, Telephone Operator
  • Diploma holders: Lab Technician (DMLT), OT Technician, Radiographer, ECG/EEG Technician, Pharmacist (D.Pharm with State Pharmacy Council registration)
  • Engineering (Diploma / Degree): Junior Engineer and Assistant Engineer (Civil, Electrical, AC&R), Biomedical Engineer
  • Graduates: Assistant, UDC, Cashier (B.Com), Store Keeper, Personal Assistant, Programmer
  • B.Sc / Professional degrees: Nursing Officer (B.Sc Nursing), Lab Technologist, Optometrist, Perfusionist, Physiotherapist
  • Postgraduates: Dietician, Medical Social Worker, Counsellor, Junior Physicist, Statistical Assistant

If you hold any graduate-level government job qualification, several Level 6 and Level 7 posts are open for you.

Age Limit

The age limit depends on the post, but the age range is generally 18 to 40 years as on the closing date (03 July 2026). Age relaxation is available as per government rules:

  • OBC: 3 years
  • SC / ST: 5 years
  • PwBD: 10 years (over and above category relaxation)
  • Ex-Servicemen: as per service rules

What is the Salary for AIIMS CRE-5 2026 Posts?

The salary for AIIMS CRE-5 posts is governed by the 7th Pay Commission, with posts placed between Pay Level 1 and Pay Level 8. This means your basic pay grows steadily, and you also get regular government allowances on top of it.

  • Pay Level 1: ₹18,000 – ₹56,900
  • Pay Level 2: ₹19,900 – ₹63,200
  • Pay Level 4: ₹25,500 – ₹81,100
  • Pay Level 5: ₹29,200 – ₹92,300
  • Pay Level 6: ₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400
  • Pay Level 7: ₹44,900 – ₹1,42,400
  • Pay Level 8: ₹47,600 – ₹1,51,100

Including allowances like DA, HRA and Transport Allowance, the approximate in-hand salary ranges from around ₹22,000 for entry-level Group C posts to over ₹1,30,000 per month for higher Group B posts, depending on your level and posting city.

Apart from a strong salary, you also enjoy job security, medical benefits, the New Pension System (NPS), and leave benefits — the real value of a central government job.

What is the Selection Process for AIIMS CRE-5 2026?

The selection is done in stages. Don’t worry — your final merit is based mainly on the written exam, so prepare well for that. You can review the exam syllabus and pattern on to plan your studies.

Stage 1: Computer-Based Test (CBT)

This is the main exam. It is 90 minutes long with 100 multiple-choice questions carrying 400 marks (4 marks per question). There is negative marking of 1/4 (minus 1 mark) for every wrong answer. Out of 100 questions, 20 cover General Knowledge, Aptitude and Computer Knowledge, and 80 are specific to your post. Qualifying marks are 40% for UR/EWS, 35% for OBC, and 30% for SC/ST and PwBD.

Stage 2: Skill / Trade / Physical Test

Depending on the post, you may have a typing test (for clerks/DEO), stenography test (for stenographers), trade test (for technical posts) or a physical standards/efficiency test (for fire and security posts). These tests are only qualifying in nature.

Stage 3: Document Verification & Medical Examination

Finally, your documents are checked by the institute and you undergo a medical examination. The merit list is prepared on the basis of your CBT marks, with normalization applied if the exam is held in multiple shifts.

How to for AIIMS CRE-5 2026?

Before you begin, keep your photo, signature, thumb impression, educational certificates and a valid email and mobile number ready. The form is fully online — no documents are sent by post. Follow these steps:

  1. Visit the official AIIMS exam portal (direct link given in the Essential Links section below).
  2. Complete the One Time Registration (OTR) if you are a new user.
  3. Log in and select the post group you want to apply for.
  4. Fill in your personal, educational and category details carefully.
  5. Upload your photo, signature and thumb impression as per the given size rules.
  6. Pay the application fee online using a Debit Card, Credit Card or Net Banking.
  7. Submit the form and take a printout of the confirmation page for your records.

Remember, you must submit a separate application and fee for each post group you apply to. Government and PSU employees must upload their NOC by 08 July 2026.

What are the Important Dates for AIIMS CRE-5 2026?

What is the Application Fee for AIIMS CRE-5 2026?

The fee is paid online only through Debit Card, Credit Card or Net Banking, and is payable separately for each post group. The fee is non-refundable; however, SC/ST candidates who appear in the exam will get their fee refunded as per rules.

title: MP Police Constable Recruitment Bharti 2025: Final Result Declared — Check Now
description: MP Police Constable 2025: Final Result Declared — Check Now The final result for MP Police Constable Recruitment 2025 has been declared. Candidates can check their result on the of
jobDescription:
MP Police Constable 2025: Final Result Declared — Check Now

The final result for MP Police Constable Recruitment 2025 has been declared. Candidates can check their result on the official MPESB website. Check MP Police Constable 2025 Final Result

Your Guide to the MP Police Constable Recruitment 2025

Here’s some exciting news for those looking for a government job in Madhya Pradesh! The Madhya Pradesh Employee Selection Board (MP ESB) has officially announced the MP Police Constable Recruitment Bharti 2025. This is a fantastic opportunity to join the esteemed MP Police force, with a total of 7500 vacancies for the post of Constable (General Duty). This recruitment drive offers a stable career with a respectable salary and numerous benefits. The application window is open from September 15, 2025, to October 22, Read on for a detailed guide on everything you need to know about this major hiring event.

Key Highlights of the MP Police Constable Notification

The MP Police Department, through the MP ESB, is looking for dedicated and eligible candidates to fill these constable positions. This recruitment is a significant opportunity for individuals who have passed the 10th or 12th grade and are aspiring to a career in law enforcement.

MP Police Constable Vacancy 2025 Breakdown

A total of 7500 vacancies have been announced. The distribution of these posts is as follows:

Eligibility Criteria for MP Police Constable Bharti

Before applying, it’s crucial to ensure you meet all the eligibility requirements set by the department.

Educational Qualification

  • For General, SC, and OBC Candidates: Must have passed the 10th class examination under the 10+2 system, or Higher Secondary, or an equivalent examination from a recognized board.
  • For ST Candidates: Must have passed the 8th class or an equivalent examination.

Age Limit (as of September 29, 2025)

The age requirements for candidates are as follows:

  • Minimum Age: 18 years
  • Maximum Age for Male (Unreserved/Other State): 33 years
  • Maximum Age for Female (All Categories): 38 years
  • Maximum Age for Male (Reserved Categories – SC/ST/OBC): 38 years
  • Maximum Age for Govt./Corporation Employees/Home Guards: 38 years

Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories as per government rules.

Physical and Medical Requirements

Candidates must meet specific physical standards to be eligible for the role.

  • Height (Male): 168 cm (160 cm for ST)
  • Height (Female): 155 cm
  • Chest (Male Only): 81 cm (unexpanded) and 86 cm (expanded). For ST candidates, it is 76 cm (unexpanded) and 81 cm (expanded). A minimum expansion of 5 cm is mandatory.
  • Candidates must be medically fit, with no knock-knees, flat feet, or vision defects. Vision standards are 6/9 for the better eye and 6/12 for the weaker eye without glasses.

MP Police Constable Salary and Benefits

A career as an MP Police Constable comes with a competitive salary and attractive benefits, making it a sought-after government job. The pay scale for the Constable post is ₹19,500 – ₹62,000.

This pay scale means that the starting basic pay is ₹19,In addition to the basic pay, selected candidates are entitled to several allowances, which significantly increase the in-hand salary. These include:

  • Dearness Allowance (DA): This is adjusted periodically to offset the impact of inflation.
  • House Rent Allowance (HRA): Provided to cover housing expenses, the amount varies based on the posting location.
  • Travel Allowance (TA): To cover travel expenses incurred for official duties.

Furthermore, employees are covered under the National Pension System (NPS), ensuring a secure future post-retirement. According to the rules, for the first three years of service, appointees will receive a stipend: 70% of the minimum pay scale in the first year, 80% in the second, and 90% in the third year.

Selection Process Explained

The selection process for the MP Police Constable Recruitment is a two-stage process designed to test both the mental and physical capabilities of the candidates.

Tier 1: Computer Based Test (CBT)

This is the first stage of the selection process.

  • Exam Pattern: The written test will consist of 100 multiple-choice questions (MCQs).
  • Duration: The exam duration will be 2 hours (120 minutes).
  • Subjects:
    • General Knowledge & Reasoning: 40 Marks
    • Intellectual Ability & Mental Aptitude: 30 Marks
    • Science & Simple Arithmetic: 30 Marks
  • Marking Scheme: Each correct answer will be awarded 1 mark.
  • Negative Marking: There is no negative marking for incorrect answers.
  • Qualifying Marks: Candidates scoring in the top 7 times the number of vacancies in their respective categories will be shortlisted for the next stage.

Tier 2: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) / Physical Standard Test (PST)

Candidates who qualify in the written exam will be called for the PET/PST. This stage is qualifying in nature and also carries marks that will be added to the final merit list.

  • Events: The PET consists of three events:
    • 800-meter run
    • Long Jump
    • Shot Put (Gola Fek)
  • The performance in these events will be awarded marks out of a total of Candidates must score a minimum of 30 marks to qualify.

The final merit list will be prepared based on the combined scores of the Written Test and the Physical Efficiency Test.

How to : Step-by-Step Guide

Follow these steps carefully to submit your application form successfully. Before you begin, make sure you have scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other necessary documents.

Required Documents:

  • 10th or 8th Class Marksheet (as applicable)
  • Caste Certificate (if applicable)
  • Domicile Certificate
  • Recent Passport-size Photograph and Signature

Application Steps:

  1. Visit the official MP ESB website. (link provided below)
  2. Click on the “Online Form” section on the homepage.
  3. Find the link for the “Police Constable Recruitment Test 2025” and click on it.
  4. You will be redirected to the application portal. Register yourself to create a profile if you are a new user.
  5. Log in with your registration details.
  6. Fill out the application form with accurate personal, academic, and contact details.
  7. Upload the scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and required documents as per the specified format.
  8. Review the filled application form carefully to check for any errors.
  9. Proceed to pay the application fee through the available online payment modes.
  10. After successful payment, submit the application form.
  11. Download and take a printout of the final application form for your records.

Important Dates

Application Fees

Essential Links

title: APSSB CHSL 2026: 207 LDC, DEO, Stockman Posts in Arunachal Pradesh Recruitment 2026
description: APSSB CHSL Examination 2026: 207 Group C Posts for 12th Pass — Complete Notification Guide If you have passed Class 12 and dream of a government job in Arunachal Pradesh, the wait
jobDescription:
APSSB CHSL Examination 2026: 207 Group C Posts for 12th Pass — Complete Notification Guide

If you have passed Class 12 and dream of a government job in Arunachal Pradesh, the wait is over. The Arunachal Pradesh Staff Selection Board (APSSB) has released the Combined Higher Secondary Level (CHSL) Examination 2026 notification (Advt. No. 05/26, dated 9 June 2026) for 207 Group C posts across nine post categories — from Lower Division Clerk and Data Entry Operator to Stockman and Fishery Demonstrator. Pay goes up to ₹81,100 per month at the top of the Level-4 scale.

Online applications open on 20 July 2026 and close on 10 August 2026 (5:00 PM), with the written exam tentatively on 20 September 2026. One application window, nine different posts — and 165 of the 207 seats are reserved for APST candidates. We at have simplified every detail below, so read the full article before you choose your post preference.

Key Highlights — APSSB CHSL 2026

APSSB, Itanagar is the body that conducts all Group C recruitment for the Government of Arunachal Pradesh. Its CHSL exam is the state-level equivalent of SSC CHSL — one combined exam covering multiple 10+2-level posts across departments.

  • Post Names: LDC, Data Entry Operator, Junior Secretariat Assistant, Agriculture Field Assistant (Jr), Field Assistant, Fishery Demonstrator, Laboratory Assistant, Record Keeper/Record Clerk, Stockman
  • Total Vacancies: 207 (APST 165 + Unreserved 42)
  • Pay: Level-1 to Level-4 — ₹18,000 up to ₹81,100 per month
  • Job Location: Arunachal Pradesh
  • Application Window: 20 July to 10 August 2026 (5:00 PM)
  • Written Exam: 20 September 2026 (tentative)

Vacancy Breakdown — APSSB CHSL 2026

The 56 LDC seats are spread across 14 departments — including Election (7), PHE & Water Supply (9, all reserved for PwBD candidates), PWD (8), Rural Works (6), Health Services (5), Prosecution (5) and TRIHMS (4). Across all posts, 15 seats are reserved for PwBD and 4 for Ex-Servicemen (horizontal, within the total). You must choose your post and department preference at the time of applying — it cannot be changed later, so decide carefully.

What is the Eligibility for APSSB CHSL 2026?

Educational Qualification

The base qualification for every post is Class 12 (10+2) pass from a recognized board — but the stream and extras differ by post:

  • Stockman, Laboratory Assistant, Agriculture Field Assistant (Jr): Class XII with Science
  • Fishery Demonstrator: Class XII Science with Biology as a subject
  • Field Assistant: Class XII with Mathematics or Geography, OR Class XII in any subject plus a Survey course from a Government-recognized ITI
  • LDC, DEO, Junior Secretariat Assistant: Class XII in any stream, plus computer typing speed of 35 WPM (10,500 key depressions per hour), plus a minimum 6-month Diploma in Computer Application recognized by APCTE/AICTE
  • Record Keeper/Record Clerk: Class XII plus a 1-year Diploma in Computer Application
  • LDC under Sainik Welfare: must be an Ex-Serviceman with at least 10 years’ service as a Combatant, preferably as Clerk

Every 12th-pass job seeker should note the DCA diploma requirement carefully — without the recognized computer diploma, you are not eligible for the clerical posts.

Age Limit

Age is counted as on the closing date (10 August 2026) and varies by post:

Relaxations: APST candidates +5 years, PwBD +10 years (15 years for APST PwBD), in-service departmental candidates with 3 years’ continuous service +5 years, and Ex-Servicemen can deduct their military service from their actual age.

What is the Salary for APSSB CHSL 2026 Posts?

Pay follows the 7th CPC pay matrix of the Arunachal Pradesh Government.

  • Level-4 posts (LDC, DEO, JSA, Stockman, AFA-Jr, Record Keeper): ₹25,500 – ₹81,100
  • Level-2 posts (Fishery Demonstrator, Laboratory Assistant): ₹19,900 – ₹63,200
  • Level-1 post (Field Assistant): ₹18,000 – ₹56,900

A Level-4 starting salary of ₹25,500 plus DA, HRA and other allowances makes the in-hand comfortably above ₹30,000 per month at entry — solid for a 10+2-level government post.

Along with the pay, these are permanent state-government posts with pension coverage under the applicable scheme, regular increments and complete job security.

What is the Selection Process for APSSB CHSL 2026?

Stage-I: Written Examination (Merit-Deciding)

The paper carries 100 questions for 200 marks in 2 hours. The best part for aspirants: no negative marking and no sectional cut-off — you need a minimum 33% aggregate to qualify. The exam is tentatively on 20 September 2026 across 15 centres (you pick 3 in priority order).

Stage-II: Skill Test (Qualifying Only)

  1. Applicable only to DEO, Junior Secretariat Assistant and LDC — a computer typing test at 35 WPM (10,500 KDPH), from 10 October 2026 onwards
  2. All other posts are decided purely on the written exam merit
  3. Document verification follows for shortlisted candidates — originals of your Class XII certificate, APST certificate, PRC, DCA diploma and other documents
  4. Final selection remains provisional until verification by the appointing authority

Practice for the typing stage early — check our Admit Card section closer to the exam for hall-ticket updates.

How to for APSSB CHSL 2026?

Keep ready: a scanned passport photo (50–100 KB, jpg/jpeg/png), scanned signature in black ink on white paper (10–50 KB), your Class XII certificate details, and your APST/PRC certificate numbers if applicable.

  1. Visit the APSSB official portal when the window opens on 20 July 2026 (link in Essential Links below)
  2. Register with your name, parentage, date of birth and a working mobile number and email
  3. Fill the CHSL 2026 application form and select your post and department preference carefully — it is final
  4. Choose your 3 exam centre preferences in priority order
  5. Upload your photograph and signature in the prescribed sizes
  6. Pay the fee online — ₹150 (APST) or ₹200 (Unreserved); PwD candidates are exempt
  7. Submit and print the application confirmation for your records

If you submit multiple forms, only the latest application is considered, and no edits are allowed after the closing date.

What are the Important Dates for APSSB CHSL 2026?

Application Fees — APSSB CHSL 2026

Payment is online only and the fee is non-refundable.

Essential Links — APSSB CHSL 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for APSSB CHSL 2026
  • Visit APSSB

With 207 posts, no negative marking and a single straightforward exam, APSSB CHSL 2026 is one of the most winnable state recruitments this year. Note the dates — applications start 20 July and close 10 August 2026 — and use the head start to prepare for the 20 September exam. For every Arunachal Pradesh government job update, keep visiting .

title: MPSEDC GET Recruitment 2026: 150 Graduate Engineer Trainees, ₹25,000 Stipend
description: MPSEDC Graduate Engineer Trainee Program 3.0: Stipend, Exam Pattern, Syllabus & Full Guide Fresh engineering graduates of Madhya Pradesh, this one is squarely for you. Madhya Prade
jobDescription:
MPSEDC Graduate Engineer Trainee Program 3.0: Stipend, Exam Pattern, Syllabus & Full Guide

Fresh engineering graduates of Madhya Pradesh, this one is squarely for you. Madhya Pradesh State Electronics Development Corporation Ltd. (MPSEDC) — the IT arm of the MP Government — has opened applications for GET Program 3.0, offering 150 Graduate Engineer Trainee seats with a stipend of ₹25,000 per month during the 1-year training at the State IT Center, Bhopal. Trainees work on live government IT projects, and top performers get a shot at an Associate Engineer role after completion.

The application window is short: 4 June to 16 June 2026, with the online exam on 1 July 2026. If you completed your B.E./B.Tech or a CS/IT degree from an MP institution within the last year (or are in your final semester), you are eligible. We at have decoded the full notification below — eligibility, syllabus weightage, exam pattern and how to . Don’t miss the deadline.

Key Highlights — MPSEDC GET Recruitment 2026

MPSEDC is the nodal agency for IT and e-governance projects of the Madhya Pradesh Government — the team behind many of the state’s digital platforms. The GET program is its flagship pipeline for grooming young engineers into government-tech professionals, and this is its third edition.

  • Program Name: Graduate Engineer Trainee (GET) Program 3.0 — 1 Year Training
  • Total Seats: 150 (plus a waitlist of up to 50)
  • Stipend: ₹25,000 per month
  • Job Location: State IT Center, Arera Hills, Bhopal
  • Application Window: 4 June to 16 June 2026
  • Online Exam Date: 1 July 2026

Vacancy & Seat Details — MPSEDC GET Recruitment 2026

There is no discipline-wise or category-wise split — selection is a single merit list from the online exam. The merit list stays valid until the next exam notification or 6 months, whichever is earlier.

What is the Eligibility for MPSEDC GET 2026?

Educational Qualification

  • B.E. / B.Tech in ANY discipline (completed or final semester), OR
  • Graduation or Post-Graduation in Computer Science / IT / Computer Application or an equivalent degree (completed or final semester)
  • The qualifying degree must be from a Madhya Pradesh institution recognized by AICTE/UGC — this applies even to non-domicile candidates
  • Your degree must have been awarded within 365 days of the advertisement — this program is strictly for recent pass-outs and final-semester students
  • Diploma and PG Diploma holders are NOT eligible
  • Final-semester candidates can apply now and submit their marksheet/provisional degree within 2 months of joining

There is no minimum percentage cutoff. If you are from a Computer Science or IT background, the syllabus will feel like home turf.

Age Limit

18 to 30 years at the time of the advertisement notification. The notification does not provide category-wise age relaxation — the same criteria apply to everyone.

What is the Stipend for MPSEDC GET 2026?

Remember, this is a traineeship, not a permanent post — the monthly payment is a stipend, not a regular salary on a pay matrix.

  • Stipend: ₹25,000 per month for the full 1-year training
  • Exit: pro-rata payment if you leave mid-way (1 month notice required)
  • Reviews: performance assessments at 6 and 12 months — only above-threshold scorers continue and complete
  • After completion: a Certificate of Completion plus the opportunity to interview for an Associate Engineer role at MPSEDC (on the payroll of empanelled third-party agencies)

For a fresh graduate, ₹25,000 per month while learning government-scale IT systems in Bhopal is a strong launchpad — many GET alumni convert this into longer IT careers in the govt-tech ecosystem.

Be aware of the practical conditions: you bring your own laptop, you get 1 casual leave per month, there is no medical/insurance benefit, and a signed NDA is mandatory before joining. There is no service bond though — you can exit with a month’s notice.

What is the Selection Process for MPSEDC GET 2026?

Online Examination — 1 July 2026

The exam is conducted by MP Online Ltd. across 7 proposed cities — Bhopal, Jabalpur, Indore, Gwalior, Sagar, Ujjain and Satna (you choose 4 centre preferences).

Syllabus Weightage

  1. Clear the online exam with at least 35% marks
  2. Make it into the top 150 of the merit list (next 50 go to the waitlist)
  3. Complete document verification at MPSEDC, Bhopal — identity proof, marksheets, degree
  4. Join within 15 days of the joining letter (final-semester candidates: within 15 days of completing their final exams)

Prepare with a clear plan — our Syllabus section has preparation resources for technical government exams.

How to for MPSEDC GET 2026?

Keep ready: clear self-attested scans of your documents, a passport-size photo not older than 6 months, your high school certificate (the only accepted age proof), and your graduation/PG certificate or marksheet.

  1. Visit the MP Online application portal (direct link in Essential Links below)
  2. Open the MPSEDC GET Program 3.0 application form
  3. Fill in your personal, academic and centre-preference details carefully
  4. Upload your photograph and documents in the prescribed sizes
  5. Pay the application fee of ₹800 online (or through an MP Online Kiosk)
  6. Submit the form and download the confirmation page
  7. If you spot a mistake later, use the one-time edit window (9-17 June) for ₹50 + GST

What are the Important Dates for MPSEDC GET Recruitment 2026?

Application Fees — MPSEDC GET Recruitment 2026

Payment is online only (MP Online portal or kiosk), and the fee is non-refundable. There is no category-wise fee concession in this program.

Essential Links — MPSEDC GET Recruitment 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for MPSEDC GET 2026
  • Visit MPSEDC

If you graduated from an MP college in the last year, the maths is simple: one ₹800 form, one 3-hour exam on 1 July, and a 150-seat shot at ₹25,000 a month plus a government-tech career track. Apply before 16 June 2026 and start revising Java, .Net and PHP today. For more Madhya Pradesh government jobs, keep visiting .

title: IIM Mumbai Management Trainee Recruitment 2026: 10 Posts, Apply by 16 June
description: IIM Mumbai MT Vacancy 2026: Stipend, Eligibility & Application Guide for Management Trainees Imagine starting your career on the campus of an IIM — without writing CAT. The Indian
jobDescription:
IIM Mumbai MT Vacancy 2026: Stipend, Eligibility & Application Guide for Management Trainees

Imagine starting your career on the campus of an IIM — without writing CAT. The Indian Institute of Management Mumbai (formerly NITIE), Powai has released a notification dated 27 May 2026 for 10 Management Trainee posts — 6 area-specific MT posts and 4 MT (Admin) posts. The monthly stipend is ₹35,000 to ₹45,000, topped up with ₹8,000 housing allowance, ₹2,000 travel allowance and ₹500 mobile reimbursement — a serious package for postgraduates and B.Tech holders starting out.

The application is completely online and the clock is ticking — the last date is 16 June 2026 (5:00 PM). If you hold a Master’s degree with 60% marks (or a B.Tech), this is one of the most prestigious addresses in Maharashtra government jobs you can put on your resume. We at have explained every detail below — read fully before applying.

Key Highlights — IIM Mumbai Management Trainee Recruitment 2026

IIM Mumbai is an Institute of National Importance under the Ministry of Education, born from the upgrade of NITIE — India’s storied industrial engineering school in Powai. Its non-faculty trainee programme places you inside the institute’s academic and administrative machinery.

  • Post Names: Management Trainee (5 areas) and Management Trainee (Admin)
  • Total Vacancies: 10
  • Stipend: ₹35,000 – ₹45,000 per month + HRA ₹8,000 + TA ₹2,000 + Mobile ₹500
  • Job Location: IIM Mumbai campus, Powai, Mumbai
  • Tenure: 1 year, extendable up to 4 years total
  • Last Date to Apply: 16 June 2026 (5:00 PM)

Vacancy Breakdown — IIM Mumbai MT Recruitment 2026

The notification gives no category-wise (UR/OBC/SC/ST/EWS) distribution for these contractual posts, and the institute may increase or decrease the numbers as per need. Women candidates are specially encouraged to .

What is the Eligibility for IIM Mumbai Management Trainee Recruitment 2026?

Educational Qualification

  • Management Trainee (area posts): a Master’s degree in the area you apply for — Analytics & Decision Science, Operations & Supply Chain, Sustainability Management, OBHR Management, or Marketing — with 60% marks or above, OR a B.Tech or equivalent professional degree from a recognized institution
  • Management Trainee (Admin): a Master’s degree in any area with 60%+, OR a B.Tech or equivalent professional degree
  • Candidates pursuing or intending to pursue a Ph.D. get preference
  • Enter your exact percentage in the form — rounding off is not allowed
  • Desirable skills: MS Office, ERP systems, and strong written and spoken English and Hindi

Whether you are a postgraduate in management or an engineering graduate hunting for a campus role, you qualify if the percentage bar is met.

Experience

Experience is not mandatory. Prior exposure to higher-education institutions, EdTech, academic research, admissions or student services counts as an added advantage.

Age Limit

  • Maximum 32 years as on 16 June 2026
  • Extendable up to 35 years for Ph.D. candidates or candidates with relevant experience
  • SC/ST/OBC/PwBD age relaxation as per Central Government rules (attach the prescribed certificates)

What is the Stipend for IIM Mumbai Management Trainee 2026?

These are contractual trainee posts with a consolidated package — a monthly stipend in place of a regular salary scale.

  • Monthly Stipend: ₹35,000 – ₹45,000
  • Housing Allowance: ₹8,000 per month (no campus accommodation is provided)
  • Travelling Allowance: ₹2,000 per month
  • Mobile Reimbursement: ₹500 per month

All-in, the package effectively touches ₹45,500 – ₹55,500 per month — excellent for a trainee role, and the IIM Mumbai name on your CV multiplies its long-term value.

The tenure is 1 year, extendable year-to-year up to 4 years based on performance. Be clear-eyed: this is a contract role with no claim to regularization, working 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM, six days a week, with occasional event-time stretches.

What is the Selection Process for IIM Mumbai MT Recruitment 2026?

The institute keeps the process flexible and merit-driven:

  1. Shortlisting — if applications are high, the institute restricts the interview list using higher qualifications and experience
  2. Assessment — a written test and/or skill/competency test and/or personal interview, as decided by the Competent Authority, evaluating domain knowledge, aptitude and suitability
  3. Document verification — originals of your degrees, marksheets, experience and category certificates at the final stage
  4. Communication — all selection-process dates come by email only, so use an email ID you actually check

No TA/DA is paid for attending the selection process. Keep an eye on our Exam Calendar for schedule updates across institutes.

How to for IIM Mumbai MT Recruitment 2026?

Keep ready: your exact percentage figures, scanned certificates, an updated CV and a valid email ID.

  1. Visit the IIM Mumbai administrative recruitment page (direct link in Essential Links below)
  2. Open the Management Trainee Recruitment 2026 application link
  3. Choose your post — an area-specific MT or MT (Admin) — matching your Master’s specialization
  4. Fill the online form with exact marks percentages (no rounding) and your details
  5. Upload the required documents and submit before 16 June 2026, 5:00 PM
  6. Save the confirmation — no interim correspondence is entertained

What are the Important Dates for IIM Mumbai Recruitment 2026?

Application Fees — IIM Mumbai MT Recruitment 2026

The notification specifies no application fee — confirm on the application portal while submitting your form.

Essential Links — IIM Mumbai MT Recruitment 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for IIM Mumbai Management Trainee 2026
  • Visit IIM Mumbai

Ten seats at an IIM campus, a ₹45,000-plus package and a brand that opens doors for life — but only if your application is in before 16 June 2026, 5:00 PM. Polish your CV tonight and submit early. For more such government job opportunities, keep visiting .

title: BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026: for 18 Manager, Secretary Grade-III, Care Taker & Junior Assistant Vacancies | Last Date 5 July
description: BIP Rajasthan Vacancy 2026: Eligibility, Pay Matrix, Exam Pattern & Complete Guide The Bureau of Investment Promotion (BIP), Rajasthan has released a fresh recruitment notification
jobDescription:
BIP Rajasthan Vacancy 2026: Eligibility, Pay Matrix, Exam Pattern & Complete Guide

The Bureau of Investment Promotion (BIP), Rajasthan has released a fresh recruitment notification on 3 June 2026 for 18 posts of Manager, Secretary Grade-III, Care Taker and Junior Assistant (Typist). BIP is the nodal agency of the Rajasthan Government that brings big industrial investments to the state, and a job here means working right at the heart of Rajasthan’s growth story from its Jaipur headquarters. Selected candidates will draw pay up to Pay Matrix Level-14 after probation — a solid, secure government job in Rajasthan.

Online applications start on 6 June 2026 and close on 5 July 2026 (11:59 PM). If you are a graduate with RSCIT/PGDCA, or an MBA with a few years of work experience, this recruitment is tailor-made for you. In this article, we at explain the vacancy breakdown, eligibility, fees, exam pattern and the step-by-step application process. Read till the end so you don’t miss any important detail.

Key Highlights — BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026

The Bureau of Investment Promotion works under the Industries Department of the Government of Rajasthan from Udyog Bhawan, Jaipur. It facilitates investment proposals, single-window clearances and investor support for the state. This is a rare direct recruitment drive by BIP, so competition will be focused but the opportunity is real.

  • Post Names: Manager, Secretary Grade-III, Care Taker, Junior Assistant (Typist)
  • Total Vacancies: 18
  • Pay Level: Level-5 to Level-14 of the Rajasthan Pay Matrix (fixed remuneration during 2-year probation)
  • Job Location: Jaipur, Rajasthan
  • Application Start Date: 6 June 2026
  • Last Date to Apply: 5 July 2026 (11:59 PM)

How Many Vacancies in BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026?

Reservation follows Rajasthan Government rules — the 18 seats include reserved seats for SC, ST, BC, EWS and General-Women candidates, with 1 post each in Manager, Secretary Grade-III and Junior Assistant (Typist) horizontally reserved for PwBD (Blindness/Low Vision) candidates. Please note: recruitment to Secretary Grade-III is subject to the outcome of writ petitions pending before the Hon’ble Rajasthan High Court, as stated in the notification. Reserved-category benefits apply only to candidates with Rajasthan domicile; candidates from other states apply under the General category.

What is the Eligibility for BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026?

Educational Qualification

  • Manager: MBA in any of these specialisations — Finance, Human Resource, Marketing, Operations Management, IT, or International Business (or an equivalent degree) from a recognized university, plus 3 years’ experience in a Government Department, PSU or an organisation registered with EPFO.
  • Secretary Grade-III: Graduation from a recognized university plus RSCIT / PGDCA or an equivalent computer application certificate, plus 3 years’ experience as above.
  • Care Taker: Graduation plus RSCIT / PGDCA or equivalent, plus 2 years’ experience of store and purchase work in a Government Department, PSU or EPFO-registered organisation.
  • Junior Assistant (Typist): Graduation plus RSCIT / PGDCA or equivalent, plus 2 years’ experience in a Government Department, PSU or EPFO-registered organisation.

If you are a graduate looking for government jobs, the last three posts are open to you with the right computer certificate and experience. The required qualification must be completed on or before 5 July 2026.

Age Limit

All posts: 18 to below 40 years as on 5 July 2026.

There is no upper age limit for widows and divorced women. One interesting condition from the notification — candidates who consume gutkha or smoke are not eligible for appointment, and the more-than-two-children norm of the Rajasthan Government applies.

What is the Salary for BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026?

During the 2-year probation period, selected candidates receive a fixed monthly remuneration as per Rajasthan Government rules.

  • Manager: ₹39,300 per month (Level-14 after probation)
  • Secretary Grade-III: ₹26,500 per month (Level-11 after probation)
  • Care Taker: ₹18,500 per month (Level-8 after probation)
  • Junior Assistant (Typist): ₹14,600 per month (Level-5 after probation)

After successful completion of probation, you move to the regular Rajasthan Pay Matrix of your post — from Level-5 up to Level-14 — with all admissible allowances like DA and HRA.

The long-term value of these posts lies in the regular pay matrix, annual increments, pension benefits under the applicable scheme, and the stability of a Rajasthan Government undertaking.

What is the Selection Process for BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026?

Selection is based on a written competitive examination (objective type, may be online or offline), followed by a skill test for some posts.

Written Examination

  • Manager: Part-I (180 marks) + Part-II (180 marks) = 360 marks — no skill test
  • Secretary Grade-III: Part-I (180) + Part-II (180) + Efficiency/Skill Test (150) = 510 marks
  • Care Taker: Part-I (80) + Part-II (180) + Skill Test (100) = 360 marks
  • Junior Assistant (Typist): Part-I (180) + Part-II (180) + Skill Test (100) = 460 marks

Question papers may be bilingual (Hindi/English). The detailed syllabus is published in the annexures on the BIP website — you can also browse our Syllabus section for preparation guidance.

Skill Test & Final Selection

  1. Candidates scoring the minimum qualifying marks in the written exam are shortlisted for the skill test — a maximum of 5 times the vacancies per post
  2. PwBD candidates get a 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks
  3. A merit list is prepared from the total marks
  4. Provisionally selected candidates appear for document verification with originals
  5. Police verification and a medical fitness certificate complete the process before appointment

How to for BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026?

Keep these ready before you start: a valid email ID and mobile number, scanned photo and signature, left thumb impression, a handwritten declaration in English, and your category/domicile certificates.

  1. Visit the BIP recruitment page on the (direct link in Essential Links below)
  2. Click on the Recruitment 2026 — link
  3. Register with your basic details — you will receive a Provisional Registration Number and password by email/SMS
  4. Fill the application form carefully and upload your photo (with live photo capture), signature, thumb impression and declaration
  5. Pay the application fee online through Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, IMPS or Wallet
  6. Submit the form and download the e-receipt
  7. Take a printout of the final application for your records

Remember — if you are eligible for more than one post, you must apply separately with a separate fee for each post.

Important Dates — BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026

Application Fees — BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026

For Manager and Secretary Grade-III posts:

For Care Taker and Junior Assistant (Typist) posts:

Payment mode is online only. Rajasthan candidates of any category with family annual income below ₹2.50 lakh pay the SC/ST-rate fee (income certificate required at verification).

Essential Links — BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for BIP Rajasthan Recruitment 2026
  • Visit BIP Rajasthan

A government posting in Jaipur with pay up to Level-14 doesn’t come around often — if you meet the eligibility, don’t sit on this one. Complete your application well before 5 July 2026 and start preparing for the written exam today. For more Rajasthan government job alerts, keep visiting .

title: DRDO ISSA Paid Internship 2026: 25 Slots, ₹5,000 Stipend — B.Tech, M.Sc & M.Tech Apply by 20 June
description: DRDO ISSA Internship Scheme 2026-27: Stipend, Eligibility & How to Apply by Email Here is a golden chance for engineering and science students to work inside India’s premier defenc
jobDescription:
DRDO ISSA Internship Scheme 2026-27: Stipend, Eligibility & How to Apply by Email

Here is a golden chance for engineering and science students to work inside India’s premier defence research ecosystem. The Institute for Systems Studies & Analyses (ISSA) — a Delhi-based lab of the Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) — has announced its Paid Internship Scheme 2026-27 with 25 slots for final-year students. The internship runs for 6 months with a stipend of ₹5,000 per month, and you get to work on real defence research problems alongside DRDO scientists.

The notification was released on 8 June 2026 and the last date to apply by email is 20 June 2026, so you have very little time. The internship starts on 3 August 2026 at ISSA, Metcalfe House, Civil Lines, Delhi. We at have simplified the entire notification below — eligibility, stipend, documents and the exact email application process. Read carefully before you .

Key Highlights — DRDO ISSA Paid Internship 2026

ISSA is the DRDO lab that builds combat simulations, war-gaming models and systems-analysis tools for India’s defence establishment. For students of computer science and mathematics, an ISSA internship is a serious resume booster — it is paid, project-based and certificate-backed.

  • Scheme Name: Paid Internship Scheme (06 Months) 2026-27
  • Total Slots: 25
  • Stipend: ₹5,000 per month (₹30,000 total for 6 months)
  • Location: ISSA, Metcalfe House, Civil Lines, Delhi
  • Internship Start: 3 August 2026
  • Last Date to Apply (Email): 20 June 2026

Internship Slots — DRDO ISSA Paid Internship 2026

The 25 slots are combined across both discipline groups — the notification does not split them branch-wise. Selection is purely on academic merit, so a strong scorecard is your real ticket here.

Who is Eligible for DRDO ISSA Paid Internship 2026?

Educational Qualification

  • Final-year students only — currently in the final year of B.E. / B.Tech (Computer Science, Computer Engineering, IT and allied branches) or M.Sc. / M.Tech (Mathematics, Applied Mathematics, Operational Research, Statistics, or the CS/IT stream)
  • The course must be full-time with First Division from a recognized Indian university or institute
  • A good academic record with 75% or above marks, or CGPA 7.5+ on a 10-point scale
  • Only Indian nationals can apply; students who studied or are studying outside India are not eligible
  • The application must be routed through your college — a reference/request letter on college letterhead signed by your Principal or HOD (format given as Annexure ‘B’ in the notification) is mandatory

If you are an engineering student or a postgraduate student in maths or statistics, and your scores meet the bar, you tick every box.

Age Limit

The notification does not prescribe any age limit — eligibility is purely by final-year student status and academic merit.

What is the Stipend for DRDO ISSA Paid Internship 2026?

This is a paid internship, not a salaried job — but for a student, the package is genuinely useful.

  • Stipend: ₹5,000 per month
  • Total: ₹30,000 for the full 6 months
  • Payment: two installments of ₹15,000 each — first after completing 3 months, second after completing 6 months, credited directly to your bank account
  • Attendance: minimum 15 working days per month of physical presence at ISSA is mandatory

Along with ₹30,000 in stipend, you earn a DRDO internship certificate on successful completion — a credential that carries real weight in placements, GATE-based PSU interviews and research admissions.

Do note: there is no TA/DA for joining, and lodging, boarding and transport in Delhi are entirely your own responsibility. Plan your stay before you accept.

What is the Selection Process for DRDO ISSA Paid Internship 2026?

The process is refreshingly simple — no written exam, no interview.

  1. Email your complete application before the last date
  2. ISSA shortlists candidates purely on academic merit (75%+ marks or 7.5+ CGPA is the minimum bar; higher scores improve your chances)
  3. Selected candidates are informed by email in the 2nd/3rd week of July 2026
  4. At joining, you submit the original college referral letter, a police verification certificate, signed undertakings under the Indian Official Secrets Act 1923 and MoD IT Rules, your bank details, and originals of your marksheets, Aadhaar and college ID with 3 passport-size photographs

Rejected candidates are not contacted, so if you don’t hear back by late July, treat it as not selected this round.

How to Apply for DRDO ISSA Paid Internship 2026?

The application is by email only — no online portal, no hard copy. Keep scanned copies of these ready: filled application form (Annexure ‘A’ from the notification PDF), college reference letter (Annexure ‘B’), 10th and 12th marksheets, all semester marksheets, character certificate, brief bio-data, Aadhaar card and college ID card.

  1. Download the notification PDF and print the application form given as Annexure ‘A’ (link in Essential Links below)
  2. Fill the form and get the Annexure ‘B’ reference letter signed by your Principal or HOD on college letterhead
  3. Scan the filled form, reference letter and all documents into ONE single PDF
  4. Name the PDF as your name in small letters plus birth year — for example, rahulkumar2004.pdf
  5. Write the email subject as “Application for paid internship with [your branch/discipline]”
  6. Email the single PDF to hrd.issa@gov.in before 20 June 2026

Emails received after 20 June 2026 will not be considered — do not wait for the last day.

What are the Important Dates for DRDO ISSA Paid Internship 2026?

Application Fees — DRDO ISSA Paid Internship 2026

There is no application fee for this internship scheme — one more reason for every eligible final-year student to .

Essential Links — DRDO ISSA Paid Internship 2026

  • Download & Application Form PDF
  • Visit DRDO ISSA Internship Page on

A DRDO lab internship with stipend, certificate and real research exposure — for a final-year student, this is about as good as it gets. If your marks meet the 75% bar, get your college letter signed this week and email your application before 20 June 2026. For more internships and government job updates, keep visiting .

title: BFUHS Sub Divisional Engineer Recruitment 2026: 6 Punjab SDE Posts, Pay ₹47,600
description: BFUHS SDE Vacancy 2026: Engineering Degree Jobs in Punjab — Eligibility, Selection & Full Details Engineering graduates in Punjab, here is a Group-A opportunity you should not scro
jobDescription:
BFUHS SDE Vacancy 2026: Engineering Degree Jobs in Punjab — Eligibility, Selection & Full Details

Engineering graduates in Punjab, here is a Group-A opportunity you should not scroll past. Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS), Faridkot has released Advertisement No. BFU-26/18 for 6 posts of Sub-Divisional Engineer (SDE) across Civil, Mechanical, Electrical and Public Health disciplines. The post carries Pay Level-8 with initial pay of ₹47,600 per month — a respectable start for a government engineering job in a reputed state university.

Online applications are open from 8 June 2026 to 28 June 2026, and a hard copy of the application with documents must also reach the university by the same date. If you hold an engineering degree in the relevant discipline and pass the simple written test, you are in the race. We at have broken down the complete notification below — read every section before applying.

Key Highlights — BFUHS Sub Divisional Engineer Recruitment 2026

Baba Farid University of Health Sciences, Faridkot is Punjab’s premier medical university, affiliating the state’s medical, dental, nursing and ayurveda colleges. Its engineering wing maintains the university’s campuses and hospital infrastructure — that is where these SDE posts fit in.

  • Post Name: Sub-Divisional Engineer (Civil / Mechanical / Electrical / Public Health)
  • Total Vacancies: 6
  • Pay: Pay Level-8, initial pay ₹47,600 per month
  • Group: A
  • Job Location: Faridkot, Punjab
  • Application Window: 8 June to 28 June 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — BFUHS SDE Recruitment 2026

SC (M/B) means the Mazhabi/Balmiki Scheduled Caste sub-category of Punjab, and both these seats are reserved for women candidates. Reservation benefits apply to Punjab-domicile candidates as per state rules.

What is the Eligibility for BFUHS SDE Recruitment 2026?

Educational Qualification

  • SDE (Civil): Degree in Civil Engineering from a recognized University or Institution
  • SDE (Mechanical): Degree in Mechanical Engineering from a recognized University or Institution
  • SDE (Electrical): Degree in Electrical Engineering from a recognized University or Institution
  • SDE (Public Health): Degree in Civil Engineering from a recognized University or Institution (yes — the notification prescribes a Civil degree for the Public Health post)

Two conditions apply to every post: you must have passed Matriculation with Punjabi as a compulsory or elective subject (or an equivalent Punjabi-language examination), and degrees from non-recognized institutions or through distance learning are not accepted. Qualifications follow the Punjab Public Service Commission’s direct recruitment norms.

Experience

Experience is not an eligibility condition — but it earns extra marks in the merit list, so attach your experience certificates if you have relevant work history.

Age Limit

18 to 37 years as on 1 January 2026.

What is the Salary for BFUHS Sub Divisional Engineer 2026?

The Sub-Divisional Engineer is a Group-A post on the Punjab pay matrix.

  • Pay Level: Level-8
  • Initial Pay: ₹47,600 per month
  • Emoluments: as per Punjab Government Finance Department rules applicable to new recruits during the initial appointment period

Your starting pay is ₹47,600 per month, and with annual increments plus the allowances admissible under Punjab Government rules, the package grows steadily over your career.

Beyond pay, a university SDE post offers stable postings in Faridkot, regular working hours compared to field engineering jobs, and the professional satisfaction of maintaining hospital and academic infrastructure.

What is the Selection Process for BFUHS SDE Recruitment 2026?

The selection is refreshingly compact — one written test plus merit ranking.

Written Test

A written test will be conducted, and scoring at least 33% marks is mandatory — candidates below 33% are declared ineligible regardless of vacancies.

Merit List & Verification

  1. The merit list is prepared from your written test marks plus experience marks, subject to Punjab Government reservation rules
  2. If two candidates score equal marks, the elder candidate is placed higher
  3. Shortlisted candidates submit the hard copy of the online application with self-attested certificates — DOB proof, marksheets, degree, experience, residence and category certificates, plus the fee receipt — to the Registrar’s office by 28 June 2026, 5:00 PM
  4. Police verification and clearance of any pending criminal proceedings are required before appointment

Keep checking our Results section for the merit list once the test is done.

How to for BFUHS SDE Recruitment 2026?

Keep ready: scanned photo and signature, your degree and matriculation certificates, category/residence certificates, and a working email ID and phone number.

  1. Visit the BFUHS website and open the recruitment section (direct link in Essential Links below)
  2. Click the Sub-Divisional Engineer (Advt. BFU-26/18) application link
  3. Register and fill the online application form carefully
  4. Upload your photograph and signature in the prescribed format
  5. Pay the application fee online and save the fee receipt
  6. Submit the form and take a printout
  7. Send the hard copy with self-attested documents and fee receipt to: Office of the Registrar, Baba Farid University of Health Sciences, Sadiq Road, Faridkot — to reach by 28 June 2026, 5:00 PM

The hard copy step is mandatory — an online submission alone will not be counted. If applying for more than one discipline, submit a separate application and fee for each.

What are the Important Dates for BFUHS SDE Recruitment 2026?

Application Fees — BFUHS SDE Recruitment 2026

The notification directs candidates to the University website for the exact fee amount and category-wise breakdown — check the fee page carefully while filling your form, and keep the payment receipt safe because it goes with your hard copy.

Essential Links — BFUHS SDE Recruitment 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for BFUHS SDE Recruitment 2026
  • Visit BFUHS

Six posts may look small, but a Group-A engineering post at ₹47,600 with a simple one-test selection is a genuinely winnable opportunity. , post your hard copy early, and give the written test your best shot — the last date for both is 28 June 2026. For more Punjab government jobs, keep visiting .

title: GUVNL Vidyut Sahayak (JE Electrical) Recruitment 2026 – 183 Posts, Salary up to ₹50,700
description: GUVNL Announces 183 Vidyut Sahayak (Junior Engineer – Electrical) Posts Great news for electrical engineering graduates of Gujarat! Under GUVNL (Gujarat Urja Vikas Nigam Limited
jobDescription:
GUVNL Announces 183 Vidyut Sahayak (Junior Engineer – Electrical) Posts

Great news for electrical engineering graduates of Gujarat! Under GUVNL (Gujarat Urja Vikas Nigam Limited), the Madhya Gujarat Vij Company Limited (MGVCL) has invited online applications for the post of Vidyut Sahayak (Junior Engineer – Electrical) on behalf of all its power distribution companies — MGVCL, DGVCL, UGVCL, PGVCL and GETCO. A total of 183 posts are to be filled, offering a stable and rewarding career to young and dynamic engineers. This is one of the most awaited government jobs in Gujarat for B.E./B.Tech Electrical candidates this year.

The fixed remuneration is ₹48,100 in the first year and ₹50,700 in the second year, with a clear path to a regular Junior Engineer post afterwards. The online application opened on 12 June 2026 and the last date to apply is 02 July 2026 (up to 6:00 PM). Selection is through a two-tier online exam (CBT). In this article, we explain everything — vacancies, eligibility, age, fees, exam pattern and how to apply, all in very easy language.

Key Highlights — GUVNL Vidyut Sahayak (JE Electrical) 2026

GUVNL and its companies are spread across the whole of Gujarat and form the backbone of the state’s power sector. A job here means good pay, technical growth and job security. Here are the key points:

  • Recruiting Body: GUVNL through MGVCL (for MGVCL, DGVCL, UGVCL, PGVCL & GETCO)
  • Post Name: Vidyut Sahayak (Junior Engineer – Electrical)
  • Total Vacancies: 183
  • Qualification: B.E. / B.Tech. in Electrical / Electrical & Electronics
  • Fixed Remuneration: ₹48,100 (1st year) and ₹50,700 (2nd year)
  • Last Date to Apply: 02 July 2026

All engineering government jobs like this are regularly updated on so bookmark us.

Vacancy Breakdown — Vidyut Sahayak (JE Electrical) 2026

The 183 vacancies are spread across the five GUVNL companies. While applying, you must indicate your company preference in order of choice. The company-wise vacancy position is given below.

Eligibility Criteria — GUVNL Vidyut Sahayak (JE Electrical) 2026

This section is very important. You must meet both the qualification and age conditions exactly as stated.

Educational Qualification

The candidate must hold a degree through regular mode in B.E. (Electrical) / B.Tech. (Electrical) / B.E. Electrical and Electronics (EEE), without ATKT in the last year or last two semesters, from a University approved by UGC/AICTE. A minimum average of 55% marks in the last year/last two semesters is required. The candidate should also have knowledge of computer operations and a good command of English and Gujarati. This is a focused recruitment for electrical engineering graduates.

Age Limit

The age is counted as on the date of advertisement, 10 June 2026:

  • Unreserved category: maximum 35 years
  • Reserved category (including EWS): maximum 40 years

Relaxation: Female candidates +5 years, Persons with Disability +10 years, Ex-armed forces +10 years, and dependents of retired GUVNL employees up to 40 years. The maximum age, after all relaxation, shall not cross 45 years.

Salary and Pay — What a Vidyut Sahayak Earns

The pay structure is clear and attractive. As a Vidyut Sahayak (Junior Engineer – Electrical), you are appointed for a fixed period of two years with the following fixed remuneration:

  • First Year: ₹48,100 per month
  • Second Year: ₹50,700 per month

After completing two years satisfactorily as Vidyut Sahayak, you may be considered for appointment to the regular post of Junior Engineer – Electrical in the pay scale of ₹45,400 – ₹1,01,200. This makes the role a solid long-term career, not just a short contract.

Selection Process — GUVNL Vidyut Sahayak Exam Pattern 2026

The selection is based on a two-tier online Computer Based Test (CBT). Here is the simple flow:

  1. First Tier Exam (100 marks): covers Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, English, Gujarati and General Knowledge. Candidates scoring 50+ (Unreserved) or 45+ (Reserved/EWS/PwD) qualify for the next stage.
  2. Second Tier Exam (100 marks): fully based on Electrical Engineering (technical subject).
  3. Final ranking is decided on the basis of the marks obtained in the Second Tier exam, followed by document verification and a pre-employment medical examination.

Since the technical Second Tier decides your final rank, strong Electrical Engineering preparation is the key. You can use the exam syllabus and preparation material on to plan your studies.

How to for Vidyut Sahayak (JE Electrical) 2026

The application is online only through the MGVCL website. Follow these steps:

  1. Visit the official MGVCL recruitment portal (link in Essential Links below).
  2. Register and generate your application number by filling the online form step by step.
  3. Indicate your company preference (Choice No. 1, 2, 3…) for MGVCL, DGVCL, UGVCL, PGVCL and GETCO.
  4. Upload your photograph and signature, and fill all details correctly.
  5. Pay the application fee online through credit card, debit card or net banking.
  6. Submit the form and keep a printout for future reference.

Documents Required for Verification

You do not upload certificates at the application stage, but keep these ready for later verification:

  • B.E./B.Tech (Electrical/EEE) degree and all semester mark-sheets
  • Equivalence certificate, if your degree is equivalent (as per UGC/AICTE list)
  • Gujarat domicile certificate (for the 85% local quota)
  • Category certificate (SC, ST, SEBC or EWS) and Disability certificate, if applicable
  • No Objection Certificate, if already in government/PSU service

Important Dates — GUVNL Vidyut Sahayak Recruitment 2026

Application Fees — Vidyut Sahayak (JE Electrical) 2026

The fee is non-refundable and must be paid online only. Bank charges are borne by the candidate.

Essential Links — GUVNL Vidyut Sahayak (JE Electrical) 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for Vidyut Sahayak (JE Electrical) 2026
  • Visit MGVCL

With 183 posts, a clear salary structure and a path to a permanent Junior Engineer role, this is a fantastic chance for every electrical engineer in Gujarat. Prepare well for the two-tier exam, before 02 July 2026, and give it your best shot. For more PSU government jobs and engineering recruitment updates, keep visiting . Your power-sector career starts here.

title: TNPSC Group 4 2025: Hall Ticket Released — Download Now Recruitment 2026
description: TNPSC Group 4 2025: Hall Ticket Released — Download Now Great news! TNPSC has released the Hall Ticket / Admit Card for the Group 4 exam. If you have applied for TNPSC Group 4, you
jobDescription:
TNPSC Group 4 2025: Hall Ticket Released — Download Now

Great news! TNPSC has released the Hall Ticket / Admit Card for the Group 4 exam. If you have applied for TNPSC Group 4, you can now download your hall ticket from the official portal. Click the link below to download your admit card directly. Download TNPSC Group 4 Hall Ticket

Introduction: TNPSC Group 4 Recruitment 2025 Overview

The Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission (TNPSC) has officially released the much-awaited Combined Civil Services Examination – IV (Group IV Services) Notification 2025 (Notification No. 07/2025, Advertisement No. 709). This recruitment drive aims to fill a massive 3935 vacancies across various departments and organizations within the Tamil Nadu government. Positions include Village Administrative Officer (VAO), Junior Assistant, Typist, Steno-Typist, and various Forest Department roles. Interested and eligible candidates are invited to through the official TNPSC portal before the deadline on May 24, 2025.

Stay updated on Government Job Alerts:

  • WhatsApp Channel:
  • Telegram Channel (All India):
  • Telegram Channel (Tamil Nadu):

TNPSC Group 4 2025: Organization Details

  • Hiring Body: Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission (TNPSC)
  • Examination Name: Combined Civil Services Examination – IV (Group IV Services)
  • Total Number of Posts: 3935
  • Job Location: Tamil Nadu

TNPSC Group 4 Vacancy Breakdown 2025

A total of 3935 vacancies are announced under the TNPSC Group 4 Notification The distribution across various posts is detailed below:

TNPSC Group 4 Eligibility Criteria 2025

Candidates must ensure they meet all eligibility conditions before applying. Eligibility is determined as of the notification date (25.04.2025) for qualifications and 01.07.2025 for age.

Educational Qualification (as on 25.04.2025)

  • Minimum General Educational Qualification: Most posts require a pass in the Secondary School Leaving Certificate (SSLC) Examination (10th Standard) or its equivalent.
  • Higher Secondary: 12th Pass / Higher Secondary Required for Forest Guard posts (with specific subjects like Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Zoology, or Botany).
  • Degree: Required for posts like Junior Executive (Office), Junior Assistant cum Typist (Labour Studies), Typist (CMDA), Steno Typist (Grade III – Various Depts.), Personal Clerk, Assistant (TNPCB). The degree should follow the 10+2+3 pattern.
  • Technical Qualifications:
    • Typist Posts: Require specific Government Technical Examination passes in Typewriting (Higher/Senior Grade and Lower/Junior Grade in Tamil and English, specific combinations vary by post).
    • Steno-Typist Posts: Require specific Government Technical Examination passes in both Typewriting and Shorthand (Higher/Senior Grade and Lower/Junior Grade in Tamil and English, specific combinations vary by post).
    • Computer on Office Automation: A pass in the Certificate Course conducted by the Directorate of Technical Education is required for several posts (Typist, Steno-Typist, Junior Executive, Assistant). Candidates with specific Computer Science/IT related Degrees/Diplomas are exempted. For some posts, this can be acquired within the probation period.
  • Co-operative Training: Required for Junior Executive (Office) post. Specific exemptions apply for certain degree holders.
  • Driving Licence & Experience: Required for Forest Guard with Driving Licence post (valid licence, 3 years experience in LMV/HMV, basic automobile mechanism knowledge, first aid certificate).
  • Knowledge of Tamil: Candidates must possess adequate knowledge of Tamil. This is generally met by passing SSLC/HSC/Degree with Tamil as a language or studying in Tamil medium.

Age Limit (as on 01.07.2025)

  • Minimum Age: 18 years for most posts; 21 years for VAO and Forest Watcher posts.
  • Maximum Age (‘Others’ Category):
    • VAO: 32 years
    • Forest Guard/Watcher Posts: 32 years
    • Other Posts (Junior Assistant, Typist, Steno, etc.): 32 years
  • Maximum Age (SC/SC(A)/ST/MBC/DC/BC/BCM Categories):
    • VAO: 42 years
    • Forest Guard/Watcher Posts: 37 years
    • Other Posts: 34 years (BC/BCM/MBC/DC), 37 years (SC/SC(A)/ST).
    • No Maximum Age Limit: For SC/SC(A)/ST/MBC/DC/BC/BCM candidates possessing a qualification higher than the minimum required for posts other than VAO and Forest roles. ‘No maximum age limit’ means the candidate should not have completed 60 years.
  • Age Concessions:
    • Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD): 10 years concession over the maximum age limit. (Not applicable for Forest Guard/Watcher posts where PwBD eligibility is limited).
    • Ex-Servicemen: Maximum age is 50 years (‘Others’) or 55 years (Reserved Categories). For Forest posts, the period of service is deducted, and the resulting age should not exceed 32 (‘Others’) or 37 (Reserved). This concession doesn’t apply if already recruited into any government service.
    • Destitute Widow: Maximum age is 37 years (‘Others’) or respective category limit (42/37 years) for reserved categories.

Physical & Medical Standards

  • General Posts: Require a certificate of physical fitness (Standard III vision or better) at the time of joining.
  • Forest Guard/Watcher Posts: Minimum physical standards are mandatory:
    • Male: Height 163cm (152cm ST), Chest 79cm (normal) with 5cm expansion.
    • Female/Third Gender: Height 150cm (145cm ST), Chest 74cm (normal) with 5cm expansion.
    • A certificate from a government medical officer is required.
    • Forest Guard with Driving Licence: Specific vision standards (6/6 distance, 0.5 near vision without glasses, full field of vision) are required. Colour blindness, squint, or Lasik surgery are disqualifications. An Eye Fitness Certificate is needed.

Important Dates for TNPSC Group 4 Exam 2025

Keep track of these crucial dates:

TNPSC Group 4 Salary & Benefits (Pay Scale)

Selected candidates will be placed in different pay levels as per the Tamil Nadu government norms. The pay levels range from Level 3 to Level 10, associated with either the Contributory Pension Scheme (CPS) or Employees’ Provident Fund (EPF) depending on the post and organization.

  • Level 10: Steno Typist (Grade III)
  • Level 8: VAO, Junior Assistant, Junior Revenue Inspector, Junior Executive, Typist, Personal Clerk, Assistant
  • Level 6: Field Assistant
  • Level 5: Forest Guard
  • Level 3: Forest Watcher

(Allowances and other benefits will be applicable as per government rules.)

TNPSC Group 4 Selection Process 2025

The selection process involves the following stages:

  1. Written Examination: A single objective-type (OMR-based) examination consisting of three parts.
    • Part A: Tamil Eligibility-cum-Scoring Test (SSLC Standard, 100 questions, 150 marks). Minimum 40% (60 marks) required to evaluate Part B & C. (Differently Abled candidates can opt for General English instead).
    • Part B: General Studies (SSLC Standard, 75 questions).
    • Part C: Aptitude and Mental Ability Test (SSLC Standard, 25 questions).
    • Total: 200 questions, 300 marks, 3 hours duration. Minimum 90 marks required for selection consideration.
  2. Ranking: A merit list is prepared based on total marks in the written exam (Parts A+B+C). Tie-breaking rules involving qualifications and age .
  3. Onscreen Certificate Verification: Shortlisted candidates (based on marks and reservation) upload documents for online verification. Ratios vary by post group (e.g., 1:3/1:2 for VAO/JA/Typist/Steno, 1:6 for Forest posts).
  4. Physical Standards & Endurance Test (for Forest Posts Only): Candidates opting for and eligible after onscreen verification for Forest Guard/Watcher posts undergo physical standard measurement and an endurance test (walk of 25 km for males, 16 km for females/third gender in 4 hours) conducted in Chennai.
  5. Physical Certificate Verification: Candidates are called based on ranks and reservation rules for verification of original documents. Ratios vary (e.g., 1:3/1:1.5 for VAO/JA/Steno, 1:3/1:2 for Typist/Watcher, 1:1.2/1:1.1 for Forest Guard).
  6. Counselling: Eligible candidates attend counselling based on their rank for final post and department allotment. Preference orders apply for Typist and Steno-Typist selections based on specific higher/lower grade qualifications in Tamil/English typing/shorthand.

How to Apply for TNPSC Group 4 Recruitment 2025

Follow these steps to :

  1. One Time Registration (OTR): If not already registered, candidates must first complete the OTR process on the TNPSC portal. The OTR fee is Rs. 150 and is valid for 5 years. Keep scanned copies of your recent photograph and signature ready as per specifications. Linking Aadhaar is mandatory.
  2. Login & Application: Log in using your OTR credentials on tnpscexams.in. Click “Apply” against the Group 4 notification.
  3. Fill Details: Verify OTR details and fill in the additional information required for the Group 4 application. Select post preferences carefully (e.g., All Posts, Posts other than Forest roles). Choose preferred examination centres (two options, one for PwBD).
  4. Upload Documents: Upload a recent photograph (taken on or after notification date, with name and date printed) and signature in the specified format and size. Other documents need to be uploaded later when called for.
  5. Fee Payment: Pay the examination fee of Rs. 100 online (Net Banking/Card/UPI) unless claiming exemption. Check eligibility for fee concession (SC/ST, PwBD, DW, Ex-Servicemen, BC/MBC) before claiming.
  6. Preview & Submit: Preview the application carefully before final submission. Submit the application before the deadline (24.05.2025). Note down the Application ID generated.
  7. Correction Window: Use the Application Correction Window (29.05.2025 – 31.05.2025) if any edits are needed after submission.

TNPSC Group 4 Application Fees 2025

  • One Time Registration Fee: Rs. 150 (Paid once, valid for 5 years)
  • Examination Fee: Rs. 100
  • Fee Exemption:
    • Scheduled Castes / Scheduled Castes (Arunthathiyars): Full Exemption
    • Scheduled Tribes: Full Exemption
    • Persons with Benchmark Disability: Full Exemption
    • Destitute Widows: Full Exemption
    • Most Backward Classes / Denotified Communities: 3 Free Chances
    • Backward Classes (Other than Muslim) / Backward Classes (Muslim): 3 Free Chances
    • Ex-Servicemen: 2 Free Chances

Note: Candidates must track their availed free chances. Claiming exemption counts as one chance used.

& Apply Link
