  • Online Application Portal:

title: Punjab & Haryana High Court Safai Sewak & Mali Recruitment 2026 – 167 Posts
description: A Big Door Opens at Punjab & Haryana High Court for Safai Sewak & Mali Posts Good news for job seekers who are looking for a stable government job with very simple eligibility. The
jobDescription:
A Big Door Opens at Punjab & Haryana High Court for Safai Sewak & Mali Posts

Good news for job seekers who are looking for a stable government job with very simple eligibility. The High Court of Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh has released Employment Notice No. 03/SS/ML/HC/2026 to fill 167 posts — 75 posts of Safai Sewak and 92 posts of Mali. These are regular jobs in the High Court establishment, and the best part is that no degree is needed. If you can read and write Hindi and the regional language, you are eligible to try. This is one of the most accessible government jobs in Punjab right now.

The online application started on 09 June 2026 and the last date to apply is 30 June 2026 (11:59 PM). Selection is done through an interview only — there is no written exam — so even first-time applicants have a fair chance. In this article on we will explain everything in very easy language: vacancies, age, fees, how to apply, and important dates. Read it fully so you do not miss any step.

Key Highlights — Punjab & Haryana High Court Safai Sewak & Mali 2026

The High Court of Punjab and Haryana is the common High Court for the states of Punjab, Haryana and the Union Territory of Chandigarh. Working here means a respected government job with job security and regular pay. Here are the quick highlights every applicant should note before applying:

  • Recruiting Body: High Court of Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh
  • Post Names: Safai Sewak and Mali
  • Total Vacancies: 167 (Safai Sewak – 75, Mali – 92)
  • Minimum Qualification: Knowledge of Hindi and the regional language (no degree required)
  • Job Location: Chandigarh
  • Last Date to Apply: 30 June 2026

If your education is limited, do not worry — these posts are perfect for candidates searching for 10th pass and below government jobs.

Vacancy Breakdown — Safai Sewak & Mali Posts 2026

The 167 vacancies are divided category-wise as shown below. The number of posts may slightly increase or decrease at the time of preparing the merit list, so apply for the category that truly applies to you.

Eligibility Criteria — Punjab & Haryana High Court Safai Sewak & Mali 2026

This is the section most candidates worry about, but here the rules are very simple. Read both parts carefully.

Educational Qualification

The essential qualification is only knowledge of Hindi and the regional language. No degree, diploma or marks percentage is demanded. However, preference will be given to candidates with extra skill:

  • For Mali posts: candidates having a diploma related to gardening from a recognised institute and/or three years of experience as a gardener.
  • For Safai Sewak posts: candidates having three years of experience as a Safai Sewak (sweeper).

If you want this preference benefit, you must already have the diploma or experience on or before the last date of application.

Age Limit

The age of the candidate should be not less than 18 years and not more than 35 years as on 30 June 2026. Upper age relaxation is given as per rules — 10 years for Persons with Disability, and for Ex-Servicemen, relaxation equal to their defence service plus 3 years. These are basic-eligibility posts, so candidates from every background searching for latest government job notifications should consider applying.

Salary and Pay — What Selected Candidates Will Earn

A very common question is about the salary. For these posts, the pay and allowances will be governed by the latest rules of the Government of Punjab that are adopted by the High Court of Punjab and Haryana for its employees. This means you get a fixed monthly basic pay along with the applicable allowances as decided by the government from time to time.

While the notice does not print an exact figure, the real value of this job is stability and government benefits — regular salary, defined duty structure, and the prestige of working in a High Court. Do remember that the duty hours may extend beyond normal working hours and even to odd hours, as clearly mentioned in the notice. So apply only if you are ready for this kind of duty.

Selection Process — How Candidates Will Be Chosen

The selection process for Safai Sewak and Mali is straightforward and is based on an interview. There is no written examination. Because a large number of applications are expected, the High Court may apply a suitable shortlisting criteria, which will be informed to candidates at the right time.

Candidates claiming the benefit of preference (gardening diploma/experience for Mali, or sweeper experience for Safai Sewak) must produce the required documents at the time of testimonial checking. Provisional eligibility does not guarantee final selection — your documents will be verified at every stage. You can keep track of admit card and interview updates for all such recruitments on

How to for Safai Sewak & Mali 2026

The application is fully online. Follow these steps carefully and apply well before the last date to avoid website rush:

  1. Visit the official application portal (link given in Essential Links below).
  2. Register using your active email ID and mobile number to generate your login.
  3. Fill in your personal, category and qualification details exactly as per your certificates.
  4. Upload your recent passport-size photograph (not older than 30 days) and signature.
  5. Pay the application fee online through net banking, debit card or credit card.
  6. Submit the form and download the printout for your records.

If you wish to apply for both posts, you must submit a separate application for each post. Once submitted, category and details cannot be changed, so check everything before final submission.

Documents Required for Application

Keep scanned copies ready before you start filling the form. You will need them during the form-filling stage and later at testimonial checking:

  • Proof of date of birth (Matriculation certificate or Birth certificate)
  • Caste/Category certificate (SC/ST/BC) issued by Punjab, Haryana or UT Chandigarh, if applicable
  • Disability certificate (for PwD candidates, not less than 40% disability)
  • Ex-Servicemen discharge book, if applicable
  • Gardening diploma or experience proof, if claiming preference
  • Recent passport-size photograph and signature

Important Dates — Punjab & Haryana High Court Recruitment 2026

Application Fees — Safai Sewak & Mali 2026

The application fee is to be paid online and is non-refundable. Online transaction charges will be extra.

Note: Dependents of Ex-Servicemen who were disabled or died during active operation are exempted from the fee, subject to uploading proof.

Essential Links — Punjab & Haryana High Court Safai Sewak & Mali 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for Safai Sewak & Mali 2026
  • Visit Punjab & Haryana High Court

This is a wonderful chance to get a stable High Court job without any degree, so do not let it slip away. Prepare your documents, apply before 30 June 2026, and give your interview with confidence. For more Haryana government job notifications and the latest updates, keep visiting every day. We wish you the very best — your government job journey can start right here.

title: DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – 20 Posts in Delhi, Stipend up to ₹12,300
description: DRDO DESIDOC Delhi Invites Applications for 20 Apprentice Posts If you want to begin your career with DRDO , here is a great paid-apprenticeship opportunity in the heart of Delhi
jobDescription:
DRDO DESIDOC Delhi Invites Applications for 20 Apprentice Posts

If you want to begin your career with DRDO, here is a great paid-apprenticeship opportunity in the heart of Delhi. The Defence Scientific Information & Documentation Centre (DESIDOC), working under the Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO), has invited applications for the engagement of Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961, for a one-year training in the Financial Year 2026-A total of 20 apprentice posts are available across three disciplines, with a monthly stipend of up to ₹12,300.

This is a wonderful start for fresh graduates, diploma holders and ITI candidates who passed out in 2024 or The training takes place at Metcalfe House, Delhi, and the application is offline (by post or by hand). If you are looking for government jobs in Delhi, this is a strong option. In this guide, we explain the vacancies, eligibility, stipend, last date and the exact apply process in very simple words.

Key Highlights — DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice 2026

DESIDOC is the information and documentation centre of DRDO. An apprenticeship here gives you real exposure to a premier defence organisation and a recognised certificate. Here are the quick highlights:

  • Recruiting Body: DESIDOC, under DRDO, Delhi
  • Post: Apprentice (3 disciplines)
  • Total Vacancies: 20
  • Monthly Stipend: ₹10,900 to ₹12,300
  • Training Period: One year
  • Application Mode: Offline (by post / by hand)

Diploma holders should also check more diploma government jobs listed on

Vacancy Breakdown — DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice 2026

The 20 vacancies are divided across three disciplines. The number of seats may increase or decrease depending on the requirement of the organisation.

Eligibility Criteria — DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice 2026

Read your discipline’s requirement carefully. Only candidates who passed in 2024 and 2025 are eligible, and post-graduate degree holders are not allowed to .

Educational Qualification

  • Library & Information Science: Graduation plus a one-year Degree in Library & Information Science from a recognised University/Institute.
  • Computer Science: Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Computer Science or a three-year Diploma in Computer Science from a recognised University/Institute.
  • Photography / Videography: A three-year Diploma in Photography or Videography, or an ITI certificate from a recognised Institute.

Candidates from a computer science and IT background have direct openings here. Also, candidates who have already done a one-year or longer apprenticeship or job after qualifying are not eligible.

Age Limit

The maximum age is 28 years as on the date of the written test/interview. Age relaxation of 5 years for SC/ST and 3 years for OBC candidates is allowed as per rules. Make sure you meet the age condition before applying.

Stipend and Salary — DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice 2026

The apprenticeship is paid, and the monthly stipend is fixed by qualification level: ₹12,300 per month for B.E./Graduation level candidates, and ₹10,900 per month for Diploma/ITI level candidates. The stipend is paid throughout the one-year training period.

Remember that this is a training engagement, not a permanent job. Completing the apprenticeship at DESIDOC/DRDO does not give any right to employment in DRDO afterwards. The true benefit is the valuable work experience and the apprenticeship certificate you earn from a top defence organisation.

Selection Process — How Candidates Will Be Chosen

The selection process is based on merit and a possible test/interview. Here is the simple flow:

  1. A Selection Board at DESIDOC shortlists candidates on the basis of merit (percentage/marks in the essential qualification).
  2. Shortlisted candidates are called for a written test and/or interview.
  3. Shortlisted candidates must bring all original certificates at the time of document verification/interview.
  4. Only selected candidates are informed through an offer letter and/or email.

All communication is done through email only, so give a correct and active email ID. Keep checking for more latest government job notifications like this.

How to Apply for DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice 2026

The application is offline. Carefully follow these steps:

  1. First, register on the NATS portal (link in Essential Links below) — this is mandatory before applying.
  2. Download the application form from the DRDO website (in the “What’s New” section).
  3. Fill the form completely and attach a recent passport-size photograph and xerox copies of all relevant documents.
  4. Send the duly filled application by post or by hand to the Director, DESIDOC, Metcalfe House, Civil Lines, Delhi – 110054.
  5. Write the name of the discipline you applied for on the envelope.

Documents Required with the Application

Keep self-attested copies of these documents ready:

  • NATS registration/enrolment proof
  • Class X and XII mark-sheets and certificates
  • Graduation / Diploma / ITI mark-sheets and certificates (passing year 2024 or 2025)
  • Caste/Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC) in the prescribed Central Government format, if applicable
  • Recent passport-size photograph

Important Dates — DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice 2026

The application window is now confirmed. The notification opened on 10 June 2026, and the last date to apply is 01 July 2026. Send your application by post or by hand so that it reaches DESIDOC before the closing date — do not wait for the last day.

Application Fees — DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice 2026

There is no application fee for this apprenticeship. Candidates only need to register on the NATS portal and send their application by post or by hand. This keeps the opportunity open to every eligible candidate free of cost.

Essential Links — DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice 2026

  • Download PDF
  • Register on NATS Portal for Apprenticeship
  • Visit DRDO

An apprenticeship at DRDO DESIDOC is a strong and proud beginning to your professional life. Register on NATS, prepare your documents, and send your application early — do not wait for the last day. For more latest government job notifications and updates, keep visiting every day. Your defence-sector career can start right here.

title: Coal India Management Trainee Recruitment 2026: 660 MT Vacancies for Civil, Electrical, Mechanical & 6 Other Disciplines — (Last Date Extended to 21 June 2026
description: Coal India MT Recruitment 2026: Last Date Extended to 21 June 2026 — Latest Update Good news for applicants! Coal India Limited (CIL) has extended the last date to for
jobDescription:
Coal India MT Recruitment 2026: Last Date Extended to 21 June 2026 — Latest Update

Good news for applicants! Coal India Limited (CIL) has extended the last date to for the Management Trainee (MT) recruitment under Advertisement No. 03/The online application window is now open until 11 June 2026 21 June 2026 (6:00 PM). All other conditions, eligibility and the selection process remain unchanged. If you have not applied yet, complete your application now — Download the official Last Date Extension Notice (PDF).

Coal India MT 2026 — Maharatna PSU Recruitment for 9 Engineering and Specialist Disciplines

Coal India Limited (CIL), a Schedule “A” Maharatna PSU under the Ministry of Coal, has released the Management Trainee Recruitment 2026 notification (Advertisement No. 03/2026) for 660 vacancies across nine non-Mining disciplines — Civil, Electrical, Mechanical, System, E&T (Electronics & Telecommunication), Geology, Industrial Engineering, Rajbhasha (Hindi) and Company Secretary. The starting basic pay is ₹60,000 per month in the E-1 grade, with the pay scale extending up to ₹1,80,000. Selection is through a 200-mark Computer Based Test — there is no interview.

The online application window opens on 12 May 2026 (10:00 AM) and closes on 11 June 2026 21 June 2026 (6:00 PM, Extended). If you are an Engineering, Geology, Hindi or Company Secretary professional looking for a long-term PSU government job, this is one of 2026’s most attractive opportunities. Read this complete CIL MT 2026 guide on for vacancy split, eligibility, fee, salary, exam pattern and step-by-step instructions.

Key Highlights — Coal India MT Recruitment 2026

Coal India Limited is the world’s largest single coal-producing company, with eight mining subsidiaries (ECL, BCCL, CCL, NCL, WCL, SECL, MCL, NEC) and a planning arm (CMPDIL) — a brand that needs no introduction in PSU careers.

  • Recruiting Body: Coal India Limited (CIL) — Maharatna PSU
  • Advertisement Number: 03/2026
  • Total Vacancies: 660 (647 fresh + 13 backlog)
  • Disciplines: Civil, Electrical, Mechanical, System, E&T, Geology, Industrial Engineering, Rajbhasha, Company Secretary
  • Grade: E-1 (Management Trainee → Officer after 1-year training)
  • Pay Scale: ₹60,000 – ₹1,80,000 per month
  • Job Location: Anywhere in India (CIL and subsidiaries)
  • Application Window: 12 May 2026 to 11 June 2026 21 June 2026 (Extended)
  • Selection Mode: Computer Based Online Test (CBT) → DV → IME (no interview)

This notification is separate from the Coal India MT Mining recruitment, which is conducted via GATE-2025 score. For Mining engineers, please refer to the dedicated Mining MT advertisement.

Vacancy Breakdown — Coal India MT 2026 (Discipline-wise)

The 660 vacancies are spread across nine disciplines as follows.

PwBD horizontal reservation accounts for 64 of the 660 posts. Vacancy figures are tentative and subject to change. Candidates can apply for only one discipline — multiple applications are not permitted.

Eligibility Criteria — Coal India MT Recruitment 2026

Educational Qualification

The minimum percentage required is 60% for UR / OBC (NCL) / EWS and 55% for SC / ST / PwBD. Engineering / M.Tech / M.Sc / MCA degrees must be regular full-time courses; for Hindi (Rajbhasha) discipline, online/correspondence/part-time/ODL degrees are also accepted if from a recognised university.

  • Civil: B.E./B.Tech in Civil Engineering.
  • Electrical: B.E./B.Tech in Electrical Engineering or Electronics & Electrical Engineering.
  • Mechanical: B.E./B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering or Mining Machinery.
  • System: 1st class B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc(Engg) in Computer Science / Computer Engineering / IT, OR any 1st class degree with MCA.
  • E&T: B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc(Engg) in Electronics & Telecommunication / Electronics & Communication / Electronics & Instrumentation / Electronics Instrumentation and Control Engineering.
  • Geology: M.Sc./M.Tech in Geology / Applied Geology / Geophysics / Applied Geophysics.
  • Industrial Engineering: Degree in Industrial Engineering or Production & Industrial Engineering or Industrial Engineering & Operation Research.
  • Rajbhasha: M.A. in Hindi (60%) AND Graduation with Hindi and English as main subjects (50%).
  • Company Secretary: Graduate in any discipline + Company Secretary qualification + Associate / Fellow membership of ICSI.

If you are a graduate engineer seeking a Maharatna PSU job, this is a perfect opening.

Age Limit

Maximum age is 30 years as on 30 April 2026. Standard category-wise relaxation applies.

What is the Salary for CIL Management Trainees in 2026?

Selected candidates start as Management Trainee in the E-1 grade with a starting basic pay of ₹60,000 per month in the pay scale of ₹60,000–1,80,000.

  • Initial Basic Pay: ₹60,000 per month
  • Pay Scale (E-1 Grade): ₹60,000 – ₹1,80,000
  • Allowances: Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance, perquisites under cafeteria approach
  • Performance Related Pay (PRP): Annual PRP linked to company and individual performance
  • Other Benefits: Leave, medical facilities, CMPF, CMPS, gratuity, CIL Executive Defined Contribution Pension Scheme

Approximate annual CTC for a Coal India Management Trainee ranges from ₹14 lakh to ₹20 lakh, depending on location, allowances and PRP.

After successfully completing one year of training-cum-probation, candidates are confirmed as Officer in the respective discipline in E-1 grade. A service bond of ₹3 lakh is signed for 60 months minimum service — ₹5,000 per month is deducted from salary and refunded without interest after 60 months.

What is the Selection Process for CIL MT 2026?

There is no interview in this recruitment. Selection is based on a single Computer Based Test of 200 marks.

  1. Online CBT: Two papers in one sitting, total duration 3 hours. Paper 1 covers General Knowledge, Reasoning, Numerical Ability and General English (100 marks, 100 questions). Paper 2 covers Professional Knowledge in the chosen discipline (100 marks, 100 questions).
  2. Marking Scheme: 1 mark per correct answer, no negative marking, no marks for unattempted questions.
  3. Language: Bilingual (English & Hindi). The English version is final in case of any error.
  4. Qualifying Marks: UR/EWS — minimum 40 marks per paper; OBC — 35 marks per paper; SC/ST/PwD — 30 marks per paper.
  5. Document Verification (DV) of shortlisted candidates.
  6. Initial Medical Examination (IME) before appointment.

Final merit is based on CBT marks only. For CS discipline, ties are resolved in favour of candidates with experience in a listed company. You can review more exam syllabus and pattern resources on our portal.

How to for Coal India MT Recruitment 2026?

Applications are accepted only through online mode via the official CIL career portal.

  1. Visit https://www.coalindia.in/career-cil/jobs-coal-india/.
  2. Click on “Recruitment of Management Trainees – Advt. 03/2026”.
  3. Read the PDF carefully and confirm eligibility for the chosen discipline.
  4. Register on the portal with a valid email and active mobile number.
  5. Login and fill the online application form — personal, qualification, category details and CBT centre preferences.
  6. Upload recent colour passport-size photograph (not older than 3 weeks) and signature in JPG/JPEG format as per specifications.
  7. Pay the application fee online and submit the application.
  8. Take a printout of the submitted application form for your records.

Only one application per candidate per discipline is allowed. After final submission no modification is permitted. Government / PSU / Autonomous body employees must apply through proper channel or submit NOC.

Important Dates — Coal India MT 2026

Application Fees — Coal India MT 2026

The application fee is non-refundable. Payment is accepted online through debit/credit card, net banking or UPI on the integrated payment gateway.

Essential Links — Coal India MT Recruitment 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for Coal India MT Recruitment 2026
  • Visit Coal India Limited

A career at Coal India means working with a Maharatna PSU that powers India’s energy story — strong salary, pan-India exposure, structured promotions and lifelong security. Apply on or before 11 June 2026 21 June 2026 in your eligible discipline and start CBT preparation early. For more PSU recruitment notifications and admit card alerts, keep visiting .

title: NALCO Non Executive Recruitment 2026 — for 268 Operator, Technician, Foreman Vacancies (Last Date Extended to 17 June 2026
description: NALCO Non Executive Recruitment 2026: Last Date Extended to 17 June 2026 — Latest Update Good news for applicants! NALCO (National Aluminium Company Limited) has extended the last
jobDescription:
NALCO Non Executive Recruitment 2026: Last Date Extended to 17 June 2026 — Latest Update

Good news for applicants! NALCO (National Aluminium Company Limited) has extended the last date to for its Non-Executive recruitment (Advt. No. 10260213). The online application window and fee payment are now open until 10 June 2026 17 June 2026 (11:55 PM). All other terms and conditions remain unchanged. If you have not applied yet, apply now — Download the official Last Date Extension Notice (PDF).

NALCO Recruitment 2026 — Big Opportunity in Aluminium PSU for ITI and Diploma Holders

National Aluminium Company Limited (NALCO), a Schedule “A” Navratna Central PSU under the Ministry of Mines, has released a fresh recruitment notification for 268 Non-Executive posts at its M&R Complex, Damanjodi (Odisha). The hiring covers 13 different posts — from Operator (HEMM) and Mining Mate to Junior Foreman, Technicians and SUPT/JOT trainees. If you hold an ITI certificate, Diploma in Engineering or B.Sc. (Hons) Chemistry, this is one of the most stable PSU jobs in 2026 you can apply for, with pay scale going up to Rs 1,15,000 per month.

The online application window is open from 21 May 2026 to 10 June 2026 17 June 2026 (Extended). Whether you are a fresher targeting the SUPT/JOT trainee posts or an experienced ITI holder ready for direct T0 grade absorption, this NALCO recruitment 2026 is a great chance to join one of India’s largest aluminium producers. The full notification, eligibility, exam pattern and step-by-step apply guide are explained below — bookmark for the latest updates.

Key Highlights — NALCO Non Executive Recruitment 2026

NALCO is one of the most respected Maharatna-class metal PSUs in India and the M&R Complex at Damanjodi houses its bauxite mines and alumina refinery. Selected candidates get IDA pay scales, performance-related pay, medical benefits, and structured promotions across T0 and S0 grades. Earlier batches of NALCO trainees have grown into permanent technicians with pay reaching well over Rs 70,000 per month within a few years of joining.

  • Organisation: National Aluminium Company Limited (NALCO)
  • Advertisement No.: 10260213
  • Total Vacancies: 268 posts (13 different post types)
  • Pay Scale: Rs 29,500 – Rs 1,15,000 (IDA) + SUPT/JOT stipend Rs 12,000 – Rs 15,500
  • Job Location: M&R Complex, Damanjodi (Koraput, Odisha)
  • Apply Mode: Online only — mudira.nalcoindia.co.in
  • Last Date to Apply: 10 June 2026 17 June 2026 (23:55 hrs, Extended)

Vacancy Breakdown — NALCO Non Executive 2026

The 268 posts are split across 13 post types in T0 (Technician), S0 (Junior Foreman) and SUPT/JOT (Trainee) grades. Trade Tests apply to operator-level posts in 60:40 weightage with the CBT, while purely SUPT/JOT and Junior Foreman posts are decided on CBT alone.

Horizontal reservation: out of the 268 posts, 20 posts are horizontally reserved for PwBD candidates (including 10 backlog) and 38 posts for Ex-Servicemen — both are carved out of the total, not added on top.

Eligibility Criteria — NALCO Non Executive 2026

Educational Qualification

The qualification varies by post type. Always read the for the exact trade and apprenticeship requirements before applying.

  • Operator (HEMM) Gr. III: 10th pass + 2-year ITI (NCVT/NCVET) in Mechanic Motor Vehicle or 1-year ITI in Diesel Mechanic with apprenticeship + Heavy Vehicle Driving Licence.
  • Mining Mate Gr. III: 10th pass + valid DGMS Mining Mate Certificate of Competency (Restricted to Open-cast Metalliferous Mines).
  • Jr. Foreman (Mines): Diploma in Mining / Mining Engineering + DGMS Mining Foreman Certificate of Competency.
  • Jr. Foreman (Electrical): Full-time Diploma in Electrical Engineering + valid Electrical Supervisor’s Certificate (MV/HT/EHT) from the Electrical Licensing Board, Odisha.
  • SUPT/JOT (Operator/Fitter/Electrical/Instrumentation): 10th pass + 2-year ITI in the relevant trade with apprenticeship certificate.
  • SUPT(JOT) – Laboratory: B.Sc. (Hons) in Chemistry.
  • Technician (Fitter/Electrical/Instrumentation) Gr. III: 10th pass + 2-year ITI in the relevant trade + apprenticeship + 2 years’ work experience in the relevant field.

If you are an ITI certificate holder looking for government jobs, this NALCO drive is one of the most rewarding entry routes into a Navratna PSU.

Age Limit (as on 10 June 2026)

  • SUPT/JOT trainees: up to 27 years
  • Gr. III (T0 grade) posts: up to 35 years
  • Jr. Foreman (S0 grade) posts: up to 40 years
  • Age relaxation: SC/ST – 5 years, OBC-NCL – 3 years, PwBD-UR – 10 years (PwBD-OBC – 13, PwBD-SC/ST – 15), Ex-Servicemen as per GoI rules. Maximum age after all relaxations cannot exceed 56 years.

What is the Salary for NALCO Non Executive 2026?

NALCO follows IDA pay scales linked to performance and DA, and the company is known for one of the better take-home packages among manufacturing PSUs. Selected candidates also receive HRA, medical benefits for self and dependents, leave benefits and gratuity.

  • T0 Grade (Technician/Operator Gr. III): Rs 29,500 – 3% – Rs 70,000 (IDA)
  • S0 Grade (Junior Foreman): Rs 36,500 – 3% – Rs 1,15,000 (IDA)
  • SUPT Trainee (first 12 months): stipend Rs 12,000 per month
  • JOT Trainee (12 + 18 months): stipend Rs 15,000 then Rs 15,500 per month
  • After Training: Placed in T0 grade Rs 29,500 – 70,000 with regular employee benefits.

In-hand monthly salary for T0 grade typically works out to around Rs 45,000 – Rs 55,000 after DA and allowances, going up to Rs 75,000+ in S0 grade.

What is the Selection Process for NALCO Non Executive 2026?

NALCO follows a multi-stage selection process. The Computer Based Test (CBT) is common across all posts, while Trade Test applies only to operator-class posts in a 60:40 weightage with CBT.

  1. Computer Based Test (CBT) — 100 objective MCQs, 100 marks, 120 minutes, 60% technical questions and 40% non-technical (Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning, GK, English). No negative marking. Languages: English and Hindi.
  2. Trade Test — only for Sl. 1, 2, 6, 8, 10 and 13 (Operator/Technician/Gr. III posts). CBT and Trade Test weighted 60:40 for final merit.
  3. Document Verification — original certificates, caste/EWS proof, experience and ITI/Diploma documents.
  4. Pre-Employment Medical Examination (PEME) — medical fitness as per NALCO Medical Board norms.

The CBT will be held at multiple cities including Bhubaneswar, Cuttack, Sambalpur, Rourkela, Visakhapatnam, Guwahati, Mumbai, Delhi, Chennai and Kolkata. You can also check the latest government job syllabus and exam pattern guidance for trade-based PSU exams.

How to for NALCO Non Executive Recruitment 2026?

Applications are accepted online only via NALCO’s recruitment portal. Manual / postal applications will not be accepted. Have your educational and category certificates ready before you start.

  1. Visit the NALCO Recruitment Portal at https://mudira.nalcoindia.co.in/rec_portal/default.aspx
  2. Click on “Click here for New Registration” and enter your name, contact and email ID.
  3. Note the Provisional Registration Number and Password (sent via SMS and email).
  4. Login and complete the application form — upload photograph (4.5 cm x 3.5 cm, 20–50 KB), signature, left thumb impression and hand-written declaration.
  5. Upload scanned copies of educational certificates, apprenticeship/NAC, caste certificate, HMV licence or DGMS certificate as applicable.
  6. Pay the application fee online via Net Banking / Debit Card / UPI.
  7. Preview the form, click “Complete Registration” and download the e-Receipt for your records.

Each candidate can apply for only one post in this notification.

Important Dates — NALCO Non Executive 2026

Application Fees — NALCO Non Executive 2026

Fee can be paid via Net Banking, Debit Card or UPI through the dedicated bank account on the NALCO recruitment portal. Fees are non-refundable under any circumstances.

Essential Links — NALCO Non Executive Recruitment 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for NALCO Non Executive 2026
  • Visit NALCO

NALCO Non Executive 2026 is a rare chance to step into a Navratna PSU with strong pay and career security — don’t miss the 10 June 2026 17 June 2026 deadline. Keep visiting for the latest PSU recruitment notifications, admit card and result updates.

title: MSC Bank Trainee Officer & Driver Recruitment 2026: for 139 Posts (Officer, Associate, Typist, Driver
description: Maharashtra State Co-operative Bank 2026: 139 Posts, Stipend & How to Apply Here is a wonderful banking opportunity for graduates and 10th pass candidates from Maharashtra. The Mah
jobDescription:
Maharashtra State Co-operative Bank 2026: 139 Posts, Stipend & How to Apply

Here is a wonderful banking opportunity for graduates and 10th pass candidates from Maharashtra. The Maharashtra State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (MSC Bank), the leading apex cooperative bank of the state established in 1911, has invited online applications for 139 trainee posts. The vacancies are spread across four cadres — Trainee Junior Officer, Trainee Associate, Trainee Typist and Trainee Driver. If you have dreamed of a stable career in the banking sector, this is a great chance to start as a trainee and grow into a confirmed bank employee.

The online application window is open from 11 June 2026 to 1 July 2026. The bank invites applications only from residents of Maharashtra, and a Maharashtra domicile certificate is mandatory. In this article, we will explain the post-wise vacancies, eligibility, stipend, selection process, important dates, fees and a simple step-by-step apply guide. Please read it fully so you don’t miss any point. Keep bookmarked for the latest job alerts.

Key Highlights — MSC Bank Recruitment 2026

Maharashtra State Co-operative Bank (MSC Bank) runs through its Head Office in Mumbai along with 6 Regional Offices and 57 branches across Maharashtra. This recruitment is conducted with the help of IBPS, which will hold the online examination. Most posts are open to any graduate candidate, while the Trainee Driver post is for 10th pass candidates with a driving licence.

  • Post Names: Trainee Junior Officer, Trainee Associate, Trainee Typist, Trainee Driver
  • Total Vacancies: 139 posts
  • Stipend: Rs 22,000 to Rs 30,000 per month during training (post-wise)
  • Job Location: Maharashtra (Head Office Mumbai)
  • Application Start Date: 11 June 2026
  • Last Date to Apply: 1 July 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — MSC Bank Trainee Posts 2026

The 139 posts are divided among four trainee cadres as shown below:

Eligibility Criteria — MSC Bank Recruitment 2026

Check the post-wise requirements carefully before you . A common condition for all posts is that you must have passed Matriculation with Marathi as one of the subjects.

Educational Qualification

  • Trainee Junior Officer: Graduate in any discipline with at least 50% marks. Preference for Law graduates, JAIIB/CAIIB/MSCIT holders, or Treasury/International Banking experience.
  • Trainee Associate: Graduate in any discipline with at least 50% marks and computer proficiency.
  • Trainee Typist: Graduate with a Government Commercial Certificate of 30 wpm Marathi and 40 wpm English typing, plus computer knowledge.
  • Trainee Driver: Passed Matriculation (SSC) with a valid LMV driving licence.

Experience

The Trainee Junior Officer post needs a minimum of 2 years’ experience in the banking field, preferably in an Urban or DCC Bank as an Officer. The other three posts do not require prior experience.

Age Limit

The age is calculated as on 1 June 2026 and differs by post:

For the Trainee Driver post, children of MSC Bank employees get 5 years relaxation in the maximum age.

What is the Salary for MSC Bank Trainee Posts 2026?

During the 12-month training period, candidates are paid a monthly stipend, and after successful training and confirmation they move to the bank’s regular grade with full emoluments.

  • Trainee Junior Officer: Stipend Rs 30,000/month, then about Rs 52,100/month
  • Trainee Associate: Stipend Rs 25,000/month, then about Rs 34,400/month
  • Trainee Typist: Stipend Rs 25,000/month, then about Rs 34,400/month (plus Rs 650 typist allowance)
  • Trainee Driver: Stipend Rs 22,000/month, then about Rs 27,700/month

After confirmation, the monthly emoluments range from about Rs 27,700 for drivers to about Rs 52,100 for junior officers, making this a financially secure start.

Along with the pay, you also get the stability and respect of working in Maharashtra’s apex cooperative bank, with a clear path to confirmation after a 6-month probation.

What is the Selection Process for MSC Bank Recruitment 2026?

The selection is based on an online examination conducted by IBPS followed by an interview or skill test. There is negative marking for wrong answers, so attempt carefully.

Online Examination (by IBPS)

For Trainee Junior Officer and Trainee Associate, the online test carries 200 marks for a duration of 120 minutes, and you must score at least 50% (100 marks) to be shortlisted for the personal interview. The paper covers Reasoning & Computer Aptitude, English Language, Banking & General Awareness, and (for officers) Professional Knowledge.

Skill Test and Driving Test

For the Trainee Typist post, a Phase-I online test is followed by a Phase-II typing skill test. For the Trainee Driver post, the online test is followed by a personal interview and driving test. You can brush up using our exam syllabus and preparation guide.

How to for MSC Bank Recruitment 2026?

Before you begin, keep your scanned photograph, signature, left thumb impression and a handwritten declaration ready as per the prescribed format. Then follow these steps:

  1. Visit the MSC Bank careers portal (direct link in Essential Links below).
  2. Click on “” and complete the registration to get your provisional number and password.
  3. Log in and fill the application form with your personal, educational and experience details.
  4. Upload your photograph, signature, thumb impression and declaration as per the size limits.
  5. Pay the application fee online for your chosen post.
  6. Verify all details carefully and submit the form.
  7. Take a printout of the e-receipt and the completed application form for future use.

MSC Bank 2026 Important Dates: Application Timeline

MSC Bank 2026 Application Fees

The fee is the same for all categories of candidates and must be paid online only. It is non-refundable.

Essential Links — MSC Bank Trainee Recruitment 2026

  • Download PDF

  • Visit MSC Bank

This is a solid chance to begin a respected banking career right here in Maharashtra, so don’t wait till the last moment. If you meet the eligibility, apply before 1 July 2026 and prepare well for the IBPS online test. For more Maharashtra government job updates, keep visiting every day.

title: CG Vyapam OT Technician Recruitment 2026: for 15 Operation Theatre Technician Posts, Last Date 3 July
description: Chhattisgarh OT Technician 2026: 15 Posts, Pay, Eligibility & How to Apply Here is a wonderful chance for paramedical students in Chhattisgarh who want a permanent government job i
jobDescription:
Chhattisgarh OT Technician 2026: 15 Posts, Pay, Eligibility & How to Apply

Here is a wonderful chance for paramedical students in Chhattisgarh who want a permanent government job in the health department. The Chhattisgarh Professional Examination Board (CG Vyapam), on behalf of the Directorate of Health Services, Chhattisgarh, has invited online applications for 15 Operation Theatre (O.T.) Technician posts. If you have completed your 12th with science and a recognised O.T. or Anaesthesia Technician course, this is a great opportunity to start a stable career in a government hospital. It is especially useful for those searching for medical and healthcare government jobs.

The online application window is open from 10 June 2026 to 3 July 2026. This recruitment is only for local residents of Chhattisgarh, and the posts are spread across the Sarguja and Bastar divisions. In this article we will explain the vacancies, eligibility, pay, age limit, selection process, important dates and a simple step-by-step apply guide. Read it fully so you don’t miss any detail, and keep saved for the latest job alerts.

Key Highlights — CG Vyapam OT Technician Recruitment 2026

The Directorate of Health Services, Chhattisgarh is recruiting O.T. Technicians (Grade III) through an examination conducted by CG Vyapam. This is a Third-Class (Group C) post in the state health department, ideal for candidates who have a paramedical background. Candidates who have passed 12th with the science stream and the required technician course can .

  • Post Name: O.T. Technician (Operation Theatre Technician), Grade III
  • Total Vacancies: 15 posts
  • Pay: Pay Level 7 (pay scale Rs 5,200 – Rs 20,200 as per the notification)
  • Job Location: Sarguja & Bastar Divisions, Chhattisgarh
  • Application Start Date: 10 June 2026
  • Last Date to Apply: 3 July 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — CG Vyapam OT Technician 2026

The 15 posts are divided between two divisions of Chhattisgarh as shown below:

In the Sarguja Division, the posts are reserved category-wise as UR 3, OBC 1, SC 5 and ST 1 (one SC post is reserved for Chhattisgarh local women). In the Bastar Division, the split is UR 1, SC 3 and ST Eligible PwBD categories include OL, HH, AAV, DW and LC.

Eligibility Criteria — CG Vyapam OT Technician 2026

Please check both your education and age carefully before you .

Educational Qualification

  • Passed 10+2 (Class 12) with Biology, Physics and Chemistry from a recognised institution.
  • Passed a recognised O.T. Technician or Anaesthesia Technician course.
  • Must hold a live registration with the Chhattisgarh Paramedical Council.

Age Limit

The age is calculated as on 1 January 2026. The candidate must be between 18 and 35 years of age. Local residents of Chhattisgarh get a 5-year relaxation in the maximum age. Further relaxation for reserved categories, women, ex-servicemen and PwBD candidates is given as per Chhattisgarh rules, but the total of all relaxations together cannot make the maximum age cross 45 years.

What is the Salary for CG Vyapam OT Technician 2026?

The O.T. Technician post is placed in Pay Level 7, with the pay scale shown in the notification as Rs 5,200 – Rs 20,200. Along with the basic pay, selected candidates also receive the regular allowances admissible to Chhattisgarh state government employees.

  • Pay Level: Level 7
  • Pay Scale (as per notification): Rs 5,200 – Rs 20,200
  • Allowances: Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance and other allowances as per state rules

Apart from the monthly pay, this is a permanent government health-department post, which means strong job security, regular increments and a respected role in government hospitals.

Candidates are advised to check the exact pay matrix and allowances in the official advertisement on the CG Vyapam portal before applying.

What is the Selection Process for CG Vyapam OT Technician 2026?

Selection is based on a written examination conducted by CG Vyapam, followed by verification stages.

Written Examination

CG Vyapam conducts a competitive written examination for the O.T. Technician posts. To qualify, candidates must score at least 33% (unreserved) or 23% (reserved categories). A merit list is then prepared division-wise and category-wise based on the exam marks. The detailed exam pattern and syllabus are published separately on the CG Vyapam portal — you can also check our syllabus and preparation guide.

Verification and Medical Fitness

Shortlisted candidates go through document verification, police/character verification, and a medical fitness check by the District Medical Board before the final appointment.

How to for CG Vyapam OT Technician 2026?

Before you begin, make sure your Paramedical Council registration and certificates are ready. Then follow these steps:

  1. Visit the CG Vyapam online application portal (direct link in Essential Links below).
  2. Register or log in with your details to start a new application.
  3. Fill the online form with your personal, educational and category details.
  4. Make sure your Chhattisgarh Paramedical Council registration is live before the last date.
  5. Upload the required documents and recent photograph and signature.
  6. Pay the application fee online as per the CG Vyapam rules mentioned in the advertisement.
  7. Submit the form before the last date and take a printout for your records.

CG Vyapam OT Technician 2026 Important Dates: Application Timeline

CG Vyapam OT Technician 2026 Application Fees

The category-wise application fee for this recruitment is charged as per Chhattisgarh Professional Examination Board (CG Vyapam) rules and is mentioned in the official advertisement on the portal. Candidates are advised to check the exact fee for their category in the notification before paying online. The fee must be paid through the online mode only and is non-refundable.

Essential Links — CG Vyapam OT Technician Recruitment 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for CG Vyapam OT Technician 2026
  • Visit CG Vyapam

If you are a Chhattisgarh resident with a paramedical background, this is a chance you should not miss. Apply before the last date of 3 July 2026 and prepare well for the CG Vyapam written exam. For more Chhattisgarh government job updates, keep visiting every day.

title: MDL Trade Apprentice Recruitment 2026: for 495 ITI, 10th & 8th Pass Apprentice Posts at Mazagon Dock
description: Mazagon Dock Apprentice 2026: 495 Posts, Trades, Stipend & How to Apply Good news for ITI, 10th and 8th pass students who want to build a career in India’s defence shipbuilding sec
jobDescription:
Mazagon Dock Apprentice 2026: 495 Posts, Trades, Stipend & How to Apply

Good news for ITI, 10th and 8th pass students who want to build a career in India’s defence shipbuilding sector. Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL), a Navratna defence PSU in Mumbai, has released a big notification for 495 Trade Apprentice posts under the Apprentices Act This is a wonderful chance to learn a real trade inside one of the country’s most respected warship and submarine builders, while earning a monthly stipend. If you are a young candidate looking for a strong start, this opportunity is made for you and other PSU job seekers.

The online application window is open from 11 June 2026 to 1 July 2026. Apprentices are selected across 18 different trades in three groups — 10th pass, ITI pass, and 8th pass. In this article we will explain every trade, the eligibility, age limit, stipend, selection process, important dates, fees, and a simple step-by-step apply guide. Read it fully so you don’t miss any detail. Keep saved as your trusted source for such updates.

Key Highlights — MDL Trade Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL) is a Government of India Navratna company under the Ministry of Defence, famous for building warships and submarines for the Indian Navy. This apprenticeship is a training opportunity (under the Apprentices Act 1961) and not a permanent job, but it gives you valuable hands-on experience and a recognised apprenticeship certificate. It is open to candidates who have done 8th, 10th (SSC), or ITI, depending on the trade. Candidates from an ITI background will find many options here.

  • Post Name: Trade Apprentice (18 trades across Group A, B & C)
  • Total Vacancies: 495 posts
  • Stipend: Monthly stipend as per the Apprentices Act 1961
  • Job Location: Mazagon Dock, Mumbai (Maharashtra)
  • Application Start Date: 11 June 2026
  • Last Date to Apply: 1 July 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — MDL Apprentice 2026 (Trade-Wise)

The 495 posts are divided into three groups based on minimum qualification — Group A (10th pass), Group B (ITI pass) and Group C (8th pass). Here is the full trade-wise list:

Eligibility Criteria — MDL Trade Apprentice 2026

Please check your group carefully before applying. The minimum qualification decides which trades you can apply for.

Educational Qualification

  • Group A (10th Pass trades): Passed SSC / Class 10 with General Science and Mathematics. ITI-passed candidates are not eligible for Group A.
  • Group B (ITI Pass trades): Passed ITI in the relevant trade from a Government or Government-recognised Industrial Training Institute.
  • Group C (8th Pass trades): Passed 8th standard (under the 10+2 system) with Science and Mathematics. ITI-passed candidates are not eligible for Group C.

Minimum 50% aggregate marks are required for General, OBC, SEBC and EWS candidates, while SC/ST candidates need only a pass class. Candidates appearing in the final ITI exam may also apply, but they must pass on or before the cut-off date mentioned in the notification.

Age Limit

The age limit is different for each group, calculated as on 1 October 2026:

Age relaxation is given as per Government rules — SC/ST 5 years, OBC 3 years, and PwD (Divyang) 10 years. Keep your valid category certificate ready to claim this benefit.

What is the Salary and Stipend for MDL Apprentice 2026?

As an apprentice at Mazagon Dock, you will be paid a monthly stipend during the training period as per the rates fixed under the Apprentices Act 1961. The stipend is credited directly to your savings bank account every month through ECS, so a PAN card is mandatory.

  • Stipend: Paid monthly as per the Apprentices Act 1961
  • Leave & holidays: As applicable during the apprenticeship training
  • Experience: Hands-on training at a Navratna defence shipyard
  • Certificate: Recognised apprenticeship completion certificate

Apprenticeship at MDL is a learning role, so the focus is on the skills and experience you gain — these open doors to many ITI and technician jobs later.

Please note that the apprenticeship does not give any right or claim to a permanent job at MDL. No hostel accommodation is provided, so plan your stay in Mumbai accordingly.

What is the Selection Process for MDL Trade Apprentice 2026?

Selection is based on a written test followed by document checks. Here are the stages explained simply:

Stage 1: Online Examination (CBT)

A Computer Based Test of 100 multiple-choice questions for 100 marks and 2 hours duration, based on the minimum-qualification syllabus of your group. For Group A and Group C, the paper covers English & General Knowledge, Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. For Group B (ITI), it covers English & General Knowledge, Trade Theory, Workshop Calculation & Science, and Engineering Drawing.

Stage 2: Document Verification and Trade Allotment

Candidates with the highest marks (group and category wise) are called for document verification. Trades are allotted in order of merit, so a higher score gives you a better choice of trade.

Stage 3: Medical Examination and Police Verification

Shortlisted candidates must be medically fit as per the Apprentices Rules A Police Verification Report is mandatory before joining. You can track your hall ticket on the official portal and on our admit card updates page.

How to for MDL Trade Apprentice 2026?

Before you start, keep your photograph, signature, certificates and category documents ready in scanned form. Then follow these steps:

  1. Visit the official MDL website and open the Careers section (direct link in Essential Links below).
  2. Go to Online Recruitment and select the Apprentice section.
  3. Click on “Create New Account” and register with your email and mobile number.
  4. Log in and fill the online application form with your personal and educational details.
  5. Upload your scanned documents, including the valid caste or disability certificate if you are claiming a fee exemption or age relaxation.
  6. Pay the application fee online for applicable categories.
  7. Submit the form and take a printout for your records. Selected candidates must also register on the apprenticeshipindia.gov.in portal.

MDL Apprentice 2026 Important Dates: Application Timeline

MDL Apprentice 2026 Application Fees

The application fee must be paid online only during the application window. Bank charges, if any, will be borne by the candidate. The fee is non-refundable.

Essential Links — MDL Trade Apprentice Recruitment 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for MDL Trade Apprentice 2026
  • Visit Mazagon Dock

This is a great opportunity to learn a trade at one of India’s top defence shipyards, so don’t let it slip away. If you meet the eligibility, apply before the last date of 1 July 2026 — early applications avoid last-minute portal rush. For more PSU and apprentice job updates, keep visiting every day.

title: Jammu University Non Teaching Recruitment 2026: for 23 Technical & Non-Teaching Posts at UIET Kathua
description: University of Jammu UIET 2026: 23 Posts, Pay, Eligibility & How to Apply Here is a good chance for candidates in Jammu and Kashmir who are looking for a stable government job. The
jobDescription:
University of Jammu UIET 2026: 23 Posts, Pay, Eligibility & How to Apply

Here is a good chance for candidates in Jammu and Kashmir who are looking for a stable government job. The University of Jammu has released a notification to fill 23 non-teaching and technical staff posts at its University Institute of Engineering and Technology (UIET), Janglote, Kathua. The posts range from Foreman and Junior Stenographer to Workshop staff, Lab Technicians, Driver and Multi-Tasking Staff. If you are searching for government jobs in Jammu and Kashmir, this is an opportunity worth your attention.

The online application portal opens on 15 June 2026 and the last date to apply is 14 July 2026. Only candidates who are domiciles of the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir are eligible to . In this article we will cover the post-wise vacancies, pay levels, eligibility, age limit, selection process, important dates, fees and a simple apply guide. Read it carefully, and keep saved for more such updates.

Key Highlights — Jammu University Non Teaching Recruitment 2026

The University of Jammu is a well-known state university, and this recruitment is exclusively for its constituent college UIET, Janglote, Kathua. The posts are spread across different pay levels under the 7th Pay Commission. Whether you are a graduate looking for a government job or a technically qualified diploma or ITI holder, there is likely a post that fits your profile.

  • Post Names: Foreman, Junior Stenographer, Workshop Assistant/Instructor, Lab Technician, Pharmacist, Junior Assistant, Store Keeper, Driver, MTS and more
  • Total Vacancies: 23 posts
  • Pay: 7th CPC Pay Matrix — Rs 14,800 to Rs 1,13,500 (level-wise)
  • Job Location: UIET, Janglote, Kathua (Jammu and Kashmir)
  • Application Start Date: 15 June 2026
  • Last Date to Apply: 14 July 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — Jammu University UIET 2026

The 23 posts are distributed across 13 different roles as shown below:

Eligibility Criteria — Jammu University Non Teaching 2026

The qualification is different for each post. Please match your degree or diploma to the post before applying.

Educational Qualification

  • Foreman: B.E./B.Tech first class in Mechanical or Production Engineering
  • Junior Stenographer: Graduate with 65 wpm shorthand, 35 wpm typing and a 6-month Computer Applications certificate
  • Workshop Assistant: Matric with National Trade Certificate and 3 years experience
  • Accounts Assistant: Graduate with 50% marks (45% for reserved); PG or Ph.D. also eligible
  • Workshop Instructor: Matriculation with a 3-year Diploma in the concerned trade
  • Lab Technician: Diploma in Civil or Computer Engineering with 5 years experience
  • Pharmacist: 10+2 with a Pharmacy Diploma from the State Medical Faculty
  • Junior Assistant: Graduate with 40 wpm computer typing speed
  • Store Keeper: B.Sc. with experience of handling stores
  • Maintenance Assistant: Matric with National Trade Certificate in Electrical trade
  • Library Assistant: Bachelor of Library Science
  • Driver: Middle pass with a commercial hill-road driving licence and 5 years experience
  • Multi-Tasking Staff (MTS): Minimum Matric, maximum 10+2

This recruitment has options for engineering and diploma holders as well as basic matriculate candidates, so read the post-wise list carefully.

Age Limit

The age is calculated as on 1 January 2026. The minimum age is 18 years for all posts and the maximum is 40 years for General candidates. Relaxation in the maximum age is given as follows:

What is the Salary for Jammu University Non Teaching Posts 2026?

The posts are paid as per the 7th Pay Commission Pay Matrix, and the pay level depends on the post you are selected for.

  • Foreman: Level-6E (Rs 35,900 – Rs 1,13,500)
  • Junior Stenographer: Level-6B (Rs 35,600 – Rs 1,12,800)
  • Workshop Assistant / Accounts Assistant: Level-5 (Rs 29,200 – Rs 92,300)
  • Level-4 posts: Rs 25,500 – Rs 81,100
  • Level-2 posts: Rs 19,900 – Rs 63,200
  • MTS: SL-1 (Rs 14,800 – Rs 47,100)

Depending on the post, the basic pay starts from about Rs 14,800 (MTS) and goes up to a starting level of Rs 35,900 (Foreman), with regular allowances added on top as per UT rules.

These are permanent UT government posts with a probation period, which means good job security along with the salary.

What is the Selection Process for Jammu University Recruitment 2026?

Selection is done through a test and skill assessment followed by an interview. The detailed scheme and syllabus will be uploaded separately on the University website.

Screening / Written Test

If a large number of applications are received, the University may hold a screening test or written test to shortlist candidates for the next stage.

Skill / Trade Test and Interview

Depending on the post, a trade test, skill test or computer test is conducted, followed by a personal interview and document verification. You can prepare with the help of our syllabus and exam pattern guide.

How to for Jammu University Recruitment 2026?

Keep your domicile certificate, category certificate, qualification documents and a photo and signature ready before you start. Then follow these steps:

  1. Visit the University of Jammu recruitment portal (direct link in Essential Links below).
  2. Register and log in to start a new application.
  3. Fill in your personal, educational and experience details carefully.
  4. Upload your J&K domicile certificate, reserved-category certificate (if any) and other documents.
  5. Pay the application fee of Rs 1,300 through the portal payment gateway.
  6. Verify everything and submit, because the form cannot be edited after submission.
  7. Take a printout of the application form to carry at the skill test or interview stage.

Jammu University 2026 Important Dates: Application Timeline

Jammu University 2026 Application Fees

The fee of Rs 1,300 is the same for all candidates and must be paid online through the portal payment gateway. It is non-refundable.

Essential Links — Jammu University Non Teaching Recruitment 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for Jammu University Recruitment 2026
  • Visit University of Jammu

If you are a domicile of Jammu and Kashmir and meet the eligibility, this is a chance you should not miss. Apply on time and don’t wait for the last date of 14 July 2026, as the portal often gets busy near the deadline. For more Jammu and Kashmir government job updates, keep visiting regularly.

title: Assam PSC Assistant Geologist Recruitment 2026: for 11 Posts, Pay up to Rs 1,10,000
description: APSC Assistant Geologist 2026: Vacancies, Pay, Eligibility & How to Apply Here is a great opportunity for geology post-graduates who want a gazetted government job in Assam. The Assam Public Service Commission (APSC) has released a notification to
jobDescription:
APSC Assistant Geologist 2026: Vacancies, Pay, Eligibility & How to Apply

Here is a great opportunity for geology post-graduates who want a gazetted government job in Assam. The Assam Public Service Commission (APSC) has released a notification to recruit 11 Assistant Geologists for the Directorate of Geology and Mining under the Mines and Minerals Department. This is a respected, well-paid post under Pay Band-4 and a wonderful career path for science students. If you are searching for government jobs in Assam, this is a chance you should seriously consider.

The online application window opens on 16 June 2026 and the last date to apply is 15 July 2026, with the fee payment window open till 17 July 2026. Applicants must be permanent residents of Assam. In this article, we will explain the vacancies, pay scale, eligibility, age limit, selection process, important dates, fees and a simple step-by-step apply guide. Read it fully and keep GovtJobsAlert.in saved for the latest updates.

Key Highlights — Assam PSC Assistant Geologist Recruitment 2026

The Assam Public Service Commission (APSC) conducts recruitment for various state government posts, and this notification is for the Assistant Geologist post in the Directorate of Geology and Mining. It is an excellent role for post-graduate science students who have studied Geology or Applied Geology. An Assistant Geologist works on mineral exploration, geological mapping, sample analysis and field survey work that supports the state’s mining and mineral policy. For a geology post-graduate, it is a rare chance to use your subject knowledge directly in a permanent, respected government role rather than only in private mining or research jobs.

  • Post Name: Assistant Geologist (Directorate of Geology and Mining)
  • Total Vacancies: 11 posts
  • Pay Scale: Rs 30,000 – Rs 1,10,000 + Grade Pay Rs 12,700 (Pay Band-4)
  • Job Location: Assam
  • Application Start Date: 16 June 2026
  • Last Date to Apply: 15 July 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — APSC Assistant Geologist 2026

The 11 posts are reserved category-wise as shown below. Three of the open posts are reserved for women.

Eligibility Criteria — Assam PSC Assistant Geologist 2026

Please make sure you meet both the education and age requirements before applying.

Educational Qualification

Candidates must have at least a Second Class (45% marks) in any one of the following from a Government-recognised University or Institution:

  • M.Sc. in Geology
  • M.Sc. in Applied Geology
  • M.Sc. Tech in Applied Geology
  • M.Tech in Applied Geology
  • Integrated M.Sc. Tech in Applied Geology

Age Limit

The age is calculated as on 1 January 2026. The candidate must be between 21 and 38 years of age. Age relaxation is provided as per Assam Government rules:

What is the Salary for Assam PSC Assistant Geologist 2026?

The Assistant Geologist is a gazetted post carrying an attractive pay scale under Pay Band-4 of the Assam Government.

  • Pay Band: Rs 30,000 – Rs 1,10,000
  • Grade Pay: Rs 12,700
  • Allowances: Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance and other allowances as admissible to Assam State Government employees

Along with the basic pay band of Rs 30,000 – Rs 1,10,000 and a grade pay of Rs 12,700, the in-hand salary becomes quite attractive once all allowances are added.

Apart from the good salary, you also get the job security, status and benefits of a permanent gazetted officer in the Assam Government. Government employees in this grade typically enjoy regular pay revisions, pension and medical benefits, paid leave and a clear promotion path within the department. For many candidates, the long-term stability of a gazetted post is just as valuable as the monthly pay itself, which is why APSC posts attract serious competition every year. Make sure you prepare well and apply early to give yourself the best chance.

What is the Selection Process for Assam PSC Assistant Geologist 2026?

The exact selection scheme will be notified later by the Commission through a corrigendum, but the recruitment is conducted by direct recruitment in the following stages:

Screening / Written Examination

The Commission may hold a screening examination or written examination to shortlist candidates, depending on the number of applications received. The exam may be conducted zone-wise across Assam.

Interview and Document Verification

Shortlisted candidates appear for a personal interview (viva-voce) along with document verification and a medical examination. The detailed syllabus will be uploaded later — you can also check our syllabus and preparation guide.

How to Apply Online for Assam PSC Assistant Geologist 2026?

Keep your documents, photograph and signature ready in the required format before you start. Then follow these steps:

  1. Visit the APSC online recruitment portal (direct link in Essential Links below).
  2. Complete the One Time Registration (OTR) using a valid email ID and mobile number.
  3. Log in and fill the application form with all the required details.
  4. Upload your documents, photograph and signature as per the prescribed size and format.
  5. Tick the declaration, preview your form, and submit it (no editing is allowed afterwards).
  6. Click on “Proceed to Pay” and complete the fee payment online.
  7. Submit the required Declaration Form and take a printout of the final application form.

Assam PSC 2026 Important Dates: Application Timeline

Assam PSC 2026 Application Fees

The fee includes a processing charge and must be paid online through the portal. The processing fee is non-refundable.

Essential Links — Assam PSC Assistant Geologist Recruitment 2026

  • Download Official Notification PDF
  • Apply Online for Assam PSC Assistant Geologist 2026
  • Visit APSC Official Website
  • Join WhatsApp Channel for Government Jobs
  • Join Telegram Channel for Government Jobs
  • Join Assam Government Jobs Telegram Channel

If you hold a Master’s degree in Geology and dream of a gazetted government job, this is a golden start to your career. Apply before the last date of 15 July 2026 and prepare well for the selection. For more Assam government job notifications, keep visiting GovtJobsAlert.in every day.

title: Central Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026: 4500 Posts, ₹15,000 Stipend, Apply by 22 June
description: Central Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 — 4,500 Posts for Graduates The Central Bank of India has released a big notification to engage 4,500 Apprentices for the financial year 2026-27 under the Apprentices Act, This is a wonderf
jobDescription:
Central Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 — 4,500 Posts for Graduates

The Central Bank of India has released a big notification to engage 4,500 Apprentices for the financial year 2026-27 under the Apprentices Act, 1961. This is a wonderful opening for fresh graduates who want to start a career in the banking sector and learn real banking work while earning a monthly stipend. The seats are spread across all states of India, so candidates from every region can apply for their own state. You can explore more such openings on our banking jobs page.

The online application process runs through the NATS portal and the BFSI SSC (BEEP) portal, and the last date to apply is 22 June 2026. Don’t worry if you are new to this — in this article, GovtJobsAlert.in explains the vacancies, eligibility, stipend, selection process, fees and the step-by-step way to apply, all in very simple language so you can complete your application easily.

Key Highlights — Central Bank Apprentice 2026

This is a one-year banking apprenticeship, not a permanent job. It gives you valuable training, a good stipend, and strong experience for future bank exams.

  • Organisation: Central Bank of India
  • Post Name: Apprentice (Banking)
  • Total Vacancies: 4,500 seats
  • Stipend: ₹15,000 per month
  • Apprenticeship Duration: 12 months
  • Last Date to Apply: 22 June 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — Central Bank Apprentice 2026 (State-Wise)

The 4,500 seats are divided across all states and union territories. Some of the largest state allocations are shown below; the full state-wise and district-wise list is in the notification PDF.

The grand total across all states is 4,500 seats, with reservation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS and PwBD candidates as per rules.

Eligibility Criteria — Central Bank Apprentice 2026

This apprenticeship is designed for recent graduates, so the eligibility is simple and clear.

Educational Qualification

  • A Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a recognised University is required — so graduates from any stream are eligible.
  • You must have completed your graduation on or after 1 January 2022 (a recent graduate).
  • You must be registered on the NATS portal before applying.
  • You must know the local language of the state you apply for (a Local Language Test is part of selection; candidates who studied that language in Class 10/12 are exempt).

Age Limit (as on 31 May 2026)

  • Minimum Age: 20 years
  • Maximum Age: 28 years (for General/EWS)
  • Age Relaxation: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and 10 years for PwBD candidates.

What is the Salary / Stipend for Central Bank Apprentice 2026?

Apprentices are paid a fixed monthly stipend (in place of a regular salary), which is a good support while you learn banking work.

  • Stipend: ₹15,000 per month (consolidated) for the full one-year period.
  • The stipend is paid directly to your bank account; no separate allowances are given.

A ₹15,000 monthly stipend plus one year of real banking experience makes this a strong launchpad for your banking career, especially if you plan to write IBPS or SBI exams later.

What is the Selection Process for Central Bank Apprentice 2026?

Selection is merit-based through an online test. The steps are:

  1. Online Test of 100 marks (General/Financial Awareness, Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude, Computer Knowledge, General English, and Bank Products & Services).
  2. Test of Local Language of the state you applied for.
  3. Document Verification at the Bank’s Regional/Zonal Office.
  4. Medical Fitness check before final engagement.

There is no negative marking in the online test. You can sharpen your basics using our exam syllabus and preparation guides.

How to Apply Online for Central Bank Apprentice 2026?

The application is online only through a two-portal process. Follow these steps:

  1. First, register yourself on the NATS portal (link in Essential Links below) and note your NATS Enrolment ID.
  2. After login, find the Central Bank of India apprenticeship advertisement and apply to it.
  3. You will then receive an email to register on the BFSI SSC (BEEP) portal to complete the final application.
  4. Fill your category and district choices, upload your photo, signature and documents.
  5. Pay the application fee online before 22 June 2026 and submit your form.

Important Dates — Central Bank Apprentice 2026

Application Fees — Central Bank Apprentice 2026

The fee must be paid online only through the BFSI SSC portal. Once paid, the fee is not refunded.

Essential Links — Central Bank Apprentice 2026

  • Download Official Notification PDF
  • Register on NATS Portal
  • Apply Online on BFSI SSC (BEEP) Portal
  • Visit Central Bank of India Recruitment Page

This Central Bank of India apprenticeship is a great first step for graduates who want to enter the banking world while earning a steady stipend. With 4,500 seats and a simple online process, your chances are good — just make sure you register on NATS early and complete both portal steps before 22 June 2026. For more banking and government job updates, keep visiting GovtJobsAlert.in. We wish you all the best for your banking career.
