Age Limit (Post-Wise)

The maximum age differs for each post (as shown below). The usual age relaxation applies on top of these limits — 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and up to 10 years for PwBD candidates, as per government rules. Age is calculated as on the normal closing date.

What is the Salary for UPSC 06/2026 Posts?

Salary depends on the Pay Level of each post under the 7th Pay Commission. Most posts in this drive fall between Level-7 and Level-13, which is a strong central-government pay package.

  • Junior-level posts begin around Level-7 (entry pay near ₹44,900 per month).
  • Senior specialist, scientific and medical posts go up to Level-12 / Level-13, with much higher pay plus allowances.

In-hand pay, along with Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance and other central benefits, makes these posts financially very attractive. The exact Pay Level for your chosen post is mentioned in the notification.

What is the Selection Process for UPSC 06/2026?

UPSC fills these posts by Recruitment by Selection, so there is usually no common written test. The steps are:

  1. Submit your Online Recruitment Application (ORA) for the post you are eligible for.
  2. UPSC screens and shortlists candidates based on qualification, experience and the marks given to your bio-data.
  3. Shortlisted candidates are called for an interview / recruitment test before the Commission.
  4. Final selection is based on overall performance, followed by document verification.

For posts that require a recruitment test, you can plan your preparation using the resources on our exam calendar and study section.

How to Apply Online for UPSC Special Recruitment Drive 06/2026?

The application is fully online through the UPSC ORA portal. Follow these steps:

  1. Visit the official UPSC Online Recruitment Application portal (link in Essential Links below).
  2. Complete the One Time Registration (OTR) if you have not done it earlier.
  3. Log in, open Advertisement No. 06/2026 and select the post you are eligible for.
  4. Fill your personal, educational and experience details carefully and upload your documents.
  5. Pay the application fee online, then submit and take a printout of your final application.

Important Dates — UPSC 06/2026

Application Fees — UPSC 06/2026

Payment is made online using Visa/Master/RuPay Credit or Debit Card, UPI, or internet banking. The fee is very low, so it should not stop anyone from applying.

Essential Links — UPSC Special Recruitment Drive 06/2026

  • Official Notification Page
  • Apply Online for UPSC 06/2026 (ORA Portal)
  • Visit UPSC Official Website

This is a rare chance to enter central government service across many departments at once, so go through the post-wise list and apply for every post you are eligible for before 3 July 2026. Prepare your documents early and apply without waiting for the last day. For more such updates, keep visiting GovtJobsAlert.in and check our latest UPSC jobs notifications regularly. Your central government career could begin right here.

title: BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026: 454 Pharmacy Officer Posts in Punjab
description: BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026 — 454 Pharmacy Officer Posts in Punjab Great news for pharmacy students in Punjab! Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS), Faridkot has
jobDescription:
BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026 — 454 Pharmacy Officer Posts in Punjab

Great news for pharmacy students in Punjab! Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS), Faridkot has released a notification to recruit 454 Pharmacists (Pharmacy Officers) for the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Punjab. This is a permanent Group-C state government job with a good pay scale, and it is one of the biggest medical and healthcare job openings in Punjab this year. If you hold a D.Pharm or B.Pharm and are registered with the Punjab Pharmacy Council, this is a great chance for a stable government career.

The online applications are invited from 11 June 2026 to 1 July 2026. Don’t worry about the process — in this article, explains the vacancies, eligibility, salary, selection process and fees in very simple words, so you can apply with full confidence. If you are searching for government jobs in Punjab, read this article fully before applying.

Key Highlights — BFUHS Pharmacist 2026

This recruitment is for the post of Pharmacist (Pharmacy Officer) across health institutions in Punjab, with a strong demand for trained pharmacy professionals.

  • Organisation: Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS), Faridkot
  • Post Name: Pharmacist (Pharmacy Officer)
  • Total Vacancies: 454 posts
  • Pay Level: Level-5 (starting pay ₹29,200)
  • Job Location: Across Punjab
  • Last Date to Apply: 1 July 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — BFUHS Pharmacist 2026

The 454 posts include 348 fresh posts and 106 backlog posts, reserved across various Punjab categories.

The grand total is 454 posts, out of which 118 posts are horizontally reserved for women candidates.

Eligibility Criteria — BFUHS Pharmacist 2026

You must meet all the qualification conditions below before the last date of application.

Educational Qualification

  • Passed 10+2 (Senior Secondary) with Science from a recognised board.
  • Hold a Diploma in Pharmacy (D.Pharm) or Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharm) or Pharm-D from a recognised institution — medical and healthcare qualification holders are encouraged to .
  • Registered as a Pharmacist with the Punjab Pharmacy Council.
  • Must have passed Matriculation with Punjabi (a Punjabi language qualifying test of Matric standard, minimum 50%, is mandatory).

Age Limit (as on 1 January 2026)

  • Minimum Age: 18 years
  • Maximum Age: 37 years
  • Age Relaxation: 5 years for SC/BC, 10 years for Physically Handicapped, and other relaxations as per Punjab Government rules.

What is the Salary for BFUHS Pharmacist 2026?

The post comes under Pay Level-5 of the 7th Pay Commission, which gives a comfortable and secure income.

  • Starting Basic Pay: ₹29,200 per month (Pay Level-5).
  • The pay scale runs up to about ₹92,300, plus Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance and other state benefits.

A starting pay of ₹29,200 with full government allowances and job security makes this Pharmacist post one of the most reliable healthcare careers in Punjab.

Beyond the basic pay, you also get regular increments, medical benefits, pension benefits as per rules, and the respect that comes with a permanent state government job. For many D.Pharm and B.Pharm holders who are currently working in private medical stores or hospitals on low salaries, this is a chance to move into a secure, well-paid role with fixed working hours and steady growth. The Health Department posts pharmacists at sub-centres, dispensaries, primary health centres and community health centres, so you may get a posting close to your home district.

What is the Selection Process for BFUHS Pharmacist 2026?

Selection is based on a 100-mark formula, with no interview. The stages are:

  1. Written Examination of 90 marks conducted by BFUHS, Faridkot (minimum 33% qualifying marks needed).
  2. Experience marks of 10 marks as per the Punjab Government rules.
  3. Punjabi Language Test of Matriculation standard (minimum 50%) — this is a mandatory qualifying test.
  4. Final merit list is prepared, followed by document verification and police verification.

The written examination usually tests your core pharmacy subjects — pharmacology, pharmaceutics, pharmaceutical chemistry, drug store management and basic health knowledge — along with general awareness. Since experience carries 10 marks, candidates who have worked in hospitals or pharmacies get an added advantage, but freshers can still score well by focusing on the 90-mark written paper. Remember, the Punjabi language test is only qualifying in nature, so prepare it seriously but don’t panic about it. To prepare well for the written paper, check our syllabus and study material section for guidance and revise your D.Pharm or B.Pharm notes thoroughly.

How to for BFUHS Pharmacist 2026?

Applications are accepted online only. Follow these simple steps:

  1. Visit the official BFUHS recruitment portal (link in Essential Links below).
  2. Register with your email and mobile number, then log in.
  3. Fill in your personal, educational and Punjab Pharmacy Council registration details.
  4. Upload your photograph, signature and required documents.
  5. Pay the application fee online and submit your form before 1 July 2026. Take a printout for your records.

Important Dates — BFUHS Pharmacist 2026

Application Fees — BFUHS Pharmacist 2026

The fee includes 18% GST and is non-refundable. Candidates applying for more than one post must apply separately with separate fees.

Essential Links — BFUHS Pharmacist 2026

  • Download PDF
  • for BFUHS Pharmacist 2026
  • Visit BFUHS

With 454 posts on offer, this is one of the best opportunities for pharmacy professionals in Punjab to secure a stable government job. Make sure your Punjab Pharmacy Council registration is ready and before 1 July 2026 — don’t wait for the last day. For more Punjab government job notifications and updates, keep visiting . Best wishes for your healthcare career.

jobDescription:
NABARD Internship 2026 — Earn ₹20,000 Stipend While You Study

Here is a wonderful short-term opportunity for postgraduate students! The National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) has announced its Student Internship Scheme (SIS) 2026-27 with 39 internship seats across the country. Interns get hands-on exposure to rural development and agricultural finance work, along with a healthy monthly stipend. If you are pursuing a master’s degree in agriculture, economics, social sciences or management, this is a great way to add real experience to your resume. Key Highlights — NABARD Internship 2026

This internship is a learning programme, not a permanent job, but it gives you valuable experience, a good stipend and a strong line on your CV for future banking and development-sector careers.

NABARD is the apex development bank of India for agriculture and rural development, and it works closely with farmers, self-help groups, rural cooperatives and farmer producer organisations across the country. During this internship, you will study a real development theme — such as women farmers in Indian agriculture, the role of computerised cooperative societies, the impact of farmer producer organisations, GI registration, or rural women entrepreneurs. You will work under experienced mentors, visit the field, and prepare a proper project report. For a postgraduate student, this kind of structured exposure is hard to get elsewhere, and it can open doors to careers in banking, rural development, agribusiness and policy research. Even the experience of clearing NABARD’s selection and working at its offices adds real weight to your resume for future exams and jobs.

What is the Selection Process for NABARD Internship 2026?

description: GSSSB Female Health Worker Recruitment 2026 — 155 Posts for Women in Gujarat The Gujarat Subordinate Service Selection Board (GSSSB) has released Advertisement No. 416/202627 to re
jobDescription:
GSSSB Female Health Worker Recruitment 2026 — 155 Posts for Women in Gujarat

The Gujarat Subordinate Service Selection Board (GSSSB) has released Advertisement No. 416/202627 to recruit 155 Female Health Workers (FHW), Class-III under the Health and Family Welfare Department, Gujarat. This is a female-only recruitment and a wonderful opportunity for women with an ANM or Female Health Worker qualification to get a secure Gujarat Government job. If you are searching for medical and healthcare government jobs, this is a great chance to serve in community health and build a stable career.

Key Highlights — GSSSB Female Health Worker 2026

This is a direct recruitment to a female-only paramedical Class-III cadre, posted at health centres across Gujarat.

Female Health Workers are the backbone of rural and community health care, handling immunisation, maternal and child health, family welfare and basic health services in villages and towns. This is a women-only recruitment, which means eligible female candidates compete only with each other, improving their chances of selection. With 155 posts on offer and a fixed starting pay of ₹26,000, this is a meaningful and secure career for women who have completed an ANM or Female Health Worker course and are registered with the Gujarat Nursing Council. The Gujarat Subordinate Service Selection Board fills these posts through a fair, computer-based merit exam, so dedicated preparation directly improves your result. Beyond the salary and job security, the role lets you serve mothers, children and families in your own community — work that is both respected and deeply satisfying.

  • Organisation: Gujarat Subordinate Service Selection Board (GSSSB)
  • Advertisement No.: 416/202627
  • Post Name: Female Health Worker (FHW), Class-III
  • Total Vacancies: 155 posts (women only)
  • Pay: ₹26,000 fixed (then Pay Level-2)
  • Last Date to Apply: 29 June 2026

Vacancy Breakdown — GSSSB Female Health Worker 2026

The 155 posts are reserved across Gujarat Government categories (all posts are for women).

The grand total is 155 posts. As this is a female-only cadre, all posts are reserved for women candidates; PwBD posts will be filled through a separate special drive.

Eligibility Criteria — GSSSB Female Health Worker 2026

Only women candidates can . The eligibility is based on your health-worker qualification.

Educational Qualification

  • Passed SSC (10th) or HSC (12th) from a recognised board — open to 12th pass candidates.
  • Completed the Female Health Worker Basic Training course OR the Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM) course with registration in the Gujarat Nursing Council.
  • Basic computer knowledge and adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi (or both).

Age Limit (as on 29 June 2026)

  • Minimum Age: 18 years
  • Maximum Age: 40 years (relaxable up to 45 years)
  • Age Relaxation: 5 years extra for reserved-category women, with further relaxation for women with disability, as per Gujarat rules.

What is the Salary for GSSSB Female Health Worker 2026?

The post offers a fixed pay during probation, followed by a regular pay scale.

  • First 5 years: a fixed monthly pay of ₹26,000.
  • After 5 years: regular appointment in Pay Level-2 (₹19,900 – ₹63,200) with full allowances.

A fixed pay of ₹26,000 from the start, rising to a regular pay scale, along with the chance to serve your community, makes this Female Health Worker post a meaningful and secure career for women in Gujarat.

What is the Selection Process for GSSSB Female Health Worker 2026?

Selection is through a single-stage computer-based competitive exam (CBRT/OMR) of 210 marks:

  1. Part-A (90 marks): Reasoning & Data Interpretation (30), Quantitative Aptitude (30), and Constitution of India, Current Affairs, Gujarati & English comprehension (30).
  2. Part-B (120 marks): questions on community health nursing, midwifery, child health and related health topics.
  3. The exam is for 3 hours, with negative marking of 1/4 mark for each wrong answer.
  4. A provisional answer key is published, objections are invited, and then the final result is declared on merit.

You can plan your preparation using our syllabus and study resources on

How to for GSSSB Female Health Worker 2026?

The application is online only through the OJAS portal. Follow these steps:

  2. Select GSSSB and Advertisement No. 416/202627 (Female Health Worker).
  3. Fill your personal and educational details, then upload your photo and signature.
  4. Confirm your application to generate the confirmation number.
  5. Pay the exam fee online before 1 July 2026 and print your application.

Important Dates — GSSSB Female Health Worker 2026

Application Fees — GSSSB Female Health Worker 2026

The fee is paid online through OJAS. Candidates who appear in the exam get the fee refunded as per board rules.

  • First 5 years: a fixed monthly pay of ₹40,800.
  • After 5 years: regular appointment in Pay Level-6 (₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400) with full allowances.

A fixed pay of ₹40,800 from day one, rising to a regular Level-6 scale, makes this X-Ray Technician post one of the most attractive paramedical jobs in Gujarat.

What is the Selection Process for GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026?

Selection is through a single-stage computer-based competitive exam (CBRT/OMR) of 210 marks:

  1. Part-A (90 marks): Reasoning & Data Interpretation (30), Quantitative Aptitude (30), and Constitution of India, Current Affairs, Gujarati & English comprehension (30).
  2. Part-B (120 marks): questions on your subject (X-Ray Technician / Radiography).
  3. The exam is for 3 hours, with negative marking of 1/4 mark for each wrong answer.
  4. A provisional answer key is published, objections are invited, and then the final result is declared on merit.

You can plan your preparation using our syllabus and study resources on

How to for GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026?

The application is online only through the OJAS portal. Follow these steps:

  1. Visit the official OJAS application portal (link in Essential Links below).
  2. Select GSSSB and Advertisement No. 424/202627 (X-Ray Technician).
  3. Fill your personal and educational details, then upload your photo and signature.
  4. Confirm your application to generate the confirmation number.
  5. Pay the exam fee online before 1 July 2026 and print your application.

Important Dates — GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026

Application Fees — GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026

The fee is paid online through OJAS. Candidates who appear in the exam get the fee refunded as per board rules.

Essential Links — GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026

  • Download PDF
  • at OJAS for GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026
  • Visit GSSSB

Make sure your X-Ray Technician certificate is ready and apply on OJAS before 29 June 2026 — don’t wait for the last day. For more Gujarat government job notifications, keep visiting . All the best for your exam.

jobDescription:

Hindi Result | Maths Result | Science Result | Sanskrit Result | Home Science Result | Commerce Result | English Result | Biology Result | Urdu Result | Arts Result

UPPSC TGT Recruitment 2025: An Overview

The Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC) has officially announced the much-awaited UPPSC Assistant Teacher (Trained Graduate Teacher – TGT) Recruitment 2025. This is a significant opportunity for aspiring educators to secure a prestigious government job in Uttar Pradesh. With a massive 7466 vacancies for both Male and Female candidates across various subjects, this recruitment drive aims to fill teaching positions in government colleges. The application window is open from July 28, 2025, to August 28, 2025. This guide provides a complete breakdown of the notification, including eligibility, salary, syllabus, and a step-by-step application process.

Key Highlights: UPPSC TGT Notification 2025

The Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC) is the state’s premier recruiting body, responsible for conducting transparent and fair examinations for various government posts. This TGT recruitment is a golden chance for candidates with a graduation degree and a B.Ed. to build a stable and rewarding career in the education sector.

UPPSC TGT Vacancy 2025 Breakdown

A total of 7466 vacancies have been announced. This comprises 7385 posts for the Male and Female branches in Government Colleges and 81 posts under the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities. The subject-wise distribution is as follows:

TGT Vacancies (Male Branch) – 4860 Posts

TGT Vacancies (Female Branch) – 2525 Posts

UPPSC TGT Eligibility Criteria 2025

Candidates must carefully review the eligibility criteria regarding education and age limit before applying.

What Is the Educational Qualification for UPPSC TGT 2025?

The subject-wise educational qualification is detailed below. A B.Ed. degree from an NCTE-recognized institution is mandatory for most posts.

  • Assistant Teacher (Hindi): Graduation with Hindi and Intermediate with Sanskrit OR equivalent. Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (English): Graduation with English Literature. Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (Maths): Graduation with Mathematics. Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (Science): Graduation with Physics and Chemistry. Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (Social Science): Graduation with at least two subjects from History, Geography, Political Science, and Economics. Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (Biology): Graduation with Zoology and Botany. Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (Sanskrit): Graduation with Sanskrit. Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (Art): Graduation with Art OR Graduation in Fine Arts (BFA). B.Ed. is a preferential qualification for BFA holders.
  • Assistant Teacher (Music): Graduation with Music OR Sangeet Vishrad/Prabhakar with a Graduation degree. Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (Commerce): Graduation in Commerce (B.Com). Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (Urdu): Graduation with Urdu. Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (Agriculture/Horticulture): Graduation in Agriculture/Horticulture. Plus B.Ed. (Male candidates only).
  • Assistant Teacher (Home Science): Graduation with Home Science. Plus B.Ed.
  • Assistant Teacher (Physical Education): Graduation with B.P.Ed. or B.P.E.
  • Assistant Teacher (Computer): B.E./B.Tech in Computer Science OR Graduate in Computer Science/Application OR Graduation with ‘A’ Level from NIELIT.

Age Limit (as of July 1, 2025)

  • Minimum Age: 21 years
  • Maximum Age: 40 years
  • Candidates must not have been born earlier than July 2, 1985, and not later than July 1, 2004.

Age Relaxation:

  • SC/ST/OBC/State Govt. Employees of U.P.: 5 years
  • Physically Handicapped (PH) of U.P.: 15 years
  • Ex-Army Personnel of U.P.: 3 years

What Is the Salary for UPPSC TGT 2025?

The UPPSC Assistant Teacher (TGT) post offers a highly attractive salary package under Pay Level-7 of the 7th Pay Commission. The pay scale is ₹44,900 – ₹1,42,400 with a Grade Pay of ₹Let’s break down the approximate monthly gross salary:

  • Basic Pay: ₹44,900
  • Dearness Allowance (DA): DA is revised semi-annually. Assuming a DA rate of 50% (as per current trends), the amount would be ₹22,450.
  • House Rent Allowance (HRA): HRA varies based on the city of posting (X, Y, Z categories). For cities in Uttar Pradesh, it can range from ₹3,600 to ₹7,200 or more.
  • Transport Allowance (TA): A fixed allowance for commuting.
  • Other Allowances: As per government rules.

The initial gross monthly salary is expected to be ₹70,000 – ₹75,000+, making it a financially rewarding career. In addition, employees are entitled to benefits under the National Pension System (NPS), medical facilities, paid leaves, and other perks associated with a government job.

UPPSC TGT Selection Process 2025 Explained

The selection process for the UPPSC TGT post will be conducted in two main stages:

Stage 1: Preliminary Examination (Objective Type)

This is a screening test to shortlist candidates for the Main examination.

  • Total Questions: 150
  • Total Marks: 300
  • Duration: 2 Hours
  • Question Split: 30 questions from General Studies and 120 questions from the candidate’s chosen optional subject.
  • Negative Marking: Yes, 1/3rd (0.33) of the marks assigned to the question will be deducted for each wrong answer.

Stage 2: Main Examination (Written/Descriptive)

Candidates who qualify the Prelims will appear for the Main exam.

  • Total Questions: 20
  • Total Marks: 200
  • Duration: 3 Hours
  • Question Split:
    • Section A: 10 Short Answer Questions (125-word limit, 8 marks each).
    • Section B: 10 Long Answer Questions (200-word limit, 12 marks each).
  • The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the Main Examination.

Detailed UPPSC TGT Syllabus 2025

A thorough understanding of the syllabus is key to success. Here is a summary of the topics for the Preliminary and Main examinations.

How to for UPPSC TGT Recruitment 2025

Candidates must have a One Time Registration (OTR) number before applying. If you don’t have one, get it from the OTR portal at least 72 hours before applying.

Documents to Keep Ready:

  • Scanned Photograph and Signature.
  • OTR Number.
  • Valid Email ID and Mobile Number.
  • Educational Qualification Marksheets and Certificates.
  • Category Certificate (if applicable).
  • ID Proof (Aadhaar, Voter ID, etc.).

Step-by-Step Application Guide:

  1. Visit the official UPPSC website (link provided below)
  2. On the homepage, go to the ‘ALL NOTIFICATIONS/ADVERTISEMENTS’ section.
  3. Find the link for “Assistant Teacher, Trained Graduate Grade (Male/Female Branch) Examination -2025” and click ‘Apply’.
  4. Authenticate using your OTR number and password/OTP.
  5. Your personal details will be auto-filled from OTR. Fill in the required educational qualifications for the TGT post.
  6. Proceed to the payment gateway and pay the application fee using Net Banking, Card, or other online modes.
  7. After successful payment, review your application form carefully.
  8. Submit the final application form and take a printout for your records.

Important Dates for UP TGT 2025

What Is the Application Fee for UPPSC TGT 2025?

Essential Links — UPPSC TGT Recruitment 2025

This recruitment is a monumental step for many teaching aspirants. With a clear strategy, dedicated preparation, and timely application, you can secure your position as an Assistant Teacher in Uttar Pradesh. Don’t miss this chance and begin your preparation today!

Your Ultimate Guide to the IBPS RRB 2025 Recruitment Drive

The Institute of Banking Personnel Selection (IBPS) has officially released the much-awaited notification for the IBPS RRB 2025 for Officer & Office Assistant Recruitment (CRP RRBs-XIV). This is a premier opportunity for aspirants seeking a stable and rewarding career in the banking sector. Offering thousands of vacancies across various Regional Rural Banks (RRBs), this recruitment drive opens doors to roles like Office Assistant (Multipurpose) and Officers in Scale I, II, and III. With an attractive salary package and significant career growth prospects, this is your chance to secure a prestigious government job. The online application process begins on September 1, 2025, and concludes on September 28, 2025.

Key Highlights of IBPS RRB Notification 2025

The Institute of Banking Personnel Selection (IBPS) is an autonomous body that conducts the Common Recruitment Process (CRP) for Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) annually. This process standardizes the selection for Group ‘A’ Officers and Group ‘B’ Office Assistants, ensuring a transparent and merit-based recruitment. The CRP RRBs-XIV aims to fill numerous positions across the country, empowering rural banking services and providing stable employment. This guide provides all essential details to help you navigate the application and selection journey successfully.

IBPS RRB 2025 Vacancy Breakdown

The notification has announced indicative vacancies across different posts. Here is a summary of the available positions:

IBPS RRB Eligibility Criteria 2025

Before applying, it is crucial to ensure you meet all the eligibility norms set by IBPS.

What are the Educational Qualifications for IBPS RRB 2025 Positions?

  • Office Assistant (Multipurpose): A Bachelor’s degree in any discipline from a recognized university. Proficiency in the local language of the respective RRB is mandatory. Working knowledge of computers is desirable.
  • Officer Scale-I (Assistant Manager): A Bachelor’s degree in any field. Preference will be given to candidates with degrees in Agriculture, Horticulture, Forestry, Animal Husbandry, Veterinary Science, Agricultural Engineering, Pisciculture, Agricultural Marketing, Cooperation, IT, Management, Law, Economics, or Accountancy. Proficiency in the local language is required.
  • Officer Scale-II (General Banking Officer): A Bachelor’s degree in any discipline with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate. Candidates must have two years of experience as an officer in a Bank or Financial Institution.
  • Officer Scale-II (Specialist Officers):
    • IT Officer: Bachelor’s degree in Electronics/Communication/Computer Science/IT with 50% marks and one year of relevant experience.
    • Chartered Accountant: Certified Associate (CA) from the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India with one year of experience.
    • Law Officer: Degree in Law with 50% marks and two years of experience as an advocate or law officer.
    • Treasury Manager: CA or MBA in Finance with one year of relevant experience.
    • Marketing Officer: MBA in Marketing with one year of relevant experience.
    • Agricultural Officer: Bachelor’s degree in Agriculture/Horticulture/Dairy/Veterinary Science or related fields with 50% marks and two years of experience.
  • Officer Scale-III (Senior Manager): A Bachelor’s degree in any discipline with a minimum of 50% marks and five years of experience as an officer in a Bank or Financial Institution.

What is the Age Limit for IBPS RRB 2025 Recruitment?

  • For Office Assistant (Multipurpose): Between 18 and 28 years.
  • For Officer Scale-I (Assistant Manager): Between 18 and 30 years.
  • For Officer Scale-II (Manager): Between 21 and 32 years.
  • For Officer Scale-III (Senior Manager): Between 21 and 40 years.
  • Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories (SC/ST: 5 years, OBC: 3 years, PwBD: 10 years) as per government rules.

IBPS RRB Salary and Benefits in 2025

A career in Regional Rural Banks is not only stable but also financially rewarding. The salary structure is based on bipartite agreements and is periodically revised. The in-hand salary for an IBPS RRB Office Assistant is approximately ₹35,000 – ₹40,000 per month, while for an Officer Scale-I, it ranges from ₹50,000 – ₹55,000 per month, varying based on the location and specific RRB policies.

The salary is composed of several components. The Basic Pay is the core of the salary. On top of this, employees receive a Dearness Allowance (DA), which is revised quarterly based on the Consumer Price Index, to offset the impact of inflation. House Rent Allowance (HRA) is another key component, which varies depending on the posting location (rural, semi-urban, or urban). Other significant benefits include Travel Allowance (TA), Medical Reimbursements, and contributions to the National Pension System (NPS), ensuring a secure future post-retirement. These comprehensive benefits make a job with RRBs a highly attractive proposition.

How is the IBPS RRB 2025 Selection Process Structured?

The selection process for IBPS RRB varies depending on the post. It is designed to test a candidate’s aptitude, knowledge, and suitability for the banking environment.

What is the Selection Process for IBPS RRB Office Assistant 2025?

The process involves two online examination stages:

  1. Preliminary Exam: This is a qualifying exam consisting of two sections: Reasoning and Numerical Ability/Quantitative Aptitude. Candidates must clear the sectional and overall cut-offs to be shortlisted for the Main Exam.
  2. Main Exam: The scores from this exam determine the final selection for Office Assistants. For Officer Scale-I, the scores are used for shortlisting for the Interview round.
  3. Interview (Only for Officer Scale-I): Shortlisted candidates are called for an interview. The final merit list is prepared based on the combined scores of the Main Exam and Interview in an 80:20 ratio.

What is the Selection Process for IBPS RRB Officers 2025?

The selection for these senior posts is a two-stage process:

  1. Single Level Examination: A single online exam is conducted to test professional knowledge and general banking aptitude.
  2. Interview: Candidates who clear the online exam are called for a personal interview. The final selection is based on the combined performance in the exam and interview, with an 80:20 weightage.

Negative Marking: For all objective tests, there is a penalty for wrong answers. One-fourth (0.25) of the marks assigned to a question will be deducted for each incorrect response.

Detailed IBPS RRB Syllabus 2025

To excel in the examination, a thorough understanding of the syllabus is essential. Here is a subject-wise breakdown of important topics:

How to for IBPS RRB 2025: Step-by-Step Guide

Follow these steps carefully to submit your application form without any errors. Before you begin, ensure you have scanned copies of your photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration.

  1. Visit the official IBPS website or use the direct link provided below.
  2. On the homepage, click on the link “CRP for RRBs”.
  3. Select the appropriate link: “ ONLINE FOR CRP- RRBs-OFFICERS” or “ ONLINE FOR CRP-RRBs- OFFICE ASSISTANTS”.
  4. Click on “CLICK HERE FOR NEW REGISTRATION” and enter your basic information like name, mobile number, and email ID.
  5. A provisional registration number and password will be generated and sent to your registered mobile number and email.
  6. Log in using these credentials and fill in the detailed application form with your personal, educational, and professional details.
  7. Upload the required documents as per the specified format and size.
  8. Choose your preferred state and examination centers.
  9. Preview your application form to verify all the details before final submission.
  10. Proceed to the payment gateway and pay the application fee online.
  11. After successful payment, a confirmation e-receipt will be generated. Download and take a printout of the final application form and the e-receipt for your records.

IBPS RRB 2025 Important Dates

Application Fees for IBPS RRB 2025

The application fee must be paid online. The category-wise fee structure is as follows:

Essential Links for IBPS RRB Recruitment 2025

This recruitment is a significant step towards achieving your dream of a banking career. With dedication and a structured preparation strategy, you can successfully clear the examination and secure your position. Start your preparation today and take a confident step towards a bright and secure future.

Good news for all candidates who cleared the LIC AAO 2025 Main Examination! The Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) has released the Interview Call Letter for the AAO recruitment If you have qualified the Main Exam and been shortlisted for the interview, you can now download your call letter from the official portal.

Download LIC AAO 2025 Interview Call Letter — You will need your Registration Number and Date of Birth / Password to log in and download the call letter. Make sure to carry a printed copy of the call letter along with all original documents to the interview venue.

LIC AAO Recruitment 2025: A Gateway to a Premier Career

The Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) has officially released the LIC AAO 2025 notification, inviting applications for 841 vacancies for the post of Assistant Administrative Officer (AAO) in both Generalist and Specialist cadres. This is a significant opportunity for graduates and professionals seeking a stable and lucrative career in the insurance sector. With an attractive approximate monthly salary of ₹1,26,000 in an ‘A’ Class city, this position offers financial security and immense growth prospects. The application process is entirely online and will be active from August 16, 2025, to September 8, 2025. If you aspire to join one of India’s most esteemed public sector undertakings, this is your moment to shine.

Key Highlights of the LIC AAO 2025 Recruitment Notification

The Life Insurance Corporation of India, a cornerstone of the nation’s financial sector, has announced its 32nd batch recruitment for Assistant Administrative Officers. This drive aims to induct bright and capable individuals into various departments to uphold LIC’s legacy of trust and service. The selection process is designed to be rigorous, ensuring that only the most deserving candidates join its ranks.

Detailed LIC AAO Vacancy Breakdown 2025

The recruitment drive offers a substantial number of positions across different specializations. Understanding the distribution of vacancies is crucial for applicants to make an informed choice.

Comprehensive Eligibility Criteria for LIC AAO 2025

Before applying, candidates must carefully review the eligibility criteria to ensure they meet all the requirements set by LIC. The cut-off date for eligibility is August 1, 2025.

Educational Qualification

  • For AAO (Generalist): A Bachelor’s Degree in any discipline from a recognized Indian University/Institution.
  • For Assistant Engineer (Civil): B.Tech/B.E. in Civil Engineering from an AICTE recognized institution.
  • For Assistant Engineer (Electrical): B.Tech/B.E. in Electrical Engineering from an AICTE recognized institution.
  • For AAO (Chartered Accountant): Bachelor’s Degree and must have passed the Final Examination of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) and be an Associate member.
  • For AAO (Company Secretary): Bachelor’s Degree and be a Qualified Member of the Institute of Company Secretaries of India (ICSI).
  • For AAO (Actuarial): Bachelor’s Degree in any discipline and must have passed at least 6 papers of the examination conducted by the Institute of Actuaries of India / Institute and Faculty of Actuaries, UK.
  • For AAO (Insurance Specialist): Bachelor’s Degree in any discipline with a Fellowship of the Insurance Institute of India (Life).
  • For AAO (Legal): Bachelor’s degree in Law with a minimum of 50% marks (45% for SC/ST/PwBD).

Experience

  • For Assistant Engineer (Civil/Electrical): Minimum three years of post-qualification work experience in planning and execution of multi-storied building projects.
  • For AAO (Insurance Specialist): Minimum five years of experience working in Life Insurance Companies regulated by IRDAI.
  • For AAO (Legal): At least two years of experience as an Advocate or as a Law Officer in a legal department after enrollment with the Bar Council.

Age Limit (as on 01.08.2025)

  • Minimum Age: 21 years (completed).
  • Maximum Age: 30 years (Candidates born not earlier than 02.08.1995 and not later than 01.08.2004).
  • For AAO (Chartered Accountant / Legal): Maximum age is 32 years.

Relaxations in the upper age limit are applicable for reserved categories as per government norms.

In-Depth Look at LIC AAO Salary and Benefits

A career as an LIC AAO is not just about the prestige; it comes with a highly competitive compensation package. The salary structure is designed to attract and retain top talent, making it one of the most sought-after positions in the public sector. The basic pay is ₹88,635/- per month in the scale of ₹ 88635-4385(14)-150025-4750(4)-169025.

This “Pay Level” or scale indicates how the salary progresses over the years with annual increments. But the take-home pay is significantly higher. The total emoluments at the minimum of the scale, including House Rent Allowance (HRA) and City Compensatory Allowance (CCA), will be approximately ₹1,26,000/- per month in an ‘A’ Class city.

Beyond the basic pay, employees are entitled to a plethora of allowances and benefits, including:

  • Dearness Allowance (DA): Adjusted quarterly based on the Consumer Price Index.
  • House Rent Allowance (HRA): Varies depending on the city of posting.
  • Transport Allowance (TA): To cover commuting expenses.
  • Defined Contributory Pension (NPS): A robust retirement savings plan.
  • Gratuity, LTC, and Cash Medical Benefit.
  • Group Insurance and Group Personal Accident Insurance.
  • Vehicle Loan (2-wheeler/4-wheeler) as per rules.
  • Other perks like meal coupons, reimbursement for briefcases, mobile handsets, and furniture.

The LIC AAO Selection Process Explained

The selection for LIC AAO is a multi-stage process designed to test a candidate’s aptitude, knowledge, and suitability for the role. It consists of three main phases.

Phase 1: Preliminary Examination

This is an online objective test and the first screening stage. It consists of three sections with separate timings. Marks from this exam are not counted for the final merit list, but candidates must qualify in each section to be shortlisted for the Main Examination.

  • Reasoning Ability: 35 Questions, 35 Marks, 20 Minutes
  • Quantitative Aptitude: 35 Questions, 35 Marks, 20 Minutes
  • English Language: 30 Questions, 30 Marks, 20 Minutes (Qualifying nature)

Phase 2: Main Examination

This is the most crucial stage. It consists of both objective and descriptive online tests. The marks obtained in the Main Examination are considered for shortlisting for the interview and for the final merit list.

  • Objective Test: Covers Reasoning, General Knowledge, Data Analysis & Interpretation, and Insurance & Financial Market Awareness. (Total 300 Marks, 2 Hours)
  • Descriptive Test: English Language (Letter Writing & Essay). (25 Marks, 30 Minutes, Qualifying nature)

Phase 3: Interview

Candidates who clear the Main Examination will be called for a personal interview. The interview carries 60 marks. The final merit list is prepared based on the combined scores of the Main Examination and the Interview.

Detailed Syllabus for LIC AAO 2025 Examination

A thorough understanding of the syllabus is the first step toward effective preparation. Here is a subject-wise breakdown of the topics.

How to : A Step-by-Step Guide

Follow these steps carefully to submit your application successfully. Before you begin, ensure you have scanned copies of your photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration.

  1. Visit the official LIC website and navigate to the “Careers” section.
  2. Click on the link for “Recruitment of AAO (Generalist/Specialist) – 2025”.
  3. Choose “Click here for New Registration” and enter your basic information.
  4. A provisional registration number and password will be generated.
  5. Log in and fill in the detailed application form with your personal, educational, and professional details.
  6. Upload the scanned documents as per the specified format and size.
  7. Verify all the details in the application form using the “Preview” tab before final submission.
  8. Proceed to the “Payment” tab and pay the application fee online.
  9. After successful payment, a confirmation e-receipt will be generated.
  10. Take a printout of the final application form and the e-receipt for your records.

Important Dates for LIC AAO 2025

Application Fees

The application fee must be paid online and is non-refundable.

Essential Links for Your Application

This recruitment is more than just a job; it’s a chance to build a distinguished career with one of India’s most trusted brands. Prepare diligently, stay focused, and seize this incredible opportunity to become an Assistant Administrative Officer at LIC. We wish you the very best in your journey!

jobDescription:
SBI Clerk 2025: Mains Exam Score Card Released — Download Now

Great news for SBI Clerk 2025 aspirants! The State Bank of India has released the Mains Examination Score Card for the Junior Associate (Clerk) 2025 recruitment. If you appeared in the SBI Clerk Mains exam, you can now download your individual score card from the official SBI website. The score card link is available for a limited period, so download it as soon as possible. Click here to download your SBI Clerk 2025 Mains Score Card.

SBI Clerk 2025 Notification: Your Guide to a Secure Banking Career

The State Bank of India (SBI) has announced its much-awaited SBI Clerk Recruitment 2025, inviting applications for the post of Junior Associate (Customer Support & Sales). This is a significant opportunity for graduates looking to build a career in the banking sector. With 5583 vacancies across the country, this recruitment drive offers a stable and well-paying government job. The starting salary for a Junior Associate in a metro city like Mumbai is approximately ₹46,000 per month, inclusive of all allowances. The application window is open from August 6, 2025, to August 26, 2025. This article provides a comprehensive guide to help you navigate the application process and prepare for the examination.

Key Highlights of SBI Clerk Recruitment 2025

The State Bank of India, a Fortune 500 company, is the largest public sector bank in India. A career with SBI is not just a job but a matter of prestige and security. The SBI Clerk (Junior Associate) role is a customer-facing position that involves handling various banking operations, including customer interactions, sales, and support. This recruitment is conducted in two phases: the Preliminary and Main examinations, followed by a local language test for selected candidates.

SBI Clerk Vacancy 2025: State-wise Breakdown

The vacancies are distributed across various states and union territories. The total of 5583 vacancies includes 5180 regular vacancies and 403 backlog vacancies. Candidates can apply for vacancies in only one state/UT.

SBI Clerk Eligibility Criteria 2025

Before applying, it is crucial to ensure that you meet the eligibility criteria set by SBI.

Educational Qualification

  • Graduation: A degree in any discipline from a recognized University or any equivalent qualification recognized as such by the Central Government.
  • Candidates having an integrated dual degree (IDD) certificate should ensure that the date of passing the IDD is on or before December 31, 2025.
  • Those in the final year/semester of their graduation may also apply provisionally, subject to the condition that, if selected, they will have to produce proof of having passed the graduation examination on or before December 31, 2025.
  • For Ex-servicemen: Matriculate Ex-servicemen who have obtained the Indian Army Special Certificate of Education or a corresponding certificate in the Navy or Air Force, after having completed not less than 15 years of service in the Armed Forces of the Union, are also eligible.

Experience

  • No prior experience is required for this post. Fresh graduates are encouraged to .

Age Limit (as on April 1, 2025)

  • Minimum Age: 20 years
  • Maximum Age: 28 years
  • Candidates must have been born not earlier than April 2, 1997, and not later than April 1, 2005 (both days inclusive).

Age Relaxation:

SBI Clerk Salary and Benefits: A Detailed Look

A job as an SBI Clerk offers a lucrative salary package and numerous benefits, making it a highly sought-after position. The starting basic pay for a Junior Associate is ₹26,730 (₹24,050 plus two advance increments admissible to graduates). The pay scale is ₹24050-1340/3-28070-1650/3-33020-2000/4-41020-2340/7-57400-4400/1-61800-2680/1-64480.

The total starting emoluments of a Clerical Cadre employee in a metro city like Mumbai will be around ₹46,000 per month. This includes:

  • Dearness Allowance (DA): This is revised quarterly based on the Consumer Price Index.
  • House Rent Allowance (HRA): This varies depending on the place of posting.
  • Other Allowances: These include transport allowance, special allowance, etc.

Apart from the salary, SBI Clerks are entitled to various perks and benefits, such as:

  • Provident Fund
  • Pension under the New Pension Scheme (NPS)
  • Medical Benefits for self and dependents
  • Leave Fare Concession (LFC)
  • Opportunities for career progression to the officer cadre.

SBI Clerk Selection Process 2025 Explained

The selection process for SBI Clerk consists of three phases:

Phase 1: Preliminary Examination

This is an online objective test of 100 marks and is of 1-hour duration. It is a qualifying examination, and the marks obtained will not be added for the final selection.

There will be negative marking of 1/4th of the mark assigned for each wrong answer.

Phase 2: Main Examination

Candidates who qualify in the Preliminary Examination will be shortlisted for the Main Examination. The marks obtained in the Main Examination will be considered for the final merit list.

There will be negative marking of 1/4th of the mark assigned for each wrong answer.

Phase 3: Test of Specified Opted Local Language

Candidates who qualify for selection and produce a 10th or 12th standard mark sheet/certificate evidencing having studied the specified opted local language will not be subjected to any language test. For others, a language test will be conducted after the Main Examination but before joining.

Detailed Syllabus for SBI Clerk 2025

How to for SBI Clerk Recruitment 2025: A Step-by-Step Guide

Follow these steps to for the SBI Clerk 2025 recruitment:

  1. Visit the : Go to the SBI careers page (link provided below)
  2. Find the Recruitment Notification: Look for “Recruitment of Junior Associates (Customer Support & Sales)” and click on “.”
  3. Register: Click on “New Registration” and provide your basic information like name, mobile number, and email ID. A provisional registration number and password will be generated.
  4. Fill the Application Form: Log in with your registration number and password and fill in the detailed application form with your personal, academic, and other details.
  5. Upload Documents: Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, left-hand thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration.
    • Handwritten Declaration Text: “I, _____ (Name of the candidate), Date of Birth _____, hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required. The signature, photograph and left thumb impression is of mine”.
  6. Preview and Submit: Carefully review your application form before final submission. No changes will be allowed after submission.
  7. Pay the Application Fee: Pay the application fee online using a debit card, credit card, or internet banking.
  8. Print the Application: After successful payment, take a printout of the e-receipt and the final application form for your records.

Documents Required for Application:

  • Scanned Photograph (4.5cm × 3.5cm)
  • Scanned Signature (with black ink)
  • Scanned Left Thumb Impression (on white paper with black or blue ink)
  • Scanned Handwritten Declaration (in English on white paper with black ink)
  • Valid Email ID and Mobile Number
  • Educational Qualification Certificates
  • Caste/Disability Certificate (if applicable)

Important Dates for SBI Clerk 2025

Application Fees for SBI Clerk 2025

Essential Links for SBI Clerk Recruitment 2025

This is a fantastic opportunity to start a promising career with the State Bank of India. Prepare well, stay focused, and seize this chance to secure a bright future. We wish you the very best in your endeavors.

Your Guide to the MPESB MP Police Subedar / SI Recruitment 2025

The Madhya Pradesh Employee Selection Board (MPESB) has released the for the much-awaited MP Police Subedar and Sub-Inspector (SI) Recruitment for This is a fantastic opportunity for young and dedicated individuals looking to build a career in the police force. The recruitment offers a handsome salary package, with a pay scale up to ₹1,14,800, making it a highly sought-after position. The application window is open from October 27, 2025, to November 10, This guide will provide you with all the essential information you need to know about the MPESB MP Police Subedar / SI Recruitment 2025, from vacancy details to the application process.

Key Highlights of the MP Police Subedar/SI Recruitment 2025

  • Post Names: Subedar, Sub-Inspector (GD-SAF), Sub-Inspector (GD-Other than SAF)
  • Total Vacancies: 500
  • Salary/Pay Scale: Level 9: ₹36,200 – ₹1,14,800
  • Job Location: Madhya Pradesh
  • Application Start Date: October 27, 2025
  • Application Last Date: November 10, 2025

The Madhya Pradesh Employee Selection Board (MPESB) is responsible for conducting various recruitment examinations for government departments in the state. This recruitment for the Police Headquarters, Home (Police) Department, aims to fill key positions in the police force with capable and deserving candidates.

MP Police Subedar/SI Vacancy Breakdown 2025

Here is a detailed breakdown of the vacancies for each post in the MPESB MP Police Recruitment 2025:

Eligibility Criteria for MP Police Subedar/SI Recruitment 2025

To be eligible for the MP Police Subedar/SI recruitment, candidates must meet the following criteria:

What Is the Educational Qualification for MPESB MP Police SI 2025?

  • Candidates must hold a Bachelor’s degree or its equivalent from a recognized university.

What Is the Age Limit for MPESB MP Police SI 2025?

  • The age of the candidates will be calculated as of November 10, 2025.
  • For Male Candidates (Unreserved): 33 years
  • For Female Candidates (All Categories): 38 years
  • For Male Candidates (Reserved Category – SC/ST/OBC): 38 years
  • Age relaxation is applicable as per government rules.

What Are the Physical Standards for MPESB MP Police SI 2025?

  • Height (Male): 167.5 cm or more
  • Height (Female): 152.4 cm or more
  • Chest (Male only): Minimum 81 cm (unexpanded) and 86 cm (expanded), with a minimum expansion of 5 cm.

What Is the Salary for MPESB MP Police SI 2025?

Candidates selected for the post of Subedar or Sub-Inspector in the Madhya Pradesh Police will be placed in Pay Level 9 of the pay matrix, with a salary ranging from ₹36,200 to ₹1,14,800 per month. This attractive salary is further enhanced by various allowances and benefits. “Pay Level” refers to the position of an employee in the salary structure as per the 7th Pay Commission recommendations.

In addition to the basic pay, the selected candidates will be entitled to:

  • Dearness Allowance (DA): This is a cost of living adjustment allowance.
  • House Rent Allowance (HRA): This allowance is provided for accommodation expenses.
  • Travelling Allowance (TA): This is provided to cover travel expenses for official duties.
  • National Pension System (NPS): A defined contribution pension system for government employees.

What Is the Selection Process for MPESB MP Police SI 2025?

The selection process for the MP Police Subedar/SI Recruitment 2025 is a two-stage process:

Stage 1: Preliminary Written Examination

  • This will be a Computer Based Test (CBT) with multiple-choice questions.
  • The exam will be for a total of 100 marks.
  • There will be no negative marking.
  • The subjects will include Hindi, English, Analytical Ability, History, Geography, Science, Civics, Basic Computer Knowledge, Reasoning, and Current Affairs.

Stage 2: Main Examination, Physical Proficiency Test, and Interview

  • Candidates who qualify in the preliminary exam will be called for the main examination.
  • The main exam will consist of two papers, each of 300 marks.
  • There will be negative marking of 1/3 for wrong answers in the main exam.
  • Followed by a Physical Proficiency Test (PPT) of 100 marks, which will include an 800-meter run, long jump, and shot put.
  • The final stage will be a personal interview of 50 marks.

How to for MPESB MP Police SI Recruitment 2025?

Before you start the application process, make sure you have the following documents ready:

  • Scanned copy of your recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  • Scanned copy of your handwritten declaration.
  • Scanned copies of your educational qualification certificates.
  • Valid mobile number and email ID.

Here’s a step-by-step guide to applying online:

  1. Visit the of MPESB. (link provided below)
  2. On the homepage, click on the “Online Form” tab.
  3. Find the link for the “Police Headquarters, Home (Police) Dept., Govt. of M.P. under Subedar/Sub-Inspector Cadre Recruitment Test – 2025” and click on it.
  4. Read the instructions carefully and proceed to fill out the application form.
  5. Enter your personal and educational details accurately.
  6. Upload the scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other required documents.
  7. Pay the application fee through the available online payment modes.
  8. Review your application form before final submission.
  9. Take a printout of the submitted application form for future reference.

Important Dates for MP Police Recruitment 2025

What Is the Application Fee for MPESB MP Police SI 2025?

BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Last Date Extended to 15 June 2026 — Latest Update

Good news for all applicants! Bank of Baroda has extended the last date to submit the online application and pay the fee for its Apprentice engagement (Addendum to Advt. No. BOB/HRM/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2026/01). You now get time until 15 June 2026 (23:59 hours) to complete your form — the earlier deadline of 8 June 2026 has been extended. If you have not applied yet, finish your NAPS / NATS registration and fee payment now, don’t wait for the last day. Read the official extension notice here: Download BoB Apprentice Last Date Extension Notice (PDF).

BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 — 5,000 Apprentice Vacancies for Graduates Across India

Bank of Baroda has released the BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 notification for the engagement of 5,000 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. This is one of the largest apprenticeship drives in the Indian banking sector this year, offering young graduates a foot in the door of one of the country’s biggest public sector banks. Selected candidates will receive a monthly stipend of Rs 15,000 during a 12-month apprenticeship period, plus practical exposure to real bank operations across all major banking jobs functions.

Online applications are open from 19 May 2026 to 8 June 2026 15 June 2026 (Extended) through the NAPS and NATS apprenticeship portals followed by BFSI SSC payment. The eligibility cut-off is simple — any graduate in any discipline aged between 20 and 28 years (as on 1 May 2026) can . This article from GovtJobsAlert.in gives you the full state-wise vacancy summary, stipend, fees, selection process and step-by-step apply guide for BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026.

Bank of Baroda Apprentice 2026 Vacancy, Stipend and Last Date at a Glance

Bank of Baroda is a leading public sector bank with thousands of branches across India and a strong international presence. Under the Apprentices Act 1961, the bank periodically engages graduates as Apprentices for 12 months of hands-on banking training. Apprentices are trainees, not employees of the bank — but the exposure makes you exam-ready for IBPS, SBI, RBI and other bank recruitment notifications.

  • Recruiting Body: Bank of Baroda (BoB)
  • Engagement: Apprentice under Apprentices Act, 1961
  • Total Vacancies: 5,000 (across 35 States and Union Territories)
  • Stipend: Rs 15,000 per month (12-month apprenticeship)
  • Minimum Qualification: Graduation Degree in any discipline from a recognised university
  • Age Limit: 20 to 28 years as on 1 May 2026
  • Application Mode: Online via NAPS / NATS portal + BFSI SSC payment
  • Last Date to Apply: 8 June 2026 15 June 2026 (Extended)

How Many Vacancies are in BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 State-Wise?

The 5,000 apprentice slots are distributed across 35 States and Union Territories with state-wise category reservation. The largest allocations are in Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh and Maharashtra, where Bank of Baroda has its strongest branch network.

Out of these 5,000 seats, 189 are horizontally reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) — 54 OC, 51 HI, 48 VI and 36 ID. PwBD reservation is horizontal and counts within the overall state quotas, not additional. The top three state allocations are:

Eligibility Criteria — BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Educational Qualification

Candidates must hold a Graduation Degree in any discipline from a recognised university or any equivalent qualification recognised by the Central Government. The discipline does not matter — Arts, Science, Commerce, Engineering and Management graduates are all eligible. For NATS-registered candidates, the graduation pass-out date must not be more than 4 years before 1 May 2026.

In addition, candidates must:

  • Register on NAPS (apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and/or NATS (nats.education.gov.in)
  • Be proficient in the local language of the chosen State / Union Territory (reading, writing, speaking, understanding)
  • Have no prior apprenticeship with Bank of Baroda or any other establishment
  • Have no more than 1 year of post-qualification work or training experience — freshers preferred

Age Limit

  • Minimum age: 20 years
  • Maximum age: 28 years (as on 1 May 2026)

Age relaxation: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (non-creamy layer), 10 years for PwBD (UR/EWS), 13 years for PwBD (OBC), 15 years for PwBD (SC/ST). Ex-Servicemen are not eligible for apprenticeship engagement.

What is the Salary for BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

Bank of Baroda Apprentices receive a flat monthly stipend of Rs 15,000 during the 12-month engagement. No other allowances, HRA, DA, bonus, leave encashment or transport allowance is payable. The stipend is paid each month after adjusting any Loss of Pay (LoP) days.

  • Monthly Stipend: Rs 15,000
  • Annual Stipend (12 months): Rs 1,80,000
  • Other allowances: None — no HRA, DA, transport, hostel or relocation support
  • Payment Mode: Direct bank transfer to apprentice’s savings account

You earn Rs 1,80,000 over the 12-month apprenticeship — a steady starter income.

Remember, this is not regular employment — apprentices have no claim on bank employment after completion. However, the 12-month real-world training is a strong asset on your CV when you appear for IBPS, SBI and RBI examinations.

What is the Selection Process for BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

The selection process has four stages in a fixed sequence. You must clear each stage to move to the next.

  1. Online Examination — 100 questions, 100 marks, 60 minutes, remote-proctored mode
  2. Document Verification — original certificate check
  3. Local Language Test — verifies proficiency in the State / UT language
  4. Medical Fitness — final fitness clearance for engagement

Online Exam Pattern

The exam is remote-proctored — you can sit from your home/laptop with a front camera, mic and stable internet. There is no negative marking, so attempt every question. Refer to the exam syllabus hub for similar 4-section bank exam preparation tips. Candidates exempted from the local language test are those whose 10th or 12th mark sheet shows the State’s specified language as a subject.

How to Apply Online for BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

The application is online only. There is a three-portal flow you must follow in order. Read each step carefully before clicking .

  1. Register on NAPS (apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) or NATS (nats.education.gov.in) Government of India apprenticeship portal.
  2. Search for the “Bank of Baroda” apprenticeship advertisement on NAPS / NATS and .
  3. Eligible candidates will receive an email from BFSI SSC inviting them to fill the final Application-cum-Examination Form.
  4. In the form, enter your personal details, choose up to 3 preferred training centres within one State / UT, and declare category / PwBD status.
  5. Make the online fee payment to BFSI SSC through the secure payment link in the form.
  6. Note down your Enrolment ID (NATS) and / or Apprentice Registration Code (NAPS) for future communication.
  7. Save the confirmation email and the completed Application Form printout.

Required documents include Aadhaar, PAN, passport-size photo, signature, Class 10 & 12 mark sheets, graduation mark sheet, savings bank passbook and caste/category certificates as applicable. All file uploads must be under 1 MB each.

What are the Important Dates for BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

Application Fees — BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Fees are payable online only through the BFSI SSC payment gateway integrated into the application form. The fee is non-refundable and is collected once your application reaches the BFSI SSC stage.

Essential Links — BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026

  • Download Official Notification PDF
  • Apply Online — BoB Apprentice 2026
  • Visit Bank of Baroda Official Website

BoB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is a strong early-career opportunity — 5,000 graduate slots, a Rs 15,000 monthly stipend, a clean 4-section online exam and real branch exposure with a top public sector bank. Confirm your application before 8 June 2026 15 June 2026 (Extended) and make sure your NAPS / NATS registration is complete in advance. Keep visiting GovtJobsAlert.in and our banking jobs page for the latest updates on the BoB exam date, admit card and result.

description: A Strong Gujarat Government Job for Engineering and Diploma Holders Good news for technically qualified youth of Gujarat! The Gujarat Subordinate Services Selection Board (GSSSB) has released the notification for Junior Industry Inspector, Class
jobDescription:
A Strong Gujarat Government Job for Engineering and Diploma Holders

Good news for technically qualified youth of Gujarat! The Gujarat Subordinate Services Selection Board (GSSSB) has released the notification for Junior Industry Inspector, Class-III under the Office of the Commissioner of Industries. There are 60 posts in total, and the selection is through a single computer-based examination. For engineering and diploma holders who want a stable Gujarat government job with a good pay scale, this is a chance worth grabbing.

This is a permanent state government post, so competition will be healthy — but with the right preparation you can make it. In this article we explain the vacancies, who can apply, the pay, the exam pattern, fees, and the exact apply steps in very simple language. If you are looking for the latest Gujarat government jobs, keep this GovtJobsAlert.in page open and read every section carefully.

Key Highlights — GSSSB Junior Industry Inspector 2026

The Junior Industry Inspector works under the Industries and Mines Department of Gujarat. The role suits candidates with an engineering or technology background. Here is a quick summary before the details:

  • Organisation: Gujarat Subordinate Services Selection Board (GSSSB)
  • Post: Junior Industry Inspector, Class-III
  • Total Vacancies: 60
  • Pay: ₹40,800 fixed per month (first 5 years), then Pay Level-5 (₹29,200 – ₹92,300)
  • Job Location: Gujarat
  • Last Date to Apply: 20 June 2026

Vacancy Details — Category-wise Breakup

The 60 posts are divided across categories as per Gujarat reservation rules. Women have 33% reservation included within each category. The table below shows the exact split from the official notification.

Ex-Servicemen have 6 posts reserved horizontally. PwD posts will be advertised separately under a special drive.

Eligibility Criteria — GSSSB Junior Industry Inspector 2026

Please read this section slowly, because the qualification rule has two paths and it is important to know which one fits you.

Educational Qualification

You are eligible if you hold either of these:

  • A Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or Technology (excluding Civil Engineering) from a recognised university, or
  • A Diploma in Engineering or Technology (excluding Civil Engineering) plus about two years of practical work as a technical worker.

Almost all branches are accepted — Mechanical, Electrical, Electronics, Computer, IT, Chemical, Instrumentation, Metallurgy, Automobile, and more — but Civil Engineering is not eligible. This is a great match for engineering and diploma graduates. You also need basic computer knowledge and adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi.

Age Limit

The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum is 35 years as on 20 June 2026. Age relaxation is given as per Gujarat government rules — for example, 5 years for general category women and reserved category candidates, and up to 10 years for reserved category women, with further relaxation for PwD and Ex-Servicemen candidates.

Salary & Pay Scale — How Much Will You Earn?

The pay structure here is attractive and grows over time. For the first five years, you receive a fixed monthly pay of ₹40,800. After completing five years of satisfactory service, you move to the regular 7th Pay Commission Pay Level-5 scale of ₹29,200 – ₹92,300, along with the usual allowances admissible to a Class-III government employee.

In simple words, you start with a healthy fixed salary and then settle into a full pay scale with regular increments. For a stable, respected Gujarat government job, this is a solid financial package that keeps improving with your service.

Selection Process — How Are Candidates Selected?

Selection is based on a single-stage competitive examination conducted by CBRT/OMR method, followed by document verification. There is no separate prelims and mains — just one objective exam that decides your merit. Candidates must score the minimum qualifying standard separately in both parts of the paper.

After the exam, a provisional answer key is released so candidates can raise objections, and then the final result and merit list are prepared. You can review the exam pattern and syllabus details to plan your preparation properly.

Exam Pattern

The single paper has two parts taken together in 3 hours (180 minutes):

  • Part-A (90 marks): Logical Reasoning & Data Interpretation (30), Quantitative/Mathematical tests (30), and Indian Constitution, Current Affairs, Gujarati & English Comprehension (30).
  • Part-B (120 marks): Questions on your concerned engineering/technology subject.

There are 210 questions for 210 marks in total. There is negative marking of 1/4 (0.25) mark for each wrong answer, and the minimum qualifying standard is 40% in each part for all categories.

How to Apply Online for GSSSB Junior Industry Inspector 2026?

The application is fully online through the OJAS portal. Follow these steps in order:

  1. Visit the official OJAS application portal (link in Essential Links below).
  2. Select GSSSB and then the Junior Industry Inspector advertisement.
  3. Fill in your personal and educational details and accept the declaration.
  4. Save the form to generate your Application Number, then upload your photo and signature.
  5. Confirm the application to get your Confirmation Number and take a printout.
  6. Pay the examination fee online before the fee deadline.

Apply early and do not wait for the last day to avoid any website rush.

Important Dates — GSSSB Junior Industry Inspector 2026

Note down each date so you stay on schedule.

Application Fees — GSSSB Junior Industry Inspector 2026

The fee is low and, even better, it is refundable to candidates who actually appear for the exam. Payment is online only through the OJAS portal.

A small online transaction fee applies in addition to the above. The exam fee is refunded to candidates who appear for the examination.

Essential Links — GSSSB Junior Industry Inspector 2026

  • Download Official Notification PDF
  • Apply Online for GSSSB Junior Industry Inspector 2026
  • Visit GSSSB Official Website

This GSSSB Junior Industry Inspector recruitment is a great chance to build a secure career with the Gujarat government. Start your preparation now, keep your documents ready, and apply online before 20 June 2026. title: PSPCL Assistant Lineman (ITI Based) Recruitment 2026: 3000 Posts, ₹19,900 Salary, by 9 July
description: A Big Power-Sector Opportunity for ITI Holders in Punjab Here is a massive recruitment that ITI holders in Punjab have been waiting for. Punjab State Power Corporation Limited (PSPCL) has announced 3000 posts of Assistant Lineman (ALM) under the
jobDescription:
A Big Power-Sector Opportunity for ITI Holders in Punjab

Here is a massive recruitment that ITI holders in Punjab have been waiting for. Punjab State Power Corporation Limited (PSPCL) has announced 3000 posts of Assistant Lineman (ALM) under the ITI-based route through Advertisement CRA 318/26. With thousands of vacancies, a secure government pay scale, and a simple eligibility of Matriculation plus an ITI certificate, this is one of the biggest openings of the year for technical youth.

PSPCL keeps the lights on across Punjab, and Assistant Linemen are the backbone of its field operations. If you have an ITI in Electrician or Wireman trade, you should not miss this. In this simple guide we cover the vacancies, eligibility, salary, exam pattern, fees, and the exact steps to . If you want to track all Punjab government jobs, bookmark this GovtJobsAlert.in page and read till the end.

Key Highlights — PSPCL Assistant Lineman (ITI) 2026

The Assistant Lineman post is a permanent Group-C role with PSPCL. Below are the key points before we go deeper:

  • Organisation: Punjab State Power Corporation Limited (PSPCL)
  • Post: Assistant Lineman (ALM) — ITI Based
  • Total Vacancies: 3000
  • Basic Pay: ₹19,900 per month
  • Job Location: Punjab
  • Last Date to Apply: 9 July 2026

Vacancy Details — Category-wise Breakup

All 3000 posts are for the single post of Assistant Lineman, divided across categories for Punjab domicile candidates. The main category split is shown below.

Around 1005 of these posts are reserved for women candidates as per Punjab rules.

Eligibility Criteria — PSPCL Assistant Lineman (ITI) 2026

Check the conditions below carefully before you fill the form. The eligibility here is simple and student-friendly.

Educational Qualification

You must have passed Matriculation (10th) or equivalent and hold an ITI certificate in Electrician trade or Wireman trade issued by NCVT or SCVT. This is a perfect opening for ITI certificate holders looking for government jobs. You must also have passed Punjabi at least at Matriculation level by the last date of application. You should have completed your ITI by the last date of online submission.

Age Limit

The age limit is 18 to 37 years as on 1 January 2026. Age relaxation is available as per Punjab government rules — 5 years for SC and Backward Class, 10 years for PwD candidates, and special relaxation for Ex-Servicemen and certain women candidates (upper age up to 40 years).

Salary & Pay Scale — How Much Will You Earn?

The Assistant Lineman post carries a basic pay of ₹19,900 per month as per the 7th CPC / Pay Matrix. During the probation period of 3 years, you are paid the minimum admissible pay along with travelling allowance and medical reimbursement as per entitlement. After probation, you become eligible for the regular allowances and increments admissible to a PSPCL employee.

For a 10th-pass ITI holder, this is a stable and respected government salary with strong long-term growth and job security in the power sector.

On top of the basic pay, PSPCL employees enjoy benefits like dearness allowance, medical facilities and a pension-secured future once their service is confirmed. For lakhs of ITI-pass youth in Punjab, a permanent Assistant Lineman post is a life-changing step that brings a steady monthly income, real career growth, and genuine social respect for the whole family.

Selection Process — How Are Candidates Selected?

Selection is through a Computer Based Online Test (CBT), followed by document checking, an offer of appointment, and 6 months of training. The test has a qualifying Punjabi language part that you must clear to be considered. You can study the exam pattern and syllabus guidance to prepare in a focused way.

Exam Pattern

The CBT has two parts and runs for 3 hours (180 minutes):

  • Part-I (Qualifying): 50 questions on Punjabi Language. You must score at least 50% (25 marks) to qualify; otherwise you are not considered further.
  • Part-II (Merit): 100 questions — Technical/concerned discipline (50), Punjabi Language Knowledge (20), General Knowledge (10), Reasoning (10), and Arithmetic (10).

Each question carries 1 mark and there is no negative marking. Merit is based on Part-II marks, with minimum qualifying marks of 25% for General and 20% for reserved categories. A useful tip: do not ignore the Punjabi qualifying part. Many strong candidates lose their seat only because they fail Part-I, so practise Punjabi alongside your technical subject and treat it as your first hurdle.

How to Apply Online for PSPCL Assistant Lineman (ITI) 2026?

The application is fully online through the PSPCL website. Follow these steps:

  1. Visit the official PSPCL recruitment portal (link in Essential Links below).
  2. Open the Assistant Lineman CRA 318/26 link and click New .
  3. Verify the OTP sent to your mobile and email.
  4. Fill the application form and upload your photo and signature in the given size.
  5. Pay the application fee online through the SBI Payment Gateway.
  6. Submit the form and print a copy for your records.

Apply early so a last-minute website rush does not stop you.

Important Dates — PSPCL Assistant Lineman (ITI) 2026

Keep these dates handy.

Application Fees — PSPCL Assistant Lineman (ITI) 2026

The fee (including GST) is shown below. Payment is online only and is non-refundable.

A small bank charge may apply in addition. No other mode of payment is accepted.

  • Download Official Notification PDF
  • Apply Online for PSPCL Assistant Lineman 2026
  • Visit PSPCL Official Website

Prepare your Punjabi and technical sections well, keep your documents ready, and apply online before 9 July 2026. jobDescription:
A Huge NAC-Route Opening for Future Linemen in Punjab

If you have completed your apprenticeship and hold a National Apprenticeship Certificate, this recruitment is made for you. Punjab State Power Corporation Limited (PSPCL) has invited applications for 3289 posts of Assistant Lineman (ALM) under the NAC-based route through Advertisement CRA 317/26. This is a separate, dedicated drive for NAC holders in the Lineman trade — with a large number of posts and a stable government pay scale.

PSPCL is one of Punjab’s most important power utilities, and Assistant Linemen play a hands-on role in maintaining the supply network. With 3289 vacancies, the chances are bright for eligible candidates. In this easy guide we explain the posts, eligibility, salary, exam pattern, fees, and how to apply step by step. To follow every Punjab government job, keep this GovtJobsAlert.in page open and read each section.

Key Highlights — PSPCL Assistant Lineman (NAC) 2026

The Assistant Lineman is a permanent Group-C field post with PSPCL. Here is a quick overview:

Vacancy Details — Category-wise Breakup
