Eligibility Criteria — PSPCL Assistant Lineman (NAC) 2026

Read the conditions carefully — this route is specifically for NAC holders, which is what makes it different from the ITI-based recruitment.

Educational Qualification

You must have passed Matriculation (10th) or equivalent and hold a National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) in Lineman Trade. You must also have passed Punjabi at least at Matriculation level by the last date of application. Candidates who have completed an apprenticeship or trade certificate are the right fit for this post. You should have obtained your NAC by the last date of online submission.

Age Limit

The age limit is 18 to 37 years as on 1 January 2026. Relaxation in upper age is allowed as per Punjab government rules — 5 years for SC and Backward Class, 10 years for PwD candidates, and special relaxation for Ex-Servicemen and certain women candidates (upper age up to 40 years).

Salary & Pay Scale — How Much Will You Earn?

The post carries a basic pay of ₹19,900 per month as per the 7th CPC / Pay Matrix. During the 3-year probation period, you receive the minimum admissible pay along with travelling allowance and medical reimbursement as per entitlement. After probation, the regular increments and allowances admissible to a PSPCL employee become applicable.

For a 10th-pass NAC holder, this is a strong, secure government income with excellent long-term stability in the power sector.

In addition to the basic pay, PSPCL provides allowances such as dearness allowance and medical benefits, and after confirmation you enjoy a pension-secured government career. For NAC holders who have already invested time in an apprenticeship, this recruitment turns that hard-earned training into a permanent, respectable job for life — exactly the kind of security most families in Punjab hope for.

Selection Process — How Are Candidates Selected?

Selection is through a Computer Based Online Test (CBT), followed by document checking, an offer of appointment, and on-the-job training. There is a compulsory qualifying Punjabi language part in the test. You can review the exam pattern and syllabus guidance before you start studying.

Exam Pattern

The CBT has two parts and runs for 3 hours (180 minutes):

  • Part-I (Qualifying): 50 questions on Punjabi Language. You must score at least 50% (25 marks) to qualify; otherwise you are not considered further.
  • Part-II (Merit): 100 questions — Technical/concerned discipline (50), Punjabi Language Knowledge (20), General Knowledge (10), Reasoning (10), and Arithmetic (10).

Each question carries 1 mark and there is no negative marking. Merit is prepared from Part-II marks, with minimum qualifying marks of 25% for General and 20% for reserved categories. A useful tip: the Punjabi qualifying paper decides everything. Clear it first, because even a top technical score is wasted if you miss the 50% Punjabi cut-off — so give it serious, regular practice.

How to Apply Online for PSPCL Assistant Lineman (NAC) 2026?

A&N Police Recruitment 2026: 498 Big Opportunity for SI, Constable & Other Posts in Andaman Police

Andaman & Nicobar Police has issued a major recruitment notice for 498 vacancies in Group-B (Non-Gazetted) and Group-C posts across six units — Executive Branch, Police Radio, Police Motor Transport, Police Marine Force, Fire & Emergency Services and India Reserve Battalion. Posts include Sub-Inspector, Constable (Executive), Firemen, Constable (Driver), Radio Operator, Radio Technician, Cipher Operator, Follower Cook, Mechanic, Lascar and many more. This is one of the biggest UT-level police recruitments of 2026, with strong 7th CPC pay (Rs.18,000 to Rs.1,12,400) and posting at Sri Vijaya Puram (Port Blair).

Online applications open on 27 April 2026 (11:00 hrs) and close on 17 May 2026 10 June 2026 20 June 2026 (Further Extended) (23:59 hrs). Candidates from Andaman & Nicobar Islands and other states across India can apply online at erecruitment.andamannicobar.gov.in. We at GovtJobsAlert.in have prepared this complete guide covering vacancy split unit-wise, eligibility, pay scale, selection process, and direct application . If you are looking for Andaman & Nicobar govt jobs, this is a great opportunity — read every section before applying.

Key Highlights — A&N Police Group B & C Recruitment 2026

Andaman & Nicobar Police is the law-enforcement agency for the Union Territory of Andaman & Nicobar Islands. Under Notification No. A-12024/1/2024-RECRUITMENTCELLPHQ-POLICE_AN/285 dated 24 April 2026, the department is filling 498 posts across six specialised units. Posts are open to candidates across India (some posts are male-only as per role demand).

  • Recruiting Body: Andaman & Nicobar Police, UT Administration
  • Total Vacancies: 498 (38 distinct post categories across 6 units)
  • Post Types: Sub-Inspector, ASI, Constable, Firemen, Driver, Radio Operator, Mechanic, Lascar, Greaser, Follower (Cook / Tailor / Barber / Carpenter / Washerman) etc.
  • Pay Range: Rs. 18,000 – Rs. 1,12,400 per month (7th CPC Pay Levels 1 to 6)
  • Job Location: Sri Vijaya Puram (Port Blair), Andaman & Nicobar Islands
  • Application Mode: Online only
  • Last Date to Apply: 17 May 2026 10 June 2026 20 June 2026 (Further Extended) (11:59 PM)

Vacancy Breakdown — A&N Police Recruitment 2026 (Unit-Wise)

The 498 vacancies are split across six units of A&N Police. Below is the unit-wise summary table — full post-wise details (with reservation) are in the official notification PDF.

The biggest single posts are Constable (Executive) — 155 vacancies, Firemen (PC) — 77 vacancies and Radio Operator (ASI) — 71 vacancies. Reservation is provided for OBC, SC, ST, EWS categories along with horizontal reservation for women, Ex-Servicemen and MSP candidates as per UT rules.

Eligibility Criteria — A&N Police Recruitment 2026

Eligibility differs post-wise. The complete press release covers broad rules; detailed qualification, age and physical standards are inside the full Recruitment Notice on the official A&N Police website.

Educational Qualification

Educational requirements vary by post. Refer to the official notification PDF for the exact qualification per role:

  • Sub-Inspector posts: Graduation in any discipline from a recognised university
  • Constable / Firemen / Driver: 10th or 12th pass from a recognised board (with valid driving licence for Driver posts)
  • Radio Operator / Technician / Cipher: 12th pass with relevant ITI / Diploma in Electronics, Radio or related trade
  • Follower posts (Cook, Tailor, Barber, Carpenter): 10th pass with trade experience

If you are a 12th pass candidate looking for government jobs, several Constable, Firemen and Follower posts are within reach.

Age Limit

Age limits vary by post and category. Detailed age criteria are listed in the full Recruitment Notice. Standard relaxations apply for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-Servicemen and MSP candidates as per UT rules. Some posts in Fire Service, IRBn combat, PMT, PMF and Follower Carpenter are open only to male candidates because of the operational nature of the role.

What is the Salary for A&N Police Recruitment 2026?

A&N Police pays as per the 7th CPC Pay Matrix with allowances applicable in the Union Territory. Salary depends on the pay level of the post:

  • Pay Level 6: Rs.35,400 – Rs.1,12,400 (Sub-Inspector grades, ASI ranks)
  • Pay Level 4: Rs.25,500 – Rs.81,100 (Dealing Assistant, Store Keeper)
  • Pay Level 3: Rs.21,700 – Rs.69,100 (Constable, Firemen, Driver class)
  • Pay Level 2: Rs.19,900 – Rs.63,200 (Nursing Assistant)
  • Pay Level 1: Rs.18,000 – Rs.56,900 (Followers, Safaikaramchari)

In-hand Salary Estimate: Rs.30,000 to Rs.55,000 per month for Constable / Firemen ranks at entry level (basic + DA + HRA + Island Special Duty Allowance).

Apart from the basic pay, A&N Police personnel get DA, HRA, Island Special Duty Allowance, medical benefits, ration allowance, family quarters (subject to availability), pension under NPS and other Central Government allowances. The job offers a stable career with promotions and inter-unit transfer options.

What is the Selection Process for A&N Police Recruitment 2026?

The selection happens in multiple stages and is fully merit-based. Candidates must clear each stage in order to move to the next.

  1. Physical Measurement & Endurance Test (PM&ET) — qualifying in nature only. Tests height, chest, weight and physical endurance like running, jumping etc. Must qualify to move forward.
  2. Written Examination — tab/computer-based or sheet-based (mode at A&N Police’s discretion). Conducted in English and/or Hindi only. Tests general awareness, reasoning, numerical ability and post-specific subjects.
  3. Trade Test — applicable for trade-specific posts (Mechanic, Driver, Carpenter, Tailor, Barber, Cook etc.). Tests practical skill in the trade.
  4. Document & Eligibility Verification — for shortlisted candidates only. Original certificates, age proof, caste/category certificate, driving licence (if applicable) verified.
  5. Final Merit List — based on Written Exam + Trade Test (where applicable). PM&ET marks are not added to final merit; they only screen.

Refer to the official syllabus and exam preparation pages for detailed pattern, sample questions and previous papers.

How to Apply Online for A&N Police Recruitment 2026?

Applications are accepted online only through the dedicated A&N Police e-recruitment portal. No offline or postal application will be accepted.

  1. Visit the official application portal at erecruitment.andamannicobar.gov.in
  2. Click on the recruitment link for “A&N Police Recruitment 2026” and register with a valid email ID and mobile number
  3. Fill out the application form carefully — choose your post preference (this is final and cannot be changed later)
  4. Upload scanned photograph, signature, education certificates, caste certificate (if applicable) and other documents in the prescribed format
  5. Pay the application fee online (if applicable as per category — refer to detailed Recruitment Notice for fee)
  6. Review every detail, submit the application and download the confirmation page for future reference

Important: Post preference once submitted is final. No subsequent change requests will be entertained, so think carefully about which unit and post you want before submitting.

Important Dates — A&N Police Recruitment 2026

Mark your calendar and apply at least 3-4 days before the deadline to avoid last-day server issues.

Application Fees — A&N Police Recruitment 2026

The press release does not specify the application fee in detail. Detailed fee structure (category-wise) is provided in the full Recruitment Notice on the official website. Standard fee structure for A&N Administration recruitment is as below — please verify with the official notification PDF before payment.

Payment must be made online through the e-recruitment portal using net banking, debit card, credit card or UPI. The fee is non-refundable.

  • Download Official Notification PDF
  • Apply Online for A&N Police Recruitment 2026
  • Visit A&N Police Official Website
